## ¬ß563 banked seeds ‚Äî 2026-06-26 (cycle 563 ¬∑ ADULT ¬∑ WIDE non-winners, both fully built + builder-reported lint/tsc-clean, then de-selected)

WIDE adult fire: 3 orthogonal explorers, each a **non-pointer input into a real physical-modeling instrument body** (answering JURY 2026-06-26 #2 "make the SOUND the resonating object, not a GPU-sim screensaver" + #3 "force a non-pointer input"; dodging every ban: pointer-drag-primary ¬∑ cosmic-glow ¬∑ GPU-sim-pulse-bell). Shipped **`970-tension-gong`** (keyboard/MIDI ‚Üí non-linear modal struck-metal; lab-first technique + the only no-hardware-dependency build ‚Üí the directest dent in the verification-debt liability). The two below returned complete + builder-reported lint/tsc-clean (READMEs written); `rm -rf`'d before the authoritative winner-only build (NOT committed) ‚Äî rebuild from these briefs.

### `971-air-column` ‚≠ê RESURRECT-FIRST (adult) ‚Äî `queued`
**For: adult / live-performance.** *"What if you could play a wind instrument with your breath and your hands in the air ‚Äî no contact, no mouse ‚Äî and the sound came from a REAL model of air resonating in a tube?"* A contactless **blown digital-waveguide flute**: the sound is neither oscillator nor sample but a **fractional-delay-line bore** carrying pressure waves up and down, reflecting sign-inverted + lowpassed at the open end (round-trip delay = pitch), driven by a **non-linear jet exciter** (jet-delay line + cubic `jet ‚àí jet¬≥` saturation + breath noise + vibrato) that makes the tube **self-oscillate** into a stable flute tone with no oscillator ‚Äî and **overblow up an octave** when breath pressure passes a threshold, exactly like a real flute jumps register. **The lab's FIRST blown air column** (every prior physical string is plucked Karplus ‚Äî never a blown waveguide w/ jet). **INPUT** = the **webcam**, hand-rolled optical flow (no MediaPipe/TF.js): each frame downsampled to a 32√ó24 grid, frame-to-frame luminance diff summed ‚Üí **motion energy = breath pressure**; horizontal **motion centroid = pitch** quantized to **D Dorian** across two octaves (move more = blow harder/overblow; move where = which note). Graceful: no camera ‚Üí `text-rose-300` notice + **keyboard fallback** (number row = notes, hold = breath, Space = blow harder/overblow) so a no-camera reviewer still hears it; no AudioWorklet ‚Üí triangle-osc voice. **OUTPUT** Canvas2D (warm wooden pipe + standing-wave node pattern + schlieren mouth-turbulence + dim motion silhouette) ¬∑ **TECH** digital-waveguide air column + non-linear jet self-oscillation + hand-rolled optical flow ¬∑ **VIBE** warm wood/paper/reed-amber (off cosmic-glow). Refs: **Julius O. Smith III, *Physical Audio Signal Processing*** (waveguide flute/slide-flute/blown-bottle) ¬∑ **Perry R. Cook, *Real Sound Synthesis*** (STK flute/jet). Ambition 3/5 (#1 never-used technique [blown waveguide] ¬∑ #2 ‚â•3 subsystems [optical flow + waveguide DSP + jet + Canvas viz] ¬∑ #3 named refs). **De-selected ONLY because** 970 hit a tighter research‚Üíbuild chain (today's ¬ß563 nlm paper) + zero hardware dependency (the verification-debt win Karel's jury keeps asking for), whereas 971's camera path can't be exercised in-box and self-oscillating jet models are notoriously finicky (risk it doesn't reliably sing). **Resurrect FIRST** on the next adult cycle ‚Äî it's *also* the camera-sensor revival the jury wanted (#3), and a contactless live-performance instrument; hand-verify the jet self-oscillation + overblow on a real device. Honest warts (builder): the jet stability is hand-tuned and untestable in-box; the on-screen standing wave is drawn from pitch+amplitude, not read from the delay buffer.

### `972-resonant-bodies` ‚Äî `queued`
**For: adult.** *"What if any sound you make ‚Äî a tap on your desk, a breath, a hiss, your voice ‚Äî could PLAY a physical object, ringing it like a struck bell, glass, or wooden gourd?"* A cross-modal **exciter‚Üíresonator** instrument: the microphone is used **not** as a pitch source and **not** as a recorder but as the raw **excitation energy** driving a bank of tuned modal resonators. A sharp **tap** is a near-impulse ‚Üí clean struck-bell attack; sustained **noise** (breath/hiss) re-excites the modes ‚Üí a bowed/blown shimmer. Four bodies, each a real modal spectrum **also tuned to a just-intoned A-minor chord**: **bell metal** (clustered inharmonic, long ring) ¬∑ **wine glass** (one dominant near-sine + faint overtones ‚Äî why a rubbed glass sings) ¬∑ **wood gourd** (1:4:10 stretch, fast dry decay) ¬∑ **ceramic bowl** (bright mid cluster). The SAME tap rings each differently. **INPUT** = **mic-as-exciter** (primary, non-pitch-detection) + keyboard (`1‚Äì4` body, `‚Üë‚Üì`/`[]` transpose, `S` strike, Space mute) ‚Äî no pointer-drag. **TECH** AudioWorklet bank of high-Q Chamberlin SVF band-pass resonators (mic ‚Üí worklet input ‚Üí modes ‚Üí sum); native `BiquadFilterNode` bandpass-bank fallback if no worklet ¬∑ **OUTPUT** Canvas2D (each resonator a ring/plate/bar pulsing with its live mode amplitude ‚Äî you see the object ring where you hear it) ¬∑ **VIBE** material/earthy (brass/glass-teal/wood/ceramic ‚Äî off cosmic-glow). **Feedback-safe:** `DynamicsCompressor` limiter (ratio 12, fast attack) + conservative gain + lowpass + headphones notice + instant Space-mute; no mic ‚Üí `text-rose-300` notice + built-in noise-burst Strike exciter so it still rings. Refs: **Andy Farnell, *Designing Sound*** (modal/resonator synthesis, exciter‚Äìresonator decomposition) ¬∑ Perry Cook modal shaker/resonator models. Ambition 2‚Äì3/5 (#2 ‚â•3 subsystems [mic capture + modal bank + material switching + viz] ¬∑ #3 named ref). **De-selected because** its core (a modal resonator/biquad-bandpass bank) is the closest of the three to ground the lab has covered (960-friction's modal body; prior filterbanks), and the mic‚Üíspeaker feedback path is a real hazard to ship unheard; 970 and 971 both offered a more clearly novel technique. **Resurrect** on a cycle wanting a tactile mic-as-instrument piece (the "play objects with any sound" idea is genuinely charming) ‚Äî best paired with a real-device hand-verify for the feedback safety. Honest warts (builder): modal frequencies are musically curated approximations, not measured impulse responses; feedback safety is reasoned, not bench-tested.

### `967-kids-home-hill` ‚≠ê RESURRECT-FIRST (kids) ‚Äî `queued`
**For: kids (4+).** *"What if a 4-year-old could FEEL the pull of musical home ‚Äî tilt the tablet to roll a glowing firefly across a meadow, and real chords pull it back toward home the way gravity pulls a ball downhill?"* The directest build of the ¬ß562 embodied-harmony research (Song Walker / kinetic tonal pedagogy): a **tonal-gravity field**. The meadow is divided into big soft regions = real C-major functions (**I=C/E/G home valley** with a constant gentle pull ¬∑ vi=A/C/E ¬∑ IV=F/A/C ¬∑ **V7=G/B/D/F = the uphill tension hill**, leading-tone B straining). Tilt rolls the firefly (velocity+friction physics); home-pull means whenever the child stops tilting it drifts back to HOME ‚Äî they *feel* music wanting to resolve. Sitting on the V builds an audible leading-tone tension (orange flicker, worried face, swelling B shimmer); tilting back lets it **swoop home and resolve** (C/E/G bell chimes, nest glows) ‚Äî an embodied, hands-caused V‚ÜíI a 4yo causes with no reading. **INPUT** device **tilt** (`DeviceOrientationEvent`, iOS-gated behind Start; pointer-drag silent fallback w/ `text-rose-300` notice) ¬∑ **OUTPUT** Canvas2D (warm sunrise meadow ‚Äî bulletproof phone render + fresh surface) ¬∑ **TECH** functional-harmony **tonal-gravity field** + nearest-tone diatonic voice-leading + warm triangle/sine partial synth w/ portamento glide + low C2/G2 drone (never silent) ¬∑ **VIBE** warm storybook sunrise. Master gain‚â§0.28‚Üílowpass 7k‚Üícomp, 1/‚àövoices norm; idle vi‚ÜíIV‚ÜíV‚ÜíI auto-demo after ~3s; full teardown. Files built (839 lines): `page.tsx` (457) + `harmony.ts` (178: real functions + `tonalGravity` + `voiceLead` + `tension`) + `audio.ts` (204) + README. Refs: **Song Walker** (embodied harmony interaction) ¬∑ **AREmbody** (AR tonal-harmony app) ¬∑ **Fred Lerdahl, *Tonal Pitch Space*** (tonal gravity) ¬∑ Aldwell & Schachter, *Harmony and Voice Leading*. Ambition 3/5 (#2 ‚â•3 subsystems ¬∑ #3 named refs ¬∑ #5 RESEARCH ¬ß562). **De-selected ONLY because** 969 hit a higher ambition (4/5, criterion #1 a never-used technique) with the rarer input (voice vs tilt ‚Äî tilt is already 3√ó in the audit) and the more surprising concept; 967 is the *cleanest, lowest-risk, most-legible* embodied-harmony build and the directest realization of the research. **Resurrect FIRST** on the next kids cycle wanting a guaranteed-everywhere render + a calm physical toy, or fold its tonal-gravity field into an adult "walk-through harmony terrain." Honest warts (builder): real on-device tilt + the iOS permission prompt couldn't be exercised in-box; tilt mapping assumes a roughly portrait hold.

### `968-kids-shake-box` ‚Äî `queued`
**For: kids (4+).** *"What if winding a magic music box were how a child plays a real chord progression ‚Äî shake the tablet to crank it one chord forward through a genuine I‚Äìvi‚ÄìIV‚ÄìV‚ÄìI cadence, and the music swells with how hard you shake?"* A whole-body cadence sequencer: each **shake-burst** (accel ‚â≥11 m/s¬≤, 300ms refractory) cranks a glowing music-box cylinder one notch and strikes the next chord; the progression loops **I‚Üívi‚ÜíIV‚ÜíV‚ÜíI**, so repeated shaking walks a real cadence over and over (*home ‚Üí wander ‚Üí rising ‚Üí **pull‚Ä¶** ‚Üí **HOME!***). The **V is dramatized as tension** (shorter/tenser voicing, leaning white-hot pins) and the returning **I as a long octave-doubled bloom-and-settle** ‚Äî going-away-and-coming-home a 4yo plays with their body. Shake harder ‚Üí more particles, brighter glow, fuller octave-doubled voicing; gentle ‚Üí soft; stillness ‚Üí tonic sustains over a soft drone. **INPUT** device **shake** (`DeviceMotionEvent`, iOS-gated behind Start; full-screen tap-pad fallback only, `text-rose-300` notice) ¬∑ **OUTPUT** raw-WebGL2 (hand-written fragment shader: turning 16-pin ring of light, per-pin flares, energy-driven warm particles; Canvas2D `drawFallback` if no WebGL2) ¬∑ **TECH** real I‚Äìvi‚ÄìIV‚ÄìV‚ÄìI cadence state machine + greedy nearest-tone diatonic voice-leading + struck-metal music-box synth (sine+triangle+inharmonic shimmer) w/ energy‚Üídynamics mapping ¬∑ **VIBE** warm golden toy-shop / music-box. Master gain‚â§0.28‚Üílowpass 7.5k‚Üícomp, ‚àö-voice norm; idle auto-demo cadence after 3s; full teardown. Files built (1197 lines): `page.tsx` (484) + `progression.ts` (153: cadence machine + `voiceLead` + tension/resolved flags) + `audio.ts` (301) + `gl.ts` (259, all GL nullables guarded) + README. Refs: music-box / barrel-organ mechanical-sequencer lineage ¬∑ Aldwell & Schachter, *Harmony and Voice Leading* ¬∑ the cadence as tonal music's fundamental tension-resolution gesture. Ambition 3/5 (#2 ‚â•3 subsystems ¬∑ #3 named refs ¬∑ #5 RESEARCH ¬ß562). **De-selected because** 969 and 967 both edged it: 969 on input-rarity + ambition, 967 on render-bulletproofness for a phone-reviewed kids piece; the shake input is genuinely fresh (1√ó in the audit) and worth resurrecting. **Resurrect** on a kids cycle wanting a physical/energetic toy or a WebGL2-glow surface. ### `966-osci-solids` ‚≠ê RESURRECT-FIRST (adult) ‚Äî `queued`
**For: adults.** The "massively bigger" sibling of `965-phosphor-draw`: instead of drawing a flat 2D shape, a **rotating 3D wireframe solid** (cube / tetra / octa / torus / trefoil knot / 3D star) is projected to 2D and traced as **one continuous pen-path**, then emitted as the stereo signal ‚Äî so you **literally HEAR a 3D object spinning** as you watch it draw itself on the green phosphor scope. Faces turning toward/away morph the timbre (sharp solids = bright/buzzy, torus/knot = rounder). Files built this fire (all tsc/eslint-clean): `page.tsx` (Start gate, pointer drag-rotate, solid picker, pitch/spin/perspective sliders, autoplay cube, teardown) + `geometry.ts` (the 6 solids as verts+edges + `buildStrokeOrder` greedy single-stroke Eulerian-ish walk) + `projection.ts` (hand-written 3√ó3 Euler rotation + blendable perspective divide + arc-length resample to 1024-pt loop, no three.js) + `audio-engine.ts` (stereo `AudioBuffer` ch0=X/ch1=Y, `loop=true`, rebuilt ~25√ó/s with crossfade between two looping sources, 18Hz DC-block, master peak 0.28) + `scope.ts` (Canvas2D phosphor: alpha-fade persistence + additive `lighter` glow + speed-modulated brightness + CRT grid) + README. Ambition 4/5 (#1 vector/oscilloscope synth as core [grep-0√ó shipped] ¬∑ #2 ‚â•3 subsystems [3D projection + single-stroke ordering + stereo synth + phosphor render] ¬∑ #3 named refs [Hansi Raber OsciStudio/osci-render; Jerobeam Fenderson; Ryoji Ikeda data-cosm 2026; Lissajous 1857] ¬∑ #5 RESEARCH ¬ß561). **De-selected ONLY because** 965 is (a) the more *interactive instrument* ‚Äî you DRAW, directly extending Karel's strongest loved cluster (`223-fourier-paint`‚ù§Ô∏è, `153-paint-compose`‚ù§Ô∏è, `100`/`152`/`158`/`160`‚ù§Ô∏è), and (b) the *cleaner* realization of the "signal = image" thesis: 965's `playbackRate`-only pitch never rebuilds the buffer, so the scope is sample-for-sample identical to the audio *continuously*, whereas 966 rebuilds + crossfades ~25√ó/s (the builder's own honest caveat: timbre updates in small steps on very fast manual spins), and (c) 965's WebGL2 float-feedback phosphor is a richer CRT glow than 966's Canvas2D. **Resurrect as the cycle-2 deepening of the oscilloscope thread** ‚Äî the natural "massively bigger" extension (add arbitrary 3D, or trace **Karel's initials / a piano** in 3D so it spins and sings; consider an AudioWorklet that rotates X/Y per-sample to kill the 25Hz stepping entirely). Honest wart: the greedy single-stroke walk leaves a few faint pen-lift lines on some solids (audible as faint clicks).

## ¬ß557 banked seeds ‚Äî 2026-06-26 (cycle 557 ¬∑ ADULT ¬∑ WIDE non-winners, BOTH fully built + self-reported tsc/eslint-clean, then de-selected)

The two below returned complete and build-clean; **rm -rf'd before the authoritative winner-only build (NOT committed)** ‚Äî rebuild from these briefs. Both are strong stand-alone adult pieces.

- **`955-cadence-river` ‚≠ê (resurrect-first adult)** ‚Äî *"What if music could compose its own endless chorale?"* A **generative functional-harmony engine** (the lab's first): a weighted T‚ÜíPD‚ÜíD‚Üíresolution state machine with four cadence types, **secondary dominants used as pivots** to modulate around the circle of fifths, and a key/Roman-numeral **memory** so it never loops ‚Äî a long-form stateful piece (different at min 6 than min 1). Realizes each chord as **SATB voice-leading** (nearest-tone motion + prepared/resolved suspensions + parallel-5/8 avoidance), voiced by warm additive organ + synth-convolution reverb (NOT granular). **INPUT** keyboard-nudge (autonomous + ‚Üë‚Üì bright/dark, ‚Üê‚Üí chromaticism, Space cadence-now, [ ] tempo) ¬∑ **OUTPUT** raw-WebGL2 four function-colored voice-ribbons flowing through pitch√ótime (Canvas2D fallback) ¬∑ **TECH** functional-harmony grammar + voice-leading + modulation (long-form) ¬∑ **VIBE** warm cathedral-dusk. Refs: Aldwell & Schachter *Harmony and Voice Leading* ¬∑ Schoenberg *Theory of Harmony* ¬∑ Tymoczko *A Geometry of Music* (2011); the deliberate **non-ML counterpoint** to 2026 neural harmonizers (arXiv 2511.14600 / 2506.18143). Builder-verified: a 200-chord dry run made proper progressions, correctly-spelled secondaries, all 4 cadence types, suspensions resolving down by step, ~10 keys visited, bass never crossing soprano. Ambition 4/5 (#1 first functional-harmony grammar ¬∑ #2 ‚â•4 subsystems ¬∑ #3 named refs ¬∑ long-form-commitment + #5 ¬ß557). **Why banked, not shipped:** the most *on-mandate* harmony build and the directest "workspace for composers" answer, BUT it's the more *expected* answer to #2 (a generative chorale is a known idea) on the non-scarce WebGL2 surface, whereas 954 also cracks JURY #1's raw-WebGPU-compute gap with a more surprising concept. Honest wart (builder): voice-leading is greedy/heuristic (parallel check only bass‚Üîsoprano) ‚Äî competent student chorale, not Bach. **Resurrect-first** on the next adult cycle wanting a warm, legible harmony piece, or as cycle-2 of a multi-cycle "the lab learns to compose" thread (add dual-Roman pivot analysis on-screen, 4‚Äì3 suspension chains, a V‚ÄìI pedal point, and snap the ribbon axis to the live key's scale degrees so a modulation visibly re-grids pitch space).

- **`956-counterpoint-weave`** ‚Äî *"What if you drew ONE melody and the music wove three more around it ‚Äî by the actual rules of species counterpoint?"* The lab's first **species-counterpoint** engine (harmony from the *horizontal* weave, the inverse of 955's vertical grammar). You draw a slow contour; it resamples to a diatonic **cantus firmus** and a **beam search** (width 6) generates 1‚Äì3 companion voices under **Fux** rules ‚Äî *enforced* (verified node-side, 0 violations): strong-beat consonance (P4 treated as dissonance between voices), no parallel 5/8, perfect consonances by contrary/oblique motion only; *rewarded*: imperfect consonances, contrary motion, stepwise lines, single climax. Implements **1st species** solidly + **2nd** (passing tones, weak-beat dissonance) + **4th** (prepared‚Üítied‚Üídissonant‚Üístep-resolved **suspensions**; 7 genuine ones on the demo CF). **INPUT** pointer-draw-contour ¬∑ **OUTPUT** Canvas2D woven luminous threads on a parchment/Bauhaus ground (colored strong-beat interval nodes, magenta suspension links, flashing `‚à•5/8 ‚úó` rejections ‚Äî the rules read sound-off) ¬∑ **TECH** generative species counterpoint + additive/subtractive synth ¬∑ **VIBE** Renaissance/Bauhaus weave. Refs: Fux *Gradus ad Parnassum* (1725) ¬∑ Palestrina ¬∑ Bach two-part inventions. Ambition 3/5 (#1 first species-counterpoint ¬∑ #2 ‚â•3 subsystems ¬∑ #3 named refs + #5 ¬ß557). **Why banked:** the most *legible* and tactile of the three (you see the rules fire), BUT Canvas2D is the least-scarce surface and "draw a line ‚Üí music" sits nearer existing draw-melody pieces; 954 was the scarcer-surface, higher-surprise pick. Honest wart (builder): companion voices are policed only against the CF, not each other (banding hides it, but it's not true 3-part counterpoint); minor-mode cadences stay modal (no raised leading tone). **Resurrect** on a kids OR adult cycle wanting a tactile theory-made-visible instrument, or deepen by making the beam search *joint across all voices* (score the full vertical sonority + inter-voice motion per bar) and add cadential leading-tone sharpening.

## ¬ß555 banked seed ‚Äî 2026-06-26 (cycle 555 ¬∑ ADULT ¬∑ DEEP non-winner, fully built then de-selected)

DEEP adult cycle: 2 Canvas2D approaches to ONE concept ‚Äî a long-form self-developing **Arabic maqam taqsim** in exact cents (chain RESEARCH ¬ß555 / AMICOR 2026 ‚Üí the named gap of maqam-native microtone synthesis). Shipped **`952-maqam-calligraphy`** (the line written across a cents-aware field). The twin below returned **complete + self-reported clean** (engine verified accurate by reading its `maqam.ts`); backed up to `scratchpad/dream-losers-555/` and `rm -rf`'d before the authoritative winner-only build (NOT committed) ‚Äî rebuild from this brief.

- **`951-maqam-road` ‚≠ê (resurrect-first adult)** ‚Äî *"What if you could watch a maqam taqsim as a road winding through a landscape ‚Äî sayr literally means 'the road'?"* Same engine as the shipped 952 (exact-cents ajnas Rast/Bayati/Hijaz/Nahawand/Kurd/Saba/Ajam/Sikah + 5 maqamat as lower+upper jins on the ghammaz + adjacency map + 8-stage sayr `qarar-open‚Üílower‚Üíascent‚Üíghammaz‚Üímodulation‚Üípeak‚Üídescent‚Üíqarar-rest` over ~5.5min + free-meter phrase generator with portamento/grace/lean/held + plucked-oud + tanbura drone, master gain ‚â§0.26), but the VISUAL is a **road receding into a warm dusk**: the road's vertical lift tracks pitch register, each note drops a glowing **lantern waypoint** that ages/fades, the active **jins colors the terrain**, **modulations are gateway sweeps / forks**, the current note is a **traveler** whose portamento draws a continuous luminous curved trail, and faint dashed guide-lines mark the neutral quarter-tones. **INPUT** autonomous + tap-to-nudge-next-modulation / **OUTPUT** Canvas2D (the only non-banned renderer this window ‚Äî WebGPU + WebGL2 both hit the 5√ó/10 audit cap) / **TECH** exact-cents maqam-sayr modulation (melodic/modal ‚Äî NOT voice-leading) / **VIBE** warm desert-dusk lantern ritual. Ambition 4/5 (#1 first non-Western maqam modal system ¬∑ #2 ‚â•4 subsystems ¬∑ #3 maqam tradition / sayr / AMICOR 2026 / 1932 Cairo Congress ¬∑ #5 ¬ß555). **Why banked, not shipped:** equally strong engine and a genuinely elegant "sayr = the road" metaphor, but 952's calligraphy lens makes pitch *visibly* exact (12-TET ghost grid vs gold quarter-tone lines = the literal "see the quarter-tones between the cracks" payoff), is the fresher visual register (951's traveler-on-a-path echoes prior journey/terrain pieces 718/marble-run), and the Arabic-calligraphy‚ÜîArabic-maqam pairing is the more surprising cross-modal idea. **Resurrect when** a warm narrative/calm adult cycle is wanted, or as the **cycle-2 deepening** of a multi-cycle maqam thread (e.g. fold the two lenses together: a road whose roadside *also* shows the cents-ruler; or add a real oud sample-bank, an Iqa' rhythmic-cycle mode, or a 53-EDO toggle).

## ¬ß552 banked seeds ‚Äî 2026-06-25 (cycle 552 ¬∑ KIDS ¬∑ WIDE non-winners, fully built then de-selected)

WIDE kids cycle: 3 orthogonal explorers, all making **PITCH/melody/harmony the playable idea** (JURY 2026-06-25 #2) on Canvas2D / hand-written WebGL2 (NOT three.js), non-granular synths, touch input. Shipped **`946-kids-bird-round`** (canon/round). The two below returned **complete + self-reported eslint-0** (READMEs written), `rm -rf`'d before the authoritative winner-only build (NOT committed) ‚Äî rebuild from these briefs.

- **`944-kids-melody-coaster` ‚≠ê (resurrect-first kids)** ‚Äî *"What if a 4-year-old could DRAW a melody with their finger and hear it sing?"* The child draws a hilly track L‚ÜíR; a cheerful glowing cart then rolls along it (steady-speed travel + a little bob) and **SINGS the melody traced by the track's HEIGHT** ‚Äî high hill = high note, valley = low note ‚Äî snapped to a C-major pentatonic so any scribble is musical. The drawn **contour literally IS the tune** (grounded in the developmental finding that melodic contour is the single most salient melodic feature for young children ‚Äî RESEARCH ¬ß552). **INPUT** touch-draw (pointer too) ¬∑ **OUTPUT** hand-written WebGL2 (sky/sun/grass/glowing track ribbon via a height texture + cart halo + finger-glow + sparkles in one fragment pass) with a faithful Canvas2D fallback ¬∑ **TECH** `resampleHeights` (path ‚Üí 24 columns) + `heightToHz` (diatonic snap) + a bright "loo" travelling voice (triangle+sine octave, light vibrato, pluck env) + quiet C/G drone ¬∑ **VIBE** bright primary-color playground. Master chain gain‚â§0.3‚Üílowpass 7k‚Üícompressor; ~2s idle auto-demo draws a default track + rolls it; full teardown. Refs: melodic-contour developmental finding ¬∑ **Crayon Physics Deluxe** (Petri Purho) draw-and-roll ¬∑ Kod√°ly contour pedagogy. Ambition 2/5 (#2 four subsystems [path sampler + contour-to-pitch mapper + travelling synth + WebGL2 render] + #3 named refs). **Why banked, not shipped:** the most *legible* 4yo delight and strongest research grounding, but "draw a melody" is the lab's most familiar kids territory; 946's canon was the more novel, less-repeated, love-aligned pick. **Resurrect when** a low-risk, instantly-graspable bright kids delight is wanted, or to seed a contour-drawing instrument for the adult lane.
- **`945-kids-harmony-garden`** ‚Äî *"What if a 4-year-old could PLANT chords and SEE which notes are friends?"* Tap a sunny field to plant glowing note-flowers (each = a C-major scale degree); the garden **sings the chord**, and flowers whose pitches form a **consonant interval grow light-thread "friend-vines"** + bloom bigger while clashing notes lean apart & stay small; as flowers are added/removed the voices **nearest-tone voice-lead** (glide, not jump). **Harmony + voice-leading IS the idea** ‚Äî the *directest* answer to JURY #2 and squarely on Karel's "workspace for composers" line, kids edition. **INPUT** touch-tap (tap a flower to remove) ¬∑ **OUTPUT** Canvas2D (the jury wants Canvas2D back ‚Äî sky/sun/swaying grass/petal flowers/quadratic glowing vines/sparkles) ¬∑ **TECH** `harmony.ts` interval-class consonance ranking (`consonanceOf`/`areFriends`) + greedy nearest-tone `assignVoiceLeading` + a 6-voice warm additive pad (triangle+detuned-sine partials, slow attack, shared vibrato, portamento re-voicing) ¬∑ **VIBE** sunny garden. Master gain‚â§0.26‚Üílowpass 7k‚Üícompressor, ‚àön loudness-normalized; ~2s idle auto-demo plants C‚ÄìE‚ÄìG one flower at a time; full teardown. Refs: **Aldwell & Schachter, *Harmony and Voice Leading*** ¬∑ Helmholtz interval consonance ¬∑ **Toca Boca** open-play. Ambition 2/5 (#2 four subsystems [consonance engine + voice-leading re-voicer + additive pad synth + Canvas2D bloom render] + #3 named refs). **Why banked, not shipped:** the most on-mandate harmony build, BUT last kids cycle 941-choir-bloom *also* shipped drag-to-pitch + voice-leading harmony ‚Äî two consecutive kids voice-leading pieces would read as "similar" (the exact critique the jury raised). **Resurrect when** the kids lane hasn't done harmony recently, or to deepen into a "build-a-chord-progression garden" (rows = bars, a playhead sweeps the planted chords into a progression).

The two below returned **complete + self-reported tsc-clean + eslint-0** (READMEs written); `cp`'d to `scratchpad/dream-losers-549/` and `rm -rf`'d before the authoritative winner-only build (NOT committed) ‚Äî rebuild from these briefs.

### `938-morphogenesis` ‚≠ê RESURRECT-FIRST (adult) ‚Äî `queued` (WebGPU compute ¬∑ the SCARCEST GPU surface in the lab right now)
**For: adult / organic-morphogenetic.** Music from a **living chemical pattern**: a **Gray-Scott reaction-diffusion** Turing system breeds spots/stripes/labyrinths on the GPU (256¬≤, 8 sub-steps/frame, ping-pong storage); pointer-drag *drips reagent* to seed growth; the evolving **texture** of the field ‚Äî activity level, edge/turbulence energy, change-rate, and 4√ó4 region activity ‚Äî is read back (async on WebGPU, sync on WebGL2 fallback, every 2nd/3rd frame), downsampled, and mapped to **granular-texture synthesis**: grain density, bandpass brightness, stereo pan over a dumb-pentatonic drone. **Primary path is a true WGSL `@compute` shader** (the scarcest GPU surface ‚Äî only 1√ó/10 in the audit, vs three.js banned at 4√ó and raw-WebGL2 warm at 3√ó) with a genuine **same-equation raw-WebGL2 fragment ping-pong fallback** (no three.js, no Canvas2D). F/k presets switch regimes (spots / coral / labyrinth / mitosis) ‚Äî each *sounds* different because the texture statistics differ. Bioluminescent teal/violet substrate with glowing chemical fronts; Anadol-ish flowing pigment. **De-selected ONLY because** 939's space-weather concept out-surprised it on the Karel-facing axis this fire ‚Äî NOT on diversity (938 is actually the *cleanest* surface dodge) or quality. **938's value:** (a) it is the directest answer to JURY #1 ("GPU is the scarce surface ‚Äî swing to WebGPU compute"); (b) reaction-diffusion is near-lab-first (texture-as-music, not pitch); (c) clean types ‚Äî **no `@ts-nocheck` needed** (WebGPU resolves via `@webgpu/types`, same idiom as 932), so it's build-safe today. **Resurrect first on the next adult fire where a fresh GPU-compute surface is wanted.** Ambition 3/5 (#2 ‚â•4 subsystems [pointer-seed + WGSL/WebGL2 RD solver + GPU feature-readback + granular synth] + #3 named refs [Turing 1952 / Pearson 1993 / Codrops-Tympanus WebGPU RD 2024] + #5 dated RESEARCH ¬ß549). Honest warts (builder-reported): feature-normalization constants (√ó3.5/√ó22/√ó40) are hand-tuned and may want real-hardware balancing; the WebGPU readback path couldn't be exercised headless (logic verified by inspection). Files built this fire: page.tsx + README.

### `937-cathedral-phase` ‚Äî `queued` (raw-WebGL2 raymarched SDF ¬∑ keyboard ¬∑ the cleanest rhythm-not-pitch build)
**For: adult / ritual-sacred.** "What if a cathedral were a **rhythm machine**?" Five **Euclidean** rhythm voices (Bjorklund: E(3,8), E(5,8), E(7,16), E(4,9), E(2,5)) on a shared 6s base cycle, each striking one minor-pentatonic stone tone; each voice has a **per-voice tempo multiplier (0.85‚Äì1.15)** so off-unison the patterns drift **out of phase** over tens of seconds and slowly re-converge ‚Äî **Steve Reich's *Piano Phase* process made architectural** ‚Äî then snap back to unison on `R`. INPUT: **computer keyboard** (1‚Äì5 toggle voices, ‚Üê/‚Üí select a ring, ‚Üë/‚Üì or +/‚àí nudge phase, R/0 re-align, space transport) + pointer-click ring step-cells (cold input, off banned touch/tilt/mic/camera). OUTPUT: **raw-WebGL2 raymarched SDF vaulted nave** (domain-repeated columns/arches/groin-vault, soft shadows, warm candle-amber light bands that flare on each voice's onsets; no three.js/Canvas2D). All voices feed a **synthesized cathedral convolution reverb** (discrete early reflections + ~4.6s exponential noise tail) so the stone space rings. Deep stone-indigo + amber, reverent/hypnotic. Degrades to audio-only + amber notice if no WebGL2. **De-selected because** 939 (space weather) and 938 (WebGPU compute, scarcer surface) edged it on surprise and surface-freshness respectively ‚Äî but it is the *cleanest* JURY-#3 answer (music purely from rhythm/phasing/space, pitch held dumb) and the ritual/cathedral vibe is absent from the last 10. **Resurrect when raw-WebGL2 is cold and a ritual/rhythm piece is wanted.** Ambition 3/5 (#2 ‚â•4 subsystems [keyboard + Euclidean/Reich phasing engine + convolution-reverb space + WebGL2 raymarch render] + #3 named refs [Reich *Piano Phase* 1967 / Toussaint 2005 / Inigo Quilez raymarching] + #5 dated RESEARCH ¬ß549). Honest wart (builder-reported): the SDF groin-vault is a smooth-min'd torus approximation ‚Äî "suggestive cathedral" more than architecturally precise. Files built this fire: page.tsx + euclid.ts (Bjorklund) + README.

### `930-kids-tilt-tide` ‚≠ê RESURRECT-FIRST (kids) ‚Äî `queued`
**For: kids (4+).** The SAME tilt‚Üísea-of-light‚Üípooling-bells concept as the shipped `931-kids-tide-pool`, but solved on the **CPU** (a 56√ó56 height grid, virtual-pipes flux per-edge `fR`/`fD`, two solver steps/frame) and rendered with **three.js** (PlaneGeometry vertex displacement, warm-cool height-shaded glow ‚Äî deep indigo, pooled gold ‚Äî slow camera drift). INPUT device-tilt (`DeviceOrientationEvent`, iOS `requestPermission()` gated in the Start tap) ‚Üí gravity vector ¬∑ OUTPUT three.js GPU water sheet ¬∑ TECH virtual-pipes shallow-water (Mei/Decaudin/Hu 2007) flow ‚Üí pooling rising-edge triggers bells (fixed warm pentatonic {C4 D4 E4 G4 A4 C5 D5 E5}, NO harmony engine, ~0.22s per-lily cooldown) ¬∑ VIBE serene moonlit tide pool. Master gain 0.24 ‚Üí 6kHz lowpass ‚Üí DynamicsCompressor, always-on C2+G2 pad; pointer-drag + idle auto-demo fallback (sounding/showing glance within ~0.6s with no sensor); `text-rose-300` on permission-denied/no-WebGL. **De-selected ONLY because** 931's all-GPU solve is the fresher output surface (three.js appeared 2√ó in last-10 kids ‚Äî 924/918; raw-WebGL2 is scarcer) and the directest JURY-#1 "GPU is the scarce surface" answer. **930 is the MOST BULLETPROOF of the two** (no float-texture dependency ‚Äî pure three.js mesh + JS solver runs anywhere) ‚Äî **resurrect-first on the next kids cycle that wants a no-float-texture-risk render path**, or fold its three.js height-shaded look into 931 as a richer visual. Ambition 3/5 (#2 ‚â•3 subsystems [tilt + virtual-pipes solver + bell scheduler + three.js render] + #3 named ref [virtual-pipes / Mei 2007] + #5 dated RESEARCH ¬ß546). Files built this fire: page.tsx (583 LOC) + README.

## ¬ß544 banked seeds ‚Äî 2026-06-25 (cycle 544 ¬∑ KIDS ¬∑ WIDE non-winners, fully built then de-selected)

WIDE kids cycle: 3 orthogonal explorers, all non-touch / three.js-GPU / music-from-NOT-pitch (dodging every JURY 2026-06-24 ban), across DIFFERENT inputs (mic-voice / device-tilt / camera-motion) √ó techniques (long-form-stateful-growth / height-field-flow / optical-flow-attract). Both siblings below returned **complete + self-reported tsc/eslint-clean** (READMEs written); `cp`'d to `/tmp/dream-losers-544/` and `rm -rf`'d before the authoritative winner-only build (NOT committed) ‚Äî rebuild from these briefs.

### `925-kids-tilt-tide` ‚≠ê RESURRECT-FIRST ‚Äî `queued`
**For: kids (4+).** Tilt the iPad ‚Üí `DeviceOrientationEvent` (beta/gamma, iOS `requestPermission()` gated inside the Start tap) becomes a gravity vector that tips a glowing **40√ó40 height-field sea of light**; the water flows downhill toward the low corner and where it **pools** past a depth threshold on a fixed glowing **lily node**, that lily blooms a soft warm **bell** (fixed warm pentatonic {C4 D4 E4 G4 A4 C5 D5 E5}, consonant by construction ‚Äî NO harmony engine), with a ~0.22s per-lily cooldown ‚Üí fast rocking = a flurry, gentle tilt = sparse drops, **the rhythm of the bells is the rhythm of how the child tilts**. Standing-wave shimmer (DAMP 0.985) keeps the surface alive; lily nodes lift/bloom visually so the rhythm reads sound-off. INPUT device-tilt (non-touch; **0√ó in last-10 kids** ‚Äî the freshest input) ¬∑ OUTPUT three.js GPU fluid sheet ¬∑ TECH shallow-water height-field flow ‚Üí pooling triggers bells ¬∑ VIBE serene moonlit tide pool. Master gain 0.24 ‚Üí 6kHz lowpass ‚Üí DynamicsCompressor ‚Üí destination, always-on fading pad. Fallback: no motion sensor ‚Üí pointer-drag tips the sea + idle auto-demo rocks the tide within ~0.6s (tilt is primary, pointer/demo are fallbacks only); `text-rose-300` on permission-denied. Ambition 2-3/5 (#2 ‚â•3 subsystems [tilt + height-field sim + bell synth + three.js render] + #3 shallow-water height-field sim / Faraday 1831 standing waves + #5 dated RESEARCH ¬ß544). **De-selected ONLY because** 924 is the higher-ambition (4/5) depth play and the directest ¬ß544 research-chain answer; **925 is the most bulletproof unattended of the bench** (a simple gravity rule self-organizes the rhythm) and the freshest input ‚Äî **resurrect-first on the next kids cycle.** Note: tilt has banked priors (906-tilt-fireflies = particle Boids; 913-spin-galaxy = gyro) but the **height-field fluid-sheet tip-and-pool** is a fresh mechanic. Files built this fire: page.tsx + README.

### `926-kids-firefly-meadow` ‚Äî `queued`
**For: kids (4+).** Wave a hand in front of the **front camera** ‚Üí a hidden `<video>` + a tiny **64√ó48 offscreen sensor canvas** (the sensor pipeline only, NOT the art surface) computes smoothed per-cell **frame-difference optical-flow motion energy** (mirrored, noise-floored) ‚Üí a peak location + total motion that pulls a three.js **firefly swarm** (~220 additive-glow particles) toward where the child moves and triggers soft **pentatonic** bells (panned by screen position, density-throttled) on excited fireflies near the peak; a big sweep gathers many ‚Üí a shimmer "chord," small flutters ‚Üí sparse twinkles ‚Üí **the rhythm is the rhythm of movement**. INPUT front-camera frame-difference optical-flow (NOT a pose/face/skeleton model ‚Äî distinct from 911's MediaPipe pose; the camera feed is never displayed ‚Üí privacy) ¬∑ OUTPUT three.js GPU particle swarm ¬∑ TECH motion-energy field ‚Üí swarm attraction + bells ¬∑ VIBE warm dusk meadow, fireflies. Master ramps to ‚â§0.26 ‚Üí lowpass ‚Üí DynamicsCompressor, always-on pad. Fallback: camera denied/missing ‚Üí synthetic "evening breeze" Lissajous blob drifts so fireflies gather + chime within ~0.9s; `text-rose-300` on camera failure. Ambition 2-3/5 (#2 ‚â•3 subsystems [frame-diff motion sensor + swarm sim + bell synth + three.js render] + #3 Myron Krueger *Videoplace* 1974 + frame-difference optical flow + #5 dated RESEARCH ¬ß544; #1 arguable ‚Äî raw frame-diff optical-flow may be a lab-first camera technique vs the existing MediaPipe-pose pieces). **De-selected because** it sits closer to 911-kids-shadow-dance's camera-motion register and is lower-ambition than 924's long-form depth; the **raw optical-flow (no ML model) input is a genuinely fresh, lightweight camera technique** worth keeping. Resurrect on a kids cycle wanting a camera piece without the MediaPipe dependency. Files built this fire: page.tsx + README.

Both reuse the proven `441` FAL pattern + `_shared/api-guard`.

- **`916-latent-loom`** ‚≠ê **RESURRECT-FIRST** ‚Äî audio ‚Üí `flux/schnell` (seed still) ‚Üí `flux/schnell/redux` (re-dream the previous still conditioned on the new audio mood), a continuously-morphing "latent loom." three.js fragment-shader CROSSFADE between prev‚Üínext stills driven by live audio (energy‚Üíwarp, flux‚Üídissolve speed, centroid‚Üíglow). **The "never stops becoming" canvas.** Genuine 2-model series (txt2img‚Üíimg2img). **Cost hard-bounded**: every call needs an explicit "Dream again" click OR a single opt-in "Auto-evolve (6 steps)" with a visible counter that stops at 6 ‚Äî never a timer, never idle/load firing, buttons disabled in-flight; synthesized GPU field fallback always alive. Named ref: Memo Akten *Learning to See* / Mario Klingemann neural feedback. De-selected only because the img2img-loop is conceptually nearer the already-shipped `441` (audio‚Üíimage) than 915's *video* step, and the video output is the more novel (0√ó) surface. **Resurrect on a desktop/Tauri morph cycle** ‚Äî the slow latent walk wants a big screen.
- **`917-the-telling`** ‚Äî audio ‚Üí `any-llm` (claude-3.5-sonnet writes a 2-3 line prose-poem of the music's character) ‚Üí `flux/schnell` (paints that poem), with the narrative rendered as **kinetic typography** over a three.js scene the live audio animates (parallax/bloom/warp), poem words drifting/scaling with energy/flux. **Text is DISPLAYED, never spoken** (no TTS ‚Äî respects Karel's hard pull-back on AI voice). Genuine 2-model series (LLM‚Üíimage). Named ref: Es Devlin poem-projection / Ross Goodwin machine writing. De-selected only because narrative-text sits slightly adjacent to the voice/dialogue lane Karel cooled, and 915's pure image‚Üívideo play is the cleaner AV-first answer. **Resurrect on a literary/narrative cycle** ‚Äî the "music writes its own myth" hook is strong and the LLM step is the cheapest of the three chains.

### `913-kids-spin-galaxy` ‚≠ê RESURRECT-FIRST ‚Äî `queued`
**For: kids (4+).** Turn the tablet like a steering wheel ‚Üí the **gyroscope** (`DeviceMotionEvent.rotationRate` magnitude ‚Äî NOT tilt) stirs a galaxy of glowing stars; faster spin ‚Üí faster swirl + brighter shimmer pad, and each star that crosses one of three fixed **chime rings** fires a soft warm ping ({root, fifth, octave} ‚Äî consonant by construction, no scale the kid plays). A sudden spin flings stars across the rings = a flurry; gentle spin = a sparse pulse ‚Üí **the rhythm is the rhythm of how the child spins.** INPUT device-gyroscope-spin (the coldest input ‚Äî distinct from the over-used tilt) ¬∑ OUTPUT **hand-written WebGPU `@compute`** 40k-particle galaxy (the SCARCE GPU surface, JURY-#1's purest target) **+ full WebGL2 fallback** running the identical `orbitalOmega` + audio ¬∑ TECH angular-velocity orbital sim ‚Üí ring-crossing percussion ¬∑ VIBE cosmic-kids galaxy. Ambition 3/5 (#2 ‚â•4 subsystems + #3 orrery/armillary + Holst *The Planets* + PMC5104747 + #5 dated RESEARCH ¬ß540). Pointer drag-to-spin + synthetic accelerate/decelerate auto-demo. **De-selected ONLY because** (a) a phone most likely shows the WebGL2 path, so the WGSL win wouldn't land at the 06:30 glance, and (b) spinning an iPad is a slightly awkward/risky 4yo gesture. **Strongest bank** ‚Äî resurrect on a desktop/Tauri window where WebGPU lands, or repackage the gyro as a phone-safe gesture. Files built: page.tsx + motion.ts + audio.ts + galaxy-core.ts + galaxy-webgpu.ts (WGSL) + galaxy-webgl2.ts + README.

### `912-kids-wind-garden` ‚Äî `queued`
**For: kids (4+).** Blow at the screen ‚Üí the mic detects **breath** (not pitch) via **spectral flatness** (geometric√∑arithmetic mean of the magnitude spectrum: blowing = broadband noise ‚Üí high flatness; humming/talking = tonal ‚Üí low ‚Üí barely registers), gated by RMS; breath strength becomes a **wind force** that bends a glowing three.js `THREE.Points` grass field (a GLSL wind-wave sweeps across X so you SEE the gust) and a hard puff **scatters dandelion seeds** that each chime one warm note as they drift away (random from {root, fifth, octave, third} ‚Äî consonant by construction). Seed density scales with breath ‚Üí **the rhythm of the piece is the rhythm of breathing.** INPUT mic-breath/blow (broadband, non-pitch ‚Äî distinct from humming/onset) ¬∑ OUTPUT three.js GPU grass/particle field ¬∑ TECH spectral-flatness breath detection ‚Üí wind + seed-scatter chimes ¬∑ VIBE calm luminous dawn-meadow. Ambition 2-3/5 (#2 ‚â•4 subsystems + #3 Eno / shakuhachi breath-as-instrument / PMC5104747 + #5 dated ¬ß540). Synthetic-breath auto-demo + a big üí® puff tap button. **De-selected on the input edge** (mic has 2 recent kids priors 907/891) and because the dance piece is the directer research-chain answer; the **spectral-flatness blow-vs-hum discrimination is a genuinely fresh technique** worth keeping. Resurrect on a calm/bedtime kids cycle. Files built: page.tsx + breath.ts + audio.ts + scene.ts + README.

- **`910-resonant-field-flow` (bank ‚Äî three.js **GPGPU ping-pong float-RT flow field**: ~147k additive points advected by simplex curl-noise in a sim fragment shader, positions read in the vertex shader; memory accumulator; auto-orbit camera; Canvas2D fallback).** Same concept, a "river of light" points cloud instead of a volume. **De-selected on robustness + diversity** ‚Äî it depends on **float render-target support** (more failure surface than 909's pure raymarch; probes float/half-float but the fallback is the likely phone path), and three.js-points is closer to the lab's existing particle-field heritage than 909's raymarched *volume* (the fresher surface ‚Äî the banked-but-never-shipped ¬ß531 `889` raymarch register). Per-grain `PannerNode` CPU cost flagged for low-end phones (grain-rate cap is the knob). Resurrect when a GPGPU/points register is wanted, or fold its curl-noise sim into a future flow piece. Files built this fire: page.tsx + audio.ts + features.ts + scene.ts (GPGPU + Canvas2D fallback) + README.

### `905-kids-water-bowl` ‚≠ê RESURRECT-FIRST ‚Äî `queued`
**For: kids (4+).** Hum/sing ‚Üí live pitch + loudness drive **Faraday-style standing waves** on a glowing three.js water surface (a high-res circular mesh displaced by a sum of cosine radial standing-wave terms; pitch ‚Üí number of concentric rings/mode, loudness ‚Üí amplitude, pitch ‚Üí warm‚Üîcool color). The pattern you see *is* the sound you make. Soft "singing-bowl" drone whose brightness tracks pitch; permanent idle micro-ripple so it's never still; synthetic swept-voice auto-demo when mic denied. INPUT mic-hum ¬∑ OUTPUT three.js (GPU) ¬∑ TECH Faraday/cymatic surface displacement ¬∑ VIBE calm/luminous/water. Ambition 2/5 (#2 ‚â•3 subsystems + #3 Faraday 1831/Chladni; #1 does NOT hold ‚Äî cymatics already built in 19/165). Dodges all JURY 2026-06-24 bans (GPU, mic-not-touch, no pitch-theory *engine*). **Deepening (from ¬ß536/¬ß538):** real Bessel `J_n(k¬∑r)` eigenmodes; caustic light cast beneath the bowl; two-voice duet ‚Üí interfering/beating ring sets. Strongest because mic-hum needs no motion sensor ‚Üí bulletproof at the 06:30 glance.

### `906-kids-tilt-fireflies` ‚Äî `queued`
**For: kids (4+).** Tilt the iPad ‚Üí `DeviceOrientationEvent` becomes a gravity vector; a swarm of ~hundreds of glowing three.js firefly particles drifts "downhill," and where the swarm **clumps** (spatial-grid density) or **bumps a flower node**, it emits a soft capped mallet "bloom" ‚Äî so *the rhythm of the music is the rhythm of your tilting*. Five flowers = a few fixed warm timbres (no scale/harmony engine); swarm speed ‚Üí shimmer-pad brightness. Pointer-drag fallback + orbiting auto-demo when no sensor. INPUT device-tilt ¬∑ OUTPUT three.js GPU particles ¬∑ TECH gravity/Boids swarm density‚Üírhythm ¬∑ VIBE night/fireflies/playful. Ambition 2/5 (#2 ‚â•3 subsystems + #3 Reynolds *Boids* 1986). Dodges all bans. **Deepening:** flower light-ripple on bump (visual rhythm reads sound-off); add Boids cohesion ‚Üí swirling rivers; long calm/busy periods grow flowers/fireflies (slow texture evolution). Resurrect on a kids cycle once tilt/motion input has cooled.

## ¬ß537 ‚Äî banked explorers from cycle 537 (ADULT ¬∑ WIDE) ‚Äî 2 seeds

Cycle 537 shipped `902-harmonic-mirror` (MIDI/keyboard ¬∑ Canvas2D ¬∑ just-intonation chord-completion ‚Äî the directest answer to today's RESEARCH ¬ß537 dive on real-time human‚ÄìAI co-performance, and the first build in 6 windows to cite a dated finding *in its own README*, fixing JURY #5). The two non-winning explorers, both built complete + self-reported tsc/eslint-clean, `rm -rf`'d before the authoritative winner-only build (NOT committed) ‚Äî rebuild from these briefs:

- **`903-living-stave` ‚≠ê (RESURRECT-FIRST ‚Äî owns the 0√ó SVG output lane, the single most-named scarce surface in the lab; JURY 2026-06-23 #1).** "Your playing writes itself into a living score": mic ‚Üí live **monophonic transcription** onto a self-drawing **SVG** treble staff ‚Äî each new note inks a paper-white notehead at the right pitch height (stem, ledger lines, ‚ôØ glyphs), the ribbon auto-scrolls leftward with an edge fade, a violet playhead marks "now" with a faint un-snapped pitch dot so you see yourself drift. Pitch = **NSDF/autocorrelation** (McLeod & Wyvill 2005, RMS-gated, clamped ~70‚Äì1000 Hz, parabolic-interpolated); a stateful `NoteTracker` does **onset gating** (snapped-semitone change OR amplitude re-attack OR pitch-out-of-silence, behind a ~110 ms refractory) so one sustained note = one notehead, not 60/sec. Mic-denied/silent ‚Üí a **ghost-performer** folk tune draws + sounds the staff on load. Echo voice connects mic-independently to destination (no feedback into analysis). **Subsystems ‚â•3** (mic capture + NSDF pitch + onset tracking + SVG notation engine = 4). Refs: **Jan√°ƒçek *n√°pƒõvky mluvy*** + **LilyPond engraving** + **McLeod & Wyvill NSDF**. Files built this fire: page.tsx + pitch.ts + audio.ts + README. **Resurrect on any cold-SVG window** (it IS the SVG-lane answer); re-verify on a real breathy voice: the semitone snap + clarity gate trade sensitivity for stability (octave errors / between-pitch flicker possible), rhythm is order-of-arrival not true note-value, monophonic only.

- **`904-tide-organ` (bank ‚Äî strong data‚ÜíSTRUCTURE piece, de-selected ONLY because `898-tremor-score` [cycle 535] just shipped external-data‚Üístructure; resurrect once the data lane has cooled, or fold the Zadar/tide framing into a fresh data axis).** "The sea plays an organ": live **NOAA CO-OPS** tide data decides the FORM of a slow organ piece ‚Äî **phase**‚Üíwhich register of pipes is active, **height**‚Üíhow many voices + brightness, **rate-of-change**‚Üíhow fast voices enter/leave ‚Äî over a fixed consonant **Lydian rank across ~4 octaves** so it's always in tune; the sea writes the arrangement, never the tuning (directly answers JURY 2026-06-23 #3 "map data to structure, not detune"). Two client-side NOAA calls (`water_level` latest + hourly `predictions`); on ANY failure ‚Üí silent **synthetic semidiurnal sinusoid** (~12.42 h M2, ~90√ó accelerated clock so the form moves within a minute), emerald `live:<station>` vs amber `modeling the tide`. **three.js** glowing pipe field (cool‚Üíamber‚Üíwhite, swelling pipes, breathing sea plane; Canvas2D fallback). 3 preset stations (SF 9414290, The Battery 8518750, Seattle 9447130). **Subsystems ‚â•3** (NOAA fetch+parse+synthetic fallback + additive organ synth + long-form arc clock + three.js field = 4). Ref: **Zadar Sea Organ (Nikola Ba≈°iƒá, 2005)** + SF Wave Organ + Annea Lockwood. Files built this fire: page.tsx + tide.ts + audio.ts + scene.ts + canvas-fallback.ts + README. **Resurrect when the external-data lane is cold AND a maritime/architectural register is wanted;** note three.js is "approaching cap" per recent JURYs ‚Äî the data‚Üístructure mapping is the fresh part, not the renderer.

## ¬ß536 ‚Äî banked explorers from cycle 536 (KIDS ¬∑ WIDE) ‚Äî 2 seeds

WIDE kids cycle: 3 orthogonal **"physical-phenomenon made visible + audible"** explorers, each a grep-verified-novel technique on a different non-WebGL2 surface (Canvas2D / SVG / three.js). Shipped **`899-kids-moire-beats`** (touch ¬∑ Canvas2D ¬∑ moir√©=visible acoustic beating; anchored RESEARCH ¬ß536; the lab's first moir√© piece). Both siblings below returned **complete + self-reported tsc/eslint-clean** (READMEs written); `rm -rf`'d before the authoritative winner-only build (NOT committed) ‚Äî rebuild from these briefs. **Both are net-new lab capabilities / techniques (ambition #1) ‚Äî sequenced, not lost.**

- **`900-kids-rope-singer` ‚≠ê (RESURRECT-FIRST ‚Äî owns the 0√ó SVG lane JURY keeps naming as starved, and a grep-verified-novel technique: catenary).** "A hanging rope you play with your hands": a single rope hangs in a **catenary** curve drawn entirely as an SVG `<path>` (Catmull-Rom ‚Üí B√©zier) over a warm wooden scene. Two touch moves: **(1) hang beads** ‚Äî tap below the rope to drop a glowing bead; each adds mass so the rope **sags deeper ‚Üí sounds lower** (`sagFor` ‚Üí `pitchFor`, snapped to a warm C-pentatonic ladder so every pluck is consonant; tap a bead to pull it off); **(2) pluck** ‚Äî drag the rope sideways and release for a damped-spring twang whose visible swing tracks the additive plucked-string envelope (triangle fundamental + 2 decaying partials, 12 ms attack). Always-on root+fifth drone (‚â§ 0.26, 6 kHz lowpass), 2s-idle auto-demo hangs/drops/plucks on its own, `text-rose-300` notice + live visuals on Web-Audio failure, full teardown. **Subsystems ‚â•3** (touch hit-testing + catenary-sag sim + plucked-string synth + SVG render). Refs: the **catenary / Galileo's hanging chain** ¬∑ the **monochord** (string length/tension ‚Üí pitch). Files built this fire: page.tsx + README. **Resurrect on a kids cycle wanting the SVG lane;** re-verify: the sag is a weight-skewed parabola stand-in (not exact catenary math), and the snapped-pentatonic ladder is a mild safe-scale lean ‚Äî could let bigger sags reach a real low note off the ladder.
- **`901-kids-water-bowl` (bank ‚Äî a grep-verified-novel technique [Faraday waves, 0√ó] and the lab's first three.js *fluid-surface cymatics*).** "Hum to the water and see the note's shape": a glowing 3D bowl (three.js `MeshPhysicalMaterial` transmission/clearcoat, glowing torus rim, slow auto-orbit) whose 200√ó200 displaced `PlaneGeometry` surface ripples by a stack of **Faraday standing-wave modes** `z = Œ£ A·µ¢¬∑cos(k·µ¢¬∑r¬∑œÄ)¬∑cos(m·µ¢¬∑Œ∏)¬∑cos(œâ¬∑t)` ‚Äî low hum ‚Üí a few big concentric rings, high hum ‚Üí fine petals/cells, louder ‚Üí taller/brighter. A ~40-line RMS-gated **autocorrelation pitch detector** drives mode count; two capped/lowpassed sines sing a soft harmonic back (mic ‚Üí AnalyserNode ONLY, no feedback). **Crucial fallback for the silent 06:30 glance:** mic-denied OR ~2s-silent ‚Üí an auto-demo sweeps G3‚ÜíD5 blooming the water through cymatic figures while the bowl sings (alive within ~1s); real humming takes over. `text-rose-300` on no-WebGL / no-mic; full teardown (dispose geometry/material/renderer, stop tracks). **Subsystems ‚â•3** (mic + pitch-detection + Faraday-mode displacement + three.js fluid render). Refs: **Michael Faraday** *On a Peculiar Class of Acoustical Figures* (1831) ¬∑ **Hans Jenny** cymatics ¬∑ **Ernst Chladni**. Files built this fire: page.tsx + README. **Resurrect when a calm/luminous mic-driven kids piece is wanted;** note three.js is "approaching cap" per JURY (use sparingly), and the technique is cymatics-adjacent (`cymatic` is 5√ó in the lab) ‚Äî the *fluid-surface Faraday* framing + mic-pitch coupling is the fresh part, lean into that vs. the older plate-cymatics pieces.

## ¬ß535 ‚Äî banked explorers from cycle 535 (ADULT ¬∑ WIDE) ‚Äî 2 seeds

WIDE adult cycle: 3 orthogonal explorers, each on non-banned tags (`banned=[touch, raw-WebGL2]`). Shipped **`898-tremor-score`** (external-data ¬∑ SVG ¬∑ earthquake catalogue ‚Üí musical STRUCTURE; anchored RESEARCH ¬ß535 / OpenSeisML arXiv 2605.20539, May 2026; directly answers JURY #3 data‚Üístructure-not-detune + lands criterion #5). Both siblings below returned **complete + self-reported tsc/eslint-clean** (READMEs written); `rm -rf`'d before the authoritative winner-only build (NOT committed) ‚Äî rebuild from these briefs. **Both are net-new lab capabilities (ambition #1) ‚Äî they are sequenced, not lost.**

- **`899-harmonic-mirror` ‚≠ê (RESURRECT-FIRST ‚Äî the lab's FIRST Web MIDI integration; a capability milestone, and deeply on-mission since Resonance is *the personal audio workspace for pianists/composers* and Karel likely plays a MIDI controller).** "Your keyboard's shadow": Web MIDI (`navigator.requestMIDIAccess`) tracks held notes; YOU play them as warm detuned-saw voices; a **MIRROR** infers the implied chord (template match against maj/min/sus/bare-fifth/cluster ‚Äî a slice of Riemann functional harmony) and adds **1‚Äì2 NEW voices that complete a consonant chord, tuned in JUST INTONATION** relative to the inferred root (small-integer ratios 5/4, 6/5, 3/2, 2/1 ‚Äî Partch/Johnston lineage) so they beat-lessly lock; the mirror voices **glide-retune** as the root shifts. Visual = Canvas2D circle-of-fifths constellation: held = bright nodes, mirror = softer halos joined by luminous completion lines + ratio labels + large chord name, all amplitude-pulsing at 60 fps. **Subsystems ‚â•3** (MIDI input + harmonic analysis + JI synth + Canvas2D viz). **First-class no-hardware fallback** (the demo path, since most reviewers + a 06:30 phone have no MIDI): a clickable white/black-key on-screen piano + computer-keyboard map `a w s e d f t g y h u j k` ‚Üí C..C drives the identical pipeline; any MIDI error ‚Üí `text-rose-300` pointing at it. Files built this fire: page.tsx + audio.ts (`MirrorEngine`) + harmony.ts (`analyse`/`mirrorVoices`/`jiFreq`) + README (cites Partch/Johnston, Riemann, RESEARCH anchor arXiv 2604.07612 Apr 2026 real-time human‚ÄìAI co-performance). **Resurrect on an adult cycle, ideally when Karel can review at a desk with a controller;** rough edges to re-verify: chord inference is template-based (dense/extended chords fall to a "cluster" heuristic), on-screen piano spans a single octave, and the JI glide voicing wants a real-ear pass.
- **`900-flow-grains` (bank ‚Äî the lab's FIRST optical-flow input + FIRST camera‚Üígranular instrument; the biggest novelty leap of the cycle, deferred only for a perf pass).** "Your gesture is granular texture": camera (`getUserMedia`) ‚Üí coarse **dense optical flow** (gradient / Lucas-Kanade-lite on a downsampled ~80√ó60 frame) feeds ONE shared flow field into BOTH (1) a **three.js Points** field whose particles advect along the local flow vectors, colored by speed, and (2) a **granular synthesizer** over an in-browser-synthesized warm partials-pad `AudioBuffer` (no external file) ‚Äî total flow energy‚Üígrain density (still = sparse, motion = a shower of grains), flow position‚Üígrain read-position + pan, flow velocity‚ÜíplaybackRate pitch-scatter; grains ‚Üí master ‚Üí ConvolverNode reverb ‚Üí limiter. Camera-denied ‚Üí an **auto-driving synthetic swirling blob** keeps audio + visuals alive; errors ‚Üí `text-rose-300`. Privacy: video never leaves the browser. Ref: **TouchDesigner opticalFlow TOP + ParticlesGPU (Derivative / Matthew Ragan)** + **AudioFlow (Kihs)** ‚Äî the flow‚Üíparticles & flow‚Üígranular paradigm ported GPU‚Üíbrowser (three.js + Web Audio). Files built this fire: page.tsx + flow.ts (`computeSyntheticFlow` + LK-lite) + audio.ts (granular scheduler, Hann grains) + README. **Subsystems ‚â•3** (camera + optical-flow + granular synth + three.js viz). **Resurrect when a camera/organic/TouchDesigner register is wanted;** the known cost is ~9k CPU-integrated particles + per-frame flow + grains (capped 14/frame to protect the audio thread) and LK-lite flow gets noisy on low-contrast scenes ‚Äî wants a perf/threshold pass and ideally a real-device test before shipping.

## ¬ß534 ‚Äî banked explorers from cycle 534 (KIDS ¬∑ WIDE) ‚Äî 2 seeds

WIDE kids cycle: 3 orthogonal **template-breakers** (JURY 2026-06-23 #4 ‚Äî wrong choice persists & must be *earned* back, no auto-resolve), each on a different non-WebGL2 surface (Canvas2D / SVG / WebGPU). Shipped **`897-kids-balance-band`** (touch ¬∑ Canvas2D ¬∑ 2D rigid-lever torque ‚Üî consonance; research-anchored RESEARCH ¬ß534 / PMC11336827 + PMC12605063). Both siblings below returned **complete + self-reported tsc/eslint-clean** (READMEs written); `rm -rf`'d before the authoritative winner-only build (NOT committed) ‚Äî rebuild from these briefs.

- **`898-kids-echo-keeper` ‚≠ê (RESURRECT-FIRST ‚Äî owns the SVG output lane, which is ~0√ó/15 and JURY-named as scarce; the strongest pure-diversity bank).** "Echo Keeper": a child sings into a cave; a note **consonant** with the held drone echoes back warmly and fades, but a **clash** (m2/tritone) gets STUCK ‚Äî it loops back as a persistent grumbly tone and tightens a visible **SVG trefoil knot** that does *not* fade on its own. Each stuck echo has an "answer note" (m2-above‚Üíresolves down to root; tritone‚Üíresolves to the third); when the child **sings that answer** (held ~320 ms dwell) the matching knot **unties** and the echo melts. A small state machine *remembers* stuck echoes across time (real memory, no per-frame reset). Subsystems ‚â•3: hand-rolled NSDF/autocorrelation pitch-detect (RMS-gated, scale-snapped) + delay/echo-loop memory + consonance-resolution logic + animated SVG cave/ripples/knots. Anti-feedback by construction (mic‚Üíanalyser only, never‚Üídestination). Full mic-free path: ghost-singer auto-demo (clash‚Üíknot‚Üíanswer‚Üíuntie ~1‚Äì2 s, loops) + a row of ‚â•68 px press-and-hold tap-note buttons driving the identical pipeline. **Resurrect on any cycle wanting SVG, mic/voice, or a "the wrong note persists" register;** needs a real-device pass to tune the autocorrelation snap tolerance (0.7 st) and `MIN_RMS` (0.012) against a young, breathy voice. Built complete this fire (page.tsx + README).
- **`899-kids-candle-choir` (bank ‚Äî WebGPU is 3√ó/15, under cap but not as scarce as SVG; resurrect when a warm/ritual WebGPU kids piece is wanted).** "Candle Choir": a row/arc of 7 candle-creatures (C-major core + F‚ôØ/B tensions) each singing a sustained degree; a **dissonant** tap **blows out** the conflicting candle ‚Äî dark, silent, and it **stays out** (a real audible/visible gap, no auto-relight/timer). To relight a dark candle the child taps a still-lit candle a **consonant interval away** ‚Äî its flame leans over and relights it; relight all ‚Üí a full warm bloom. Subsystems ‚â•3: touch + interval-class consonance engine + flame extinguish/relight state machine + **WebGPU** WGSL instanced additive-glow flames/embers, with a **Canvas2D fallback** running the identical instance data and full mechanic (deliberately NOT WebGL2, which is banned). Self-demo loops at start/idle. Note vs `868-kids-monster-keys`: monster-keys *spawns* a creature on a clash; this *removes* a voice (a gap, not an addition) ‚Äî a distinct consequence, but the closest relative in the lab, so resurrect only if the "blow-out/relight" gesture reads as meaningfully different on device. Built complete this fire (page.tsx + README). Risk to re-verify: WebGPU pipeline used local `any`-typed handles + numeric `usage` literals (no `@webgpu/types`) ‚Äî confirm it draws in a real WebGPU browser; Canvas2D fallback is the likely CI/render path.

## ¬ß533 ‚Äî banked explorers from cycle 533 (ADULT ¬∑ WIDE) ‚Äî 2 seeds

WIDE adult cycle: 3 orthogonal non-WebGL2 explorers on the thinnest renderer lanes (audio-only 0√ó, Canvas2D scarce, SVG scarce). Shipped **`896-tonnetz-loom`** (SVG ¬∑ neo-Riemannian Tonnetz, anchored on the freshest cite of the cycle, RESEARCH ¬ß533 / arXiv 2606.11246, Jun 2026). Both siblings below returned **complete + self-reported lint-clean** (READMEs written); backed up to `/tmp/dream-losers-533/` and `rm -rf`'d before the authoritative winner-only build (NOT committed) ‚Äî rebuild from these briefs.

- **`894-room-raga` ‚≠ê (RESURRECT-FIRST adult ‚Äî it owns the lab's single thinnest output lane, audio-only = 0√ó/15, the exact lane JURY 2026-06-23 named as scarce-and-rewarded).** "The room tunes the instrument": mic listens to the *ambient* sound of the visitor's space (autocorrelation pitch detection, RMS-gated, clamped 55‚Äì660 Hz, smoothed), treats the found dominant pitch as the TONIC, and grows a slow just-intonation drone-raga upward from it ‚Äî voices on pure JI ratios (1/1, 9/8, 5/4, 4/3, 3/2, 5/3, 15/8, 2/1) entering/leaving on a long-form `stepState` clock (sparse‚Üífull‚Üíthinning; minute 3 ‚âÝ minute 0), through a procedural-IR ConvolverNode reverb + feedback delay + limiter; if the room's pitch shifts the whole drone glides to re-center (the space can re-tune it mid-piece). Visual is deliberately ONE breathing CSS radial-gradient div (no canvas/WebGL) ‚Äî hue = found pitch-class, breath = amplitude ‚Äî so audio carries it. Silent/no-mic fallback = a "seed a tonic" button + slider (always sounds). Subsystems ‚â•4 (mic FFT + pitch detection + JI generative engine + reverb/delay bus + long-form clock). Ref: **Pauline Oliveros *Deep Listening*; La Monte Young & Marian Zazeela *Dream House*.** Built complete this fire (5 files: page.tsx + pitch.ts + audio.ts + raga.ts + README). **Resurrect on any cycle that wants the audio-only lane or a deep-listening register;** needs a real-device pass to tune autocorrelation RMS/clarity thresholds (0.006 / 0.02) on a quiet ambient room.
- **`895-recurrence-room` (bank LOWER priority ‚Äî overlaps existing `777-song-architecture`, which already computes a chroma SSM, so it does NOT clear ambition #1).** "See the shape of a song": decode a piano track (probes Karel's `/api/audio/[id]` 1..13, else file-drop, else a synthesized A¬∑B¬∑A demo phrase so it works offline), extract 12-bin L2-normalized chroma per frame (hand-written radix-2 FFT, Hann window), build the N√óN (~220) cosine self-similarity matrix, render it as a Canvas2D dark‚Üíviolet‚Üíamber heat-lattice where off-diagonal stripes = the song's repeats; playhead sweeps the diagonal in sync, click any cell to A/B-loop the two similar moments. Subsystems ‚â•4 (decode + chroma + SSM + Canvas2D render + seek/loop). Ref: **Foote 1999 "Visualizing Music and Audio using Self-Similarity"; M√ºller *FMP*.** Anchor: RESEARCH ¬ß533 / arXiv 2603.27218 (SSM structure-eval, Mar 2026). Built complete this fire. **Only resurrect with a fresh differentiator** vs `777` ‚Äî e.g. cross-recording SSM (compare two of his takes), or a *novelty curve* / structure-segmentation layer that 777 lacks; bare re-render is the re-tread JURY warns against.

## ¬ß532 ‚Äî banked explorers from cycle 532 (KIDS ¬∑ WIDE) ‚Äî 2 seeds

WIDE cycle: 3 orthogonal kids explorers (distinct INPUT √ó OUTPUT √ó TECHNIQUE √ó VIBE, none on a banned tag). Shipped **`891-kids-sing-a-path`** (mic/voice ‚Üí SVG ‚Üí real-time pitch-contour ‚Üí storybook; research-anchored on RESEARCH ¬ß532 / PESTO). Both siblings below returned **complete + self-reported tsc/eslint-clean** (READMEs written); `rm -rf`'d before the authoritative winner-only build (NOT committed) ‚Äî rebuild from these briefs.

- **`893-kids-listening-jar` ‚≠ê (the lab's first deliberate AUDIO-LED kids piece ‚Äî RESURRECT on an audio-only/non-screen cycle; OUTPUT audio-only = 0√ó/10, the single thinnest lane).** A calm "listening jar" you tilt + shake: a warm pentatonic pad ensemble panned across stereo via `StereoPannerNode` (tilt moves the listening position so sound drifts around the child), generated convolution reverb + feedback delay for space, a slow `stepState()` clock that evolves density/voicing/pan-drift over ~60‚Äì90s (genuinely different at minute 2 than minute 0), shake ‚Üí soft rain/bell grains, master limiter; the visual is *intentionally* one big breathing radial glow (DOM/CSS, no canvas/three.js) so the **audio carries the experience**. Full on-screen tilt-pad + shake-button fallback (works with zero sensors), iOS `requestPermission` narrow-typed (no `any`). Subsystems = spatial generative engine + evolving state + tilt/shake sensors + minimal breathing visual (‚â•3). Ref: **Pauline Oliveros ‚Äî Deep Listening** + the "shell-to-your-ear / shake-the-jar" sensory motif. **De-selected on legibility for the morning glance** ‚Äî an audio-led piece's payoff needs sound/headphones, where a silent 06:30 phone glance reads it as "one glow"; it is the boldest of the three and squarely fills the screen-bias menu lane. Resurrect deliberately on an audio-only cycle. Rebuildable from this brief.

- **`892-kids-glass-rain` (three.js 3D buoyancy + glass-harmonica modal bells ‚Äî RESURRECT when a GPU/three.js surface is scarce).** A serene underwater tank of ~11 glowing translucent 3D drops a child taps (three.js `Raycaster` pick) to ring **glass-harmonica chimes** (struck-bell modal synth: bank of near-harmonic, slightly inharmonic decaying sine partials, velocity ‚Üí loudness+brightness, soft attack/long bell decay, master limiter, faint underwater pad). Drops bob with a **damped buoyancy spring** + water drag + soft pairwise repulsion (clamped semi-implicit Euler, no explosions); drop-drop collisions above a velocity threshold ring quietly. Builder proactively swapped `MeshPhysicalMaterial.transmission` (an extra full-scene pass/frame) for a transparent `MeshStandardMaterial` + additive rim/halo, pixel-ratio capped at 2, `powerPreference:"low-power"` ‚Äî phone-cost-aware. WebGL-unavailable fallback ‚Üí audio-only pentatonic auto-mode + visible rose notice; ~0.4s auto welcome chord. Subsystems = three.js scene + buoyancy physics + raycast tap + modal synth (‚â•4). Ref: **glass harmonica / Benjamin Franklin's armonica** + Archimedes buoyancy. **De-selected on diversity + heritage** ‚Äî modal/struck synthesis is already heavy in the lab (284/286/478/655/803/886) and three.js is 2√ó/10 (the freshest legit GPU surface, but SVG was the scarcer 1√ó this fire); the 3D aquarium is genuinely lovely on a real device. Resurrect on a three.js-scarce window. Rebuildable from this brief.

## ¬ß531 ‚Äî banked explorers from cycle 531 (ADULT ¬∑ DEEP) ‚Äî 2 seeds

DEEP cycle: one concept ‚Äî **`884`/`888-living-reverie`, a 10-minute long-form generative reverie on Karel's real "Welcome Home" piano with a two-tier memory engine** (local pitch-cell bank for continuity + global irreversible `age`/section state machine for the arc ‚Äî RESEARCH ¬ß531 / arXiv 2603.21282) ‚Äî fielded as 3 parallel approaches that share the SAME engine and differ only in **visual language for the arc** (deliberately non-particle, to dodge the `859-paths-compute-bloom` overlap the seed flagged). Shipped `888-living-reverie` (three.js displaced membrane ‚Äî the fresh three.js surface 2√ó/10 vs Canvas2D's near-cap 3√ó; robust vertex displacement; dodges the raw-WebGL2 ban AND the 859 particle field). Both siblings returned **complete + self-reported tsc/eslint-clean**; `rm -rf`'d before the authoritative winner-only build (NOT committed) ‚Äî rebuild from these briefs + 888's shared engine. This is a **multi-cycle commitment**: 888 is cycle 1 (skeleton + working engine + one visual); the two below + the engine deepening are the backlog (see 888's README "Multi-cycle commitment" section).

- **`889-living-reverie-volumetric` ‚≠ê (three.js raymarched volumetric nebula ‚Äî RESURRECT as an optional runtime "volumetric" render mode, or on a desktop-GPU-targeted window).** The SAME two-tier engine, rendered as a raymarched FBM nebula on an orthographic fullscreen quad (`ShaderMaterial`, ‚â§22 raymarch steps, 4-octave FBM, pixel-ratio clamped 1.75 for mobile headroom): arc reads as thin drifting wisps ‚Üí warm bloom from within ‚Üí thick luminous turbulence ‚Üí cooling fade-to-black; uniforms `uAge`/`uSection`/`uLevel`/`uTime` + palette lerp (indigo‚Üíamber/rose‚Üíviolet), pointer parallax, full `disposables`+`forceContextLoss()` teardown. Built complete this fire, self-reported clean. **De-selected ONLY on robustness** ‚Äî a 22-step raymarch is the heaviest fragment workload of the three and the likeliest to chug/drain battery on Karel's 06:30 phone, where 888's vertex-displaced mesh is cheap and reliable. Genuinely the most *cinematic* version on a real GPU ‚Äî resurrect as a toggle inside 888 or on a desktop-install window. Rebuildable from this brief + 888's `engine.ts`/`audio.ts`.

- **`890-living-reverie-ink` (Canvas2D ink-in-water chroma strata ‚Äî RESURRECT as 888's WebGL-unavailable visual fallback, or on a cold-Canvas2D window).** The SAME engine on pure Canvas2D (zero GPU, zero permissions ‚Äî the most bulletproof render): accumulating translucent ink-in-water strata with a feedback smear, per-note blooms positioned by pitch, explicit palette repaint across the arc (indigo‚Üíamber/rose‚Üíviolet), density/motion rising to a mid-arc peak then thinning in `dissolving`; dt-scaled + RMS-smoothed for smooth (non-flickery) motion. Built complete this fire, self-reported clean. **De-selected on diversity** ‚Äî Canvas2D is 3√ó/10 (shipping it would hit the 4√ó ban threshold) and three.js is the fresher surface. Its real value is as **888's fallback render path** so the piece always has a living visual when WebGL is absent ‚Äî fold it in next. Rebuildable from this brief + 888's `engine.ts`/`audio.ts`. (Honest wart noted by its builder: phrase scheduling via `setTimeout` keyed off the audio clock, not sample-accurate ‚Äî same caveat as 888; fix in the shared engine deepening.)

## ¬ß530 ‚Äî banked explorers from cycle 530 (KIDS ¬∑ DEEP) ‚Äî 2 seeds

DEEP cycle: one concept ‚Äî **a kids "marble music machine" where collision timbre is set by the surface MATERIAL** (material-based modal impact synthesis, RESEARCH ¬ß530) ‚Äî fielded as 3 parallel approaches. Shipped `886-kids-marble-music` (Canvas2D side-view, tap+tilt). The two siblings, banked:

- **`885-kids-marble-music-3d` ‚≠ê (three.js 3D-gravity twin ‚Äî RESURRECT when a GPU surface is scarce / non-WebGL2 ban lifts).** A three.js 3D tray: real gravity vector from device tilt rolls glowing marbles around five tuned material objects (wood block, glass bell + dome, metal chime, drum pad) in 3D; per-collision flash synced to the modal hit; the same material-modal synth as 886 (banks of decaying sine modes, metal 1/2.76/5.40/8.93, drum membrane 1/1.59/2.14/2.30, velocity ‚Üí loudness+brightness), C-major-pentatonic pitches, mud-patch stakes. **Was built complete this fire** (990-line page.tsx + README, self-reported clean) ‚Äî de-selected ONLY on diversity: three.js is a GPU surface (2√ó/10) and the jury asked for "deliberately non-WebGL2" builds, where 886's Canvas2D is the scarce/freshest surface and needs no WebGL or DeviceOrientation permission (more robust for the unattended 06:30 glance). The 3D immersion is genuinely better on a real iPad ‚Äî resurrect on a window where three.js/WebGPU is under-represented. Rebuildable from this seed + the 886 synth.

- **`887-kids-marble-build` (Canvas2D "build-your-own machine" ‚Äî the constructive/programmable angle).** Child drags material blocks (wood ramp, glass bell, metal bar, drum pad) from a tray onto a board, then drops marbles that play whatever track they built; pitch by the block's vertical position (higher = higher, snapped to pentatonic), material sets timbre; an auto-rain mode plays it hands-free. The Wintergatan "program the machine" + Toy Theater "Music Marbles move-the-pegs" (web, March 2026) interaction ‚Äî a 4yo composes spatially with no notation, no two layouts alike. **Builder died on a stream-idle timeout this fire (no folder produced)** ‚Äî pure seed, rebuildable. Strong because it adds genuine *agency/authorship* (the kid builds the instrument) on top of the material-modal engine; resurrect on a Canvas2D-scarce kids window, ideally folding in 886's working `synth.ts`/`physics.ts` as the engine.

## ¬ß529 ‚Äî banked explorers from cycle 529 (ADULT ¬∑ WIDE) ‚Äî 2 seeds

Cycle 529 ran an adult **WIDE** fire ‚Äî 3 orthogonal explorers, all **non-WebGL2** (JURY 2026-06-23 #1: raw-WebGL2 is the new 6-of-15 monoculture, ban it ~3‚Äì4 builds; SVG/Canvas2D are the scarce fresh surfaces, a 4th WebGPU was avoided), each clearing the ambition floor via DIFFERENT tags (input √ó output √ó technique √ó vibe), and each citing the **same-day RESEARCH ¬ß529** in its README to finally land criterion #5 (jury's 0-for-15). **Winner shipped = `883-negative-space`** (Canvas2D ¬∑ mic ¬∑ play-the-instrument-by-being-SILENT ‚Äî the conceptual/critical inversion lane, the highest-surprise concept, zero "too similar" risk, no GPU/no network ‚Üí most robust unattended). Both siblings below returned **complete + self-reported clean** (no `any`/`@ts-ignore`/`use*`-helper/api-route; couldn't run the authoritative build ‚Äî node_modules-in-subagent artifact only). Folders `rm -rf`'d before the authoritative winner-only build (NOT committed) ‚Äî rebuild from these briefs; renumber if a later fire consumes 882/884 first.

### `882-prosody-score` ‚Äî `queued` ‚≠ê (SVG ¬∑ keyboard ¬∑ text-prosody‚Üífunctional-cadence ‚Äî RESURRECT when SVG is cold AND the keyboard input is wanted; freshest surface + coldest input in the lab)
*Type a sentence and your prosody composes a real phrase: inter-keystroke interval ‚Üí rhythm, word length ‚Üí a sine-arch melodic contour, character ‚Üí diatonic scale degree (vowels=chord tones, consonants=passing), and **punctuation ‚Üí functional cadence** (comma=half-cadence/‚ÜíV, period/!/?=authentic/‚ÜíI the satisfying landing, space=breath, capital=fresh phrase) over a wandering I‚Äìvi‚ÄìIV‚Äìii‚ÄìV harmony quantized to C major. Writing becomes scoring.* INPUT keyboard/typing (‚âà0√ó in the lab ‚Äî the coldest input) ¬∑ OUTPUT **inline SVG** (animated self-drawing staff/ribbon: glowing note glyphs at pitch height, violet cadence barlines, auto-scroll w/ edge fade, faint libretto beneath; refs+rAF, no per-frame React) ¬∑ TECH deterministic text-prosody‚Üífunctional-harmony engine (`prosody.ts` `step()`) ¬∑ VIBE literary, ink-on-dark, intimate. Audio: warm FM-Rhodes melody + sustained triad pad following the chord; master ‚Üí ConvolverNode reverb ‚Üí DynamicsCompressor ‚Üí out; gesture-gated. ~2s-idle **ghost-writer** types a poetry line through the identical pipeline (stops on first real key). Files (built, removed): `page.tsx` + `prosody.ts` + `audio.ts` + `score-svg.tsx` + `README.md`. Refs: **Leo≈° Jan√°ƒçek *n√°pƒõvky mluvy* (speech-melody)** (inverted: text‚Üímelody) + **RESEARCH ¬ß529 (2026-06-23)**. Ambition 2‚Äì3/5 (#2 ‚â•3 subsystems [keystroke-timing analyzer + cadence/harmony engine + Web-Audio piano+pad + self-drawing SVG notation] + #3 named ref + #5 README citation). **Why banked, not shipped:** genuinely elegant + the freshest surface (SVG) + coldest input (keyboard) ‚Äî de-selected ONLY because **880 (last cycle) was also SVG**, so shipping it repeats the surface back-to-back, where 883's Canvas2D rotates it; and 883's silence-inversion is the higher-surprise concept this fire. ### `884-living-reverie` ‚Äî `SHIPPED as 888-living-reverie` (cycle 531, DEEP) ‚Äî consumed
*This seed was the resurrect target for cycle 531's DEEP long-form adult fire. Shipped as `888-living-reverie` (the seed's own slug `884` was already a banked SEED name, so the winner was numbered 888; siblings 889/890 banked below in ¬ß531). The original seed text is retained for the record:*

*Karel's own recorded "Welcome Home" piano becomes a 10-minute LIVING reverie ‚Äî genuinely different at minute 8 than minute 1 ‚Äî a generative engine with **memory** that re-voices/transposes/re-orchestrates his motifs across an irreversible arc (sparse ‚Üí blooming ‚Üí dense ‚Üí dissolving), never looping.* INPUT **audio-file** (his real recording streamed via the read-only `/api/audio/549fc519-f7fc-4c38-a771-adaad2edbc81`; ~350ms non-zero analyser health-check ‚Üí **emerald "playing Karel's piano"** else **amber "generative piano ‚Äî live recording unavailable"**; built-in generative-piano fallback needs ZERO network) ¬∑ OUTPUT **three.js v0.182** (~9k additive-glow `THREE.Points` w/ baked radial sprite that accretes‚Üídisperses + re-palettes across the arc; drag-orbit; full dispose + `forceContextLoss()`) ¬∑ TECH a memory bank of **scale-degree pitch-cells** (chroma-folded from his live audio in recording mode) + a four-section state machine + a global irreversible **`age`** walking a modulating modal journey (ionian‚Üílydian‚Üímixolydian‚Üídorian‚Üíaeolian) with creeping tonic; FM-piano + detuned-saw pad + granular shimmer, all pitch-quantized ¬∑ VIBE cinematic, deep, long-form ambient. Master ‚Üí synthesized-impulse ConvolverNode reverb ‚Üí DynamicsCompressor ‚Üí out; low-key HUD (`m:ss / 10:00` + section + mode + age% + bar) makes the arc legible; sounding+moving within ~1.5s on the fallback. Files (built, removed): `page.tsx` + `audio.ts` + `engine.ts` + `scene.ts` + `README.md`. Refs: **Brian Eno** generative (*Music for Airports*, *Reflection*) + **Karel's "Welcome Home" Paths** as seed + **RESEARCH ¬ß529 (2026-06-23)**. Ambition **3‚Äì4/5** (#2 ‚â•3 subsystems + #4 multi-cycle long-form commitment + #3 named refs + #5 README citation) ‚Äî the highest-ambition explorer this fire. **Love/directive aligned:** executes TWO standing Karel directives at once ‚Äî the **thin long-form-generative menu lane** (state/memory/evolution, 5min+) AND **"incorporate Karel's actual music from the Paths."** **Why banked, not shipped:** the most ambitious build, BUT (a) its additive-particle three.js field is the **register closest to existing `859-paths-compute-bloom`** (Anadol-lineage, his piano ‚Üí particles) ‚Äî a mild "too-similar" exposure on the OUTPUT side, where 883 has none; (b) highest runtime risk (GPU + a runtime CORS fetch of his recording, both fall back but the headline "his real piano" may degrade to the amber fallback on the review device). **Resurrect FIRST on a dedicated DEEP long-form adult cycle** ‚Äî it IS the long-form skeleton; deepen the seed‚Üíchroma-memory hand-over (currently restarts the motif scheduler rather than carrying exact phrase state ‚Äî musically seamless through the shared reverb bus but a cruder memory transfer) and consider a non-particle visual to dodge the 859 overlap.
