## ¬ß528 ‚Äî banked explorers from cycle 528 (KIDS ¬∑ WIDE) ‚Äî 2 seeds

Cycle 528 ran a KIDS **WIDE** fire ‚Äî 3 orthogonal explorers, all **non-WebGL2** (jury 2026-06-23 #1: WebGL2 is the new 6-of-15 monoculture, banned ~3‚Äì4 builds; SVG/Canvas2D/audio-only are the 0‚Äì1√ó fresh surfaces), each carrying a *persistent-wrong, no-auto-resolve, child-earns-the-fix* mechanic to finally break the always-consonant kids template (jury #4). Each cleared the ambition floor honestly via #2 (‚â•3 subsystems) + #3 (named refs) ‚Äî NOT #1, since the "never-used technique" candidates were verified false before spawning (reaction-diffusion already in 248/747/518; L-system already in 799). **Winner shipped = `880-kids-rubber-band-band`** (SVG, 0√ó surface; drag-pluck a wall of rubber-bands, over-stretch latches a persistent out-of-tune state you fix by turning the tuning peg). Both siblings below returned **complete + self-reported tsc/eslint clean** (no `any`/`@ts-ignore`/`use*`-helper/api-route). ### `879-kids-grumble-garden` ‚Äî `queued` ‚≠ê (Canvas2D grumble-spread garden ‚Äî a SECOND full template-break; resurrect when Canvas2D is scarce)
*Tap a dark warm night-garden to plant glowing flowers that sing a C-major-pentatonic note; ~1 tap in 5 lands a clashing note and a dark buzzing **grumble-weed** sprouts there and slowly **spreads to its neighbours** (a real diffusion growth sim), droning a spicy beating cluster that does **NOT** auto-resolve. To earn the calm the child taps **on and around** the weed to "water" it ‚Äî each watering pushes the grumble value down until the patch re-blooms into a flower with a sweet chime.* INPUT touch ¬∑ OUTPUT **Canvas2D** (no WebGL/WebGPU/three.js) ¬∑ TECH 14√ó10-grid grumble-spread diffusion sim (`stepGarden`: each bud's `grumble` value diffuses to its 4 neighbours/tick; self-decay deliberately tiny `-0.012/s` so a weed left alone keeps spreading ‚Äî only watering removes it) + interval/consonance engine + Web Audio (sweet bell chimes + a persistent beating grumble voice per weed, root+m2+tritone saw cluster capped 0.12, own 1600Hz lowpass) ¬∑ VIBE living night-garden with real stakes. Kids-safe `master ‚â§0.3 ‚Üí lowpass 7000 ‚Üí comp`, grumble capped *below* chime peak (0.12 vs 0.16) + extra lowpass so it's a *gentle* spicy buzz, never louder/harsher; always-on C3+G3 pad; ~1.4s hands-free auto-demo plays plant‚Üígrumble-spread‚Üíwater-to-rebloom then stops on first tap; full teardown. Files (built, removed): `page.tsx` 373 + `audio.ts` 215 + `garden.ts` 207 + `render.ts` 184 + `README.md` 71. Refs: **RESEARCH ¬ß528 / PMC11336827** (the dissonance must be a LARGE unmistakable contrast) + Toca Boca Band tension-and-release. Ambition 2‚Äì3/5 (#2 ‚â•4 subsystems + #3 + #5 README-citation). **Why banked, not shipped:** genuinely strong and a clean template-break (the weed truly persists with no auto-resolve), BUT (a) **Canvas2D was just shipped 877** (last cycle) and (b) the **garden/growth vibe is thick in the lab** (248-kids-stir-garden, 322-kids-voice-garden, 799-kids-sing-garden) ‚Äî the "too similar" risk. **Resurrect** on a cold-Canvas2D kids window; consider a fresh vibe re-skin (not a garden) to dodge the growth-garden cluster while keeping the grumble-spread mechanic.

### `881-kids-thunder-marbles` ‚Äî `queued` (three.js tilt-marbles ‚Äî **LOVE-ALIGNED**, resurrect when three.js/tilt are scarce)
*Tilt a glowing 3D bowl to roll luminous marbles that ring floating bells (roll velocity ‚Üí brightness/volume, C-major-pentatonic music box); slam two together with violent shaking and one **cracks into a persistent storm-marble** that rumbles a low beating dissonant drone and rolls emitting sour notes ‚Äî calmed only by the child rolling it **slowly** into the bowl's center **cradle** to rest, which heals it back with a rising chime.* INPUT **tilt** (DeviceOrientation, iOS `requestPermission` inside the Start tap) + pointer-drag fallback ¬∑ OUTPUT **three.js** (additive-glow marbles + emissive bowl/rim + floating bell ring + cradle marker) ¬∑ TECH custom rigid-sphere-in-bowl integrator (curvature restoring force + tilt downhill push + rolling friction + rim bounce + marble‚Äìmarble impulse) + collision-relative-velocity‚Üíaudio + a storm/heal state machine (per-marble storm flag, calm timer, dedicated beating-drone voice; `HARD_HIT` trips the crack, `CALM_SPEED`+`CALM_HOLD` in the cradle heals ‚Äî **no timer auto-rescue**) ¬∑ VIBE playful warm slightly-stormy night-bowl. Kids-safe `master 0.22 ‚Üí lowpass 7000 ‚Üí comp`, always-on pad, storm rumble a clearly dissonant ~6Hz beating LOW drone but *quieter* than the bells; ~1‚Äì2s hands-free auto-demo rings bells‚Üícracks a storm-marble‚Üíguides it to the cradle to heal then stops on first tilt/drag; all three.js geometries/materials tracked in a `disposables` array + `renderer.dispose()` + listeners removed + storm voices stopped + `audioCtx.close()` on unmount. Files (built, removed): `page.tsx` 901 + `README.md` 53. Refs: **cross-modal child movement‚Üísound mapping PMC5104747** [foundational] + the **marble-run / kinetic-toy lineage** + **RESEARCH ¬ß528 / PMC11336827**. **Love signal:** **169-kids-marble-run‚ù§Ô∏è + 83-kids-tilt-rain‚ù§Ô∏è** ‚Äî squarely a loved direction. **Why banked, not shipped:** strong and love-aligned, BUT (a) **three.js is 2√ó/10 where the winner's SVG is 0√ó** (the freshest surface won on diversity), and (b) it needs **WebGL + a DeviceOrientation permission prompt** ‚Üí more failure surface for an unattended 06:30 glance than the winner's zero-permission SVG. **Resurrect FIRST among kids seeds** on the next kids night where three.js/tilt are scarce ‚Äî it's the directest love-aligned pick.

Builder shipped it complete + lint/type-clean: a real **WGSL `@compute`** pass integrates a particle field (settle-onto-sphere + tangential drift + twinkle), instanced additive glowing point-quads colored by section, richness‚Üíglobal bloom, drag-to-rotate, Canvas2D dot-globe fallback if no WebGPU, ~250ms offline auto-demo. **De-selected ONLY on diversity + robustness, not quality:** Canvas2D was 0√ó in the last 10 and jury-re-legitimized (the freshest legit surface), while WebGPU was 3√ó and shipping it risked the 4√ó monoculture the jury flags; and the Canvas2D winner renders identically on any 06:30 review device where 878's headline globe degrades to its weaker fallback. **Resurrect:** ship as-is on the next adult night when WebGPU has cooled ‚Äî or, better, fold both into ONE "Biosphere" piece with a map/globe **renderer toggle** over the shared structure engine (avoids two near-identical concepts; the ¬ß523/525 "consolidate the thread" note applies). Two polish edges to fix on resurrect: (1) the design-notes link points at `/dream/878-biosphere-globe/README.md` (a 404 route) ‚Äî use an in-page notes panel like 877; (2) the section-active highlight uses `nowPlaying.endsWith(label)` string-matching ‚Äî drive it off the event's section id instead. ## ¬ß526 ‚Äî 2026-06-23 (cycle 526 ¬∑ KIDS ¬∑ WIDE) ‚Äî two banked explorers

Cycle 526 ran a KIDS **WIDE** fire ‚Äî 3 orthogonal explorers, each a NEVER-USED-or-rare technique on a non-three.js GPU surface (three.js HARD-banned this cycle = 4√ó in the last 10 shipped; Canvas2D banned by JURY 2026-06-22). Each cleared the ambition floor. **Winner shipped = `874-kids-frost-garden`** (tap to plant glowing seeds; a genuine diffusion-limited-aggregation sim grows a luminous coral/frost dendrite, each newly-frozen tip ringing a soft pentatonic chime ‚Äî DLA-as-instrument is unexplored anywhere in 2026; the research-driven primary, calm-bedtime, WebGL2, touch=0√ó in last 10). Both siblings below returned **complete + self-reported tsc/eslint clean** (no `any`/`@ts-ignore`/`use*`-helper/api-route). Folders `rm -rf`'d before the authoritative winner-only build (NOT committed) ‚Äî rebuild from these briefs.

### `875-kids-shell-creature` ‚Äî tilt morphs a glowing sea-creature through the **Gielis superformula** (status: built complete, self-reported tsc/eslint clean, banked ‚Äî ‚≠ê strong, NEVER-USED technique)
- **The question:** "What if a 4-year-old gently TILTS a tablet and a glowing sea-creature slowly MORPHS through living shapes ‚Äî round jellyfish, soft starfish, curling shell, blooming flower ‚Äî each shape humming its own warm chord, a calm lullaby you steer by rocking?"
- **Built this fire (page.tsx + superformula.ts + gl.ts + audio.ts + README).** INPUT tilt (DeviceOrientation beta/gamma ‚Üí superformula `(m,n1,n2,n3)`) + pointer-drag + ~1.5s-idle auto-drift, all feeding ONE steering vector (EMA-smoothed). TECHNIQUE the **real Gielis Superformula** (2003) `r(œÜ)=(|cos(mœÜ/4)/a|^n2+|sin(mœÜ/4)/b|^n3)^(‚àí1/n1)` ‚Äî steering blends params across jellyfish‚Üístarfish‚Üíshell‚Üíflower, so the morph is formula-driven not hand-drawn. OUTPUT hand-written **WebGL2 / GLSL ES 3.00** additive-glow `gl.POINTS` ring (dim inner membrane pass + bright rim pass) over near-black; breathing wobble + hue drift keep it alive when still. AUDIO each shape ‚Üí an always-consonant chord color over a warm pad (round=maj6 / starfish=add9 / shell=sus2 / flower=maj7), `setTargetAtTime`-glide only (no retrigger clicks); kids-safe `master 0.26 ‚Üí lowpass ‚â§6500 ‚Üí comp(‚àí10,20:1)`, analyser off master. iOS gate (AudioContext + `DeviceOrientationEvent.requestPermission()` in the ‚â•72px Start tap). Degrades: no tilt/desktop/denied ‚Üí drag + auto-drift + `text-rose-300` notice; no WebGL2 ‚Üí notice, audio survives.
- **Named ref:** **Johan Gielis, *The Superformula* (Amer. J. Botany, 2003).** **Ambition 3/5** (#1 superformula never-used in the lab [grep 0 folders/0 readmes] ¬∑ #2 ‚â•3 subsystems [tilt + superformula geometry + chord synth + WebGL2 render] ¬∑ #3 named ref).
- **Why banked, not shipped:** genuinely strong + a clean never-used technique, BUT the *musical* interaction ‚Äî "tilt ‚Üí an always-consonant chord morphs" ‚Äî is exactly the **JURY 2026-06-22 banned "drag-one-thing ‚Üí always-consonant chord morphs" kids template** (5√ó; cosmetic agency, no harmonic stakes). The superformula is a beautiful *visual* wrapper on a rut the jury named. **Resurrect** when that template is cold AND give the morph a real consequence (a region where the chord goes genuinely spicy and the child must steer *back* to resolve ‚Äî fold in 868/849's freedom-to-be-wrong mechanic), OR ship it purely as a calm visual-morph toy on a window where cosmetic-consonance is acceptable. The superformula geometry engine itself is reusable for an adult shape-morph piece with real harmonic stakes.

### `876-kids-shadow-zoo` ‚Äî whole-body camera **frame-diff motion** ‚Üí **WebGL2** glowing creature zoo (status: built complete, self-reported tsc 0 / eslint 0, banked ‚Äî supersedes the three.js ¬ß524 `867` seed)
- **The question:** "What if a 4-year-old could wave their WHOLE BODY in front of the tablet and conjure a zoo of glowing creatures that bloom and sing wherever they move ‚Äî no screen to touch, just dance?" (A flailing toddler is the ideal user; there is no pose to hit.)
- **Built this fire (page.tsx + vision.ts + audio.ts + gl.ts + README) ‚Äî the clean WebGL2 re-skin of the ¬ß524 `867` three.js seed.** INPUT front-camera **frame-differencing** (32√ó24 offscreen 2D grab ‚Üí Rec.601 luminance diff ‚Üí coarse 16√ó12 mirrored motion-energy grid, fast-attack/slow-decay IIR; NO MediaPipe/ML/landmarks, analysis-only, frames discarded). Hot cells bloom ~2400 additive `gl.POINTS` creatures over a 700-pt breathing starfield + probabilistically fire warm **C-pentatonic** notes (no wrong notes); total motion opens master lowpass + brightens; 4 vertical zones = 4 timbres/hues (amber/gold/violet/teal). OUTPUT raw **WebGL2 / GLSL ES 3.00** (NOT three.js ‚Äî this is the renderer fix vs `867`). Kids-safe `master 0.26 ‚Üí lowpass ‚â§6500 ‚Üí comp(‚àí10,20:1)`, always-on detuned pad, analyser off master. iOS gate (AudioContext + `getUserMedia` in the ‚â•72px Start tap, mirrored). Degrades: camera denied/absent ‚Üí synthetic figure-8 `stepSynthetic` auto-demo through the IDENTICAL pipeline (blooms + sings within ~1s) + `text-rose-300` notice; no WebGL2 ‚Üí notice, pad + auto-melody keep playing. Full teardown.
- **Named refs:** **Myron Krueger *Videoplace* (1975)** ¬∑ **Daniel Rozin** mirrors. **Ambition 2/5** (#2 ‚â•3 subsystems ¬∑ #3 named refs). Love-aligned: camera/body kids loves `104-kids-mirror-draw`‚ù§Ô∏è, `234-kids-hand-creature`‚ù§Ô∏è. **Honest limit (README):** frame-diff reads *change* not *presence* ‚Äî a perfectly still child fades (by design, to encourage movement); a busy moving background adds noise.
- **Why banked, not shipped:** solid + joyful, and now a clean non-three.js implementation (resolves the ¬ß524 three.js-renderer objection), BUT (a) the body‚Üí**safe-pentatonic** mapping is the exact "*if you have a body, do something a touchscreen can't*" critique the jury leveled at `811` ‚Äî the most ambitious *input* on the safest possible *musical idea*; (b) camera input was 2√ó in the last 10 shipped. **Resurrect** on a cold-camera window AND give the body a consequence a touchscreen can't (e.g. limb *speed/direction* ‚Üí real harmonic motion, or a two-body sync mode per RESEARCH ¬ß524's Moving-Mandala synchrony study ‚Äî the cold multi-user/social menu). This WebGL2 build now supersedes the ¬ß524 `867-kids-shadow-zoo` three.js seed as the canonical version.

## ¬ß524 ‚Äî 2026-06-23 (cycle 524 ¬∑ KIDS ¬∑ WIDE) ‚Äî two banked explorers

Cycle 524 ran a KIDS **WIDE** fire ‚Äî 3 orthogonal explorers, each on a DIFFERENT GPU surface (Canvas2D HARD-banned by JURY 2026-06-22), DIFFERENT input, technique, and vibe. **Winner shipped = `868-kids-monster-keys`** (every chromatic note plays; a large-contrast clash spawns a friendly wobble-monster you CALM by resolving ‚Äî the directest answer to the jury's #1 unfilled kids ask, provocation #3 "freedom-to-be-WRONG, commit to it fully"; raw-WebGL2 = the scarcest GPU surface, so shipping it kept three.js AND WebGPU off the 4√ó ban threshold). Both explorers below returned complete + verified clean (no `any`/`@ts-ignore`/`use*`-helper/api-route; their one `getContext("2d")` each is a NON-viz radial glow-sprite/texture use). ### `867-kids-shadow-zoo` ‚Äî whole-body camera **frame-diff motion** ‚Üí three.js glowing creature zoo (status: built complete + verified clean, banked ‚Äî **TOP kids RESURRECT-FIRST** ‚≠ê)
- **The question:** "What if a 4-year-old could conduct a whole ZOO of glowing singing creatures with their WHOLE BODY ‚Äî just by moving in front of the camera, no precise gestures, no landmarks?" A flailing toddler is the ideal user; there is no pose to hit.
- **Built this fire (page.tsx + vision.ts + audio.ts + scene.ts + README).** INPUT front-camera **frame-differencing** ‚Äî 32√ó24 offscreen canvas, per-cell abs luminance diff between frames, IIR-smoothed to a coarse 16√ó12 motion-energy grid, mirrored (NO MediaPipe, NO landmarks, NO ML, analysis-only, never recorded/transmitted). Hot cells bloom ~2400 additive `THREE.Points` creatures over a breathing starfield + probabilistically fire warm **C-major pentatonic** notes (no wrong notes ‚Äî freeform joy); total motion opens a master lowpass + brightens; 4 vertical zones = 4 timbres/hues (amber/gold/violet/teal). OUTPUT **three.js v0.182**, baked radial glow sprite. Kids-safe `master ‚â§0.26 ‚Üí lowpass ‚â§6.5k ‚Üí comp(‚àí10,20:1)`, always-on detuned-sine pad, analyser off master only. iOS gate (AudioContext + getUserMedia in the ‚â•72px Start tap). **Degrades:** camera denied/absent ‚Üí `text-rose-300` notice + a synthetic figure-8 motion-blob auto-demo through the IDENTICAL pipeline (creatures bloom + sing within ~1s, zero camera); no WebGL ‚Üí notice, pad + auto-melody keep playing. Full teardown (rAF + `track.stop()` + three.js dispose + `forceContextLoss()` + `audioCtx.close()`).
- **Named refs:** **Myron Krueger *Videoplace* (1975)** ¬∑ **Daniel Rozin** mirror works. **Ambition 2/5** (#2 ‚â•3 subsystems [camera frame-diff + motion‚Üíspawn/harmony engine + three.js glow render + ambient pad = 4] ¬∑ #3 named refs). Love-aligned: camera/body kids loves `104-kids-mirror-draw`‚ù§Ô∏è, `234-kids-hand-creature`‚ù§Ô∏è.
- **Why de-selected (NOT a quality flaw):** (a) **diversity** ‚Äî three.js is 3√ó/10, so shipping it would hit the **4√ó audit-ban threshold**, where the winner's raw-WebGL2 is the scarcest GPU surface (2√ó); (b) it stays in the freeform-pentatonic "no-wrong-notes" register the lab is thick on, where 868 commits to the jury's actually-unfilled freedom-to-be-WRONG ask; (c) camera-dependent (the winner needs zero hardware for the 06:30 glance). Genuinely fresh (whole-body, landmark-free) and the directest embodied/"do something a touchscreen can't" answer ‚Üí **resurrect FIRST on the kids lane** when three.js isn't over-represented (or re-skin to WebGL2/WebGPU). Ships clean as-is. Code in `/tmp/dream-losers-524/867-kids-shadow-zoo/`.

### `870-kids-ripple-pond` ‚Äî tap ‚Üí **WebGPU height-field** ripples whose edge-reflections re-ring chimes (status: built complete + verified clean, banked)
- **The question:** "What if a 4-year-old could drop stones into a calm glowing pond at bedtime ‚Äî each splash spreads real ripples that bounce off the edges, and every reflection rings a soft chime ‚Äî an endless, low-stakes, calming pond of light and sound?"
- **Built this fire (page.tsx + gpu.ts + gl.ts + audio.ts + README).** INPUT single-tap. OUTPUT **WebGPU `@compute`** 2D wave height-field ‚Äî triple-buffer ping-pong storage buffers run `next = (2¬∑cur ‚àí prev + c¬≤¬∑laplacian)¬∑damping` with reflecting (Neumann) edges on a 256√ó256 lattice; a splat compute pass on each tap; a pickup-sampling compute pass reads 6 fixed "lily-pad" points back to the CPU, and a sharp swing at a pickup re-triggers a soft chime (pitch ‚Üê pickup height within a warm A2‚ÄìC4 pentatonic, no wrong notes) ‚Äî so the longer ripples bounce, the more the pond keeps singing. Render = height+gradient ‚Üí dusk-blue‚Üíviolet‚Üísoft-gold caustics. Kids-safe `master 0.24 ‚Üí lowpass 5.2k ‚Üí comp(‚àí10,20:1)`, ‚â•50ms attacks, always-on breathing drone. iOS gate. **Degrades:** no WebGPU ‚Üí a hand-written **WebGL2** ping-pong float-FBO version of the SAME physics + `readPixels` pickups (audio keeps playing); no WebGL2 ‚Üí notice, audio still runs; untouched ~1.5s ‚Üí stones auto-drop (ripples + chimes within ~1s). Full teardown (`GPUDevice.destroy()` / `WEBGL_lose_context` + buffers freed + `audioCtx.close()`).
- **Named refs:** KIDS.md **"puddle-jumper"** seed ¬∑ love-aligned **`133-kids-ripple-pond`**‚ù§Ô∏è (this one runs a real GPU height-field, not analytic rings) ¬∑ **Brian Eno** ambient. Honest ambition **2/5** (#2 ‚â•3 subsystems [tap + GPU wave-height-field sim + pickup-readback‚Üíchime scheduler + WebGPU render + drone = 5] ¬∑ #3 named refs) ‚Äî explicitly NOT claiming "first wave-equation" (`827-waveguide-mesh` already runs FDTD).
- **Why de-selected (NOT a quality flaw):** (a) **diversity / monoculture guard** ‚Äî WebGPU is 3√ó/10 across 859/866/869, so shipping a 4th WebGPU piece in 4 cycles is the renderer-monoculture the jury warns against; (b) the **calm bedtime register was JUST shipped last cycle** (`866-kids-rainstick-sky`, JURY #4), so it's the freshly-satisfied ask, where 868 fills the still-open #3. The GPU wave-physics is genuine and now device-real on iPadOS (RESEARCH ¬ß524: Safari 26 WebGPU) ‚Üí **resurrect** when WebGPU is scarce again and a calm piece is wanted; or fold into a longer generative pond. Code in `/tmp/dream-losers-524/870-kids-ripple-pond/`.

## ¬ß523 ‚Äî 2026-06-23 (cycle 523 ¬∑ ADULT ¬∑ WIDE) ‚Äî two banked explorers

Cycle 523 ran an adult **WIDE** fire ‚Äî 3 orthogonal explorers, each clearing the ambition floor via DIFFERENT tags (input √ó output √ó technique √ó vibe), each on a GPU surface (Canvas2D HARD-banned, raw-WebGL2 at the 4√ó audit-ban threshold). **Winner shipped = `869-spatial-grove`** (whole-body MediaPipe-Pose ‚Üí HRTF-spatial-navigation of a fixed grove of long-form-evolving voices ‚Üí WebGPU compute canopies ‚Äî answers the loudest standing OPEN adult ask, jury #2 pose-driven spatial-audio room, with the scarcest renderer + coldest input). Both explorers below returned complete + verified clean (eslint 0 / tsc 0, hand-audited ‚Äî no `any`/`@ts-ignore`/`use*`-helper/api-route; 870's one `getContext("2d")` is a NON-viz radial glow-sprite texture uploaded to three.js, which renders the visible scene). Loser code saved to `/tmp/dream-losers-523/`; both folders `rm -rf`'d before the authoritative winner-only build (NOT committed).

### `870-live-accompanist` ‚Äî a generative jazz trio that LISTENS and follows you in real time (status: built complete + verified clean [eslint 0/0, tsc 0], banked ‚Äî **TOP adult RESURRECT-FIRST** ‚≠ê)
- **The question:** "What if a pianist could play, and a generative jazz trio LISTENED and followed in real time ‚Äî a walking bass and soft comping that track your tempo, your chord, and your phrasing, instead of a fixed backing track?" The directest answer to Karel's standing **journey-engine-alternatives** ask (a jazz-responsive arc) + **live-performance fitness** (#3 of "what Karel cares about most").
- **Built this fire (page.tsx + analysis.ts + audio.ts + midi.ts + scene.ts + README).** INPUT **Web MIDI** (`navigator.requestMIDIAccess`, built-in DOM `MIDIAccess`/`MIDIMessageEvent` types ‚Äî no `any`/no dep) with an on-screen clickable keyboard + a mic autocorrelation pitch detector as fallbacks. `analysis.ts` (`NoteAnalyzer`): a decaying 12-bin **chroma** + a **maj/min/dom7/min7 chord-template matcher** over all 12 roots, plus a median-folded **tempo** estimate (60‚Äì200 BPM, smoothed) from inter-onset intervals. `audio.ts` (`AccompanimentEngine`): a Web-Audio **FM Rhodes** comping voice, a round **walking bass**, brushed-noise drums, on a **look-ahead 8th-note sequencer** that reads the live detected `{root, quality, bpm}` each step so the trio re-voices within a beat or two of your change; master ‚Üí `DynamicsCompressor` ‚Üí limiter. OUTPUT **three.js (v0.182)** intimate club scene: 3 additive-glow presences, a **12-pitch-class ring** that lights to the detected chord, an amber beat pulse, bass-note floor ripples, drag-orbit (hand-rolled glow sprite from a tiny offscreen Canvas2D texture ‚Äî permitted non-viz use). **Degrades:** no MIDI ‚Üí on-screen keys + mic; **auto-demo** = a "ghost pianist" loops a ii‚ÄìV‚ÄìI in F through the IDENTICAL detect‚Üíaccompany‚Üíscene pipeline so the trio audibly follows within ~1.5s, zero hardware; no WebGL ‚Üí `text-rose-300` notice, audio keeps playing. Full teardown (rAF + osc stop + `audioCtx.close()` + mic stop + three.js dispose/`forceContextLoss()` + MIDI listener removal).
- **Named refs:** *Music Minus One* / reactive-accompaniment tradition ¬∑ **Antescofo / IRCAM score-following** (real-time musician-following). **Ambition 2/5** (#2 ‚â•3 subsystems [MIDI/mic input + tempo+chord detection + generative accompaniment engine + three.js scene = 4] ¬∑ #3 named refs; arguably #1 ‚Äî first reactive-accompaniment/score-following-lite in the lab, claimed soft).
- **Why de-selected (NOT a quality flaw):** (a) **diversity** ‚Äî three.js is 3√ó/10, so shipping it would hit the **4√ó audit-ban threshold** (where 864‚Üí863's curation already turned on this exact axis), and the mic-fallback input touches the over-rep touch+mic 8√ó; the winner's WebGPU is the scarcest surface and pose is the coldest input. (b) Builder-flagged perceptual risk: detection from sparse single-note input is tuned to feel like a *human follower* (re-voices in a beat or two), which could read as "sluggish" to a reviewer expecting instant snapping. Genuinely strong and the directest live-performance-fitness / jazz-responsive answer the lab has ‚Üí **resurrect FIRST on the adult lane** when three.js isn't over-represented (or re-skin the viz to WebGPU/WebGL2 to dodge the three.js cap). Brief + code in `/tmp/dream-losers-523/870-live-accompanist/`.

### `871-solar-wind-aurora` ‚Äî the live solar wind plays a sonified aurora; geomagnetic storms intensify it (status: built complete + verified clean [eslint 0/0, tsc 0], banked)
- **The question:** "What if the live solar wind streaming past Earth right now played an aurora ‚Äî the real, current space-weather data turned into a shimmering drone-scape and a curtain of light, with geomagnetic storms audibly intensifying it?"
- **Built this fire (page.tsx + spaceweather.ts + audio.ts + gpu.ts + webgl-fallback.ts + README).** INPUT **live NOAA SWPC** (keyless + CORS): `solar-wind/plasma-1-day.json` (speed/density/temp), `solar-wind/mag-1-day.json` (Bz), `noaa-planetary-k-index.json` (Kp) ‚Äî `AbortController` + 6s timeout, header-name-based defensive parsing (typed `string`/`number`, fill-value filtering, most-recent valid sample), re-poll. `deriveSignals` maps wind-speed‚Üíshimmer energy/tempo, density‚Üídrone thickness/loudness, Bz/Kp‚Üían `activity` signal that intensifies everything. `audio.ts`: a detuned-overtone cold-bright pad (activity-gated partials) + density-driven sub + wind/activity-rated bell shimmers; master ‚Üí `DynamicsCompressor` ‚Üí tanh `WaveShaper` limiter ‚Üí 5s fade; analyser taps master as a dead-end. OUTPUT **WGSL `@compute`** integrating ~60k particles into folding **aurora curtains** (ripple speed/amplitude ‚Üê wind+activity) + additive glow point-quads, cold-green‚Üíteal‚Üíviolet‚Üímagenta as activity rises. **Degrades:** fetch fail/blocked ‚Üí a `makeSyntheticSample` evolving space-weather model drives the SAME pipeline within ~1s + an amber/`text-rose-300` "(simulated ‚Äî live feed unavailable)" badge; no WebGPU ‚Üí notice + a hand-written **WebGL2** aurora on the identical mapping (audio keeps playing); neither ‚Üí audio still plays. Full teardown (rAF + abort fetch + clear poll + osc stop + `audioCtx.close()` + `GPUDevice.destroy()`/`WEBGL_lose_context`).
- **Named refs:** **Ryoji Ikeda *datamatics* / *data-verse*** (data-as-music/image) ¬∑ **NOAA SWPC** real-time feeds ¬∑ the aurora borealis. **Ambition 2/5** (#2 ‚â•3 subsystems [live NOAA fetch+parse + sonification engine + WGSL compute aurora + synthetic fallback = 4] ¬∑ #3 named refs).
- **Why de-selected (NOT a quality flaw):** (a) **too-similar guard** ‚Äî `864-marine-gamelan` (the LAST adult build) was already real-world-data-sonification-with-consequence; shipping 871 would be back-to-back data-sonification, the exact "too similar in design and theme" trap Karel and the jury hammer. (b) The winner answers the lab's loudest *unaddressed* adult ask (jury #2 spatial-audio room, still 0√ó) which 871 does not. Fresh data source (space weather, grep-0√ó) + the scarcest renderer (WebGPU) make it a strong standalone ‚Üí **resurrect** on a later adult cycle once data-sonification has cooled (e.g. ‚â•3 adult cycles after 864), or fold the aurora curtains into a long-form generative space-weather piece (a quiet-sun ‚Üí storm ‚Üí recovery arc over a real 24h compressed to minutes ‚Äî also fills the thin long-form-generative menu). Brief + code in `/tmp/dream-losers-523/871-solar-wind-aurora/`.

## ¬ß522 ‚Äî 2026-06-23 (cycle 522 ¬∑ KIDS ¬∑ WIDE) ‚Äî two banked siblings

Cycle 522 ran a kids **WIDE** fire ‚Äî 3 orthogonal explorers, each a GPU surface (Canvas2D HARD-banned, raw-WebGL2 at the 4√ó audit-ban threshold) on a DIFFERENT off-touch/off-mic input. **Winner shipped = `866-kids-rainstick-sky`** (accelerometer-shake ‚Üí calm WebGPU-compute rain ‚Äî the scarcest GPU surface + the regressed JURY #4 calm register + most robust unattended). Both siblings below returned complete + verified clean (eslint 0 / tsc 0, hand-audited ‚Äî no `any`/`@ts-ignore`/`use*`-helper/api-route; the one `getContext("2d")` in each is a NON-viz use [867 = offscreen webcam frame-diff buffer; 868 = radial glow-sprite texture for three.js], both render via three.js). ### `867-kids-shadow-zoo` ‚Äî whole-body camera **motion-energy** ‚Üí three.js glowing creatures (status: built complete + verified clean, banked ‚Äî **TOP kids RESURRECT-FIRST** ‚≠ê)
- **The question:** "What if a 4-year-old could conduct a zoo of glowing singing creatures with their WHOLE BODY ‚Äî just by moving in front of the camera, no precise gestures, no landmarks?" A flailing toddler is the ideal user; there is no pose to hit.
- **Built this fire (page.tsx + README).** Front camera sampled into a **32√ó24 offscreen buffer**, **frame-differenced** into a coarse motion-energy grid (NO MediaPipe / NO landmarks ‚Äî deliberately robust + light). Hot cells bloom ~2600 additive-glow **`THREE.Points`** creatures over a breathing starfield and probabilistically fire warm **C-major pentatonic** notes; total motion opens a lowpass + brightens the chord. Kids-safe `master 0.26 ‚Üí lowpass 6.2k ‚Üí comp(‚àí10/20:1)`, ‚â•55ms attacks, always-on detuned-sine ambient pad, analyser off master only. iOS gate (AudioContext + `getUserMedia` in the ‚â•72px Start tap, `facingMode:"user"`, mirrored). Full teardown (rAF + `track.stop()` + three.js dispose + `forceContextLoss()` + `audioCtx.close()`). **Degrades:** camera denied/absent ‚Üí `text-rose-300` notice + an **auto-demo synthetic motion-blob sweeps a figure-8** so creatures bloom + sing within ~1s; never a dead screen.
- **Named ref:** **Myron Krueger *Videoplace* (1975)** ‚Äî the foundational full-body camera-interaction artwork (silhouette/motion ‚Üí responsive graphics+sound); optionally **Daniel Rozin** mirrors. **Ambition 2/5** (#2 ‚â•3 subsystems [camera motion-energy field + motion‚Üíspawn/harmony engine + three.js additive-glow point render + ambient bed = 4] ¬∑ #3 Krueger).
- **Why de-selected (NOT a quality flaw):** (a) **diversity** ‚Äî three.js would go 3√ó‚Üí4√ó (over-rep) this window, where 866's WebGPU is the scarcest surface; (b) **runtime risk** ‚Äî camera-dependent (the winner needed none for a no-hardware 06:30 glance). Genuinely fresh (whole-body, landmark-free) and the directest answer to the jury's standing embodied/"do something a touchscreen can't" ask. **Resurrect FIRST on the kids lane** when three.js isn't over-represented and a camera piece is wanted ‚Äî ships clean as-is.

### `868-kids-monster-keys` ‚Äî every chromatic note plays; **dissonance is a friendly wobble-monster you CALM** (status: built complete + verified clean [eslint 0/0, tsc 0], banked)
- **The question:** "What if a 4-year-old had the freedom to play a genuinely WRONG (dissonant) note ‚Äî and dissonance was a friendly wobbly monster they could *calm* by resolving it, instead of a forbidden mistake?" The directest answer to **JURY 2026-06-22 #3** ("give a kid the freedom to be WRONG ‚Äî dissonance reachable AND resolvable, a real harmonic decision with a consequence; `841`'s 'spicy = a creature's mood' gestures at this, commit to it fully").
- **Built this fire (page.tsx + audio.ts + harmony.ts + README).** Every chromatic note sounds; each note is a glowing creature. A clashing interval (m2 / tritone) spawns a gentle **wobble-monster** ‚Äî soft sawtooth + a **+6-cent beating partner** + a slow shimmer LFO (wobble/beating, NEVER louder/harsher); the child **calms it by adding a consonant note** (3rd/5th) ‚Üí it settles into a happy creature with a soft reward bloom (real tension‚Üíresolution, zero fail state). **`harmony.ts`** scores spice from clashing interval classes + computes which consonant note resolves the chord. **Input: Web MIDI** (feature-detected `requestMIDIAccess`, native `MIDIAccess` types ‚Äî no `any`) **+ a big ‚â•84px on-screen colored creature-key row** as the always-available primary. **Output: three.js** additive-glow creatures (consonant = round/steady; monster = soft shivering spikes + beating shimmer). Kids-safe `master ‚â§0.26 ‚Üí lowpass 6.2k ‚Üí comp(‚àí10/20:1)`, always-on pad, analyser off master only. **Degrades:** ~0.7s **auto-demo plays the full spawn-a-monster-then-resolve-it arc** unattended; `text-rose-300` fallback if WebGL unavailable.
- **Named refs:** consonance‚Üídissonance **"tension and release" pedagogy** (resolving dissonance builds a child's confidence) ¬∑ **Toca Band** relational-character model. **Ambition 2/5** (#2 ‚â•5 subsystems [MIDI + on-screen keys + interval-analysis monster/resolve engine + three.js creatures + ambient bed] ¬∑ #3 tension-and-release pedagogy).
- **Why de-selected (NOT a quality flaw):** (a) **diversity** ‚Äî three.js over-rep (would be 4√ó) vs the winner's scarce WebGPU; (b) the harmony-agency template is the most-trodden kids register (the jury flagged 841 as the *third* "stack/shape intervals" piece) ‚Äî 866's calm-shake answers a register the lab is actually *missing*. BUT 868 genuinely fulfils the jury's "commit to freedom-to-be-wrong fully" ask better than anything shipped ‚Üí **resurrect SOON** for the #3 thread (note: pair the on-screen keys as the primary since most kids lack MIDI; consider a non-three.js GPU re-skin if three.js is hot).

### `865-tide-gamelan` ‚Äî the SAME concept as a **three.js** 3D ocean+gong scene (status: built complete + eslint/tsc-clean, banked ‚Äî **top adult RESURRECT-FIRST** ‚≠ê)
- **The question:** identical to 864 ‚Äî the live, real state of the world's oceans plays a bronze gamelan and rough seas DETUNE the metal ‚Äî but rendered as a **3D scene you orbit** rather than a flat caustic shader, so the swell is a literal undulating sea-surface you look across.
- **Built this fire (page.tsx + marine.ts + audio.ts + scene.ts + README).** Same data + audio spine as 864 (keyless client-side Open-Meteo Marine fetch, 4 presets, synthetic fallback; modal physical-modeling bronze synth, slendro/pel√∂g, the `roughness`‚Üídensity/register/Q/¬±35¬¢-detune consequence, period‚Üítempo, direction‚Üípan, swell‚Üídrone; both deepenings ‚Äî groove-quantize toggle + dual-sea equal-power crossfade). **Differentiator (`scene.ts`, three.js v0.182):** a CPU-displaced **sum-of-sines ocean PlaneGeometry** (amplitude‚Üêswell+roughness, speed‚Üêperiod, drift‚Üêdirection) under a floating **6√ó4 emissive bronze gong-field** (additive-halo metallic discs that bob/tilt with the mesh and bloom on strike), drag-orbit + gentle auto-rotate; heavier seas rock the whole field + tilt the horizon. Full dispose (geometries/materials, `renderer.dispose()` + `forceContextLoss()`), `ctx.close()`, abort fetch, clear intervals, panic mute, analyser tap never routed.
- **Named refs:** gamelan slendro/pel√∂g ¬∑ **Ryoji Ikeda *datamatics*** ¬∑ **Bob Sturm *Music from the Ocean***. **Ambition honest 2/5** (#2 ‚â•3 subsystems [live fetch + state-machine + fallback ¬∑ modal bronze synth w/ groove scheduler ¬∑ dual-sea crossfade ¬∑ three.js displaced-mesh + gong-field scene = 4‚Äì5] ¬∑ #3 named refs).
- **Why de-selected (NOT a quality flaw):** (a) **runtime risk** ‚Äî builder-flagged 80√ó80 CPU vertex displacement + per-strike node graphs may want a lower segment count / strike cap on weak mobile, vs 864's pure-GPU fragment shader; (b) the strike‚Üîgong mapping is **approximate** (keys wrap into a 24-gong field ‚Äî conveys activity, not strict 1:1), vs 864's tight audio-led bloom; (c) the resurrect target (860) was always the WebGL2-caustic build, so 864 is the *faithful* "develop what you have" answer the jury asked for. 865 is genuinely distinct (a 3D sea you orbit, not a flat shader) and worth shipping. **Resurrect FIRST on the adult lane** ‚Äî apply a lower mesh segment count (e.g. 48√ó48) + a per-frame strike cap first, then it ships clean; ideal for a cycle wanting a three.js 3D adult piece (note three.js is 3√ó/10).

### `863-kids-solfege-choir` ‚Äî the SAME concept as a raw-WebGL2 GLSL **"ladder of light"** (status: built complete + eslint/tsc-clean, banked ‚Äî **top kids RESURRECT-FIRST** ‚≠ê)
- **The question:** identical to 862 ‚Äî a 4yo SINGS a melody with their bare hand via the Curwen/Kod√°ly solf√®ge hand-signs, and the choir sings the scale degree back ‚Äî but rendered as a vertical **staircase of light** rather than a choir of orbs, so the music's *pitch height* is literally legible in space.
- **Mechanic:** reuses the exact same pipeline ‚Äî front-camera **MediaPipe HandLandmarker** (CDN `webpackIgnore` runtime import, pinned `tasks-vision@0.10.14`, analysis-only) ‚Üí a geometric **7-sign classifier** (per-finger extended/curled booleans + hand orientation ‚Üí do/re/mi/fa/sol/la/ti) with a ~250ms dwell + hand-height ‚Üí octave/brightness, the **~2s-stillness Kod√°ly echo** replaying the last ~6 notes, and the kids-safe choir (`master ‚â§0.28 ‚Üí lowpass 6500 ‚Üí comp ‚àí10/20:1`, always-on open-fifth drone, no wrong notes). **Render: raw WebGL2** ‚Äî a hand-written vertex+fragment GLSL fullscreen-quad shader draws **7 luminous rungs bottom‚Üítop = do‚Üíti** (matching the pitch height **Kod√°ly added to Curwen's signs** ‚Äî do low, la high), the actively-signed rung blooms a soft radial glow, height-shift brightens the whole ladder, perpetual shimmer keeps it breathing; a tiny Canvas2D HUD draws the live hand skeleton (the only 2D). Ghost-hand auto-demo (do-re-mi-sol-mi-do) + ‚â•72px tap-sign buttons on the same pipeline; full WebGL2 teardown (`WEBGL_lose_context` + delete programs/buffers/VAOs). INPUT MediaPipe hands ¬∑ OUTPUT raw WebGL2 ¬∑ TECHNIQUE solf√®ge-sign classification ‚Üí ladder-of-light choir + call-response ¬∑ VIBE warm-luminous kids. Refs: **John Curwen 1870** ¬∑ **Zolt√°n Kod√°ly** (vertical pitch-height) ¬∑ **arXiv 2604.27957, April 2026** (conducting-gesture recognition). Ambition #2 (4 subsystems) + #3 (named refs).
- **Why banked, not shipped:** a clean, genuinely good build whose vertical-ladder rendering is arguably the *smarter* conceptual mapping (Kod√°ly's pitch-height made literal) ‚Äî de-selected purely on **diversity**: raw-WebGL2 is **4√ó in the last 10 shipped** (856, 853, 847, 834), AT the diversity-audit ‚â•4 ban threshold, whereas the winner's three.js is the scarcer GPU surface (2√ó). The winner also edges it on kid-appeal (press-and-hold tap-sign sustain vs 863's 520ms-tap; characterful orbs over an abstract ladder for a 4yo). **Resurrect first next kids cycle** where raw-WebGL2 isn't over-represented ‚Äî or fold the ladder in as a "staircase mode" toggle of a unified solf√®ge piece. Caveat: real-hand sign-classification accuracy + the CDN/WASM MediaPipe load are build/lint-verified, not camera-verified (no camera in sandbox); the classifier favours distinct shapes over clinical accuracy. Brief + code in `/tmp/dream-losers-520/863-kids-solfege-choir/`.

## ¬ß518 ‚Äî 2026-06-22 (cycle 518 ¬∑ KIDS ¬∑ WIDE) ‚Äî two banked explorers

Cycle 518 ran a kids **WIDE** fire: three orthogonal explorers, each a GPU surface (Canvas2D HARD-banned) on a *starved* embodied input that dodges the over-rep touch/mic (8√ó) and the just-used MediaPipe-hands (853)/tilt (849). Research-anchored to embodied music pedagogy (Dalcroze/Orff/Curwen-Kod√°ly) + the June-2026 WebGPU audio-reactive wave. **Winner shipped = `856-kids-rumble-band`** (gamepad dual-stick conduct + beat-synced controller rumble; raw WebGL2 ‚Äî the cross-modal/haptic cold menu, off camera entirely ‚Üí most bulletproof for a no-hardware 06:30 glance, and avoids a 4th MediaPipe-hands build in a row). The two below returned complete + verified clean (tsc/eslint 0); removed before the authoritative winner-only build (not committed). Briefs preserved here; loser code saved to `/tmp/dream-losers-518/`.

### `855-kids-solfege-signs` ‚Äî make the Curwen/Kod√°ly **solf√®ge hand-signs** in the air ‚Üí a choir sings the matching scale degree (status: built complete + tsc/eslint-clean, banked ‚Äî **top kids RESURRECT-FIRST** ‚≠ê)
- **The question:** a 4yo SINGS A MELODY with their bare hand ‚Äî making the classic solf√®ge hand-signs (fist = do, flat hand = fa, point up = ti‚Ä¶) ‚Äî and a choir of glowing creatures sings the matching note back, building a little tune by changing hand shapes. Real music pedagogy, not just "wave your hand."
- **Mechanic:** front-camera **MediaPipe HandLandmarker** (CDN, analysis-only) ‚Üí a geometric 7-sign Curwen/Kod√°ly classifier (do/re/mi/fa/sol/la/ti from finger-extended booleans + hand orientation) with a ~250ms dwell so a held shape rings ONE clean note; hand height = octave/brightness, openness = vibrato; after ~2s stillness the choir replays the last ~6 signed notes (Kod√°ly/Orff echo). Kids-safe chain (master ‚â§0.28 ‚Üí lowpass ‚â§7k ‚Üí comp ‚àí10/20:1), always-on open-fifth drone, no wrong notes. **Output: three.js** choir of 7 additive-glow creature-orbs (active sign blooms); small Canvas2D HUD only for the hand-skeleton overlay (primary surface is WebGL). Ghost-hand auto-demo + ‚â•64px tap-sign fallback on camera-deny. INPUT MediaPipe hands ¬∑ OUTPUT three.js ¬∑ TECHNIQUE solf√®ge-hand-sign classification ‚Üí scale-degree choir + call-response ¬∑ VIBE warm wondrous. Refs: **John Curwen 1870 / Zolt√°n Kod√°ly method**; **CHI "Music Corner" 2024** (gesture rhythm game inspired by solf√®ge hand-signs). Ambition #2 (4 subsystems) + #3 (named refs).
- **Why banked, not shipped:** the most *pedagogically substantive* explorer (teaches real solf√®ge) and genuinely strong ‚Äî de-selected ONLY to avoid a **4th MediaPipe-hands build right after 853** (cycle 517 shipped bare-hand conducting; 731-star-reach/444-aurora-hands/851-banked also hands), exactly the "too similar in design and theme" trap the jury hammers. **Resurrect first next kids cycle** where hands isn't freshly over-used ‚Äî it's the embodied build with the deepest learning payload. Caveat: real-hand sign-classification accuracy + the CDN/WASM MediaPipe load are build/lint-verified, not runtime-verified (no camera in sandbox); `mi`/`sol`/`la` are the most orientation-dependent signs. Brief + code in `/tmp/dream-losers-518/855-kids-solfege-signs/`.

### `857-kids-color-wand` ‚Äî wave any bright colored toy/scarf ‚Üí a glowing comet that sings (status: built complete + tsc/eslint-clean, banked)
- **The question:** a 4yo grabs any brightly-colored object, waves it in front of the tablet, and it becomes a magic wand ‚Äî a comet of light that trails across the screen and SINGS; the wand's COLOR chooses the instrument, its HEIGHT chooses the note.
- **Mechanic:** `getUserMedia` ‚Üí 160√ó120 offscreen canvas ‚Üí RGB‚ÜíHSV ‚Üí 12-bin hue histogram + weighted-centroid to find the largest bright/saturated blob (the physical object; analysis-only, never stored). Hue‚Üítimbre (red bell / blue flute / green pluck / yellow music-box), height‚ÜíC-pentatonic pitch, speed‚Üíloudness + comet length; ~2s stillness ‚Üí soft melodic echo. **Output: WebGPU** (compute advection of ~24k particles + instanced additive glow, WGSL, hand-rolled local type shim so it compiles with no `@webgpu/types` dep) with a **genuinely functional raw-WebGL2 fallback** (same mapping) ‚Äî never Canvas2D-primary. Ghost-wand auto-demo + pointer/swatch fallback on camera-deny. INPUT camera color-blob (NOT landmarks, NOT optical-flow) ¬∑ OUTPUT WebGPU (+WebGL2 fallback) ¬∑ TECHNIQUE color-object tracking ‚Üí hue=timbre/height=pitch comet ¬∑ VIBE dreamy luminous. Refs: 2026 WebGPU audio-reactive particle work; Newton/Scriabin color‚Üítimbre; Daniel Rozin camera mirrors; tangible "wave an object." Ambition #2 (4 subsystems) + #3 (named refs).
- **Why banked, not shipped:** brings the freshest GPU surface (WebGPU, 0√ó in the last 15) and the June-2026 research hook ‚Äî de-selected on **runtime risk**: the WebGPU path is unverified on real hardware (likely falls to the WebGL2 path on Karel's review device), color-blob tracking is finicky on busy/colorful backgrounds + low light, and it's camera-dependent (the winner needs no camera at all). **Resurrect** when WebGPU can be hardware-verified, or ship as the WebGL2 version and treat WebGPU as a progressive enhancement. Brief + code in `/tmp/dream-losers-518/857-kids-color-wand/`.

## ¬ß517 ‚Äî 2026-06-22 (cycle 517 ¬∑ ADULT ¬∑ DEEP) ‚Äî two banked explorers

Cycle 517 ran an adult **DEEP** fire: ONE concept ‚Äî *"The Motet Room": navigate/sculpt a FIXED constellation of HRTF-spatialized choir voices with your body* (the Janet Cardiff *Forty Part Motet* inversion; differs from `677-presence-field`, which attaches a voice to each body joint ‚Äî here voices are pinned and the body moves the listener/sculpts the field) ‚Äî attacked via 3 parallel approaches at three body granularities, each on a different GPU surface. **Winner shipped = `853-motet-room-reach`** (two-hand conducting; raw WebGL2 ‚Äî scarcest GPU surface + richest agency via persistent re-sculpting). The two below returned complete + verified clean; removed before the authoritative winner-only build (not committed). Briefs preserved here.

### `852-motet-room-walk` ‚Äî physically WALK the listener through a fixed oval of voices (status: built complete + tsc/lint-clean, banked ‚Äî **top adult RESURRECT-FIRST** ‚≠ê)
- **The question:** the literal Janet Cardiff gallery experience ‚Äî step left and the soprano corner blooms in your left ear; lean/step toward the camera and you move *deeper* into a fixed oval of ~22 sustained choir voices that never move; you do.
- **Mechanic:** **MediaPipe PoseLandmarker** (CDN `webpackIgnore` runtime import, pinned `@mediapipe/tasks-vision@0.10.21`, never bundled) ‚Üí hip/torso-centre x ‚Üí listener **X**; shoulder-width depth proxy ‚Üí listener **Z**; EMA-smoothed. ~22 voices each a formant cluster (fund+5th+oct+formant partial) on its own fixed `PannerNode` (`HRTF`); only `listener.positionX/Y/Z` moves via `setTargetAtTime`. **three.js** room: fog, floor grid, one additive-halo orb per voice in an oval, each pulsing with its own analyser amplitude; first-person camera follows the listener. Auto-demo (figure-eight walk) + pointer-drag fallback. INPUT body-pose camera ¬∑ OUTPUT three.js ¬∑ TECHNIQUE HRTF nav through fixed field ¬∑ VIBE installation/sacred-ambient. Refs: Cardiff & Bures Miller *The Forty Part Motet* (2001). Ambition #2 (4 subsystems) + #3 (named ref).
- **Why banked, not shipped:** the most *legible* "I move through a fixed field" and the truest Cardiff homage ‚Äî de-selected purely on **diversity**: three.js is already 2√ó in the last 15 (846, 849-ish/805) whereas the winner's raw WebGL2 is the scarcer surface (1√ó), and the winner's two-hand persistent re-sculpting has more *agency-with-consequence* (the jury's core ask). **Resurrect first next adult fire** where three.js isn't over-represented ‚Äî or fold into a single "Motet Room" with a walk/reach/lean mode toggle (multi-cycle DEEP). Caveat: pose path + CDN model fetch + three.js render are build/lint-verified, not runtime-verified (no camera/GPU in sandbox). Deepen: replace synth voices with looped choir-vowel samples (true Tallis colour); `UnrealBloomPass` + floor reflections; full listener orientation (turn shoulders ‚Üí rotate facing); face-scale depth instead of shoulder-width.

### `854-motet-room-lean` ‚Äî head-tracked binaural: turn your head, the choir stays world-anchored (status: built complete + tsc/lint-clean, banked)
- **The question:** the purest, most minimal Motet Room ‚Äî a fixed choir of 16 voices anchored in the real room; the only instrument is your own head. Turn to face the tenors and they come to the front of your hearing; the field stays put in world space while you turn (true head-tracked binaural / Apple Spatial Audio).
- **Mechanic:** **MediaPipe FaceLandmarker** (`outputFacialTransformationMatrixes:true`) ‚Üí yaw/pitch/roll from the 4√ó4 matrix (landmark-derived yaw fallback) + face-centre/eye-distance ‚Üí small listener translation; drives `listener.positionX/Y/Z` + `forwardX/Y/Z` + `upX/Y/Z` via `setTargetAtTime`. 16 SATB voices (detuned saws ‚Üí per-part formant bandpass ‚Üí lowpass) each on a fixed `HRTF` panner; the same pose vectors drive the **three.js** camera so picture and sound rotate together; orbs colour-coded by part. Auto-demo (slow head pan) + pointer fallback; "headphones recommended" hint. INPUT head/face camera ¬∑ OUTPUT three.js ¬∑ TECHNIQUE head-tracked HRTF binaural ¬∑ VIBE meditative installation. Refs: Cardiff *Forty Part Motet*; Apple Spatial Audio head-tracking; **RESEARCH ¬ß517** (browser facial-tracking binaural). Ambition #2 + #3 (+#5 ties to ¬ß517).
- **Why banked, not shipped:** conceptually the most elegant and the directest realization of ¬ß517's research hook ‚Äî de-selected on (a) three.js again (diversity), and (b) "lean" is the least visually *active* of the three for a no-camera morning glance (a slow head-pan auto-demo vs. the winner's two visibly gesturing hands). **Resurrect** as the head-tracked-binaural reference piece, or as the "lean" mode of a unified Motet Room. Caveat: live FaceLandmarker matrix‚Üíyaw sign/scale tuning is the one thing needing a real webcam + ear; auto-demo/pointer paths deterministic. Deepen: convolution reverb keyed to a chapel impulse (Cardiff's was a chapel); per-listener HRTF selection; drive real *Spem in Alium* part-entrances so turning to a section catches its line at the right moment.

## ¬ß516 ‚Äî 2026-06-22 (cycle 516 ¬∑ KIDS ¬∑ WIDE) ‚Äî two banked explorers

Cycle 516 ran a kids **WIDE** fire: three orthogonal explorers, each implementing JURY 2026-06-22 #3 (freedom to be WRONG ‚Äî dissonance reachable AND resolvable) through a DIFFERENT starved embodied input, all on GPU surfaces (Canvas2D hard-banned). **Winner shipped = `849-kids-star-bowl`** (tilt ‚Üí roll stars to a dissonant rim ‚Üí tilt home to resolve; three.js; calm night-blue ‚Äî uniquely hit BOTH jury kids asks: #3 freedom-to-be-wrong AND #4 the regressed calm register). The two below returned complete + verified clean (eslint --max-warnings=0, tsc 0); removed before the authoritative winner-only build (not committed). Rebuild from the briefs in `/tmp/dream-losers-516/`.

### `851-kids-hand-choir` ‚Äî bare-hand conducting: spread hands = tension, together = resolution (status: built complete + lint/tsc-clean, banked ‚Äî **top kids RESURRECT-FIRST** ‚≠ê)
- **The question:** a 4yo conducts a choir of glowing lights with their *bare hands in the air* ‚Äî hands close ‚Üí warm consonant chord; spread wide ‚Üí the top voice glides up through tritone/maj-7th tension (gentle beating + shimmer); bring hands home ‚Üí resolves. Hand height ‚Üí register. One hand: open fingers = tension, fist = resolve.
- **Mechanic:** **MediaPipe HandLandmarker** (CDN `webpackIgnore` runtime import, pinned `@mediapipe/tasks-vision@0.10.14`, analysis-only, never bundled/stored) ‚Üí hand-distance ‚Üí 3-voice consonance‚Üîdissonance engine with `setTargetAtTime` glide-resolution ‚Üí kid-safe polyphonic synth (master 0.26 ‚Üí lowpass 6.5k ‚Üí comp ‚àí10/20:1) ‚Üí **raw WebGL2** additive glow field. Ghost-hands auto-demo on camera-deny. INPUT off-glass MediaPipe hands ¬∑ OUTPUT raw WebGL2 ¬∑ TECHNIQUE hand-distance‚Üíharmony-tension ¬∑ VIBE warm wondrous glow. Refs: tension-release pedagogy (MasterClass/mapflc), MediaPipe HandLandmarker, L√©on Theremin (pitch-by-hand-distance in empty air). Ambition #2 (4 subsystems) + #3 (named refs).
- **Why banked, not shipped:** genuinely strong and the directest answer to the jury's standing "if you have a body, do something a touchscreen can't" ask (body/hands starved, last meaningful use 811/731). De-selected this cycle on (a) the winner uniquely also hits #4 calm, and (b) MediaPipe pose/hands was used recently (811 cycle-502, 731), whereas tilt is colder. **Resurrect first next kids cycle** ‚Äî it's the embodied build the jury most wants. Caveat: real-hand path + CDN model fetch are build/lint-verified, not runtime-verified (no camera in sandbox). Brief saved to `/tmp/dream-losers-516/851-kids-hand-choir/`.

### `850-kids-monster-keys` ‚Äî every chromatic note plays; dissonance is a friendly wobble-monster you calm (status: built complete + lint/tsc-clean, banked)
- **The question:** a piano with NO safe-scale snapping ‚Äî a 4yo can hit a genuinely "wrong"/dissonant note on purpose, watch a googly wobble-monster spring up and shiver (soft beating + shimmer, never harsh), then find the calm note that settles it into a happy glowing creature. The *purest* freedom-to-be-wrong: real chromatic freedom, not a pre-vetted field.
- **Mechanic:** **Web MIDI** (`requestMIDIAccess()`) primary input (big on-screen chromatic key row fallback ‚Äî touch) ‚Üí live interval classifier vs a held C3 root ‚Üí polyphonic synth (master 0.26 ‚Üí lowpass 6.5k ‚Üí comp ‚àí10/20:1, 6-voice cap) with glide-to-consonance resolution ‚Üí **raw WebGL2** additive monster point-sprites with procedural googly-eye/mouth fragment shader + ping-pong RGBA16F bloom trail. INPUT Web MIDI (+ touch fallback) ¬∑ OUTPUT raw WebGL2 ¬∑ TECHNIQUE chromatic-free-play‚Üílive-consonance-classification‚Üícreature-mood ¬∑ VIBE bright playful daylight monsters. Refs: tension-release pedagogy, Toca Band interaction model, Web MIDI API. Ambition #2 (4 subsystems) + #3 (named refs).
- **Why banked, not shipped:** excellent and the most literal #3 ("every note plays"), but (a) it's the deliberately bright/active explorer and this cycle the jury also begged for a CALM kids piece (#4) which only the winner delivers; (b) most kids lack a MIDI keyboard so the realistic primary input is the touch fallback ‚Äî landing on the over-represented touch input ‚Äî whereas tilt/hands are starved. Resurrect on a cycle where bright-active is fine and pair the MIDI path with a non-touch fallback. Brief saved to `/tmp/dream-losers-516/850-kids-monster-keys/`.

## ¬ß515 ‚Äî 2026-06-22 (cycle 515 ¬∑ ADULT ¬∑ DEEP) ‚Äî one banked sibling

Cycle 515 ran an adult **DEEP** fire: one concept ‚Äî **Feedback Ecology II**, cycle-2 of `820`, the network's topology evolving itself (Lorenz drift + Hebbian rewiring) on a GPU surface ‚Äî built via two parallel rendering approaches. **Winner shipped = `847-feedback-ecology-ii`** (raw WebGL2 + ping-pong FBO feedback trails). The sibling below returned complete + verified clean; removed before the authoritative winner-only build (not committed). Rebuild from the brief.

### `848-feedback-ecology-ii` ‚Äî the SAME concept as a luminous orbiting **three.js** 3D graph (status: built complete + tsc/eslint-clean, banked ‚Äî **top adult RESURRECT-FIRST** ‚≠ê)
- **The question:** identical to 847 ‚Äî the 820 resonator network drives its own topology (chaotic-attractor coupling drift + Hebbian edges) as a long-form no-input instrument ‚Äî but rendered as a glowing **3D** graph rather than a flat 2D field, to read the topology change *in space*.
- **Mechanic:** reuses 820's ear-safe audio engine + the same Lorenz (œÉ=10/œÅ=28/Œ≤=8/3) global modulation (x‚Üícoupling, y‚Üíself-feedback, z‚Üídetune/Q) + Hebbian edge plasticity. **Render:** emissive spheres on a tilted 3D ring (brightness/scale ‚àù energy, 820's per-node hues) with additive halo shells; a single additive `THREE.LineSegments` whose per-vertex color/opacity ‚àù live Hebbian weight √ó signal flow (watch links strengthen/fade); a slowly **orbiting camera** so it reads as a living object; a faint Lorenz `THREE.Line` "weather" trail + Lorenz-driven scene fog/background hue; a live drift readout. Used the **additive-halo bloom fallback** (no `UnrealBloomPass`) for build robustness. INPUT no-input/self-oscillating ¬∑ OUTPUT three.js 3D graph ¬∑ TECHNIQUE Lorenz drift + Hebbian self-rewiring ¬∑ VIBE dynamical-system / luminous-orbiting.
- **Why banked, not shipped:** a clean, genuinely good build ‚Äî de-selected purely on **diversity + ambition**, not quality. three.js is 2√ó in the last 15 shipped (846, 805) whereas 847's raw WebGL2 is the scarcer GPU surface (1√ó), and 847's **ping-pong FBO feedback-trail** is a more substantive and *thematically apt* GPU technique for a feedback instrument (luminous decaying energy trails = the David-Tudor ecosystem look). **Resurrect first next adult cycle** where three.js isn't over-represented ‚Äî or fold its 3D-orbit camera + sphere layout into 847 as a "3D mode" toggle (cycle-3 deepening of the 820 thread). Ambition: #4 (multi-cycle, 820 thread) + #1 (chaotic-attractor self-rewiring topology) + #3 (Lorenz 1963; adaptive pulse-coupled networks; Tudor/Nakamura). Brief saved to `/tmp/dream-losers-515/848-feedback-ecology-ii/`.

## ¬ß512 ‚Äî 2026-06-22 (cycle 512 ¬∑ KIDS ¬∑ WIDE) ‚Äî two banked explorers

Cycle 512 ran a kids **WIDE** fan-out: 3 ways to give a 4-year-old **harmonic agency without a pre-approved scale** (JURY 2026-06-21 #4), each via a DIFFERENT input. Research-anchored: RESEARCH ¬ß512 today ‚Äî the **Tonnetz** "consonance = compact closed shape, dissonance = dangly bits" framing + child-harmony-education work. **Winner shipped = `841-kids-hum-stack`** (mic/voice ‚Äî build the chord upward from the child's own sung pitch; the cleanest dodge of the touch over-rep the audit bans). Both non-winners returned complete + with READMEs; removed before the authoritative winner-only build (not committed). Rebuild from the briefs below.

### `839-kids-chord-garden` ‚Äî a kids **Tonnetz** lattice: plant flower-notes; a triad is a closed glowing triangle, far-apart notes sprout a wiggly creature (status: built complete, banked ‚Äî **top kids RESURRECT-FIRST** ‚≠ê)
- **The question:** what if a 4yo could *shape harmony* by planting glowing flowers on a pitch garden, and dissonance became a playful wiggly creature instead of a "wrong" note?
- **Mechanic:** the play surface is a **Tonnetz** triangular pitch lattice (row = perfect fifth +7; up-right diagonal = major third +4; up-left = minor third +3) ‚Äî so **any three mutually-adjacent nodes are a real consonant triad.** Tap buds to plant 2‚Äì4 sustained in-tune flower-voices that layer into a chord over an always-on pad. **Consonance reads as a CLOSED SHAPE** (compact triad ‚Üí petals open, warm amber glow fills the triangle, golden cords join the notes); **dissonance reads as DANGLY BITS** (far-apart notes sprout a friendly googly-eyed wiggly creature that hums a gentle detuned shimmer ‚Äî playful, never a fail). `üå¨Ô∏è New` reseeds; 3 magic-seed preset-triad buttons for instant delight. INPUT touch/drag ¬∑ OUTPUT Canvas2D (warm organic) ¬∑ TECHNIQUE Tonnetz lattice + consonance-as-closed-shape geometry ¬∑ PALETTE warm garden.
- **Why banked, not shipped:** the most *elegant* legibility of harmony in the fan-out, BUT touch-primary (the input the diversity audit bans at 5√ó) and the Tonnetz already exists adult-side (`797-tonnetz-walk`). 841 (voice) was the cleaner diversity dodge. **Resurrect first next kids cycle** ‚Äî it's the strongest standalone idea, just needs a cycle where touch isn't over-represented (or re-skin the input to drag-on-a-lattice that doesn't count as the over-rep'd tap-target pattern). Ambition: #3 named-ref (Tonnetz/Euler/neo-Riemannian) + soft #5 (RESEARCH ¬ß512).

### `840-kids-harmony-sea` ‚Äî tilt a boat across a circle-of-fifths √ó chord-color plane; calm vs choppy water shows consonance (status: built complete, banked)
- **The question:** what if a 4yo could *steer a boat through harmony* by tilting the iPad ‚Äî calm consonant waters vs choppy playful "spicy" waters?
- **Mechanic:** tilt (DeviceOrientation, drag fallback) sails a boat over a 2-D harmonic plane ‚Äî **X = circle of fifths** (root motion, bass pad glides by portamento), **Y = chord color** (major ‚Üí minor ‚Üí add9 ‚Üí sus/cluster). Consonance is legible in the **water**: glassy/glowing/calm in consonant regions, choppy/sparkly + a beating detune in tense ones ‚Äî exciting, not a fail. 3 islands anchor pretty chords with a chime. Always-on sea-pad drone. INPUT tilt (+drag) ¬∑ OUTPUT Canvas2D procedural water ¬∑ TECHNIQUE 2-D harmonic-field navigation ¬∑ PALETTE ocean.
- **Why banked:** conceptually overlaps `828-kids-feelings-sun` (a 2-D field ‚Üí chord-morph already shipped) and tilt was just used in `805`. Build-verified clean by its own builder (lint 0, tsc 0). Resurrect only if a future cycle wants the *body-steered* harmonic-field specifically (it's the most physical of the three).

## ¬ß511 ‚Äî 2026-06-22 (cycle 511 ¬∑ ADULT ¬∑ WIDE) ‚Äî two banked explorers

Cycle 511 ran an adult **WIDE** fan-out (deliberate alternation off the ¬ß510 DEEP; 3 orthogonal explorers directly attack the "too similar" mandate). All three rest his recording and dodge the answering-agent thread (JURY 2026-06-21 #1) and the touch-input over-rep (5√ó in the last 10). Research-anchored: RESEARCH ¬ß511 today ‚Äî **Skylight** (viral June-2026 ADS-B ceiling projector ‚Äî the sky made *visible* but never *heard*) + **"Quasiperiodic Music"** (arXiv 2009.04667). **Winner shipped = `837-quasicrystal`** (de Bruijn pentagrid ‚Üí Penrose aperiodic tiling ‚Üí never-repeating self-similar long-form score; the highest-ambition, highest-surprise, grep-0√ó lab-first). Both non-winners built complete + clean + build-verified (tsc 0, eslint 0); folders saved to `/tmp/dream-losers-511/` then removed before the authoritative winner-only build (not committed). Rebuild from the briefs below.

### `836-flightpaths` ‚Äî the live ADS-B sky over a city, SUNG: each real aircraft a sustained spatial voice (status: built complete, banked ‚Äî top adult RESURRECT-FIRST)
**Built to demoable this fire (page.tsx ~450 lines + audio/render/sim/geo/types modules + README; tsc 0 / eslint 0).** A Canvas2D clinical radar (range rings, heading grid, altitude-shaded blips, 60s fading trails, callsign + FL labels, Ikeda data-instrument palette) over a polyphonic Web Audio engine: every aircraft tracked in a 250 km box around a hub (LHR/JFK/HND/DXB presets) = one sustained voice with a real lifecycle (fade-in on entry, glide while tracked, fade-out on exit). **Mapping:** altitude ‚Üí pitch on an A-Dorian just-intonation lattice ¬∑ ground speed ‚Üí filter brightness/partials ¬∑ compass bearing ‚Üí stereo pan (`StereoPannerNode`) ¬∑ vertical rate ‚Üí vibrato/glide ¬∑ traffic density ‚Üí harmonic richness + a sub-drone bed. **Data:** client-side `fetch` to `api.airplanes.live/v2/point/{lat}/{lon}/{nm}` polled ~8s (no api route ‚Üí no guard); on ANY failure (CORS/offline/rate/auth) it falls back seamlessly to a **16-aircraft simulated traffic field** running the identical pipeline + an amber "simulated sky" badge ‚Äî fully demoable with no network. Animates a sky on load; gesture-gated AudioContext; full teardown (rAF + abort fetch + nodes + `audioCtx.close()`). **Named ref:** **Skylight** (Tom's Hardware, June 2026 ‚Äî the viral ADS-B *visual* projector this piece SONIFIES, the un-done step) + airplanes.live + Andrea Polli airspace-sonification lineage. **Ambition #1+#2** ‚Äî #1 first aircraft/ADS-B/transit data source in the lab (grep-0√ó: NOAA space-weather / USGS seismic / Wikimedia / Open-Meteo are owned; aircraft never) ¬∑ #2 ‚â•3 subsystems (live ADS-B poll/parse + per-aircraft spatial-panned polyphonic synth w/ lifecycle + density‚Üíharmony engine + Canvas2D radar = 4). The directest answer to **JURY 2026-06-21 #3** ("music *about* something other than music" ‚Äî real-world-data sonification, a cold register). **Why de-selected (not a quality flaw):** the concept's whole thesis is *realness*, and the live feed will often be CORS/network-blocked ‚Üí most visitors see the (well-built) simulated sky, softening "real aircraft" to "plausible flight sim." **Resurrect FIRST next adult cycle** ‚Äî it's a genuine real-world-data lab-first; clean next step = verify the airplanes.live CORS/feed actually returns in-browser (if blocked, swap to an alternate CORS-friendly ADS-B mirror or a tiny same-origin proxy under a guarded api route), then ship; deepening = HRTF `PannerNode` for true overhead spatialization + a "follow one flight" focus mode.

### `838-hocket-loom` ‚Äî play ONE live line; the instrument shatters it across an interlocking ensemble (hocket / kotekan / phase) (status: built complete, banked)
**Built to demoable this fire (page.tsx + engine/synth/loom-viz modules + README; tsc 0 / eslint 0).** A live-performance instrument: play via **Web MIDI** (auto-detect, "MIDI connected ‚úì" / amber "no MIDI ‚Äî use your keyboard") or a **computer-keyboard piano row** (A‚ÄìL chromatic from C4), and every note is distributed in real time across 2‚Äì6 distinct FM/physical-model timbres (marimba/metallophone/plucked/flute/tabla/vibes) by a **`HocketEngine`** with three interlock modes: **hocket** (medieval round-robin ‚Äî consecutive notes hop voices), **kotekan** (Balinese polos/sangsih ‚Äî two parts fill each other's gaps into a continuous stream), **phase** (Reich canonic drift). Density + BPM-grid controls; a **canvas voice-loom** (N color-coded scrolling lanes, glowing pitch-mapped onset pills, primary vs echo opacity, playhead) makes the braided interlocking legible; an idle ‚â•2s **seed melody** auto-plays through the same engine so it's never silent, yielding the moment you play. Master `gain ‚Üí lowpass ‚Üí DynamicsCompressor`; gesture-gated; full teardown (rAF + key listeners + MIDI release + nodes + `audioCtx.close()`). **Named refs:** medieval hocket (Ars Nova / Notre-Dame) ¬∑ Balinese kotekan ¬∑ Steve Reich phase. **Ambition #1+#2+#3** ‚Äî #1 first dedicated hocket/kotekan engine (grep: only an incidental "kotekan" mention in `426-euclid-engine` README, no engine) ¬∑ #2 ‚â•3 subsystems (Web MIDI + keyboard input + real-time hocket distribution engine + 6-timbre ensemble synth + canvas loom viz = 4) ¬∑ #3 named refs. MIDI input is starved (1√ó in last 10). **Why de-selected (not a quality flaw):** canon/echo/interlock is the lab's most-trodden thematic ground (`302-mirror-canon-round`, `375-tempo-canon`, `559-kids-clap-canon`, `298-kids-echo-friend`, etc.) ‚Äî lowest surprise of the three even though the hocket *mechanism* is new. **Resurrect when** a live-performance / MIDI cycle is wanted (Karel priority #3); clean next step = make the kotekan interlock *quantize to a shared pulse* so polos/sangsih genuinely lock (currently echo-offset), and add a "freeze loop" so a played phrase becomes a sustained interlocking ostinato you solo over.

## ¬ß510 ‚Äî 2026-06-22 (cycle 510 ¬∑ KIDS ¬∑ DEEP) ‚Äî one banked sibling

Cycle 510 ran a kids **DEEP** fire on ONE concept ‚Äî *the color you MIX is the chord you HEAR* ‚Äî via two physically-opposite color models, both raw WebGL2 with GPU `readPixels` so the heard chord exactly equals the seen color. Winner shipped = **`834-kids-paint-mixer`** (subtractive **Kubelka-Munk pigment** mixing ‚Äî blue+yellow=green like real paint, a lab-first; resurrects the ¬ß508 ‚≠ê `829-kids-color-blend` and upgrades it from approximate RGB blend ‚Üí physically-correct pigment optics + real pixel-readback). The sibling below built complete + clean and was de-selected on novelty (additive RGB light mixing is the *ordinary* mix; the pigment one is the lab-first + the fresher surprise). Saved to `/tmp/dream-losers-510/`, removed before the authoritative build (not committed). Rebuild from the brief below.

### `835-kids-light-mixer` ‚Äî mix beams of colored LIGHT (red+green=yellow, all=white) and the glow you make IS the chord (status: built complete, banked)
**Built to demoable this fire (page.tsx 846 lines + README; builder reports red+green‚Üíyellow / all‚Üíwhite verified in-shader, readPixels‚ÜíHSV‚Üígliding chord, kids-safe chain).** A **raw WebGL2** GLSL ES 3.00 additive-light field: 3 big glowing orbs (warm gold-red ¬∑ green ¬∑ blue-violet) drag over a near-black procedural starfield; a single full-screen fragment shader accumulates each light additively (`core exp(-d¬≤/r¬≤k) + halo + domain-warped bloom`) with a Reinhard tone-map that preserves hue ratios ‚Äî so overlaps ADD like stage spotlights (gold+green‚Üíyellow, all three‚Üíbrilliant white). Each ~40 ms frame `readPixels` a 16√ó16 block under the active light ‚Üí HSV ‚Üí `applyColorHarmony`: hue‚Üíchord quality over a fixed C+G drone (warm‚Üímajor/add9, green‚Üísus, blue‚Üíminor, violet‚Üímaj7), brightness (light pile-up‚Üíwhiter pixel‚Üíhigher value)‚Üía shimmer C4 octave layer when all three overlap; all glides via `setTargetAtTime` (TC 0.06), drones fade in ~1.2s, no retriggering. Kids-safe `masterGain 0.28 ‚Üí lowpass 7 kHz ‚Üí DynamicsCompressor(‚àí10/20:1)`; iOS gesture-gated; ‚â•64px targets; auto-drift after ~1.5s idle; WebGL2-null ‚Üí CSS `mix-blend-mode:screen` fallback + `text-rose-300` notice; full GL/audio teardown. **Named refs:** Newton 7-color‚Üî7-note ¬∑ Scriabin *clavier √Ý lumi√®res* ¬∑ additive (RGB-light) color theory (the deliberate contrast to 834's subtractive paint). **Ambition ~2/5** (#2 ‚â•3 subsystems [touch drag + additive-light GLSL field + readPixels color‚Üíchord synth + WebGL2 render = 4] + #3 named refs). **Why de-selected (not a quality flaw):** additive RGB light mixing is the *physically ordinary* mix (and the original 829 framing) ‚Äî 834's Kubelka-Munk *pigment* mixing is the genuine lab-first, more kid-native (a 4-year-old knows finger-paint, not stage lighting), and the directer embodiment of the ¬ß510 research. **Resurrect when** the kids lane wants the cold WebGL2 surface and a light-mixing (vs paint) color piece, or as the "light room" companion to 834's "paint room"; clean next step = let the child *toggle* paint‚Üîlight mode to feel the difference (blue+yellow=green vs blue+yellow=gray), which is itself a tiny color-science lesson.

## ¬ß502 (Cycle 502 ‚Äî kids WIDE fire, 3 orthogonal explorers, 2 banked)

Cycle 502 ran a kids **WIDE** fan-out (deliberate alternation off the DEEP 501; JURY 2026-06-20 kids guidance is *spread*-shaped). Three orthogonal explorers, each clearing the ambition floor via DIFFERENT input √ó output √ó technique, all off every current ban (touch-primary / Canvas2D-primary / his-recording-conductor / bright-active-kids) AND off the three.js 3√ó-in-a-row monoculture (799‚Üí803‚Üí805). Research-anchored: RESEARCH ¬ß502 today ‚Äî **¬°Otro!** (IAIA Digital Dome premiere Apr-2026, real-time movement‚Üísonification) + **Frid et al.** (children movement-sonification, Frontiers 2016). **Winner shipped = `811-kids-body-ribbons`** (full-body UPIC ‚Üí glowing SVG ribbons; the jury-#4 most-starved body-input pick on the jury-#1 starved SVG surface). Both non-winners were built to demoable and self-reported clean; folders saved to `/tmp/dream-losers-502/` then removed before the authoritative build (not committed). Rebuild from the briefs below.

### `812-kids-breath-bubbles` ‚Äî blow softly into the tablet and grow a slow drifting cloud of glowing bubbles that chime as they rise and pop (status: queued)
**The brief that was built and banked.** The mic reads **breath ENERGY only** (RMS off an `AnalyserNode.getByteTimeDomainData()`, analysis-only ‚Äî never pitch/speech/recognition, never wired to output, frames discarded, nothing recorded/sent). A soft blow spawns glowing bubbles; **harder blow = bigger, lower-toned** (warm amber/rose, rises slow); soft blow = small/bright/rises fast; they drift up and sway, and on reaching the top (or a big gust) **pop with a soft C-major-pentatonic sine-bell chime** (+ 2nd-harmonic shimmer, 1‚Äì3s tail) ‚Äî accreting into a calm rising cloud over minutes, long-form, never a hard loop. **Bonus realized:** a multi-pop (3+ together) **harmonizes into a chord** so the cloud *sings*, not just chimes. **Output: raw WebGL2 (GLSL ES 3.00)** additive `gl.POINTS` glow-sprites (iq soft exponential falloff + faint rim, `SRC_ALPHA, ONE`) over a dim warm-dusk gradient ‚Äî the iPad-reliable scarce renderer (last WebGL2 ship was `788`, cycle 494; off the three.js streak AND the banned Canvas2D). Synth only (rests his recording). Kid-safe `gain 0.28 ‚Üí lowpass 7000 ‚Üí comp(‚àí10/20:1)`, always-on C3‚ÄìG3‚ÄìC4 pad, soft attacks/long decays, ‚â•64px Start, iOS AudioContext+getUserMedia (EC/NS/AGC off) both in the first tap, full teardown (rAF + mic tracks + nodes + `audioCtx.close()` + delete GL program/buffer/VAO + lose-context). Degrades: mic denied ‚Üí a **ghost-breath auto-demo** spawns+pops hands-free within ~2s + `text-rose-300` notice; no WebGL2 ‚Üí Canvas2D bubble field that still drifts+chimes. Refs: **Toshio Iwai** (Electroplankton) ¬∑ breath/wind-controller lineage (EWI) ¬∑ **Inigo Quilez** (additive glow). Ambition ~2/5 (#2 ‚â•3 subsystems [breath-RMS analysis + particle spawn/growth physics + WebGL2 render + pop-chime synth] + #3 refs). **Why banked not shipped:** honest self-framing ‚Äî blow-to-bubbles is a familiar toy form, and `811` better serves the jury's #4 body-input gap + #5 build-on-your-own-research. **Resurrect when** a breath/vocalization piece is wanted on a cold mic window; clean next step = let bubble *clusters* sustain a held chord while aloft, not only on pop.

### `813-kids-sky-lullaby` ‚Äî the REAL sky right now (sun, clouds, moon phase) sings a 4-year-old a slow dusk lullaby they stir with a tilt (status: queued ‚Äî ‚öÝÔ∏è finish a coordinate-space pass first)
**The brief that was built and banked.** The child's **live local sky** from **Open-Meteo** (free/no-key/CORS; `current=temperature_2m,cloud_cover,is_day,weather_code` + `daily=sunrise,sunset`; coords from `navigator.geolocation`, London silent default) is the **composer**: `is_day`+time ‚Üí key & register (day = warm major celesta; dusk/night = soft suspended pads, octave-down), `cloud_cover` ‚Üí note density, `temperature` ‚Üí timbral warmth (sine‚Üítriangle), **computed moon phase** (synodic approximation from the date) ‚Üí a slow shimmering high voice (bright near full, near-silent at new). A **Chris-Wilson look-ahead scheduler** plays a sparse pentatonic ostinato that **mutates/re-seeds every ~45‚Äì90s** (Eno, never an exact loop); the child **tilts** a glowing star-cursor across the sky (drag fallback) and where it rests adds a key-snapped twinkle. **Output: inline SVG/DOM** (deep-blue‚Üíviolet‚Üígold gradients, blurred moon/cloud/star shapes; rAF repaint) ‚Äî the renderer the lab is genuinely scarce on (off the three.js streak AND banned Canvas2D). Kid-safe `gain ‚â§0.28 ‚Üí lowpass 7000 ‚Üí comp(‚àí10/20:1)`, soft attacks / 2‚Äì4s releases, always-on C2+G2+C3 drone bed, ‚â•64px Start, iOS AudioContext + `DeviceOrientationEvent.requestPermission()` both in the first tap, full teardown (scheduler/rAF/nodes/`audioCtx.close()`/abort fetch). Degrades: geo denied ‚Üí default city; fetch/CORS fail ‚Üí baked "example sky" + `text-rose-300` notice while audio plays hands-free within ~2s. Refs: **Brian Eno** (*Music for Airports* / *Reflection*) ¬∑ **Andrea Polli** + **Natalie Miebach** (weather/data sonification) ¬∑ **Open-Meteo**. (This is the ¬ß500-banked `806-kids-sky-lullaby` renumbered + rebuilt; differentiated from `575-kids-sky-song`'s bright weather toy ‚Äî this is its quiet, dusk-calm, moon-aware, tilt-stirred, generative twin.) Ambition ~2/5 (#2 ‚â•3 subsystems [live API + generative scheduler + SVG render + synth] + #3 refs + soft-#5 RESEARCH ¬ß502). **Why banked not shipped:** `811` was the jury-pointed pick (body 4√ó‚Üí0√ó + the unbuilt full-body UPIC); ALSO this build's agent was killed **mid-fix on a known SVG coordinate-space bug** (moon/cursor positions mixing pixel coords with the `viewBox 0 0 100 100` SVG-unit space) ‚Äî **resurrect requires finishing that coordinate pass + a browser check** before it can ship clean. Otherwise the most robust banked seed (always sounds + animates on Start regardless of geo/network).

## ¬ß501 (Cycle 501 ‚Äî adult DEEP fire, ONE concept √ó 3 resonator engines, 2 banked)

Cycle 501 ran an adult **DEEP** fan-out ‚Äî ONE concept (*play or sing into your mic and exactly the right strings ring back: a bank of ~88 tuned strings that vibrate **sympathetically** to your live spectrum, the way a grand piano's strings hum when you hold the sustain pedal*), attacked via **3 resonator engines**. The realization of the standing adult RESURRECT-FIRST `798-sympathetic-strings`. Renderer-diverse on purpose ‚Äî all three are **SVG/DOM** (the jury-starved 1√ó surface), dodging the new **three.js 3√ó-in-a-row monoculture** (799‚Üí803‚Üí805 ‚Äî flagged in MORNING) and the banned Canvas2D. Research-anchored: RESEARCH ¬ß501 today ‚Äî **Electronic Audio Experiments *Prismatic Wall*** (sympathetic string resonator, tuned-delay-line Karplus-Strong, Jan-2026 reviews; 4 interval-spacing modes) + arXiv *Dialogue in Resonance* (May-2026) + *Interactive Neural Resonators*; refs **Karplus-Strong/Jaffe‚ÄìSmith** (CMJ 1983), sitar *tarab* / viola d'amore, Henry Cowell string-piano. **Winner `808-sympathetic-strings`** (real Karplus-Strong tuned-delay-line bank in a Blob-URL AudioWorklet ‚Äî the literal Prismatic-Wall model; FFT per-string excitation scaling, sustain-pedal feedback physics 0.92‚Üî0.997, ghost exciter, click-to-pluck, level‚ÜíSVG glow, full teardown; the only candidate implementing the named reference's delay-line model rather than a bandpass/comb approximation, and the most distinct from `803`'s modal-bandpass banks). Both losers demoable + self-reported clean; folders saved to `/tmp/dream-losers-501/` and `rm -rf`'d before the authoritative winner-only build (not committed). **Max committed folder after this fire is `808`** (banked names below renumber when resurrected). Resurrect by re-spawning the brief:

- **`809-sympathetic-modes`** (banked, IDEAS ¬ß501). *Same concept via an **FFT harmonic-gated high-Q BiquadFilter bandpass resonator bank** (61‚Äì88 strings; per-string gate opens when live FFT energy sits at the string's pitch+harmonics, slow release = sympathetic tail).* INPUT mic/click/ghost ¬∑ OUTPUT SVG ¬∑ TECH FFT-gated bandpass bank + sustain release physics ¬∑ VIBE dark/nocturnal/contemplative. Built clean at 809 (`page.tsx` 503 + `audio.ts` 455 + README; self-reported demoable). **Why it lost:** the bandpass-resonator-bank DSP **rhymes with `803-body-chimes`** (cycle 499, damped-sine modal bandpass banks) ‚Äî two cycles apart, the "too similar" risk; and it's a *bandpass approximation* of sympathetic strings, where the winner is the literal tuned-delay-line model. **Resurrect when:** a cycle wants the most CPU-frugal (no worklet) sympathetic engine, or to A/B the harmonic-gating legibility against 808's continuous excitation. Honest 3/5 (#2 + #3 + soft #5).
- **`810-sympathetic-comb`** (banked, IDEAS ¬ß501). *Same concept via a **native feedback-COMB-filter bank** (DelayNode `delayTime = 1/freq` + feedback GainNode + one-pole lowpass damping per loop; ~48 combs, all-native, no worklet ‚Äî prioritizes robustness).* INPUT mic/click/ghost ¬∑ OUTPUT SVG ¬∑ TECH feedback-comb bank + per-comb AnalyserNode energy tap ¬∑ VIBE nocturnal shimmer. Built clean at 810 (`page.tsx` 643 + `audio.ts` 540 + README; self-reported demoable). **Why it lost:** the all-native comb approach is the most robust but the *least* faithful to the Prismatic-Wall tuned-delay-line model (a comb rings at `1/delay` but lacks KS's loop-filter string timbre); lost on concept-fidelity, not quality. **Resurrect when:** a worklet-free fallback build is wanted, or to compare comb vs KS string color. Honest 3/5 (#2 + #3 + soft #5). **Banked idea worth folding into 808 next cycle:** its per-comb AnalyserNode energy tap is a cleaner per-string level source than 808's worklet `postMessage` levels.
