### `714-kids-feed-monster` ‚Äî `queued` (genuinely funny + a fresh *framing* of the loop-stacker as FEEDING). **Drag silly foods into a hungry googly monster's mouth ‚Äî it CHOMPS them in rhythm and BURPS a bubbly note, and the more you feed it the groovier the beat gets.** Built files (self-reported clean; removed): `page.tsx` (5 draggable foods [üç© round kick ¬∑ üçå boing pluck ¬∑ üå∂Ô∏è spicy hat + comic red-shiver ¬∑ üßÅ bell ding ¬∑ üç¶ brr tremolo], each on its own 16-step pattern; drop-in-mouth toggles that food's looping layer into a **Chris-Wilson look-ahead scheduler** [25ms tick / 120ms ahead / 100 BPM] over an always-on soft shaker pulse; squash-stretch jaw synced to chomps + belly that grows + comic descending **BURP** w/ bubble particles, more frequent the more it's eaten; belly-tap spits a layer out [no penalty]; instant <50ms chomp blip on feed; safe chain `master(0.28)‚Üílowpass(7500)‚Üícomp(‚àí10/20:1)`; 2.5s-idle ghost-hand auto-demo) + `README.md`. **Tags:** touch-drag (object-to-target as the sequencer-trigger) INPUT ¬∑ Canvas2D googly-monster OUTPUT ¬∑ "feeding as a step-sequencer" loop-stacking groove TECH ¬∑ silly hungry-monster VIBE (laugh-first). **Why it lost:** its underlying mechanic (Incredibox look-ahead loop-stacking) is the EXACT thing `702`/`711`/`696` already shipped ‚Äî lowest diversity of the three despite the charming feeding framing. **Refs:** Toca Kitchen ¬∑ Incredibox/Sprunki ¬∑ Cookie Monster/Pou. **Resurrect when** the kids side has been off loop-stackers for several cycles and wants a warm character toy; deepen with more foods, a "too-full" comic hiccup mode, and foods that change the monster's color/shape.

## ¬ß465 ‚Äî 2026-06-18 ¬∑ adult WIDE fire (3 divergent ambitious explorers on 3 different INPUTS √ó techniques √ó vibes ‚Äî deliberately breaking the adult side's 4-cycle DEEP streak per JURY 2026-06-17 "the median has gone comfortable"; lead = the literal realization of JURY #4 "sonify a non-market live stream for free") ‚Äî 2 banked seeds

Cycle 465 ran an adult **WIDE** fan-out: 3 briefs on 3 different inputs (external-live-data / WebMIDI / autonomous-sim), each clearing the ambition floor via different tags, none a banned tag. Research-led by RESEARCH ¬ß465 (the EMSC / SeismicPortal **free no-auth real-time WebSocket** ‚Äî `wss://www.seismicportal.eu/standing_order/websocket` ‚Äî is one URL away from the JURY-#4 ask). **Winner: `712-seismic-room`** ‚Äî the lab's first real-world external-live-data instrument (each earthquake on Earth ‚Üí a struck tone + a ripple on a Canvas2D world map; Gutenberg‚ÄìRichter Poisson fallback always sounds). Chosen as the literal JURY-#4 build + the today's-research‚Üítoday's-build chain + the most bulletproof glance. Both losers were demoable + self-reported clean tsc+eslint for their folders; the authoritative winner-only `npm run build` (exit 0, 503/503 static) is the gate; losers `rm -rf`'d before it. Resurrect by re-spawning the brief ‚Äî **max committed folder after this fire is `712`** (so 713/714 below are free to reuse as real numbers; renumber only if a later fire consumes them first):

### `713-shadow-duet` ‚Äî `SHIPPED ¬ß467` (realized as `718-duet-paths`, the concatenative "wired to your Paths piano" version Karel named; heuristic `716` + style-learner `717` variants banked ¬ß467) ‚≠ê RESURRECT FIRST [now points at the ¬ß467 banks] (the lab's FIRST Web MIDI piece + the jazz-responsive alt-arc Karel asked for + serves Karel-the-pianist directly). **Play a phrase (MIDI keyboard / computer-keyboard / on-screen keys) and a machine improviser ANSWERS IN THE GAPS ‚Äî it listens to your last phrase and trades fours with you, like a duet partner with taste.** Built files (clean tsc+eslint, no `any` ‚Äî minimal Web MIDI typings; removed): `page.tsx` (single-file: `navigator.requestMIDIAccess()` w/ note-on/off `0x90`/`0x80` parse + device-name emerald badge + `onstatechange` hot-plug; computer-keyboard fallback `a s d f g h j k l` + `w e t y u o` pre-snapped to **D Dorian** w/ `z`/`x` octave shift + on-screen mini-keyboard; **phrase detection** [ends after ~750ms silence past ‚â•2 notes ‚Üí analyze pitch-set / register / density / contour]; **response generation** [score 12 tonics against Dorian/Mixolassign ‚Üí infer key+mode; answer with COMPLEMENTARY register + INVERTED contour + density-matched + consonant chord-tone landing, via a weighted Markov-ish step-walk ‚Äî an answer, never an echo]; **ducking turn-taking** [strike mid-shadow ‚Üí it yields]; idle ~6s auto-demo; warm FM e-piano [pan L] vs cool synth-flute [pan R] + binding pad + limiter; Canvas2D piano-roll **river** [your notes warm amber/rose, shadow's cool cyan, flowing past a now-line]; full teardown) + `README.md`. **Technique:** real-time machine-improviser call-and-response (phrase analysis ‚Üí key inference ‚Üí intentional answer). **Why it lost:** 712 is the *literal* JURY-named build (#4 non-market live stream) + the today's-research chain + more bulletproof (no interaction needed for a glance); 713's WebMIDI wasn't from this cycle's dive. **Why resurrect ‚≠ê:** it's the lab's **FIRST WebMIDI** (a genuine never-used technique = ambition #1), it directly serves **Karel-the-pianist** + realizes the **jazz-responsive alternate journey-arc** Karel explicitly asked for + the live-performance-fitness priority, and it softens the standing solo monoculture (you're never alone ‚Äî a second voice answers). Honest 3/5 (#1 first WebMIDI + #2 ‚â•4 subsystems [MIDI/keyboard input + phrase analyzer + generative responder + dual-voice synth + Canvas piano-roll] + #3 named refs [George Lewis *Voyager* ¬∑ jazz trading-fours]). Tags: **MIDI/keyboard INPUT** (off banned touch-primary) ¬∑ Canvas2D OUTPUT ¬∑ machine-improviser call-and-response TECH (NOT a harmonic event as subject ‚Äî the subject is *conversation/turn-taking*) ¬∑ jazz-responsive VIBE (NOT hushed). **Deepen on revival** (from its README): velocity-sensitive dynamics (soft phrase ‚Üí tender answer); style-learning over a session (the shadow grows to sound like *your* partner); harmonic memory (the pad follows a slow form seeded by your recurring pitch sets). Strongest next step: feed it Karel's actual Paths piano (analyze a real recording's phrases ‚Üí the shadow answers *his* playing).

### `714-physarum-choir` ‚Äî `queued` (the autonomous slime-mold agent-network choir ‚Äî resurrect for an autonomous-generative / WebGPU-compute slot). **Thousands of agents grow a living Physarum transport network across the screen, and you HEAR the network forming, branching, and collapsing as an evolving choir; tap to drop "food" and the colony converges over the next seconds (with a bloom tone).** Built files (clean tsc+eslint, no `any`, helpers `make*`/`step*`/`diffuse*`/`draw*`/`sample*`/`read*`; removed): `page.tsx` (single-file: Jones-2010 agent model ‚Äî ~6k agents sense [3 sensors] / steer [TURN_ANGLE + jitter] / move / deposit on a 360√ó220 `Float32Array` trail field, then 3√ó3 diffuse + decay; luminous black‚Üíviolet‚Üíamber‚Üíwhite LUT rendered via offscreen `ImageData` scaled full-bleed; **sonification** from a downsampled 16√ó16 feature grid every ~100ms [density‚Üídrone richness, busiest columns‚Üí3‚Äì6 Dorian voices, Œîdensity‚Üíswell plucks]; pointer food drop = bright trail blob + x-position-pitched stereo bloom; limiter; WebGPU feature-detected for a badge only; full teardown) + `README.md`. **Technique:** Physarum agent-based transport network (a genuinely never-used lab technique). **Why it lost:** lowest floor of the three (honest **2/5** ‚Äî #1 first-physarum + #3 named refs); shipped **Canvas2D-primary** with the WebGPU path softened to a capability badge, so the renderer is the hot Canvas2D and the GPU *reach* wasn't realized; autonomous generative-viz is a register the lab is less *starved* on than live-data or WebMIDI. **Why resurrect:** the physarum network is beautiful + long-form-evolving (minute 3 ‚âÝ second 10), and the **real** version is the deepening ‚Äî a genuine **WebGPU-compute port** (storage-buffer agents + storage-texture trail, 50k‚Äì200k agents, async feature read-back) would make it the lab's first true compute-shader agent sim and a real Anadol-scale reach. Honest 2/5 (#1 first physarum agent-sim + #3 named refs [Jeff Jones 2010 *Artificial Life* 16(2) ¬∑ Sage Jenson/mxsage "mold"]). Tags: autonomous-sim + pointer-food INPUT ¬∑ Canvas2D (‚Üí WebGPU on revival) OUTPUT ¬∑ physarum agent-network sonification TECH (NOT a harmonic event) ¬∑ organic/emergent/luminous VIBE. **Deepen on revival:** the WebGPU-compute port (the headline); multi-species physarum (two cross-attracting trail channels ‚Üí two interleaved choirs, call-and-response between colonies); long-memory audio (drifting modal centre + reverb send swelling with cumulative network area for a true 10-minute arc).

## ¬ß464 ‚Äî 2026-06-18 ¬∑ kids WIDE fire (3 divergent GROOVE + LAUGH explorers on 3 different INPUTS ‚Äî answering JURY 2026-06-17 #1 "groove/rhythm, not a cadence" [harmonic-event banned on kids], #3 "ship a multi-user / two-player piece" [STILL 0√ó across the window ‚Äî the most-starved register], #5 "MAKE A KID LAUGH"; none a banned tag [no voice-leading, no hushed, no audio-first+SVG, no WebGL2, no lone-finger touch]) ‚Äî 2 banked seeds

Cycle 464 ran a kids **WIDE** fan-out: 3 briefs whose subject is shared/embodied groove and play, each on a *different* input. Research-led by RESEARCH ¬ß464 (Incredibox/Sprunki's 2026-live stackable-loop-on-shared-clock mechanic + arXiv:2504.03958 ensemble *entrainment* ‚Äî quantize both players to ONE clock so togetherness is engineered). **Winner: `711-kids-beat-buddies`** ‚Äî the lab's **FIRST two-player / multi-user kids piece** (two kids on opposite halves of one rotated-split tablet stack silly looping pads onto one shared 104 BPM groove; both-active ‚Üí high-five + confetti); chosen because it's the only one of the three hitting JURY #3 (multi-user 0√ó), the most bulletproof glance (pure tap + auto-demo, no sensor/permission), and dodges every banned tag. Love-signal backing: Karel's loves of `98-kids-drum-circle`, `169-kids-marble-run`, `83-kids-tilt-rain`, `217-dance-avatar`, `234-kids-hand-creature` all sit in these lanes. Both losers were demoable + self-reported clean for their folders; authoritative winner-only `npm run build` is the gate; losers `rm -rf`'d before it. Resurrect by re-spawning the brief, **renumber on revival** ‚Äî max committed folder after this fire is **711**:

### `712-kids-roll-a-beat` ‚Äî `queued` (**‚öÝ RENUMBER ON REVIVAL ‚Äî folder `712` was consumed by `712-seismic-room` in cycle 465; use the next free number**) (the tilt/physics slapstick groove ‚Äî resurrect for a tilt/device-motion kids slot when tilt-physics has cooled). **Tilt the tablet ‚Üí a googly ball rolls a bouncy room and BONKS ~6‚Äì8 friend-blobs; each bonk is QUANTIZED onto a shared ~104 BPM groove so just rolling around plays a silly drum beat, and impact SPEED ‚Üí comedic pitch.** Built files (clean by inspection; removed): `page.tsx` + `physics.ts` (hand-rolled circle-vs-wall + circle-vs-circle, tilt-gravity, friction, restitution, speed-clamp, ~0.13s per-blob bonk rate-limit, returns velocity-carrying bonk events) + `audio.ts` (`makeRhythmEngine`: look-ahead scheduler, soft kick+shaker base pulse, 6 synth comedy voices honk/boing/raspberry/pop/woodblock/bell + wall-tom, bonks flushed onto the 16th grid, velocity‚Üípitch, safe `0.28‚Üílowpass(7500)‚Üícomp` master) + `README.md`. **Technique:** collision-velocity-sonification *quantized to a groove* ‚Äî slapstick timing becomes musical timing. Fallback: iOS `requestPermission()` tilt, **Arrow/WASD** on desktop/denied with `text-rose-300` hint, 2.5s-idle **auto-drift demo**, full teardown. Refs: wooden **tilt-labyrinth maze**; **Toca Boca**; **12 Principles "Squash & Stretch."** **Why it lost:** tilt-physics-bonkers is the *most-trodden* machine in the lab (286-jelly-choir, 451-jelly-storm, 603-yell-blob, banked 703-boing-zoo/706-tilt-bonk) ‚Äî lower novelty than a multi-user *first*; the groove-quantize twist is the fresh bit. **Why resurrect:** the **quantize-the-chaos-onto-a-beat** mechanic is genuinely fresh and the toy is reliably funny + nearly bulletproof (WASD fallback covers any device); ship it the moment a tilt/motion kids slot opens and 286/451/703 have cooled, OR fuse its groove-quantizer into a future marble piece. Honest 4/5 (#1 novel quantized-collision-sonification + #2 ‚â•3 subsystems [physics + rhythm scheduler + Canvas2D renderer + multi-input] + #3 named refs + #4 open-ended loop). ## ¬ß460 ‚Äî 2026-06-17 ¬∑ kids WIDE fire (3 divergent SILLY / GROOVE explorers on different embodied inputs ‚Äî answering JURY 2026-06-17 #1 "groove, not a cadence" [harmonic-event banned on kids], #3 "ship a multi-user piece" [0√ó across the whole window], #5 "MAKE A KID LAUGH"; each a different INPUT √ó renderer √ó silly mechanic, none a banned tag) ‚Äî 2 banked seeds

Research-led by RESEARCH ¬ß460 (Paul Pu Liang, *A Vision for Multisensory Intelligence*, arXiv 2601.04563, Jan 2026 ‚Äî synchronized real-time cross-modal feedback + sensing human state for *shared/collaborative* systems; plus the multiplayer-browser-music landscape: JAM with Chrome, Plink, Toca Band). **Winner: `702-kids-monster-band`** (two phones over same-origin BroadcastChannel build ONE goofy 16-step monster groove neither kid could make alone ‚Äî the directest hit on JURY #3 multi-user [the genuinely unbuilt register, breaking the standing solo monoculture] **and** #1 groove **and** #5 laugh, three provocations at once). ### `703-kids-boing-zoo` ‚Äî `queued` ‚≠ê (the most BULLETPROOF + outright funny ‚Äî RESURRECT FIRST when a fresh-input kids slot opens; zero deps, zero GPU surface, no permission gate that can deny). **Tilt the iPad ‚Üí a zoo of 8 fat googly jelly animals tumbles, piles, and BONKS, and every bonk honks a silly note** ‚Äî a tumbling pile becomes emergent slapstick rhythm, never a chord. Each animal owns one fixed pitch (G3 C4 D4 E4 G4 A4 C5 D5); a hard collision or fast wall-smack honks *that* animal's note + squash-stretches it. Tilt harder ‚Üí faster tumbling ‚Üí busier groove. Built files (clean by builder inspection; removed): `page.tsx` + `README.md`. **Technique:** hand-rolled plain-TS **2D physics** (circular bodies, gravity-from-tilt via DeviceOrientation beta/gamma, wall bounce w/ restitution, O(n¬≤) pairwise impulse resolution, n=8 = cheap); **collision-velocity ‚Üí sound** with impact speed **clamped** (`MAX_BONK_SPEED`, never harsh) + **debounced ~80ms per body & per pair** (a resting pile never machine-guns); Web Audio bonk = triangle pitch-swoop + FM wobble + band-passed noise thock; master `0.3 ‚Üí lowpass(2.6kHz) ‚Üí compressor`; Canvas2D squash-&-stretch blobs w/ googly eyes that flick toward velocity. Fallback: iOS `requestPermission()` tilt, Android auto-listen, **mouse/touch "tip-the-box + flick"** on desktop/denied, + 2.5s idle auto-demo gravity wobble; full teardown. Refs: **Toca Boca** (no-fail no-words play); cartoon **"Boing!" Foley**; the **2D physics-sandbox lineage** (Crayon Physics / Powder Game). **Why it lost:** physics + tilt are *trodden* lab tags (286-kids-jelly-choir, 303-kids-wind-harp, 603-kids-yell-blob; tilt is a familiar input) and it misses the jury's loudest ask (multi-user), so it's a lower-novelty *machine* than the multi-user winner. **Why resurrect ‚≠ê:** it is the **most bulletproof glance of the three** (no network, no GPU, no camera/permission that can fail ‚Äî works for *any* reviewer on *any* device including desktop via the drag fallback) and the most reliably *funny*; ship it the moment a low-risk silly-kids slot is wanted, or when the winner needs a no-permission sibling. Honest 2/5 (#2 ‚â•4 subsystems [2D physics engine + collision-sonification + tilt input + Canvas2D viz] + #3 named refs). Deepen on revival: Verlet soft-body jelly (bonk timbre from constraint stress); per-species voices (lion roar-honk / duck quack-honk); tilt-to-tempo quantized layer + collectible-treats bassline.

### `704-kids-funny-face-party` ‚Äî `queued` (the directest EMBODIED / off-glass camera piece ‚Äî resurrect for a body-input kids slot; revives the freeze-dance register). **Your own silly faces and wiggles drive a goofy cartoon creature in a FREEZE-DANCE party** ‚Äî front camera (on-device only) ‚Üí the googly creature mirrors & exaggerates every bob/wiggle, the silly groove gets busier the more you move, a big move / open mouth fires WHOOP/raspberry/boing, and every 8‚Äì12s the music shouts **FREEZE!** and cuts: hold still ‚Üí confetti + happy chime; keep wiggling ‚Üí goofy wobble + giggle. A real game *and* a joke, never a cadence. Built files (clean by builder inspection; removed): `page.tsx` + `README.md`. **Technique:** **no MediaPipe / no deps** ‚Äî self-rolled: hidden `<video>` ‚Üí tiny **32√ó24 offscreen canvas** ‚Üí per-pixel luma **frame-difference** = motion energy (0‚Äì1) + a weighted motion **centroid** (drives googly-eye gaze) + a **center-region** sum approximating "big move / open-mouth" (the gag trigger, armed/re-armed so one big move = one gag); cheap, inside rAF, <100ms. Web Audio look-ahead scheduler runs a ~100 BPM silly loop whose shaker/blip density scales with motion + **drives a groove‚Üífreeze‚Üíresult state machine**; master `lowpass ‚Üí compressor ‚Üí 0.3`. Fallback: camera denied/unsupported ‚Üí an auto-dancing **ghost** synthesizes its own motion + performs the freeze-dance itself (also nudges after 2.5s idle); mic never used; privacy stated with a üì∑ icon, denied message in `text-rose-300`. Refs: Apple **Photo Booth / Animoji**; classic **freeze-dance** party games; **Daniel Rozin's interactive mirrors**. **Why it lost:** camera-motion ‚Üí character is a *recently-trodden* input (677-presence-field, 690-kids-weather-hands, 652-kids-dance-band) ‚Äî less of a novel *machine* than a multi-user first, and the "open mouth" cue is approximated by center-region activity rather than true mouth detection (honest, but soft). **Why resurrect:** it is the directest **off-glass / embodied-body** answer (JURY's standing "get off the glass") and the **freeze-dance game loop is a genuinely fresh mechanic** the lab hasn't built ‚Äî the comedy payload is the freeze, not the mirror; resurrect when a body-input kids slot is open and 677/690 have cooled. Honest 2/5 (#2 ‚â•4 subsystems [camera-motion analysis + creature renderer + groove scheduler + freeze game state machine] + #3 named refs). ## ¬ß459 ‚Äî 2026-06-17 ¬∑ adult DEEP fire (one concept ‚Äî *the live global GitHub public-events firehose = the planet's act of creation, as a living ambient score*, extending 683's live-data engine into a free, keyless, NON-market stream per JURY 2026-06-17 #4; 3 approaches differ by renderer √ó metaphor √ó vibe) ‚Äî 2 banked seeds

Directly answers JURY 2026-06-17 #4 ("683's WebSocket engine could sonify a *non-market* live stream ‚Äî transit, seismic, language-usage ‚Äî for free"); the lab had sonified the market (683), wiki (437), sky (618), seismic (613/233) but **never the live worldwide act of *making***. All three clear an honest **2/5** floor (#2 ‚â•3‚Äì4 subsystems + #3 named refs [Hatnote "Listen to Wikipedia"; github.audio; Brian Foo *Data-Driven DJ*; Ikeda / Anadol]; **#1 NOT claimed** ‚Äî live-data sonification exists in the lab at 683/437/618). All dodge every banned tag (live-external-data INPUT off banned touch-primary ¬∑ NOT a harmonic-event/voice-leading TECH ¬∑ off banned WebGL2 + audio-first-SVG ¬∑ planetary/clinical/organic VIBE off banned hushed-tender). **Winner: `699-world-forge`** (three.js rotating Earth alive with geo-located event sparks, longitude-panned audio, aggregate-rate‚Üídrone; renderer-coolest, most legible/transcendent, bulletproof glance). Both losers were demoable + self-reported clean tsc/eslint for their folders; `rm -rf`'d only after the winner's authoritative `npm run build` passed. Resurrect by re-spawning the brief, renumber on revival ‚Äî **max committed folder now 699**:

### `701-living-coral` ‚Äî `queued` ‚≠ê (the freshest CONCEPT ‚Äî **long-form ACCRETING MEMORY** made visible; RESURRECT FIRST as the directest "reach for the ceiling" the jury asked for ‚Äî it echoes the celebrated 689-dream-chapters "memory you can see"). Every GitHub event accretes onto a single luminous **diffusion-limited-aggregation reef**: pushes attach branch nodes, stars light them, PRs thicken branches, forks sprout new seeds ‚Äî so the structure is **visibly fuller at minute 5 than minute 1** (an hour of the planet making things, held as long-form memory). A generative D-Dorian drone drifts with the rolling event rate. **WebGPU compute (hand-written WGSL) particle field, primary; a first-class real Canvas2D growth path as the expected fallback** (also accretes). Built files (clean ‚Äî self-reported `tsc`+`eslint` clean; removed): `page.tsx` (mount builds Structure + auto-selects WebGPU/Canvas2D, ~2.5s idle seeded drift, Begin unlocks audio + launches feed, HUD with backend/feed/nodes/elapsed, full teardown incl. `device.destroy()`), `growth.ts` (DLA/L-system reef model, persistent node set, rolling-rate window), `audio.ts` (detuned modal partial bed + breathing lowpass LFO + reverb + 4 event voices; master ‚â§0.4‚Üílowpass‚Üícomp‚Üídest), `gpu.ts` (WGSL compute easing particles toward accretion targets + additive instanced render), `render2d.ts` (Canvas2D filaments/blobs/star-halos), `feed.ts` (synthetic + keyless GitHub poll, metered, graceful fallback), `README.md`. Refs: **Refik Anadol** *Machine Memoirs* (data accretion as remembered form); **DLA & L-systems**; **Hatnote "Listen to Wikipedia"**; **github.audio**. **Why it lost:** WebGPU was just used (683-world-pulse) ‚Äî renderer rotation ‚Äî and the WGSL compute path is the riskiest unverified surface for an unattended 06:30 glance (mitigated by the Canvas2D fallback, but that growth path is itself the most complex of the three fallbacks). **Why resurrect ‚≠ê:** it is the only one of the three that adds a genuinely **new ambition axis ‚Äî long-form memory/state/evolution** (a categorical-diversity-menu hit: "the piece is *different* at minute 5"), the directest answer to the jury's "the lab clears the floor but no longer reaches for the ceiling," and a natural sibling to 689. **Resurrect when** the WebGPU/Canvas2D renderer slot is clean AND there's a browser to verify the growth path. Honest 2/5 (#2 ‚â•4 subsystems + #3 named refs). Tags: live-external-data INPUT ¬∑ WebGPU(+Canvas2D fallback) OUTPUT ¬∑ accreting-growth / long-form-memory TECH ¬∑ organic-Anadol VIBE.

### `700-commit-rain` ‚Äî `queued` (the **Ikeda austere** take ‚Äî the world's creation falling as data-rain). GitHub events stream as **falling grains of light + monospace `glyph + repo` labels** down a black field, white/cyan, with a registration grid + star scan-flashes + a live throughput readout (ev/min ¬∑ total ¬∑ LIVE vs SYNTHETIC); pitch by repo-hash in a drifting D-Dorian set, timbre by event type, an **inharmonic 5-partial drone** that thickens/brightens with global throughput. **Canvas2D** (retina), zero GPU surface. Built files (clean ‚Äî self-reported `tsc -p` + `eslint` zero errors; removed): `page.tsx` (field+feeds start instantly = idle auto-demo, Begin unlocks audio, readout strip, amber source notice, design-notes toggle, full teardown), `field.ts` (rolling 12s throughput aggregate + Canvas2D renderer), `audio.ts` (inharmonic drone bed + per-event grains, throughput‚Üígain/brightness, repo-hash‚Üípitch, screen-x‚Üípan; master ‚â§0.32‚Üílowpass‚Üícomp‚Üídest), `feed.ts` (SyntheticFeed instant + LiveFeed keyless poll ~78s, metered, graceful fallback), `README.md`. Refs: **Ryoji Ikeda** *data.matrix* / *test pattern*; **Hatnote "Listen to Wikipedia"**; **github.audio**; **Brian Foo**. **Why it lost:** the falling-grain data-spectacle is the most *familiar* AV form of the three (lower surprise, against the jury's "the median has gone comfortable"), and shipping it would push **Canvas2D to 4√ó in the rolling-10 (triggering a next-cycle ban)**. **Why resurrect:** the most **bulletproof** of the three (no GPU path at all) and the cleanest austere/clinical register; resurrect when Canvas2D has cooled and an Ikeda data-spectacle is wanted. Honest 2/5 (#2 ‚â•3 subsystems + #3 named refs). Tags: live-external-data INPUT ¬∑ Canvas2D OUTPUT ¬∑ data-sonification / streaming-grain TECH ¬∑ Ikeda-clinical VIBE.

## ¬ß458 ‚Äî 2026-06-17 ¬∑ kids WIDE fire (3 divergent SILLY / GROOVE / off-glass explorers ‚Äî directly answering JURY 2026-06-17 #5 "MAKE A KID LAUGH" [silly pole 0√ó] + #1 "groove, not a cadence" [harmonic-event banned on kids] + #2 "get off the glass"; each a different embodied input √ó renderer √ó comedy mechanic) ‚Äî 2 banked seeds

Cycle 458 ran a kids **WIDE** fan-out: 3 briefs whose *subject* is groove, play, and laughter (not a harmonic event), each off the glass and on a different embodied input √ó renderer √ó silly mechanic. Research-led by RESEARCH ¬ß458 (Incredibox; the vocal-percussion / beatbox onset-classification literature ‚Äî Hazan & Stowell 2010, the AVP dataset 2019). **Winner: `696-kids-mouth-band`** (make a mouth noise ‚Üí onset+spectral classification ‚Üí a silly drum in a looping beat a goofy creature bops to; the directest research-chain build, a qualified lab-first vocal-percussion classifier, serves KIDS.md's vocalization goal, bulletproof Canvas2D glance). All three were demoable + self-reported tsc/eslint-clean for their folders; the two losers were `rm -rf`'d only after the winner's authoritative `npm run build` passed. Resurrect by re-spawning the brief, renumber on revival ‚Äî **max committed folder now 696**:

### `698-kids-dance-mirror` ‚Äî `queued` ‚≠ê (the directest **OFF-GLASS body/dance** piece ‚Äî RESURRECT FIRST for an off-glass kids slot; revives the jury-named abandoned dance register). Dance in front of the webcam ‚Üí a rubbery **DOM/CSS** cartoon character **mirrors & EXAGGERATES** your moves (lean‚Üíovershoot-lean, jump‚Üíboing-up+squash, wild‚Üíjelly-wobble) while a bouncy groove **builds** with motion energy (more wiggling = more honks/boings/sparkle over a ~100 BPM look-ahead beat); **FREEZE comedy** ‚Äî stop moving and the character freezes mid-pose with a slide-whistle "screech-halt" + held breath, springing back to silly life when you move (the freeze-dance beat is the emotional payload). Camera is **analysis-only**: each frame ‚Üí a tiny 64√ó48 offscreen canvas ‚Üí frame-diff motion-energy + centroid, then discarded ‚Äî **never shown, recorded, or sent** (no network/api/guard). Built files (clean ‚Äî self-reported `tsc --noEmit` + `eslint` clean; removed): `page.tsx` (getUserMedia in the Start tap, 64√ó48 motion analysis, DOM/CSS springy character via CSS transforms, build-with-energy groove scheduler, freeze/wake detection, Ghost-Dancer auto-demo on deny + 2.5s idle visual preview, full teardown) + `README.md`. Refs: **Daniel Rozin's mechanical mirrors** (*Wooden Mirror* ‚Äî the piece-mirrors-you lineage); **Just Dance** move-and-copy; **Toca Boca / Sago Mini** consequence-free play. **Why it lost:** camera-motion‚Üícharacter is a *trodden* lab input (652-kids-dance-band, 524-kids-hand-firebird, 234-kids-hand-creature, 101-camera-song), so it's less of a novel *machine* than the mouth‚Üídrum-kit winner, and it has no same-day research chain (refs foundational). **Why resurrect ‚≠ê:** it is the **directest "GET OFF THE GLASS"** answer (JURY #2, the never-moved ask) and literally *revives the danceable/groovy/body register the jury named as built-once-at-652-then-abandoned* ‚Äî "the kids side fled rhythm for harmony"; **DOM/CSS is the freshest renderer** (0√ó in the recent window, zero GPU surface). Honest 2/5 (#2 ‚â•3 subsystems [webcam motion analysis + DOM/CSS character physics + build-with-energy groove scheduler + comedy synth] + #3 named refs). Tags: webcam-motion (off-glass, analysis-only) INPUT ¬∑ DOM/CSS OUTPUT ¬∑ mirror-&-exaggerate + freeze-comedy + building-groove TECH ¬∑ dance-party/silly VIBE. Risk on revival: motion-energy normalization needs per-device/lighting tuning (ghost-demo + smoothing cover the glance).

### `697-kids-shake-circus` ‚Äî `queued` (the charming **three.js squash-&-stretch** shake toy ‚Äî resurrect when a fresh-GPU kids slot opens). Hold the tablet like a giant rattle and **shake it** ‚Üí a rubbery 3D jelly clown on a striped circus podium **squashes, springs, and BOINGS** (volume-preserving Pixar squash-&-stretch via a damped 1-D spring + per-vertex jelly wobble), every shake honks a cartoon horn / springs a boing, drops a bead into a looping ~100 BPM bounce groove, and makes the creature sillier (brighter, googlier, bigger grin); a big shake occasionally **POPS confetti** + a slide-whistle reveal. Built files (clean ‚Äî self-reported `tsc --noEmit` + `eslint` clean; removed): `page.tsx` (three.js via dynamic `import("three")`, DeviceMotion shake [gravity high-passed] + DeviceOrientation tilt with iOS `requestPermission()` in the Start tap, drag/spacebar fallback, ghost auto-shake demo, **Canvas2D wobble-blob fallback** on WebGL fail, full `renderer.dispose()` teardown) + `README.md`. Refs: **the 12 Principles of Animation ‚Äî "Squash & Stretch"** (Thomas & Johnston, *The Illusion of Life*, Disney); circus/slapstick comedy SFX (boing / clown-honk / slide-whistle). **Why it lost:** **three.js was just used** (677/689 adult) ‚Äî renderer rotation ‚Äî and shake‚Üícreature is a lower-surprise *machine* than the mouth‚Üídrum-kit winner; no research chain. **Why resurrect:** the **purest charm/character** of the three (the squash-stretch jelly clown is the most outright *funny* object), and shake-as-a-rattle is a genuinely embodied kids input the lab barely uses; resurrect when WebGL/three.js is cold again, or ship its Canvas2D path as primary to dodge the GPU count. Honest 2/5 (#2 ‚â•3 subsystems [motion+tilt sensor + spring/jiggle physics + bounce-groove scheduler + comedy synth + three.js scene] + #3 named refs). Tags: DeviceMotion-shake+tilt INPUT ¬∑ three.js OUTPUT (Canvas2D fallback) ¬∑ shake-energy‚Üísquash-stretch + building-bounce-groove TECH ¬∑ circus/silly VIBE. Risk on revival: real-device shake threshold calibration.

## ¬ß457 ‚Äî 2026-06-17 ¬∑ adult DEEP fire (one concept ‚Äî *a shared multi-participant groove room: networked control-signals, local synthesis, one shared clock* ‚Äî 3 interaction models; the lab's return to MULTI-USER, the JURY 2026-06-17 most-starved register [0√ó in the last 15], with GROOVE not a cadence) ‚Äî 2 banked seeds

Cycle 457 ran an adult **DEEP** fan-out: ONE concept ‚Äî a serverless same-origin `BroadcastChannel` groove room where clients broadcast only lightweight *control signals* (presence, a shared beat-zero epoch, conflict-free last-write-wins edits) and **synthesize all audio locally**, phase-locked to one shared clock (the 2026 collaborative-web-music principle, RESEARCH ¬ß457) ‚Äî attacked via 3 interaction models. Directly answers JURY 2026-06-17 #3 (multi-user, the genuinely unbuilt register) + #1/#5 (groove/rhythm/play, NOT another tension-resolution cadence). All three are Canvas2D, multi-user, groove-first, and clear an honest **2/5** floor (#2 ‚â•3 subsystems + #3 named refs; #1 not claimed ‚Äî BroadcastChannel exists in 319/334/341). **Winner: `694-pulse-ring`** (shared rotating clock ‚Üí emergent group polyrhythm via per-player subdivisions; the freshest *machine*, the purest multi-user payoff, the directest "groove not cadence," bulletproof Canvas2D). Both losers were demoable + clean-by-inspection for their folders; `rm -rf`'d only after the winner's authoritative `npm run build` passed (exit 0, 494/494). Resurrect by re-spawning the brief, renumber on revival ‚Äî **max committed folder now 694**:

### `693-ensemble-floor` ‚Äî `queued` ‚≠ê (the most LEGIBLE / crowd-pleasing multi-user piece ‚Äî RESURRECT FIRST when an accessible "band room" co-play is wanted). A collaborative **16-step drum machine** as a lit dance floor: 5 instrument lanes (Kick, Snare, Hat, Bass, Chord-Stab) on one shared step-grid at 110 BPM in A-minor/dorian. Each participant **claims a lane** (tap a lane header ‚Üí it takes their color; can't steal a human-owned lane) and everyone toggles a **shared groove**; all clients hear the same combined beat, locally synthesized, phase-locked to one broadcast `performance.now` epoch. CRDT-lite grid (per-(lane,step) last-write-wins timestamps); late joiners request + merge full state. Solo ‚Üí bots claim empty lanes after 3s with tasteful patterns (amber "ü§ñ session players"), retire when a real peer is heard (emerald "üë• N players"); 2.5s-idle auto-demo builds the groove. Canvas2D lit pulse-grid with sweeping playhead + trigger flashes, one rAF loop, full teardown. Refs: **The Hub / League of Automatic Music Composers** ("send data, not audio"); **Toshio Iwai's Tenori-on** (the lit grid-instrument). **Why it lost:** a step-sequencer is the most *familiar* form of the three (lower surprise, against the jury's "the median has gone comfortable"), and its independent lanes are less of an *emergent* collective than 694's polyrhythm. **Why resurrect ‚≠ê:** it's the most immediately readable "everyone makes the groove together," the friendliest first multi-user piece to put in front of someone, AND the cleanest base to layer a real **WebRTC/WebSocket cross-device** transport onto (the control-signal design ports directly). Honest 2/5 (#2 ‚â•3 subsystems [4: BroadcastChannel sync + shared-epoch scheduler + 5-voice synth + Canvas2D grid] + #3 named refs). Tags: multi-participant/lane-claim INPUT ¬∑ Canvas2D OUTPUT ¬∑ shared-clock step-sequencer groove TECH ¬∑ band-room/dance-floor VIBE. Pure Web Audio + Canvas2D + same-origin BroadcastChannel (no network/api/guard ‚Äî runs forever via auto-demo).

### `695-trade-fours` ‚Äî `queued` (the accumulating LOOP-STATION jam ‚Äî love-aligned to `172-loop-station`‚ù§Ô∏è). A shared always-on group **groove** (kick/snare/hat + walking-ish bass) runs at 100 BPM for everyone; the **solo spotlight** rotates round-robin through the roster **every 4 bars** (jazz "trading fours"). Whoever holds it plays an A-dorian riff (pads or keys A‚ÄìK); their 4-bar riff is **captured and folded into a shared arrangement** that every client keeps hearing on later passes ‚Äî so the piece **accumulates** into a full collective jam (loop-station, made multiplayer). Networked-control (spotlight handoffs + captured note-lists + shared epoch over `BroadcastChannel`); solo ‚Üí 1‚Äì2 bot session players join the rotation + auto-play your turns on idle. Canvas2D jam-room: player orbs around a ring, the travelling spotlight beam + bar-countdown, drifting note-trails for the accumulating arrangement, a solo-pad strip. Refs: jazz **"trading fours"**; the **loop-station / live-looping tradition** (Reggie Watts / KT Tunstall). **Why it lost:** the most complex/fragile of the three (riff capture + replay across bars, ~970 lines), and its *turn-taking* mechanic already exists in the lab at `334-kids-pass-the-song` (sequential, not the simultaneous-emergent collective the multi-user register most wants to showcase). **Why resurrect:** the **accumulation/long-form** angle is genuinely under-served (the arrangement is *different* at minute 5 than minute 1 ‚Äî a multi-user long-form piece) and it's the directest extension of Karel's loved `172-loop-station`; resurrect by leaning hard on the accumulation/arrangement-grows angle (and a fresh framing) to dodge the `334` turn-taking overlap. Honest 2/5 (#2 ‚â•3 subsystems [4: BroadcastChannel relay + shared-transport scheduler + groove+lead synth + Canvas2D stage] + #3 named refs). Tags: multi-participant/spotlight-relay INPUT ¬∑ Canvas2D OUTPUT ¬∑ rotating-spotlight + captured-riff-accumulation TECH ¬∑ jam-room/loop-station VIBE.

## ¬ß456 ‚Äî 2026-06-17 ¬∑ kids WIDE fire (3 divergent explorers, each a *real non-pentatonic harmonic event* via different input √ó output √ó technique √ó vibe; one fully off-glass) ‚Äî 2 banked seeds

Cycle 456 ran a kids **WIDE** fan-out: 3 distinct harmonic-event briefs, deliberately spread across tags so none repeated the rolling-10. Research-led by RESEARCH ¬ß456 (embodied music-cognition; three.js-2026 velocity-feedback; RoboJam/ToCoPlay collaboration). **Winner: `690-kids-weather-hands`** (off-glass, eyes-off, tilt+motion+mic energy ‚Üí continuous major‚Üîminor **mode-morph**; the only off-glass candidate ‚Üí lands JURY 2026-06-16 #2 AND #1 at once). Both losers were demoable + ESLint/tsc-clean for their folders; `rm -rf`'d only after the winner's authoritative `npm run build` passed (exit 0). Resurrect by re-spawning the brief, renumber on revival ‚Äî **max committed folder now 690**:

### `691-kids-sky-steps` ‚Äî `queued` ‚≠ê (the cleanest **on-glass modulation** piece ‚Äî RESURRECT FIRST for an on-glass kids harmonic-event slot). A glowing cloud-staircase rising into a night sky; every tap climbs one step AND the sustained drone chord (root+fifth+third+octave) **modulates up a perfect fifth around the circle of fifths** via a common-tone glide ‚Äî the harmonic *floor* shifts under the child (JURY #1's exact "a drone that shifts under them"). The climb is **Shepard-tone-tempered** (each voice a 7-octave bell-weighted stack with a wrapped fractional-octave offset `+7/12 mod 1`) so the key rises *endlessly* while register stays child-safe. Raw **WebGL2** fragment shader (night sky + stars + aurora + perspective staircase, hue driven by circle-of-fifths position) + Canvas2D fallback. Refs: Shepard/Risset endless-glissando; circle of fifths as a map of nearby keys. **Why it lost:** on-glass (the winner had to be the off-glass one to move the 13/15 screen metric) + Shepard tones already exist in the lab (132/40/187/313). **Why resurrect ‚≠ê:** it's the directest, most child-perceptible "the whole world travels to a new key" ‚Äî a clean modulation lesson with a genuinely clever endless-ascent. WebGL2 is the coldest GPU renderer (last 675 @ 450). Pure Web Audio + WebGL2 + Canvas2D fallback (no network/key ‚Äî runs forever via auto-climb).

### `692-kids-two-friends` ‚Äî `queued` (the **collaborative / social** harmonic event ‚Äî two-finger multi-touch, two players). Two glowing creatures (amber left, cyan right); each player drags one up/down to pick a note on a 2-octave C-major scale; the **real interval** between them is scored consonant (unison/3rd/4th/5th/6th/octave ‚Üí warm glow + a catenary "hold-hands" light bridge) vs. dissonant (2nd/tritone/7th ‚Üí shimmer/wobble), and on a dissonance the piece **holds the tension ~1.1s then performs an actual voice-leading resolution** (`resolveStep` nudges the nearer voice one scale-step, preferring upper-voice-down ‚Äî the classic suspension gesture) into a consonant lock. three.js (orbs + additive halos + light bridge) + Canvas2D fallback; solo degrade = a free auto-partner. Refs: RoboJam (Martin et al.); ToCoPlay; group-synchrony / joint-attention in early-childhood music. **Why it lost:** on-glass + the most crowded space of the three ‚Äî two-player kids pieces already exist (154-clap-back, 156-star-connect, 341-star-pair, 489-pond-pair, 508-accord-call) and tension‚Üíresolution overlaps 669-hum-answer + 272-tune-purr. **Why resurrect:** "harmony as a thing two people MAKE together" is a genuinely under-served *social* register; resurrect by leaning harder on the **group-synchrony timing** angle (shared pulse, turn-taking) rather than just interval-scoring, to clear the crowded "two-voice resolution" space. three.js (used 677/689 adult ‚Äî re-audit on revival).

## ¬ß455 ‚Äî 2026-06-17 ¬∑ adult DEEP fire (one concept ‚Äî *a generative meditation that DREAMS its own visuals through a real AI image pipeline* ‚Äî 3 technical approaches; directly answering JURY 2026-06-16 #4 "AI-pipeline chain, STILL ZERO in last 15; Karel wants AI image INSIDE an AV piece") ‚Äî 2 banked seeds

Both reuse the proven guarded `fal-ai/flux/schnell` + procedural-fallback pattern. Resurrect by re-spawning the brief, renumber on revival ‚Äî **max committed folder now 689**:

### `687-latent-oracle` ‚Äî `queued` ‚≠ê (the cleanest **closed loop** of the three: a drifting D-Dorian drone auto-evolves through weather phases [calm‚Üíluminous‚Üíveiled‚Üíresolving]; every ~40s it composes a prompt from its current weather ‚Üí flux image ‚Üí **WebGL2 ping-pong feedback dissolve** [Anadol latent-flow, old dreams smear into new] ‚Üí dominant color sampled offscreen ‚Üí fed back into the synth's **filter cutoff / dissonance / tint** so the SOUND moves toward what it just dreamed. A genuine audio‚Üíimage‚Üí**audio** loop, long-form & stateful.) Refs: Refik Anadol latent data-paintings + Memo Akten *Learning to See*. **Why it lost:** the warping-shader-backdrop register is the most familiar of the three in the lab; 689's navigable accumulating-memory gallery is the bigger, more Karel-surprising concept. **Why resurrect:** it's the only one that closes the loop all the way back into the synthesis ‚Äî the most conceptually complete "the music dreams and is changed by its dream." **Resurrect when** the rolling-10 is cold on WebGL2 (it was 3√ó at cycle 455). Pure Web Audio + WebGL2 + guarded flux route + procedural value-noise fog fallback (runs forever, zero network).

### `688-spectral-seance` ‚Äî `SHIPPED` ‚úÖ as **`704-spectral-seance`** (cycle 461, 2026-06-17) ‚Äî the audio‚Üíimage‚Üí**audio** *return trip* was resurrected off Canvas2D onto a raw **WebGL2** raster/scan-line output (dodging the jury's Canvas2D ban exactly as this seed prescribed), re-sonifying each dreamed image column-by-column through a 48-osc bank. Mic-PCM caveat was sidestepped: the descriptor reads the *generative* bed's own spectrum (no mic), so the loop is fully closed without raw-PCM capture. See INDEX `704-spectral-seance`. *Original seed below for provenance:*
### `688-spectral-seance` ‚Äî `queued` (the most *surprising* single mechanic ‚Äî the **return trip**: a generative inharmonic spectral bed [ratios 1, 2.07, 3.31‚Ä¶ so it never collapses to a chord] ‚Üí a windowed-DFT spectral descriptor [low/mid/high split, centroid brightness, flatness roughness, density] ‚Üí controlled-vocabulary prompt ‚Üí flux image ‚Üí the image **re-sonified** column-by-column through a 64-osc bank [low rows = low pitch, bright = loud], a cyan Ikeda scan-line sweeping in time = "play the picture, the image is now an instrument"). Refs: Ryoji Ikeda data-spectacle + the image-as-spectrogram lineage (Xenakis **UPIC**, Metasynth, **ANS synthesizer**). **Why it lost:** **Canvas2D output** (jury-banned 4√ó + just-used in 454) and a mic caveat (the shared mic hook doesn't expose raw PCM, so mic biases the descriptor rather than being the literal captured waveform). **Why resurrect:** the audio‚Üíimage‚Üíaudio *return trip* is the most original idea in the batch and a true cross-modal-translation menu hit; **resurrect off Canvas2D** (re-skin onto a WebGL grain/raster output to dodge the ban) and wire literal mic PCM via a dedicated AudioWorklet/ScriptProcessor capture. Guarded flux route + deterministic procedural-image fallback re-sonified identically (closed loop runs with zero network).

## ¬ß454 ‚Äî 2026-06-17 ¬∑ kids WIDE fire (3 orthogonal "real harmonic event" explorers, all NON-pentatonic, each a different harmonic *event* √ó renderer √ó input ‚Äî directly continuing JURY 2026-06-16 #1 "kill the pentatonic crutch; let a kid HEAR a real harmonic event") ‚Äî 2 banked seeds

Cycle 454 ran a kids **WIDE** fan-out across three orthogonal ways to give a 4yo a *real harmonic event* without a pentatonic scale-snap, each a different INPUT √ó OUTPUT √ó TECH √ó VIBE, all dodging the over-represented audio-first/SVG output (4√ó in the last 10) and the hot WebGL2 (3√ó). Lead bent by RESEARCH ¬ß454 (arXiv:2605.07489, He/Li/Sun/Huang, May 8 2026 ‚Äî a *retrieve‚Üíedit‚Üírerank* chord-generation decomposition, which is almost exactly the architecture of a child-driven reharmonizer). **Winner: `684-kids-every-note`** (chromatic-touch ‚Üí real-time reharmonization/chord-chasing ‚Üí Canvas2D aurora; the directest hit on JURY #1's spirit "let a kid meet a wrong note and discover it isn't scary," the only candidate clearing an honest **3/5** floor, the *coldest* renderer [Canvas2D 0√ó in the last 10, zero GPU-failure surface], and the visible same-day research chain). The two losing explorers were demoable + each self-reported **clean `tsc --noEmit` + `next lint`** for their files; folders removed only after the winner's authoritative `npm run build` passed. Resurrect by re-spawning the brief, renumber on revival ‚Äî **max committed folder now 684**:

### `685-kids-two-moons` ‚Äî `queued` (the 6+/FAMILY-tier flagship ‚Äî tilt a moon between a GOLD sun [MAJOR] and a BLUE sun [MINOR] ‚Üí continuous major‚Üîminor MODE-GLIDE via a cents-glided third ‚Üí **three.js** cosmic scene ¬∑ kids 6+ / cosmic-awe). The 4th bank of the two-suns/moons concept (663‚Üí668‚Üí674‚Üí682‚Üí685); still seeking a ship slot.
Root + fifth stay pinned while the THIRD glides continuously by cents (+400¬¢ major ‚Üí the un-fretboardable neutral ~+350¬¢ "blue third" ‚Üí +300¬¢ minor) via `osc.frequency.setTargetAtTime` (~0.12s), with coupled secondary cues (lowpass opens toward major 1.8k‚Üí6k, a small arpeggio-tempo nudge) + a morphing parallel sixth ‚Äî so the child hears the single note that turns happy‚Üîsad continuously, not as a switch. Built files (clean, removed): `page.tsx` (three.js via dynamic `await import("three")` w/ WebGL-fail notice; two breathing emissive suns w/ halos, drifting moon that tints toward the nearer sun, 1400-pt starfield, sky/fog color-lerp; `DeviceOrientationEvent` w/ iOS `requestPermission` in the Begin tap + mouse-drag + ‚Üê/‚Üí fallbacks; ~16s hands-free auto-demo gold‚Üíblue‚Üígold; full rAF/listener/`renderer.dispose()`/geometry+material/audio cleanup) + `audio.ts` (`TwoMoonsAudio`: pinned root 220Hz + fifth, cents-glided third, morphing parallel sixth, kids-safe `gain ‚â§0.34 ‚Üí lowpass ‚â§7.5k ‚Üí comp ‚Üí dest`) + `README.md`. **Why banked, not shipped:** (1) **6+/family tier, not the 4yo core** ‚Äî the major/minor‚Üíaffect mapping lands reliably ~age 7 (per ¬ß448/¬ß450/¬ß452 anchor), so its payload is shakier for the kids-default audience 684 serves; (2) renderer **three.js was just used** (677-presence-field) ‚Äî shipping it back-to-back works against renderer rotation; (3) it's the 3rd-4th iteration of this concept without yet finding its slot. **Why resurrect:** the cleanest **opener of a deliberate Kids-6+/family tier** (where mode reads), OR ‚Äî its strongest next-cycle hook ‚Äî let the mode-glide drive a real *modulation* (the whole key, not just the third, shifts at the extremes) so it reads as travel between two worlds; or fold the cents-glided third into an adult piece. Honest ambition 2/5 (#2 ‚â•3 subsystems [tilt ¬∑ cents-glide mode engine ¬∑ tempo-coupled detuned synth ¬∑ three.js scene = 4] + #3 named refs [Deryck Cooke *The Language of Music*; the third-as-affective-pivot; the neutral/"blue" ~350¬¢ third]). ### `686-kids-wish-well` ‚Äî `queued` ‚≠ê the most FUNDAMENTAL harmonic event (a note stretched into TENSION that then RESOLVES home) ‚Äî drag-up-then-release ‚Üí real **suspension / appoggiatura** over a drone ‚Üí **WebGPU** particle bloom (real Canvas2D fallback) ¬∑ kids 4+ / tender-magical.
Drag a glowing wishing-star UP and a "wish voice" glides up a whole step from the consonant third to the **suspended 4th** ‚Äî a genuine non-chord-tone that grates against the always-on root+fifth drone; the harder the pull, the more pronounced the lean (slightly louder + a *detuned partial fading in* so the ear hears real **beating/roughness**, psychoacoustic dissonance, not just a different note). On RELEASE the voice **glides down by step, sus4‚Üí3** (`setTargetAtTime`) + a soft bell scaled by pull marks the warm arrival ‚Äî the satisfying *click* of resolution. Built files (clean ‚Äî `tsc --noEmit` clean, `eslint` 0, `next build` compiled the route exit 0; removed): `page.tsx` (drag/release input, ghost-hand auto-demo loop [pull‚Üíhold‚Üírelease, stops on first touch], full unmount: rAF cancel, GPU destroy, audio close, listener removal) + `audio.ts` (drone + suspension voice + arrival bell, kids-safe `gain ‚â§0.30 ‚Üí lowpass ‚â§6.2k ‚Üí comp ‚Üí dest`) + `gpu.ts` (hand-written WGSL compute advecting ~40k particles, gather-on-tension / expanding-ring-on-bloom, instanced additive render; silent fallthrough if `navigator.gpu` absent/init throws) + `render2d.ts` (real Canvas2D ~2.6k-particle fallback ‚Äî the EXPECTED path on most devices, first-class strain‚Üíbloom) + `star.ts` (shared star+tether overlay) + `field.ts` (shared scene state) + `README.md`. **Why banked, not shipped:** (1) renderer **WebGPU was just used last cycle** (683-world-pulse) ‚Äî back-to-back works against rotation; (2) 684 uniquely cleared 3/5 + had the same-day research chain, where 686 is an honest 2/5; (3) 684's Canvas2D is even *more* glance-bulletproof (no GPU path at all). **Why resurrect FIRST when a fresh-renderer kids harmonic-event slot opens** ‚Äî tension‚Üíresolution is arguably the single most fundamental harmonic event and 686 teaches it viscerally; **re-audit WebGPU on revival** (now warm ‚Äî or ship the Canvas2D path as primary to dodge the count and add zero GPU-failure surface). Honest ambition 2/5 (#2 ‚â•3 subsystems [drag input ¬∑ suspension/appoggiatura audio engine ¬∑ WebGPU/WGSL field ¬∑ Canvas2D fallback = 4] + #3 named refs [suspension/appoggiatura 4‚Äì3; J.S. Bach chorale 4‚Äì3 suspensions BWV 253‚Äì438; Helmholtz roughness / Meyer *Emotion and Meaning in Music* / Huron *Sweet Anticipation*]). ### `681-kids-every-note` ‚Äî `queued` ‚≠ê the FRESHEST-CONCEPT resurrect (the directest answer to JURY #1's spirit "let a kid meet a wrong note and discover it isn't scary") ‚Äî touch 12 chromatic petals ‚Üí real-time REHARMONIZATION / chord-chasing ‚Üí raw **WebGL2** aurora garden ¬∑ kids / luminous-wonder.
The deliberate **INVERSE of the pentatonic "no-wrong-notes" crutch**: instead of *restricting* the child to five safe pads, give the **full chromatic palette** (12 petals, ‚â•64px) and let the **harmony chase the note** so any note becomes consonant ‚Äî play a "wrong" note and the world blooms a new color to embrace it. Built files (all clean, removed): `page.tsx` (12-petal ring + auto-demo that taps a phrase including deliberate out-of-key notes + WebGL2 canvas + Canvas2D fallback + live chord readout + full unmount cleanup) + `harmony.ts` (the engine: 10-chord lush candidate palette ‚Äî diatonic I/ii/iii/IV/V/vi `Cmaj9‚Ä¶Am9` **plus borrowed colors** ‚ô≠VI A‚ô≠maj7, ‚ô≠VII B‚ô≠maj9, V/V D7, chromatic-mediant E‚ô≠maj7; `scoreFit` [chord-tone > 9/13 extension > ‚ô≠9-clash penalty], `voiceLeadCost` [greedy nearest-tone], `pickReharmonization` [fit-dominant, voice-leading tie-break]) + `audio.ts` (4 gliding pad voices + bell on top, master ‚â§0.36 ‚Üí lowpass 7k ‚Üí comp) + `gl.ts` (GLSL ES 3.00 aurora that re-tints to the chord hue + blooms a ring per tap, extra shimmer on borrowed chords, `WEBGL_lose_context` dispose) + `README.md`. **Why banked, not shipped:** it is the freshest *concept* and the directest jury-#1-spirit hit, BUT (1) it is **on-glass** while 680 lands the jury's never-moved off-glass ask; (2) its renderer **WebGL2 is HOT** (658/659/675 all WebGL2 in the recent window) where 680's inline-SVG is the scarcest + has zero GPU failure surface; (3) the reharmonization *payload* is subtler for a 4yo than 680's felt closure (per ¬ß452 developmental anchor). **Why resurrect FIRST** when an on-glass kids harmonic-event fire is wanted ‚Äî but **re-audit the renderer on revival** (re-skin to inline-SVG or three.js to dodge the hot WebGL2 count, which would also lift its diversity score). Honest ambition 2/5 (#2 ‚â•3 subsystems [chromatic touch ¬∑ reharmonization engine ¬∑ WebGL2 garden ¬∑ gliding synth = 4] + #3 named refs [FlowComposer reharmonization; *consonance is contextual*]; **#1 NOT claimed** ‚Äî the lab already uses "reharmonization" in `637-slow-burn`; the fresh axis is *child-driven chord-chasing that embraces chromatic notes*). Honest ambition 2/5 (#2 ‚â•3 subsystems [tilt ¬∑ cents-glide mode engine ¬∑ tempo-coupled detuned synth ¬∑ three.js scene = 4] + #3 named refs [third-as-affective-pivot; the neutral/"blue" ~350¬¢ third; Deryck Cooke *The Language of Music*]). ## ¬ß449 ‚Äî 2026-06-16 ¬∑ adult WIDE fire (3 orthogonal explorers across under-built adult registers, all dodging the JURY 2026-06-16 bans) ‚Äî 2 banked seeds

Cycle 449 ran an adult **WIDE** fan-out across three orthogonal under-built adult registers, each a different input √ó output √ó technique √ó vibe, none touching a banned tag (no pentatonic, no Canvas2D, no scheduler-bed-as-only-idea, no warm-cozy, none the frozen groove/EDM). Lead = JURY #5's explicitly-named open register, **tender/intimate**. **Winner: `671-glasswork`** (the realization of the long-banked `660-glasswork` tender seed ‚Äî a generative glass music-box driven by real exhaustive nearest-tone voice-leading, inline-SVG; the directest hit on the jury's named adult ask AND the most bulletproof glance candidate [pure SVG + Web Audio, zero GPU/sensor/network surface]). The two losing explorers, both demoable + each self-reporting clean isolated `tsc`/ESLint, moved to `/tmp` and removed only after the winner's authoritative `npm run build` passed; resurrect by re-spawning the brief, renumber on revival ‚Äî **max committed folder now 671**:

### `672-cinema-swell` ‚Äî `queued` (a CINEMATIC orchestral-swell instrument ‚Äî hold chords ‚Üí film-score string divisi blooms ¬∑ MIDI + computer-keyboard + on-screen keys ¬∑ **three.js** ¬∑ cinematic-narrative / Zimmer-Richter)
The **directest fill of the cinematic / narrative journey-engine register** Karel names in his care #4 ("EDM build-and-drop, ritual, jazz responsive, **cinematic narrative** ‚Ä¶ as alternate arcs") and a clean companion to the tender winner. You HOLD chords (MIDI keyboard via Web MIDI, or `a w s e d f t g y h u j k o l p ;` on the computer keyboard, or an on-screen mini-keyboard) and a full film-score string section blooms around them in real time ‚Äî it RESPONDS, it is not a fixed look-ahead bed. **Rule-based responsive orchestration** (`orchestra.ts ‚Üí setChord`): the held chord is spread into **divisi** across three register groups ‚Äî celli (low + octave-down doubling), violas (mid inner triad + octave doubling), violins (high top voice + octave-up sheen) ‚Äî re-arranged on every chord change; each group is detuned-saw bowed-string voices through a lowpass with ensemble vibrato. **Swell:** the longer you hold, the louder/brighter the section grows (crescendo env), softly releasing on lift. A slow drifting **tension curve** (over ~30‚Äì60 s, nudged up by dense/dissonant chords, down by consonant rest) adds a low ostinato/heartbeat pulse and brightens the top when high, so the piece has a felt narrative arc ("different at minute 2"). **Output: three.js** pooled concert-hall stage ‚Äî three banks of emissive columns (warm‚Üícool by register) whose height/brightness/glow track each section's live swell, tension tinting the whole stage, a heartbeat floor-disc flash, slow camera drift, full `dispose()`. **Auto-demo:** if no input within ~2 s, a **ghost conductor** plays a slow Richter-ish progression so the stage is fully alive hands-off; real input takes over seamlessly. Master ends in a `DynamicsCompressor` brick-wall limiter (master 0.36). Graceful: no Web MIDI (Safari) ‚Üí amber notice + working keys; no WebGL ‚Üí rose notice (audio + keys still play). No API route, no guard, no new deps (`three` already in package.json). Named refs: **Hans Zimmer** (cumulative orchestral swells) ¬∑ **Max Richter** (*On the Nature of Daylight* ‚Äî string voicing) ¬∑ **J√≥hann J√≥hannsson** (slow cinematic builds); technique anchor: orchestral **divisi** + crescendo-as-expression. **Ambition honest ~2/5** (#2 ‚â•3 subsystems [MIDI+keyboard input handler ¬∑ rule-based divisi orchestration engine ¬∑ swell + tension-curve state machine ¬∑ three.js stage = 4] + #3 named refs; **#1 NOT claimed** ‚Äî Web MIDI already exists in the lab [`291-harmonograph`], the fresh move is the responsive cinematic-orchestration instrument). **Why it lost (lane-leverage, not quality ‚Äî it was a strong, build-clean candidate):** the binding jury directive THIS cycle is #5's explicitly-named **tender/intimate** register (671 hits it dead-on), and the jury also froze groove/EDM-adjacent energy for a week ‚Äî cinematic is the lower-leverage swing here and three.js is the hotter renderer vs. 671's scarce inline-SVG. **Resurrect** as the lead of a cinematic/journey-engine-alternative adult fire (it IS the cinematic-narrative arc Karel keeps asking for), or as the orchestral companion in a "registers" suite; its strong next-cycle hook: let the tension curve drive a real harmonic *modulation* (key changes at arc peaks) so the narrative has acts, not just dynamics. Builder reported `tsc --noEmit` + ESLint clean in isolation; full working code is a fast re-spawn.

### `673-kepler-chimes` ‚Äî `queued` (the literal MUSIC OF THE SPHERES ‚Äî orbital mean-motion resonance ‚Üí audible consonant polyrhythm that LOCKS ¬∑ tilt + tap/arrow fallback ¬∑ raw **WebGL2** ¬∑ cosmic-Kepler awe)
The **awe/cosmic reach** of this fire and the most *surprising* concept ‚Äî a star with orbiting planets where each planet chimes once per orbit and the orbital **periods snap toward small-integer mean-motion resonance** (2:1, 3:2, 4:3, 5:3 ‚Äî the Laplace chain that locks the real TRAPPIST-1 system), so **stable orbital resonance becomes an audible, consonant polyrhythm you HEAR slide from phasing into a locked groove.** Deliberately NOT the chaotic three-body problem (that's `432-three-body`) ‚Äî this is about *stable consonance*. **Orbital integrator** (`orbits.ts`): circular orbits tied by Kepler's third law (`T¬≤ ‚àù a¬≥`, inner = faster/higher), a chime when phase crosses the +x perihelion gate. **Resonance-snap engine:** each non-anchor planet's period is exponentially pulled toward `anchorPeriod √ó ratio` toward the nearest small-integer ratio, with a per-planet badge flipping `‚óã` (drifting) ‚Üí `‚óè` (in resonance) ‚Äî you watch AND hear the lock. With 3‚Äì5 planets the combined chime pattern is a long, slowly-phasing polyrhythm realigning only after the LCM of the periods (genuinely different at minute 2). **Synth** (`synth.ts`): always-on detuned star drone + breathing LFO; a 4-partial bell chime per planet, pitch from its just-intonation resonance ratio, register from orbital period, panned by screen-x; master ends in a `DynamicsCompressor` brick-wall limiter (0.34). **Output: raw WebGL2 / GLSL ES 3.00** (`gl.ts`): fullscreen `gl_VertexID` starfield, additive orbit trails/bodies/flares/rings, a drone-pulsing central star, full GL teardown. **Input:** device tilt (beta/gamma nudge the newest planet up/down the chain; iOS `requestPermission()` in-gesture) with **tap-to-add-planet-at-radius + arrow/drag fallback** so it's fully playable with no tilt. **Auto-demo:** seeds 3 planets in a TRAPPIST-like chain, slightly detuned so they audibly slide into lock over the first seconds ‚Äî alive at a glance with zero hardware. No API route, no guard, no new deps. Named refs: **Johannes Kepler**, *Harmonices Mundi* (1619 ‚Äî the harmony of the spheres) ¬∑ **Matt Russo / System Sounds** TRAPPIST-1 sonification (the 8:5:3 mean-motion chain as rhythm/pitch) ¬∑ mean-motion orbital resonance. **Ambition honest ~2/5** (#2 ‚â•3 subsystems [orbital integrator ¬∑ resonance-snap polyrhythm engine ¬∑ consonance-mapped bell synth + drone ¬∑ raw-WebGL2 renderer = 4] + #3 named refs; **#1 NOT claimed** ‚Äî orbital/n-body visuals exist [`432-three-body`, `308-orbit-choir`]; the fresh move is STABLE resonance-chain ‚Üí consonant polyrhythm + tilt input). **Why it lost (lane-leverage + glance-verifiability, not quality):** the binding directive is #5's tender register (671), and 673 is the riskier glance candidate (raw WebGL2 + tilt + the resonance-lock is a by-ear effect hard to verify in a silent sandbox), where 671 is the lowest-risk pure-SVG piece; cosmic/orbital is also partly-trodden territory. **Resurrect** when an awe/cosmic register is wanted or as the headline of a "sonify a physical system" fire ‚Äî it's genuinely fresh and beautiful; its strong next-cycle hook: let tilt/drag actually *break* a resonance (push past `‚óè` back to `‚óã`) so the player can hear the polyrhythm dissolve into chaos and re-lock ‚Äî making the lock/unlock the playable instrument. Builder reported `tsc --noEmit` + ESLint clean in isolation; full working code is a fast re-spawn.

## ¬ß448 ‚Äî 2026-06-16 ¬∑ kids WIDE fire (3 orthogonal harmonic-event explorers, all dodging the JURY 2026-06-16 bans) ‚Äî 2 banked seeds

Cycle 448 ran a kids **WIDE** fan-out across three orthogonal ways to give a 4-year-old a *real harmonic event* ‚Äî the three the jury named that 662-gamelan-bend didn't already cover: **mode flip**, **dissonance-that-resolves**, **shifting drone**. None pentatonic-snap, none Canvas2D, none warm-cozy, one fully off-glass. **Winner: `669-kids-hum-answer`** (off-the-glass / audio-first ‚Äî the directest hit on the jury's loudest standing directive "get a builder off the glass; the kids side has never shipped one" + the ‚≠ê PRIORITY banked-seed flag from ¬ß446; and on the firmest developmental ground per RESEARCH ¬ß448 ‚Äî children perceive tonal *closure/its denial* emotionally before mode). The two losing explorers, both demoable + each self-reporting clean isolated `tsc`/ESLint, moved to `/tmp` and removed only after the winner's authoritative `npm run build` passed; resurrect by re-spawning the brief, renumber on revival ‚Äî **max committed folder now 669**:

### `668-kids-two-suns` ‚Äî `queued` (continuous major‚Üîminor MODE MORPH via a cents-glided third ¬∑ tilt + drag ¬∑ **three.js** ¬∑ cosmic-dramatic)
*Tilt a pale moon between a warm gold sun (MAJOR) and a cool blue sun (MINOR); root+fifth stay pinned while the **third glides continuously by cents** ‚Äî +400¬¢ ‚Üí the neutral "blue third" ~+350¬¢ (a note no piano can play) ‚Üí +300¬¢ ‚Äî so the child hears the ONE note that bends happy into sad. Tempo-coupled (gold faster/brighter, blue slower/dimmer) so it works for a pre-mode-literate kid.* Built clean + demoable: raw three.js scene (two breathing/glowing suns, drifting moon, 1400-pt starfield, sky-color lerp), `frequency.setTargetAtTime(...,0.04)` continuous third-glide over a detuned pad, tilt (iOS-permission-in-gesture) + auto drag fallback + 1.5s no-event probe + `text-rose-300` note, ~5s idle auto-demo, safe master chain. **Why banked, not shipped:** the strongest *renderer-diversity* candidate (three.js, off banned Canvas2D) and a genuinely fresh continuous-third-glide technique ‚Äî but lost to 669 on two counts: (1) the off-glass directive is the jury's single loudest *unaddressed* kids ask and 669 is the only off-glass sibling; (2) RESEARCH ¬ß448 ‚Äî the "happy‚Üîsad mode flip" premise is developmentally shaky for the 4yo target (mode‚Üíemotion mapping doesn't land reliably until ~7; tempo and *change* do). **Resurrect:** the fastest "continuous mode-morph" resurrect, ideally re-pitched as an **older-kid / family** piece (the age where mode reads), or fold the cents-glided third into an adult piece. This upgrades ¬ß446's banked 663-two-suns (SVG) to three.js. Tags: tilt INPUT ¬∑ three.js OUTPUT ¬∑ continuous-mode-morph TECH ¬∑ cosmic-dramatic VIBE.

### `670-kids-drone-cave` ‚Äî `queued` (SHIFTING DRONE / moving pedal-point ¬∑ gamepad + keyboard ¬∑ **WebGL2** ¬∑ mysterious-discovery)
*Guide a glowing firefly through a luminous cave; ring a small FIXED set of crystal bells while a sustained tanpura-style drone (root+fifth) slowly **glides its root** room to room ‚Äî A ‚Üí F ‚Üí C ‚Üí E ‚Üí home (i‚Äì‚ô≠VI‚Äì‚ô≠III‚ÄìV). The same bells sound bright over home, warm over F, open over C, genuinely tense over E, then resolve ‚Äî the child discovers "the floor note changes the feeling," even though their own notes never changed. Harmony is relational to the bass.* Built clean + demoable: hand-rolled WebGL2 fbm cave shader (mood uniform lerps cyan/gold‚Üîviolet/indigo with the harmony), two-osc drone with `exponentialRampToValueAtTime` ~3.2s root glides, Gamepad API + arrow/WASD fallback + `gamepadconnected` live-switch, ~5s idle auto-demo, safe master chain. **Why banked, not shipped:** a genuinely fresh harmonic mechanism for the lab (moving pedal-point) and a strong mysterious register ‚Äî but lost on (1) renderer: WebGL2 is over-represented (4√ó in the jury's last-15), while 669's audio-first is the rarest/best-rated output; (2) the off-glass directive again favored 669; (3) gamepad-fly-through-musical-space overlaps shipped 636-star-pilot. **Resurrect:** ship next kids cycle when WebGL2 is cold and a screen-render is wanted; the drone-walk is worth keeping ‚Äî consider re-skinning to three.js or pairing the moving-pedal engine with a different input. Tags: gamepad INPUT ¬∑ WebGL2 OUTPUT ¬∑ shifting-drone/pedal-point TECH ¬∑ mysterious-discovery VIBE.

## ¬ß446 ‚Äî 2026-06-16 ¬∑ kids WIDE fire (3 orthogonal explorers, all attacking JURY 2026-06-16 #1 "ban the pentatonic crutch ‚Äî give a kid a real harmonic event") ‚Äî 2 banked seeds

Cycle 446 ran a kids **WIDE** fan-out across three orthogonal ways to give a 4-year-old a *real harmonic event* (no pentatonic, no scale-snap), with one builder pushed fully **off the glass** (JURY #2). **Winner: `662-kids-gamelan-bend`** (the only never-used technique ‚Äî inharmonic modal bronze synthesis ‚Äî and the most literal answer to JURY #1: bend a bronze bar into and out of tune across real slendro/p√©log *laras*). The two losing explorers, both demoable + each self-reporting clean `tsc --noEmit` in isolation, `rm -rf`'d after all three builders' completion notifications confirmed; resurrect by re-spawning the brief, renumber on revival ‚Äî **max committed folder now 662**:

### `663-kids-two-suns` ‚Äî `queued` (continuous major‚Üîminor MODE MORPH ¬∑ tilt + drag ¬∑ inline-SVG ¬∑ cosmic-night)
*Tilt the tablet to drift a pale moon between a warm gold sun (happy/MAJOR) and a cool blue sun (sad/MINOR) ‚Äî the chord's THIRD glides continuously by cents (‚âà+400‚Üí+300, passing through the neutral ~+350 "blue" third no piano can play) while root+fifth stay pinned, so the child hears the one note that turns happy into sad.* Built clean and demoable: SVG night sky (two breathing suns, drifting moon, twinkling stars, sky tint that warms‚Üîcools with the harmony), `frequency.setTargetAtTime(...)` ~40ms glide so it's a smooth morph not a snap, a gentler parallel sixth morph (+900‚Üí+800), always-on detuned pad, tilt (iOS-permission-in-gesture) with auto drag fallback + `text-rose-300` note + 1.5s no-event probe, auto-demo sine-drift between the suns. **Why banked, not shipped:** lovely + clean, but the *concept* (a happy‚Üîsad slider) is the simplest of the three and the technique morph is a 2/5 ambition vs. 662's never-used bronze synthesis. **Resurrect:** the strongest fast resurrect for a "continuous mode-morph" lane ‚Äî pairs well with a future adult version. Tags: tilt INPUT ¬∑ SVG OUTPUT ¬∑ continuous-mode-morph TECH ¬∑ cosmic-night VIBE.

### `664-kids-hum-answer` ‚Äî `queued` ‚≠ê PRIORITY off-glass resurrect (mic pitch-detect ‚Üí 4‚Äì3 SUSPENSION that resolves ¬∑ audio-FIRST/eyes-closed)
*A child hums one note, closes their eyes, and an unseen choir answers ‚Äî leaning on a tense held note (the 4th), then stepping DOWN to resolve it (the major 3rd, the sigh of release).* The fire's **off-the-glass builder** (JURY 2026-06-16 #2 ‚Äî "get at least one builder off the glass; 13 of 15 are screens"): near-black screen, ONE breathing radial glow that pulses with the post-limiter amplitude (hue shifts violet‚Üíblue tense‚Üíresolved), works fully eyes-closed. Built clean + demoable: autocorrelation pitch detection (Chris-Wilson approach, RMS gate + parabolic refine + clarity gate, 75‚Äì1000Hz, octave-collapse), formant-bandpass "ah" voices (NO AI voice, NO samples), `answerWithSuspension` (textbook 4‚Äì3 suspension), settle-detector with 6.3s cooldown, self-playing demo loop on mic-denied + `text-rose-300` note, full track/rAF/timer/node cleanup. **Why banked, not shipped:** hits BOTH top jury provocations, but its *core technique* (mic autocorrelation ‚Üí harmonized answer) closely repeats `280-kids-echo-canyon` ‚Äî fresh framing, most-trodden technique. By the lab's tie-breaker (novel technique > redundant technique w/ fresh framing) 662 won. **The screen-monoculture metric still needs an off-glass SHIP ‚Äî resurrect this first next kids cycle** (or push it further: harmonize the child's real pitch with a *suspension chain* that walks several resolutions, or add a second eyes-closed "breath" layer). Tags: mic INPUT ¬∑ audio-only/off-glass OUTPUT ¬∑ pitch-detect + suspension-resolution TECH ¬∑ hushed/sacred VIBE.

## ¬ß445 ‚Äî 2026-06-16 ¬∑ adult WIDE fire (3 orthogonal explorers across genuinely-open adult registers) ‚Äî 2 banked seeds

Cycle 445 ran an adult **WIDE** fan-out across three under-built adult registers, each a different no-banned-tag input √ó output √ó technique √ó vibe, bent toward the RESEARCH ¬ß445 "continuous, live-steered, never-landing causal stream" frame (Lyria RealTime / LiveBand arXiv 2606.03803, June 2026). **Winner: `659-pulse-cathedral`** (the jury's most-named missing adult register ‚Äî *ecstatic/euphoric* ‚Äî finally **shipped**: an EDM build-and-drop journey-engine arc with an audible+visible sidechain pump and a Shepard-folded forever-climbing bass; the directest hit on JURY 2026-06-15 provocation #2 "build the missing middle ‚Äî ecstatic/danceable" AND Karel's explicit care #4 "EDM build-and-drop as an alternate journey arc"). The two losing explorers, both demoable + each self-reporting clean `tsc --noEmit` + `eslint` in isolation, `rm -rf`'d AFTER all three builders' completion notifications confirmed; resurrect by re-spawning the brief, renumber on revival ‚Äî **max committed folder now 659**:

### `660-glasswork` ‚Äî `queued` (the TENDER / wistful 3am music-box ¬∑ inline-SVG ¬∑ adult / intimate-melancholy)
The lab's tender-register fill, and the strongest single resurrect for an adult fire that wants intimacy over energy (the jury named *tender* as part of the unbuilt missing middle alongside ecstatic). A slow generative celesta/glass piece in **D Dorian** (rare Lydian #11 tint): the next chord is a tension/resolution-weighted random walk (never a fixed loop, ~6‚Äì10s/chord); on each change every voice moves to the **nearest** chord tone (real voice-leading, doubling-penalized) so the pad audibly glides; a never-repeating melody is weighted over chord tones + Dorian passing tones with a per-phrase sine register-arch and woven-in rests. Voices (all synth, no samples): FM music-box bell (fast attack/long decay) ¬∑ glassy triangle pad ¬∑ soft sub ¬∑ synthetic-impulse ConvolverNode reverb + filtered feedback-delay shimmer. **Inline-SVG** renderer (the scarce renderer): a constellation of soft glowing nodes (`<radialGradient>` + `<feGaussianBlur>`) that bloom on each note, faint threads tracing voice-leading between sounding chord tones; React redraw throttled ~26fps, most state in refs. Pointer bends it (up ‚Üí brighter/higher register, right ‚Üí busier), click "drops a stone" pulling harmony toward rest; idle ~1.4s ‚Üí autonomous. `page.tsx`(443) + `theory.ts`(chords/voiceLead/melody palette) + `compose.ts`(look-ahead grid + melody) + `synth.ts` + `README.md`. Named refs: **Brian Eno** (*Music for Airports*, *Reflection*) ¬∑ **Harold Budd** ¬∑ **Erik Satie** (*Gymnop√©dies*). Ambition honest **2/5** (#2 ‚â•3 subsystems ‚Üí 5 [harmony walk ¬∑ voice-leading ¬∑ melody+scheduler ¬∑ 4-voice synth+reverb ¬∑ SVG] + #3 named refs; **no #1** [generative-ambient voice-leading is worked territory], no #5). **WHY IT LOST (lane-leverage, not quality ‚Äî it was a bulletproof, beautiful candidate):** the ecstatic winner fills a register that is **0√ó shipped** on the adult side and answers a *second* standing Karel directive (EDM journey arc), where tender ‚Äî though genuinely missing ‚Äî is the lower-leverage swing and the lower ambition (2/5, no #1); 660 also flirts with cozy-adjacency (mitigated by framing as melancholy, not warm). **Resurrect** as the tender-register fire, or as the intimate companion in a "registers of the missing middle" suite. The full SVG build is a fast re-spawn.

### `661-murmur-engine` ‚Äî `queued` (boids murmuration AS the music ‚Äî emergence-as-composition ¬∑ Canvas2D ¬∑ adult / awe-organic dusk-ambient)
The lab's emergence-sonification reach: a self-organizing **boids** flock (~320 agents, Reynolds separation/alignment/cohesion, O(n) via a uniform spatial-hash grid, fixed-60Hz sim decoupled from render) whose **aggregate statistics ARE the score** ‚Äî centroid height ‚Üí pitch register, mean scatter/speed ‚Üí swell, the **alignment order parameter** `|‚ü®ƒ•‚ü©|`‚àà[0,1] ‚Üí harmonic consonance/chorus stability, local density/cohesion ‚Üí chord voicing richness ‚Äî low-pass smoothed so the music never jitters; 5 detuned-saw choir-ish pad voices in a consonant D-dorian/pentatonic scale + soft sine flutter-grains on scatter/regroup events; genuinely different at minute 3 than minute 0 (the flock is). Pointer = a diving hawk (repulsion ‚Üí dramatic scatter-and-regroup). **Canvas2D** dusk-gradient sky (tinted by flock height) + additive `lighter` light-trail flock over a low-alpha persistence wash + predator glow. `page.tsx`(377) + `boids.ts`(sim + `computeStats`) + `synth.ts` + `README.md`. Named refs: **Craig Reynolds, "Flocks, Herds and Schools" (SIGGRAPH 1987)** ¬∑ **Vicsek et al. 1995** (order-parameter / collective-motion phase transition). Ambition honest **2/5** (#2 ‚â•3 subsystems ‚Üí 4 [boids sim ¬∑ flock-analysis ¬∑ generative synth ¬∑ Canvas2D] + #3 named refs; **no #1** [`537-kids-sky-murmuration` + `8-particle-life` already in the corpus ‚Äî flocking is not lab-first], no #5). **WHY IT LOST:** (1) **renderer** ‚Äî Canvas2D is HOT (3√ó in the rolling-10: 636/640/652); shipping it pushes the screen-renderer monoculture the jury warns about to 4√ó, where the winner's WebGL2 is the *scarcest* screen renderer; (2) **lowest novelty** ‚Äî emergence-as-music + boids are both already in the corpus, so no #1; (3) lower-leverage register (awe-ambient) vs. the winner's 0√ó-shipped ecstatic. **Resurrect** with a **scarce renderer** (WebGL2 GPU instancing for 5‚Äì10k agents, or SVG) and a sharper sonification (e.g. the order-parameter driving a real phase-transition in the arrangement ‚Äî flock "boils" ‚Üí the music breaks), which would lift it toward a #1. The Canvas2D build is a fast re-spawn.

## ¬ß444 ‚Äî 2026-06-16 ¬∑ kids DEEP fire (one concept ‚Äî his-real-piano garden ‚Äî via 2 interaction models) ‚Äî 1 banked seed

Cycle 444 ran a kids **DEEP** fan-out: ONE ambitious concept (the lab's FIRST kids piece built on Karel's REAL recorded piano ‚Äî granular-resynthesized, body-steered by tilt, pentatonic-quantized so no wrong notes) attacked via two **interaction models**. **Winner: `658-kids-piano-garden`** (tilt rolls a glowing seed; where it dwells his piano BLOOMS into a persistent singing flower ‚Üí the garden grows, genuine long-form state). The losing approach, demoable + hand-verified (no node_modules in its sandbox), `rm -rf`'d AFTER both builders' completion notifications confirmed; resurrect by re-spawning the brief, renumber on revival ‚Äî **max committed folder now 658** (note `656` deliberately left FREE for a ¬ß443 banked-seed [653/654] revival):

### `657-kids-piano-garden` ‚Äî `queued` (tilt = a WIND that scatters Karel's REAL recorded piano into a swarm of singing FIREFLIES ¬∑ WebGL2 point-field ¬∑ kids / luminous-joyful)
The **bulletproof, instantly-joyful momentary-instrument register** of the his-real-piano concept, and the fastest single resurrect for a kids fire that wants pure delight over accumulation. Same core as the winner ‚Äî loads Karel's *Welcome Home* recording via the read-only `/api/audio/549fc519-‚Ä¶` route (`audio.ts` verbatim, synth fallback) and **granular-resynthesizes** it with every grain `playbackRate`-quantized to a ¬±2-octave C-major pentatonic (no wrong notes), ‚â§25-voice cap, kid-safe `master ‚â§0.5 ‚Üí lowpass ‚â§7.4k ‚Üí comp` chain ‚Äî but the **interaction is tilt-as-WIND, not a rolled seed**: vertical tilt (beta) ‚Üí brightness/register, horizontal tilt (gamma) ‚Üí stereo pan + the **wind direction of a flow field**; grain **density + spread scale with tilt magnitude** (level = sparse gentle motes, big tilt = a joyful dense swarm). Each grain onset spawns a glowing **firefly mote** (‚â§520) in a **WebGL2 additive point-sprite field** (Canvas2D fallback) that drifts on the tilt-driven wind, flares at onset, fades over its life. DeviceOrientation + iOS-gesture unlock, drag + arrow-key fallbacks (`text-rose-300` notice), ~2.5 s idle auto-demo "breeze", ~12-min lullaby fade. `page.tsx` (714, single-file) + `audio.ts` (verbatim) + `README.md`. Named refs: Curtis Roads *Microsound* ¬∑ Xenakis ¬∑ Eno ¬∑ Karel's *Welcome Home*; love-aligned `227-paths-granular`‚ù§Ô∏è + the `262-aurora`‚ù§Ô∏è/`130`‚ù§Ô∏è/`236`‚ù§Ô∏è particle/swarm lane. Ambition honest 2‚Äì3/5 (**#1** first kids piece on his real recording [shared with the winner] + **#2** 4 subsystems [loader ¬∑ granular engine ¬∑ tilt + flow field + fallbacks ¬∑ WebGL2 point-field] + **#3** named refs). **WHY IT LOST (lane-value, not quality ‚Äî it was the bulletproof candidate):** it is a *momentary* instrument ‚Äî motes fade and nothing accumulates ‚Äî where the winner's **persistent singing flowers** give the garden genuine multi-minute state/evolution (the quality the jury praised in `624`), a stronger #2 and the bigger concept; the winner's modular engine/renderer split is also cleaner, and 657 has a dead `padGain` bus (wired to master with no source ‚Äî harmless since grains always spawn, but a loose end). **Resurrect** as a kids fire when pure momentary delight beats accumulation, or as the WIND sibling of a his-music-for-kids suite; the full single-file build is a fast re-spawn.
