## ¬ß443 ‚Äî 2026-06-16 ¬∑ adult WIDE fire (3 orthogonal explorers across the jury's open adult lanes) ‚Äî 2 banked seeds

Cycle 443 ran an adult **WIDE** fan-out across 3 jury-named open adult lanes, each a different no-banned-tag input √ó output √ó technique √ó register, all bent toward the RESEARCH ¬ß443 "continuous, live-steered, never-landing stream" paradigm (Lyria RealTime). **Winner: `655-breath-sanctuary`** (the jury's demanded 3rd off-screen/embodied piece ‚Äî an eyes-closed JI overtone-choir + singing-bowl ritual steered by your *breath*; the scarcest renderer [audio-only] + freshest input [mic-breath]; provocation #3). The two losing explorers, both demoable, `rm -rf`'d AFTER all three completion notifications confirmed; resurrect by re-spawning the brief, renumber on revival ‚Äî **max committed folder now 655**:

### `654-latent-chain` ‚Äî `queued` (audio ‚Üí image ‚Üí words ‚Üí image ¬∑ the lab's FIRST 2-model AI pipeline CHAIN ¬∑ WebGL2 audio-warped texture ¬∑ adult / latent-Anadol)
**The boldest bank of this fire AND the directest serve of Karel's most-wanted standing directive** ("AI image generation INSIDE an AV experiment ‚Äî the image responds to/shapes the audio"). Karel's piano (`/api/audio/549fc519-‚Ä¶`, A-minor synth fallback) ‚Üí `AnalyserNode` features (RMS, spectral centroid, spectral-flux onset, 12-bin chroma‚ÜíKrumhansl key, valence) ‚Üí a `Mood` ‚Üí prompt words ‚Üí **Model 1 `fal-ai/flux/schnell` text‚Üíimage** ‚Üí **Model 2 `fal-ai/florence-2-large/more-detailed-caption` image‚Üícaption** (caption SHOWN on screen as "the machine reads its dream", salient words re-mixed into the next prompt = a self-narrating latent walk) ‚Üí the latest image becomes a **WebGL2 audio-warped texture ground** (strip-ripple ‚Üê energy+flux, energy zoom/pulse, RGB smear on onset, violet bloom); always-on procedural WebGL2 Mood-field fallback. **Budget-safe + the glance-fix it lost on last time:** guarded route (first two `POST` lines `guard(req)`, 501 w/o `FAL_KEY`), a `{probe:true}` zero-spend key check, AI strictly opt-in behind "Dream the picture" (15 s cooldown) **OR exactly ONE auto-fired step on Start when the probe confirms a key** so the chain is *visible* to a key-holding reviewer. Files (removed): `page.tsx`, `analysis.ts`, `synth.ts`, `renderer.ts`, `api/route.ts` (guarded), `README.md`. Ambition honest **3/5 with a HARD #1** (first 2-model AI chain ‚Äî grep-0√ó + #2 four subsystems + #3 Anadol *Machine Hallucinations* / Akten *Learning to See*). **WHY IT LOST AGAIN (glance-verifiability + production-risk for an UNATTENDED ship, NOT ambition ‚Äî it was the highest-ambition candidate):** the headline (the chain) is still only *seen* with a live `FAL_KEY` a reviewer triggers; at a silent 06:30 unattended deploy it defaults to the procedural field, so the very thing that makes it a #1 goes unexercised ‚Äî AND it carries paid-AI + network + a builder-flagged-uncertain florence-2 model-id (could 500 every caption, graceful-degrades to Mood-only) straight onto Karel's live auto-deployed app. **Resurrect for a fire when Karel is AWAKE to click "Dream the picture" with a confirmed prod `FAL_KEY` + the florence-2 id verified live** ‚Äî this is the lab's strongest unmined frontier and Karel's top directive; lead with the live chain and consider the cycle-2 hook (let Model-2's caption words bend the synth's key/timbre so the picture re-tunes the piano). The ¬ß441 bank of the same concept now has a working WebGL2 renderer + probe + auto-fire-on-key in the removed code.

### `653-endless-lift` ‚Äî `queued` (continuous gospel-house key-LIFT that never drops, Shepard-folded so it climbs forever ¬∑ WebGL2 rising-light ¬∑ adult / ecstatic-euphoric ¬∑ **tsc+eslint VERIFIED clean**)
The **directest fill of the jury's most-named open adult corner** ‚Äî the ecstatic/euphoric *missing middle* ("not doom, not cozy"; the adult side has tender [637] + hypnotic [632] + old EDM-drop [387] but no euphoric ascent) AND the most bulletproof glance candidate (pure Web Audio + WebGL2, zero network/sensor/key surface). A four-on-the-floor gospel-house bed (kick ¬∑ offbeat hats ¬∑ backbeat clap ¬∑ rising sub-bass ¬∑ maj7-add9 pad ¬∑ square Rhodes/organ stab ¬∑ bell/voice sparkles through a dotted-eighth delay) assembled live by a **Chris-Wilson look-ahead scheduler** (25 ms poll, ~120 ms ahead); the harmonic stack **modulates up a semitone every 16 bars and never drops**, every voice **Shepard-folded** (pitch class placed in whichever octave sits highest under a fixed C4-centered bell window) so the key climbs forever in-band ‚Äî perpetual rise is the point. ONE live **PRAISE** lever (‚ñ≤ / ‚Üë‚Üì, hold-to-ramp) adds layers + brightens; **M** mutes; live "now in D‚ô≠ ¬∑ +5 lifts" readout; long-form state (minute 2 higher/fuller/brighter than minute 0). **WebGL2** rising shafts of light (fullscreen-quad shader from `gl_VertexID`, no buffers; hue revolves once per octave of lift) ‚Äî renderer swapped from the ¬ß441 Canvas2D bank to dodge the Canvas2D-at-3√ó line. Files (removed): `page.tsx`, `synth.ts`, `gl.ts`, `README.md` ‚Äî **builder ran `tsc --noEmit --strict` + `eslint` both clean** (fastest, lowest-risk resurrect of the trio). Ambition honest **2‚Äì3/5** (#2 four subsystems [scheduler ¬∑ gospel-house bed ¬∑ Shepard-fold key-lift engine ¬∑ WebGL2 renderer] + #3 named refs [**Roger Shepard 1964**; Floating-Points gospel-house lift; Lyria RealTime continuous-steered-stream frame]; **#1 SOFT** ‚Äî Shepard tones foundational [40/132/187], Shepard-fold-as-gospel-lift is fresh framing). **WHY IT LOST (lane-size + #1-softness, not quality ‚Äî it was the most bulletproof candidate):** a competent *register-fill* of an already-partly-worked lane vs. 655's structural diversity win (the off-screen/screen-monoculture gap the jury prizes most) + scarcer renderer. **Resurrect as the lead of a euphoric/ecstatic adult fire** (it IS the ecstatic register the jury keeps naming, and a continuous-ascent journey-engine alternative Karel asked for) ‚Äî the fastest single resurrect for that corner, already build-verified clean.

## ¬ß440 ‚Äî 2026-06-16 ¬∑ kids WIDE fire (3 registers of the jury's "active-wonder middle") ‚Äî 2 banked seeds

Cycle 440 ran a kids **WIDE** fan-out across 3 orthogonal NON-touch registers of the active-wonder middle. **Winner: `645-kids-piano-creatures`** (the lab's first kids MIDI ‚Äî play a keyboard, an invisible band answers, no wrong notes). The two losing explorers, both demoable + build-clean, banked:

### `647-kids-clap-parade` ‚Äî `queued` (mic-RHYTHM/onset ‚Üí a marching parade that BUILDS ¬∑ inline-SVG ¬∑ kids / joyful-danceable groove)
**The strongest bank of this fire** ‚Äî the *directest fill of the jury's named "missing middle"* (danceable/groovy) on the kids side, AND the cleanest glance (inline SVG = zero GPU failure surface, the exact quality that won `637-slow-burn` its fire). A 4-year-old CLAPS / stomps / beatboxes and a marching cut-paper band of glowing creatures grows, marches, and grooves with them. **Input is ENERGY/ONSET detection, deliberately NOT pitch** (a real departure from the trodden kids voice-PITCH lane 88/158/179/244/322): spectral-flux + low/high band energy off an `AnalyserNode`, adaptive running-mean threshold, ~120 ms refractory debounce so one clap = one onset; inter-onset-interval median ‚Üí tempo estimate octave-folded to 70‚Äì150 BPM. 4 subsystems (onset/tempo detector ¬∑ Chris-Wilson look-ahead scheduler driving a 5-voice C-major groove ¬∑ arrangement state machine idle‚Üíbuild‚Üígroove gated by smoothed energy [so minute 2 ‚âÝ minute 0] ¬∑ pooled-marcher + confetti-physics SVG renderer, 14-marcher pool recycled). Named refs: New Orleans second-line / drumline; body-percussion (Stomp); call-and-response. Kid-safe chain + spacebar / big "CLAP!" button fallback + ghost-drummer auto-demo. Ambition honest 2‚Äì3/5 (#2 4 subsystems + #3 named refs; #1 soft ‚Äî onset detection is known but mic-rhythm-as-groove-builder is fresh for kids). **Resurrect as the next kids-groove fire** (it IS the danceable register the jury keeps naming) ‚Äî or as the seed of an ADULT groove cousin. Build-clean (`tsc --noEmit` 0), `page.tsx` ~30 KB. Lost only to 645's harder #1 (first kids MIDI) + Karel-pianist resonance.

### `646-kids-sky-conductor` ‚Äî `queued` (camera body-MOTION ‚Üí conduct a generative sky-orchestra ¬∑ Canvas2D ¬∑ kids / soaring active-wonder)
Lift your arms and the music soars to the constellation bells; sweep them and the strings swell ‚Äî a non-touch whole-body conductor, no instrument, no touching. Dependency-free webcam **frame-differencing** on a 64√ó48 mirrored downsample ‚Üí motion energy + vertical centroid (arms-up) + L/R balance (NO npm dep; optional MediaPipe-Pose via dynamic CDN import inside one try/catch, but frame-diff is the reliable default). Mapping: height ‚Üí register (low gestures = cellos, high = flutes/bells), energy ‚Üí dynamics, big sweep ‚Üí string swell, stillness ‚Üí soft sustained pad (never silent); all snapped to C Lydian ‚Üí no wrong moves. 5 subsystems (frame-diff analyzer ¬∑ Chris-Wilson scheduler ¬∑ retuning string pad ¬∑ arm-height melody ¬∑ 8-beat-advancing harmonic progression). Canvas2D night-sky constellation orchestra (clusters brighten/shoot per section) + aurora ribbon trailing the conductor + energy-driven horizon glow; arrow-key / drag fallback on camera denial; ghost-conductor auto-demo. Named refs: orchestral conducting gesture vocabulary; the Theremin; Memo Akten body-as-instrument; Daniel Rozin. Ambition honest 2/5 (#2 5 subsystems + #3 refs; **#1 NOT claimable ‚Äî camera/body-conducting is trodden** via 113-kids-conductor-wand, 217-dance-avatar‚ù§Ô∏è, 617-shadow-monster). Build-clean (eslint 0 / tsc 0), `page.tsx` ~29 KB. **Resurrect only with a sharper differentiator** from the existing body/conducting cluster (e.g. two-kid duet conducting, or pose-driven instrument *assignment* the child sculpts) so it isn't a fourth body-motion entry; and note Canvas2D was at 3√ó when banked (would hit 4√ó ‚Äî pair a resurrect with a renderer re-audit).

## ¬ß438 ‚Äî 2026-06-15 ¬∑ kids DEEP fire (a TRUE cycle-2 of `624-kids-day-meadow`) ‚Äî 1 banked seed

Cycle 438 ran a kids **DEEP** fan-out: ONE concept (a genuine cycle-2 deepening of the jury-named kids standout `624-kids-day-meadow` ‚Äî answering JURY 2026-06-15 provocation #1 "finish a cycle-2 for once" ‚Äî that swaps touch-to-plant ‚Üí **tilt**, honoring provocation #4's touch ban + pulled by Karel's loved `83-kids-tilt-rain`‚ù§Ô∏è) attacked via **2 interaction models**. **Winner: `640-kids-day-wind`** (tilt steers a glowing WIND SPIRIT that plants where it dwells AND plays the garden like a wind-harp where it sweeps; swept gestures captured as a traveling motif, re-voiced into the current phase ‚Äî a procedural MusicWeaver *Motif Memory Retrieval*). The losing approach, banked:

### `641-kids-tilt-meadow` ‚Äî `queued` (tilt = GRAVITY on the whole scene ‚Üí seed-physics garden + breeze wind-harp ¬∑ Canvas2D ¬∑ kids / playful-physics wonder)
The same cycle-2 of 624 (diurnal phase machine + motif-memory bank + Chris-Wilson scheduler reimplemented compactly, NOT imported; + traveling-motif MMR) but the input is **tilt-as-gravity on the entire scene**, not a steered cursor. `DeviceOrientation` beta/gamma ‚Üí a gravity vector `g` (neutral-hold zeroed, smoothed, clamped); the sun/moon arc, clouds, and rolling terrain all tip with `g`. A stream of glowing **seeds** is integrated with semi-implicit Euler (gravity + slope-roll + friction + soft bounce) and rolls into the **valleys the child sculpts by tilting**; where a seed comes to rest ~0.5s it **sprouts** into a region-appropriate living thing (low valley=flower, hill=bird, night-airborne=star). The same `g.x` drives a breeze line that brushes grown things (wind-harp, rate-limited), and brushes feed the 7-slot traveling-motif buffer. 5 subsystems (diurnal machine ¬∑ motif bank ¬∑ scheduler ¬∑ seed-physics integrator ¬∑ breeze wind-harp) + named refs (MusicWeaver MMR arXiv 2509.21714; LUMIA handheld embodied composition arXiv 2512.17228; Iwai *Electroplankton*) = honest 2/5 kids. Builder reported `tsc --strict` exit 0 + eslint clean (could not run `npm run build` ‚Äî no node_modules in its sandbox). **Why it lost:** (1) the gravity-physics planting is **indirect** ‚Äî a 4-year-old can't reliably plant *where they intend* because physics decides where seeds settle, weakening the kids legibility/immediate-cause-effect mandate that 640's direct "steer the glowy wind" model nails; (2) it reads more as a *new physics toy adjacent to* 624 than a clean **deepening** of it (640 keeps 624's recognizable plant-a-thing-at-a-spot interaction), so 640 is the truer answer to the jury's "finish a cycle-2" ask; (3) the falling-seed-emergence core overlaps the ¬ß436-banked `634-kids-tilt-river` (tilt‚Üífalling particles) more than 640's wind-harp does; (4) the seed integrator is the only runtime-jank surface (640 has none). **Why resurrect:** it is the **bolder, more surprising** mechanic ‚Äî a real particle sim a child sculpts by leaning ‚Äî and genuinely fun; resurrect when a tilt-physics register is wanted, and use its own strong next-cycle hook: **persistent sculpted terrain** ("save a valley" ‚Äî hold a tilt and the terrain *keeps* that low spot so seeds keep gathering there even after the world levels, each saved valley owning its own slowly-mutating chord) to make it a distinct landscape-sculpting instrument rather than 640-with-physics. Full working code is in git history at the folder path; treat as a fresh build, and de-overlap from 634 by keeping the sprout-on-rest (not emergent-chime) framing.

## ¬ß434 banked seeds (cycle 434, ADULT DEEP ‚Äî ONE concept ["refract Karel's own piano like light through a prism: HPSS strings/hammers split (the legible ground, from 606) ‚Üí NMF-fan the strings into pitched register voices you solo/mute/re-mix"], attacked via two **NMF-legibility** approaches; the TRUE cycle-2 of the 606 decomposition spine the jury begged for two juries running; the loser build-complete [self-reported clean `eslint` + `tsc --noEmit` in isolation; both builder agents COMPLETED before removal ‚Äî completion notifications confirmed], `rm -rf`'d after losing to `630-piano-refract`; resurrect by re-spawning the brief, renumber on revival ‚Äî max committed folder now 630)

- **`629-piano-prism`** ("Piano Prism", status: `queued` ¬∑ honest ~3/5 ¬∑ **resurrect for a cycle-3 "true note solos" fire ‚Äî its post-hoc sort is the seed of a chroma-template path**) ‚Äî **"What if Karel's piano could be refracted into pitched voices you solo/mute ‚Äî using PLAIN unsupervised NMF + a post-hoc spectral-centroid SORT to order the components low‚Üíhigh?"** Same spine as the winner (606's `audio.ts` + `hpss.ts` copied verbatim ‚Üí HPSS strings/hammers ‚Üí re-STFT the strings ‚Üí KL-NMF K=4 ‚Üí per-component Wiener-masked ISTFT ‚Üí 5 looping voices [Hammers + 4 strings], instant gain-only solo/mute, WebGL2 prism + Canvas2D fallback, keyboard/tilt off-glass, ~2.5s auto-demo). **The DIFFERENCE (and why it lost):** 629 uses **random init + post-hoc centroid pitch-SORT** (Low/Low-mid/High-mid/High) ‚Äî its own README admits "the pitch-sort is post-hoc and unsupervised ‚Äî separation is still the random-init lottery; labels are descriptive." It makes the *labels* stable but the *component content* unstable run-to-run; the winner `630`'s warm-start makes the components themselves deterministic. **LOST to 630 on reliability + legibility (not ambition ‚Äî identical floor: #1 first-NMF + #2 ‚â•3 subsystems + #3 Lee&Seung/Smaragdis&Brown/Fitzgerald + #4 cycle-2).** **Resurrect as the basis of a genuine cycle-3:** warm-start `W` from harmonic-comb **chroma templates** (one basis per pitch class, not per register) so each voice maps to a TRUE note ‚Üí per-note solos + a "transcription" read-out, the arXiv 2510.08816 deep-NAE direction; the post-hoc-sort code is a useful fallback for components a chroma init doesn't claim. Built files (removed): `page.tsx`, `nmf.ts` (random-init + centroid-sort), `gl.ts`, `audio.ts` + `hpss.ts` (from 606), `README.md`.

- **`628-data-matrix`** ("data.matrix", status: `queued` ¬∑ honest 2/5 ¬∑ **the cleanest *edges* + scarcest-renderer resurrect ‚Äî a near-instant ship for the next abrasive/clinical adult fire**) ‚Äî **"What if the musical SCORE itself were the visual ‚Äî a strobing, crisp, scalable VECTOR lattice that composes itself and sounds, with the austere clinical edge of Ryoji Ikeda rather than warm ambient glow?"** A 12√ó16 **inline-SVG** grid *is* the score: each row is a self-mutating **Euclidean rhythm** `E(k,16)` (Bjorklund/Toussaint `buildEuclid`); a bright cyan scan-column sweeps left‚Üíright (rAF, 16th-note resolution) and every lit cell it crosses fires a dry edged **FM blip** (square carrier + detuned sine modulator, click attack, ~90 ms decay, high-passed 600 Hz) pitched by row over a cold detuned cluster with **tritone tension** (NOT a major/pentatonic wash); densities drift ¬±1 and phases rotate every bar so it never settles; downbeat adds a band-passed noise tick; master capped `0.42` ‚Üí hard compressor. Hundreds of cells mutated via cached `setAttribute` refs (no per-frame React re-render). Keyboard-first: space play/pause, ‚Üë/‚Üì tempo, ‚Üí/‚Üê density, s strobe (+ mirrored ‚â•44px buttons). INPUT keyboard (off the fingertip) ¬∑ OUTPUT **inline-SVG** vector (the *scarcest* renderer, 0√ó ‚Äî directly answers the jury's "commit to a renderer / off Canvas2D") ¬∑ TECH Euclidean-rhythm sequencer + scan-line playhead + edged FM synth ¬∑ VIBE Ikeda clinical / abrasive-precise / strobing (the directest *edges* answer to JURY 2026-06-14 #1). Refs: **Ryoji Ikeda** (*test pattern* / *data.matrix*), **Godfried Toussaint** (*The Euclidean Algorithm Generates Traditional Musical Rhythms*), **Vera Moln√°r**, **Iannis Xenakis** (*UPIC*). Ambition: #2 (‚â•3 subsystems) + #3 (named refs). **WHY IT LOST (lane-value, not quality):** the most bulletproof candidate in-sandbox (pure SVG + Web Audio, no network/sensor/GPU) and the cleanest edges/scarce-renderer pick ‚Äî BUT 626 wins the **bigger open lane** (audio-FIRST off-screen, the jury's most-repeated 0√ó ask + the "massively bigger" mandate), where 628 is a refined-but-smaller grid sequencer (honest 2/5). **The fastest single resurrect for the next abrasive/clinical fire** ‚Äî and the one to reach for to give inline-SVG a real shipped entry. ## ¬ß429 banked seeds (cycle 429, ADULT WIDE ‚Äî three orthogonal **edged / off-Canvas2D-or-off-screen / non-fingertip** explorers, each hitting a DIFFERENT still-open JURY 2026-06-14 adult lane; both losers build-complete [clean `eslint` + standalone `tsc` in isolation; all 3 builder agents COMPLETED before removal ‚Äî completion notifications confirmed; AND a full `npm run build` with all 3 present passed **exit 0** + all three prerendered static before any removal, so both are proven-to-compile], `rm -rf`'d after losing to `618-solar-organ`; resurrect by re-spawning the brief, renumber on revival ‚Äî max committed folder now 618)

- **`619-combustion`** ("Combustion", status: `queued` ¬∑ honest 2‚Äì3/5 ¬∑ **the strongest resurrect for the NEXT edges/abrasive adult fire ‚Äî research-bound AND now built twice [¬ß427 as 614, ¬ß429 as 619], a near-instant ship**) ‚Äî **"What if an instrument were a MACHINE ‚Äî a procedural combustion engine you rev off the glass?"** A DSP realization of the **Pulse-Train-Resonator (PTR)** model (implements **arXiv 2603.09391**, *Physics-Informed Neural Engine Sound Modeling with Differentiable Pulse-Train Synthesis*, March 2026): inertial RPM (fast rise under load, slow drag-down = mass) ‚Üí a 4-stroke firing pulse train at `cylinders/2 √ó RPM/60` Hz with per-cylinder amplitude jitter + a slow idle-hunt wobble (lumpy, alive idle) ‚Üí each firing injects a ~4 ms combustion grain (resonant noise burst + ignition click) into a bank of **five recursive Karplus-Strong / comb feedback delay lines** (~9‚Äì31 ms, one-pole damping in-loop, feedback rising with RPM) modeling exhaust-pipe resonance ‚Üí a throttle/RPM-tracking resonant SVF + `tanh` overdrive = the redline scream ‚Üí snap-shut-at-speed = an **overrun backfire** (extra over-amp ignitions + noise pop). Runs sample-accurate in a **self-contained AudioWorklet** (processor source as a plain-JS string, Blob-URL loaded inside the Start gesture ‚Äî no asset/CDN/npm; ScriptProcessor fallback). INPUT all off the glass ‚Äî keyboard (W/‚Üë throttle, S/‚Üì brake, Space rev-blip, 4/6/8 cylinders, `[`/`]` redline) + device-tilt (iOS `requestPermission` in gesture) + an on-screen ‚â•44px pedal ¬∑ OUTPUT **WebGPU/WGSL** tachometer/combustion field (firing flashes at the live rate, heat-haze ‚Üë with RPM, redline white-out, backfire strobes; gunmetal/sodium-orange/hot-white; Canvas2D tachometer fallback + backend badge) ¬∑ TECH pulse-train + recursive-KS exhaust physical-model engine synthesis ¬∑ VIBE mechanical/abrasive/loud. The lab is **0√ó on engine/pulse-train physical-modeling + 0√ó on a procedural-machine instrument**. Ambition: #1 (first engine/machine instrument) + #3 (PTR paper) + the research‚Üíbuild chain. Clean on every JURY 2026-06-14 ban (off Canvas2D, off touch, off warm-JI, off cozy). **LOST to 618 (lane-freshness + alive-with-the-world, not quality):** edgy + the cleanest research chain, BUT the abrasive lane was just visited by `601-feedback-howl`, and 618 is alive with REAL live planetary data at a silent glance where the engine needs *playing* (the 2.5s auto-demo carries it, but it's a played instrument). **The fastest single resurrect for the next edges fire** ‚Äî built files (removed): `page.tsx` (554), `engine-worklet.ts` (685, PTR DSP + Blob worklet + ScriptProcessor fallback), `field.ts` (356, WGSL + Canvas2D), `README.md` (96).
- **`620-empty-room`** ("empty room", status: `queued` ¬∑ honest 2/5 ¬∑ **the best build-complete candidate for the perennially-0√ó OFF-SCREEN / audio-first lane ‚Äî resurrect to finally give that lane its SECOND entry beyond 576, paired with real-headphone tuning**) ‚Äî **"What if you sat in the dark with headphones and were NOT alone ‚Äî inharmonic presences move around you in real 3D sound, and they react to where you turn?"** The lab's swing at the off-screen/audio-FIRST lane the jury names every fortnight (still 0√ó after 600+ builds): the experience IS the spatialized sound; the screen is deliberately near-black (a faint Canvas2D radar ring is a glance-aid only). 4‚Äì6 procedural **presences** on a circle around the listener, each a `PannerNode` with `panningModel:'HRTF'` (AudioListener at origin); **DeviceOrientation** compass yaw rotates you *inside* the field (iOS `requestPermission` in the Start gesture). Mechanic: turn TOWARD a presence and it **hushes and drifts back** (hides when faced); turn AWAY and the ones behind you **creep closer + louder, footsteps quickening** ‚Äî the room never resolves. Each voice = 4 detuned sines at non-just ratios `[1, 1.41, 1.93, 2.71]` (‚âà‚àö2, ~2, e ‚Äî unsettling, NOT a warm pad) + a filtered-noise granular footstep that fires more when behind you. Master `gain ‚Üí DynamicsCompressor ‚Üí destination`; "üéß best with headphones, eyes closed" prompt; full fallbacks (drag-to-turn + ‚óÑ ‚ñ∫ arrow keys/buttons + `text-rose-300` notice; ~2.5s idle auto-rotates the field). INPUT device-orientation/head-turn (off the glass) ¬∑ OUTPUT audio-FIRST (HRTF binaural) + austere radar ¬∑ TECH HRTF binaural moving-source spatialization ¬∑ VIBE dark/present/unsettling. Refs: **Janet Cardiff & George Bures Miller** (*The Forty Part Motet* / audio walks); **Pauline Oliveros** (*Deep Listening*); **Sonic4D** (arXiv 2506.15759). **Honest: NOT lab-first binaural** ‚Äî `576-presence-room` did head-tracked binaural; the fresh axes are the **off-screen / audio-first register** + the **unsettling evasion-and-approach** mechanic. Ambition: #2 (‚â•3 subsystems: orientation listener + HRTF moving-source spatializer + procedural inharmonic+footstep synth + radar visual) + #3 (named refs). **LOST to 618 (glance-legibility + verifiability, not concept):** the boldest diversity swing (finally attacks the 0√ó off-screen lane), BUT its payoff lives entirely in headphones + eyes-closed head-turning ‚Äî the MOST unverifiable-in-sandbox surface and the weakest at a silent near-black 06:30 glance ‚Äî and it overlaps 576's head-tracked binaural. **Resurrect** the moment the lab commits to an off-screen fire with real-headphone verification ‚Äî the HRTF + orientation + fallbacks are all written. Built files (removed): `page.tsx` (723), `README.md` (104).

## ¬ß428 banked seeds (cycle 428, KIDS WIDE ‚Äî three orthogonal **non-fingertip / off-Canvas2D / funny** explorers, each clearing the JURY 2026-06-14 bans [Canvas2D OUTPUT 6√ó, touch INPUT 4√ó, warm-JI bed ~7√ó, cozy VIBE ~9√ó]; both losers build-complete [clean `tsc --noEmit` + `eslint`/`next lint` in isolation; all 3 builder agents COMPLETED before removal ‚Äî completion notifications confirmed, no re-materialization race; AND a full `npm run build` with all 3 present passed exit 0 before any removal, so both are proven-to-compile], `rm -rf`'d after losing to `617-kids-shadow-monster`; resurrect by re-spawning the brief, renumber on revival ‚Äî max committed folder now 617)

- **`615-kids-face-monster`** ("Silly Face Monster", status: `queued` ¬∑ ~2/5 ¬∑ **resurrect for a face/expression kids fire, or fold its blendshape‚Üísynth mapping into a face-puppet suite** ‚Äî the most legible cause‚Üíeffect of the trio) ‚Äî **"What if a 4-year-old makes SILLY FACES at the camera and a giant googly cartoon MONSTER mirrors + exaggerates them, and makes funny noises?"** Camera via **MediaPipe FaceLandmarker** with **blendshapes** (the 52-coefficient *Blendshapes GHUM* model), loaded dynamically from the jsDelivr CDN at runtime (ESM `vision_bundle.mjs` + `.wasm` fileset + `face_landmarker.task` ‚Äî **no npm dep**, one `try/catch`). Mapping: `jawOpen` ‚Üí monster mouth stretches open (rubber squash-stretch) + a ROAR/HONK (wider = louder + lower + more vowel-y); `browInnerUp`+`browOuterUp` ‚Üí googly eyes POP + BOING; `mouthSmile` ‚Üí sparkles + GIGGLE + rosy cheeks; `mouthPucker`/`mouthFunnel` ‚Üí kazoo TOOT. Per-control **EMA smoothing** so the creature moves like rubber; a dropped/empty frame is swallowed by holding last values. Rendered **WebGL2** ‚Äî a single full-screen SDF fragment shader draws the whole creature procedurally from a few uniforms (Canvas2D fallback draws the same creature). Robust no-camera path: any failure ‚Üí `text-rose-300` notice + three ‚â•88px ROAR/BOING/GIGGLE buttons + A/S/D(/F) keys + ~2.5s idle ghost-face auto-demo (open‚Üíroar, brows‚Üíboing, smile‚Üígiggle). Kid-safe master `gain ‚â§0.55 ‚Üí lowpass 7.2k ‚Üí comp ‚Üí out`, always-on bed, pre-built nodes (<50ms), no fail states, friendly-not-scary (round googly eyes, rounded teeth, rosy cheeks). Tags: **camera/face-blendshape INPUT** (non-fingertip) ¬∑ **WebGL2 OUTPUT** (off Canvas2D) ¬∑ face-expression‚Üísynth-param mapping (comedic face-puppet) TECH ¬∑ silly-monster-mirror VIBE. Refs: Tex Avery / Looney Tunes squash-and-stretch; Snapchat / Toca Boca face filters; MediaPipe **FaceLandmarker "Blendshapes GHUM"** (arXiv 2309.05782). **Ambition 2/5 (#2 ‚â•3 subsystems [camera + FaceLandmarker + WebGL2 SDF + synth = 4] + #3 named refs; honest: NOT first-MediaPipe-face ‚Äî 258/423/576 exist ‚Äî first COMEDIC face-puppet).** **LOST to 617 (renderer-lane + concept-size + diversity, NOT quality):** the most legible cause‚Üíeffect (make a face, monster copies) and the cleanest fallback, BUT it renders **WebGL2 not the jury-demanded full WebGPU spectacle** (617/616 deliver WGSL), face-puppet/mirror overlaps the lab's existing **face cluster** (258-mirror-pets/423-face-beat/576-presence-room), and "become a giant body-monster" is a bigger swing than "mirror my face." Built files (removed): `page.tsx` (414), `render.ts` (442, WebGL2 SDF + Canvas2D), `face.ts` (117, blendshape map + EMA + ghost demo), `audio.ts` (269), `README.md` (89) ‚Äî a faithful re-spawn is a fast cycle.
- **`616-kids-tilt-zoo`** ("Tilt Zoo", status: `queued` ¬∑ ~2/5 ¬∑ **the camera-FREE WebGPU-spectacle safety net ‚Äî resurrect if 617's camera path proves flaky on review, or for a phone-tilt / physics-toy kids fire**; love-aligned to `98-kids-drum-circle`‚ù§Ô∏è/`169-kids-marble-run`‚ù§Ô∏è playful-percussive cluster) ‚Äî **"What if a 4-year-old TILTS the tablet like a tray and a crowd of glowing googly creatures rolls, tumbles, and BONKS around ‚Äî every bonk a cartoon honk/boing ‚Äî and the harder you tilt, the sillier the chaos?"** **DeviceOrientation** tilt (beta/gamma ‚Üí gravity vector; iOS `requestPermission()` in the Start gesture) drives a hand-rolled 2D rigid-body pile (`physics.ts`: gravity, wall restitution, O(n¬≤) circle-circle separation + impulse, squash-on-impact, rolling spin, wobbling pupils that lean with the tray; ~24‚Äì40 creatures, capped/recycled). Each creature-creature/creature-wall bonk fires ONE of five randomized cartoon-foley voices (boing/sproing/slide-whistle/wood-tok/kazoo-honk; size‚Üípitch, velocity‚Üíloudness; deliberately NOT a warm chord) + a low doink on hard tilts; voice budget 24, steal-oldest. Rendered **WebGPU/WGSL** instanced (one quad per creature, per-instance storage buffer each frame, fragment shader draws each googly creature procedurally) with a **full Canvas2D fallback** (`render2d.ts`, identical world) + live backend badge. Robust camera-free fallback: DeviceOrientation denied/absent ‚Üí on-screen ‚â•72px tilt-pad + arrow keys + mouse-drag + `text-rose-300` notice; WebGPU absent ‚Üí Canvas2D; ~2.5s idle ‚Üí slow lissajous auto-tilt so creatures keep tumbling + honking. Kid-safe master `gain 0.55 ‚Üí lowpass 7.2k ‚Üí comp(-18/12/6) ‚Üí out`, always-on bed, ‚â•64px controls, no fail states. Tags: **device-tilt INPUT** (camera-FREE, non-fingertip) ¬∑ **WebGPU/WGSL OUTPUT** (off Canvas2D ‚Äî the jury's demanded spectacle) ¬∑ rigid-body-physics-pile + cartoon-foley TECH ¬∑ chaotic-googly-slapstick VIBE. Refs: Carl Stalling / Raymond Scott; Treg Brown (Looney Tunes foley); one-man-band. **Ambition 2/5 (#2 ‚â•3 subsystems [device-orientation + physics + WGSL instanced renderer + foley = 4] + #3 named refs).** **LOST to 617 (concept-size, NOT quality or reliability ‚Äî arguably the most RELIABLE of the trio):** the cleanest 06:30-glance story (camera-free, no permission, no CDN model) and a true WebGPU spectacle, BUT a tilt-tray physics toy is a *smaller* concept than "your whole body becomes a giant monster," and 617 also answers the jury's embodied-spatial singleton ask (#5) that a physics tray doesn't. **The fastest resurrect if 617's camera/segmentation proves flaky on Karel's review** ‚Äî it's the same WebGPU-spectacle lane, minus the camera risk. - **`612-piano-larynx`** ("Piano Larynx", status: `queued` ¬∑ ~3/5 ¬∑ **resurrect for an adult his-material fire, but PAIR it with a real-piano-loading path** ‚Äî its weakness was a synthesized default modulator; wire it to the existing public `/api/audio/[id]` like `583`/`606` do, or lead with the file-drop, so the talking effect runs on Karel's ACTUAL recorded tone) ‚Äî **"What if Karel's piano could SPEAK?"** Cross-synthesis instrument: **LPC analysis** (Hann frames 1024/50% hop, pre-emphasis 0.97, autocorrelation ‚Üí **Levinson-Durbin** ‚Üí order ~6‚Äì28 all-pole filter + residual gain per frame) tears the piano's time-varying formant/spectral envelope out; a buzzy band-limited sawtooth **carrier** (glottal source, morphable toward white noise = whisper) is run through the **time-varying all-pole IIR** (per-sample coefficient smoothing ‚Üí click-free; DC-block + `tanh` soft-clip ‚Üí stable), offline-rendered + looped. The point you HEAR: move the carrier pitch and the formants HOLD ‚Äî the piano's "vowels" stay while the pitch glides under them (source/filter decoupling = the talking effect). The lab's **first LPC / cross-synthesis** (grep 0√ó). INPUT audio-file (his piano, via file-drop or `/api/audio`) ¬∑ OUTPUT **WebGL2** "spectral throat" (the all-pole frequency response as a writhing formant ridge + live-spectrum ghost + carrier-pitch bar; cold X-ray cyan/bone/violet ‚Äî uncanny, not cozy) ¬∑ TECH LPC source-filter cross-synthesis ¬∑ VIBE uncanny/vocal/unsettling. Refs: **Paul Lansky ‚Äî *Six Fantasies on a Poem by Thomas Campion* (1979); Charles Dodge ‚Äî *Speech Songs* (1972)**. Ambition: #1 first-LPC + #3 named-refs + #4 his-material (3/5). ## ¬ß425 banked seeds (cycle 425, ADULT DEEP ‚Äî ONE concept ["take Karel's OWN recorded piano APART into hidden layers you can solo, mute & remix ‚Äî paint with the components of his timbre"], attacked via three decomposition TECHNIQUES; the lab's FIRST source-separation [grep-verified: NMF/HPSS/SMS/source-separation all 0√ó across 600+ prototypes]; the directest hit on JURY 2026-06-14 provocation #4 ["mine the vein that produced both 4/5s ‚Äî a fresh analysis/resynthesis technique on Karel's own material; do it again ‚Äî neural source separation, NMF stem-splitting, DDSP"]. Both losers build-complete [clean tsc+eslint in isolation, builders reported green], `rm -rf`'d after losing to `606-piano-vivisection`; resurrect by re-spawning the brief, renumber on revival ‚Äî max committed folder now 606. **These two are the strongest cycle-2 deepening path for the decomposition spine.** ‚Äî **UPDATE (cycle 434): `607-piano-prism` CONSUMED ‚Äî resurrected as the DEEP fire that shipped `630-piano-refract` (the warm-started register-NMF realization of this exact seed, the first honest cycle-2). `608-piano-sieve` (SMS sinusoidal+residual) still banked for a dedicated spectral-freeze / phase-vocoder fire.**)

- **`607-piano-prism`** ("Piano Prism", status: `queued` ¬∑ honest 3/5 [#1 first NMF in lab + #2 ‚â•5 subsystems + #3 Lee&Seung/Smaragdis] ¬∑ resurrect as the **decomposition cycle-2** ‚Äî the most technically advanced of the trio) ‚Äî **"What if Karel's white piano sound could be refracted, like light through a prism, into six hidden spectral 'voices' he can solo, mute, and re-mix?"** STFT magnitude S (Hann 1024/hop 256, ~9s @22kHz mono) ‚Üí **NMF** factorization S‚âàW¬∑H (W = 6 F√óK spectral basis components, H = K√óT activations) via KL-divergence multiplicative updates (~64 iters, chunked w/ progress bar); per-component soft Wiener mask Mk=Vk/Œ£Vj applied to the original complex STFT (magnitude scaled, **phase kept**) ‚Üí ISTFT overlap-add ‚Üí six independently-mixable looping AudioBuffers (sum behind per-component gains ‚Üí solo/mute is instant). WebGL2 prism: each component = a colored spectral band (W column) + scrolling activation lane (H row), soloed glow / muted dim, R32F texture. Off-glass: keyboard 1‚Äì6 solo / 0 reset / ‚Üê‚Üí select / ‚Üë‚Üì gain / space; device-tilt "spectral spotlight" (iOS-gated); secondary on-screen faders. ~2.5s idle auto-demo cycles the solo. Tags: **audio-file (his piano) + keyboard/tilt INPUT** ¬∑ **WebGL2 OUTPUT** ¬∑ **NMF spectral factorization TECH** ¬∑ **prism/dissective VIBE**. Refs: Lee & Seung (Nature 1999); Smaragdis & Brown (ISMIR 2003); arXiv 2603.04032 (2026). **LOST to 606 (legibility + reliability, not ambition):** arguably the *most ambitious/beautiful* (the prism metaphor, 6 learned components), BUT NMF components are **unsupervised spectral clusters, not nameable** (the builder flagged this) so a 06:30 glance shows abstract bands, and separation quality depends on random-init convergence ‚Äî where 606's "strings vs hammers" is instantly graspable and near-always clean. **Resurrect** as the decomposition spine's cycle-2, or **fuse**: HPSS pre-split (606) ‚Üí NMF the harmonic layer into pitched components. Built files (removed): `page.tsx` (633), `gl.ts` (265), `nmf.ts` (202), `audio.ts` (362), `README.md`.
- **`608-piano-sieve`** ("Piano Sieve", status: `queued` ¬∑ honest 3/5 [#1 first SMS in lab + #2 ‚â•5 subsystems + #3 Serra&Smith] ¬∑ resurrect for a **spectral-freeze / phase-vocoder** fire) ‚Äî **"What if you could sift Karel's piano into its pure singing TONE (deterministic partials) vs. the NOISE around it (hammer thunk, breath, room air) ‚Äî and freeze & stretch each?"** STFT ‚Üí per-frame **peak-pick** above a smoothed local noise floor (relative-prominence test + main-lobe spread) ‚Üí soft tonal mask in [0,1], tonalMask+residualMask=1; both applied to the original complex STFT (phase kept) ‚Üí ISTFT ‚Üí tonalBuf + residualBuf, equal-power **sift** morph tone‚Üîresidual, plus **freeze** (held grain) and **stretch** (playbackRate). WebGL2 "sieve": gold/white sharp partial LINES foreground vs a grey-blue grainy NOISE CLOUD behind, sift visibly separating them, freeze halts the playhead. Off-glass: keyboard 1/2 solo, ‚Üê‚Üí sift, f freeze, ‚Üë‚Üì stretch, space; device-tilt sift (iOS-gated); secondary buttons. ~2.5s idle auto-demo sweeps the sift. Tags: **audio-file + keyboard/tilt INPUT** ¬∑ **WebGL2 OUTPUT** ¬∑ **sinusoidal+residual (SMS) TECH** ¬∑ **forensic-sieve VIBE**. Refs: Serra & Smith *Spectral Modeling Synthesis* (1990); arXiv 2603.04032 (2026). **LOST to 606 (robustness, not concept):** tonal-vs-noise is intuitive and freeze/stretch are a lovely playground, BUT peak-picking is per-frame (not true partial-tracking), and freeze/stretch are explicitly approximate (the builder flagged the `playbackRate` stretch slightly pitch-shifts) ‚Äî riskier first-glance behavior than 606's bulletproof median-filter split. **Resurrect** for a dedicated spectral-freeze / phase-vocoder time-stretch fire where freeze/stretch are the headline (do it with a real phase-vocoder). Built files (removed): `page.tsx` (778), `gl.ts` (320), `audio.ts` (416), `README.md`.

Big ‚â•72px SHAKE!/STOMP! buttons + SPACE/ENTER + mouse-wiggle fallbacks (works fully on desktop with no sensor; `text-rose-300` notice when motion denied) + 2.5s idle auto-demo. Tags: **device-motion INPUT** ¬∑ **three.js OUTPUT** ¬∑ rigid-physics-pile + cartoon-foley TECH ¬∑ chaotic-junkyard-silly VIBE. Built files (removed): `page.tsx` (344), `audio.ts` (317), `physics.ts` (191), `scene.ts` (184), `README.md` (87).

- **`605-kids-blow-parade`** ("Blow Parade", status: `queued` ¬∑ ~2/5 ¬∑ resurrect for a **breath-input** kids fire ‚Äî blow is a genuinely under-mined sub-modality, distinct from yell/voice ‚Äî or a WebGPU-forward giggly toy) ‚Äî **"What if a 4-year-old BLOWS into the mic and a parade of silly balloon-creatures inflates bigger and bigger, then raspberry-deflates and zooms around when they stop?"** Mic **blow detection** (`detect.ts`: broadband energy + spectral-flatness + low-peakiness gates that reject tonal yells; sustain gate + asymmetric smoothing) drives balloon INFLATION (radius grows, rising squeak whose pitch climbs like a stretched balloon neck); stopping rips a **whoopee-cushion raspberry** (buzzy osc + noise + flutter LFO, pitch falling) and the balloon zooms+tumbles erratically. Renderer-agnostic parade model (`scene.ts`, recycles balloons so it always cycles) behind a `Renderer` interface with TWO backends (`render.ts`, 457 lines): hand-written **WGSL/WebGPU** instanced googly-balloon quads AND a **fully functional Canvas2D fallback** (same parade/gradients/eyes/squash) ‚Äî the most robust render story of the trio. Wind gauge shows blow strength (legible cause‚Üíeffect); hold-to-blow button + Space fallback; 2.5s idle auto-demo; kid-safe `gain‚Üílowpass‚â§8k‚Üíbrick-wall comp`. Tags: **mic-BLOW INPUT** (breath, not voice) ¬∑ **WebGPU OUTPUT** (Canvas2D fallback) ¬∑ breath-energy + balloon-physics + raspberry-synth TECH ¬∑ giggly-parade VIBE. Refs: party blower / whoopee-cushion foley; balloon physics; Toca Boca. **Ambition 2/5 (#2 *five* subsystems + #3 named refs).** **LOST to 603 (instrument-vs-toy + risk surface):** the funniest single payoff (a raspberry is universally kid-beloved) and the freshest INPUT sub-modality (breath), with the safest render fallback, BUT **blow is a binary energy GATE** (the balloons make their own sounds) where 603's yell‚Üíhonk *transforms the kid's own voice* into the sound ‚Äî a real instrument, the paradigm that won 601 ‚Äî and blow-detection thresholds (energy 0.06 / flatness 0.18) are the trickiest-to-tune unverified surface of the trio. **Resurrect** for a breath-input fire (the blow detector is already written + gated ‚Äî fast to revive), or pair the raspberry/inflation with a non-mic trigger. Built files (removed): `page.tsx` (369), `detect.ts` (96), `audio.ts` (240), `scene.ts` (219), `render.ts` (457), `README.md`.

## ¬ß423 banked seeds (cycle 423, ADULT WIDE ‚Äî three orthogonal **abrasive / off-Canvas2D / non-fingertip** explorers, the swing OUT of the cozy attractor JURY 2026-06-14 demanded; both losers build-complete [clean tsc+eslint in isolation], `rm -rf`'d after losing to `601-feedback-howl`; resurrect by re-spawning the brief, renumber on revival ‚Äî max committed folder now 601)

## ¬ß422 banked seeds (cycle 422, KIDS DEEP ‚Äî one instrument "Singing Water" attacked by **three synthesis cores**; both losers build-complete [clean tsc+eslint in isolation], `rm -rf`'d after losing to `597-kids-water-bowls`; resurrect by re-spawning the brief, renumber on revival ‚Äî max committed folder now 597)

- **`598-kids-water-bowls`** ("Singing Water ‚Äî modal", status: `queued` ¬∑ ~2/5 ¬∑ resurrect for a synthesis-forward kids fire that wants the *truest glass timbre*) ‚Äî same instrument as the shipped `597`, but the rub/tap drive a **modal additive bank**: each bowl is 5 always-running sine "modes" at inharmonic ratios `1, 2.01, 3.0, 4.2, 5.4√ó`, each gated by its own gain. **Tap** = one impulse into all modes (per-mode exponential decay 2.4s‚Üí0.5s, higher modes shorter) = a glassy struck-bell ring. **Rub** = a continuous **stick-slip friction source** (looping noise + a soft saw, band-passed near pitch, `frictionGain` tracks smoothed drag speed) re-energizing the bank, PLUS direct lift of the upper mode gains with speed ‚Üí a sustained song that gets richer/brighter as you rub, ~0.5s release. Kid-safe chain (lowpass 7500 + comp ‚àí18/6/12), JI Lydian-ish tuning, multi-touch, idle auto-demo. **LOST to 597:** arguably the *most authentic glass timbre* (a real inharmonic mode bank vs. 597's bandpass-noise), but the **doubled excitation path** (friction source AND direct mode-gain lift) is muddier to tune and less faithful to the ¬ß420 seed's literal "high-Q-bandpass-noise" friction-resonator spec; 597 is also the more robust, cleaner-engineered ship. **Resurrect** as a "true modal physical model" deepening (could fold real mode coupling / sympathetic resonance between bowls). Tags: touch tap+rub INPUT ¬∑ Canvas2D OUTPUT ¬∑ **modal additive synthesis TECH** ¬∑ serene-watery VIBE. Refs: Jal Tarang; Franklin glass armonica (1761); modal synthesis. Built files (removed): `page.tsx` (526), `audio.ts` (239), `README.md`.

- **`599-kids-water-bowls`** ("Singing Water ‚Äî bowed", status: `queued` ¬∑ ~2/5 ¬∑ resurrect for an **AudioWorklet** physical-modelling kids/adult fire) ‚Äî same instrument, but the resonator is a real **bowed-string / stick-slip waveguide** in an inline `AudioWorkletProcessor` (registered via Blob URL inside the unlock gesture): a tuned delay-line + nonlinear friction curve driven by a `bowVelocity`/`bowForce` param, with a hard ¬±0.85 clamp + DC blocker so the feedback never runs away. Rub speed ‚Üí bow velocity (sing sustains in the stick-slip sweet spot, louder/brighter with speed); tap = a raised-cosine strike of the same waveguide. Has a robust **DelayNode-comb fallback** wired to the identical API for browsers without AudioWorklet. **LOST to 597:** the **most physically authentic** stick-slip of the three, but an inline-Blob worklet is the **least robust** path for an unverifiable-in-sandbox kids ship that must "just work" at a silent 06:30 glance (addModule timing, prod Blob handling), and the ¬ß420 seed named the simpler bandpass primitive. **Resurrect** as the lab's first real **AudioWorklet physical model** (the worklet code is already written + clamped ‚Äî fast to revive once we can browser-verify it). Tags: touch tap+rub INPUT ¬∑ Canvas2D OUTPUT ¬∑ **bowed-waveguide AudioWorklet TECH** (lab-first worklet) ¬∑ serene-watery VIBE. Refs: Jal Tarang; Franklin glass armonica (1761); Helmholtz/bowed-string friction. Built files (removed): `page.tsx` (448), `audio-engine.ts` (386), `worklet-source.ts` (141), `visuals.ts` (240), `README.md`.

## ¬ß420 banked seeds (cycle 420, KIDS WIDE ‚Äî three orthogonal explorers off grep-verified **un-simulated physical instruments** in the 588-deep lab [plinko/Galton, jal-tarang/glass-armonica, sundial/shadow-sequencer ‚Äî all 0√ó before this cycle]; both losers build-complete [clean tsc+eslint in isolation], `rm -rf`'d after losing to `593-kids-sun-shadows`; resurrect by re-spawning the brief, renumber on revival ‚Äî max committed folder now 593)

- **`592-kids-water-bowls`** ("Singing Water", status: ‚úÖ **SHIPPED ¬ß422 as `597-kids-water-bowls`** ‚Äî the friction-excited resonator was rebuilt + shipped as the winner of a kids DEEP fire; see INDEX/RESEARCH ¬ß422) ‚Äî **"What if a 4-year-old could play a row of glowing water bowls ‚Äî tap to ring them, and RUB a finger round the rim to make them SING?"** Six glowing bowls, water-level‚Üípitch (higher water = lower pitch, real physics, teaches itself). **Tap = struck inharmonic bell** (1, 2.01, 3.0, 4.2√ó partials). **Rub = a sustained glass-armonica friction tone** ‚Äî the heart of the piece and the genuinely new browser synthesis: a **friction-excited resonator** = high-Q bandpass (Q‚âà14‚Äì28) filtered noise at the bowl pitch + pure shimmer partials, where **drag SPEED drives amplitude + brightness + Q** in real time (rub faster ‚Üí louder/brighter, stop ‚Üí it decays). Tap and rub are two excitations of ONE resonator. Tuning: JI Lydian-ish `1/1, 9/8, 5/4, 11/8, 3/2, 5/3` over ~F3 (the 11/8 #4 = watery floating color, NOT pentatonic). Canvas2D ripples + standing-wave caustics, ambient water bed, idle auto-rub demo, kids-safe limiter, multi-touch. Tags: touch tap+rub INPUT ¬∑ Canvas2D OUTPUT ¬∑ **friction-resonator synthesis TECH** (lab-first) ¬∑ serene-watery VIBE. Refs: Indian **Jal Tarang**; Benjamin Franklin's **glass armonica** (1761). **LOST to 593 (warmth/glance, not quality):** the most *technically novel* (a new synthesis primitive vs. 593's new structure), but 593's golden-hour moving-sun is warmer + more arresting at a silent 06:30 glance, and tap-vs-rub disambiguation is a hair more demanding for a 4yo than 593's single drag. **Resurrect** as the lead of a synthesis-forward kids fire (fold in pour-to-retune + sympathetic resonance). Built files (removed): `page.tsx` (~14k), `synth.ts`, `README.md`.

- **`591-kids-plinko-bells`** ("Bell Drop", status: `queued` ¬∑ ~3/5 ¬∑ resurrect for an emergent/autonomous-music or STEM kids fire ‚Äî love-aligned to Karel's `236-particle-life-song`‚ù§Ô∏è/`130-tsl-particle-compute`‚ù§Ô∏è/`262-aurora-particle`‚ù§Ô∏è cluster) ‚Äî **"What if dropping a ball and watching it bounce taught a 4-year-old probability ‚Äî and built a warm chord while it does?"** A sonified **Galton/quincunx board (1894)**: tap the top (or an 88px Drop button) ‚Üí a luminous ball falls, left/right "coin-flip" off each peg row (each bounce a quiet panned wooden tick), landing in one of **9 just-intonation bins**; balls **accumulate into a visible histogram** and the bell curve *blooms as a warm major chord* ‚Äî the bins are arranged so consonance mirrors outward from the peak, the most-hit center bins on root/4th/5th, so "the middle is home." Emergent, autonomous, self-playing (off-finger reward), with a dashed normal-curve guide so the math is legible. Canvas2D, ambient root+fifth pad, idle auto-drop demo, optional tilt-to-bias, kids-safe limiter. Tags: touch-drop (+tilt) INPUT ¬∑ Canvas2D OUTPUT ¬∑ **Galton/binomial-physics ‚Üí histogram-as-chord TECH** (lab-first) ¬∑ playful-emergent-STEM VIBE. Ref: Francis Galton, the **quincunx** / bean machine, *Natural Inheritance* (1894). **LOST to 593:** the richest *concept* and most autonomous (Karel loves emergent music), but cooler/cerebral and less immediately gorgeous at a glance than 593's golden-hour sky. **Resurrect** for an emergent/generative or STEM-leaning kids fire. Built files (removed): `page.tsx` (473), `audio.ts`, `physics.ts`, `tuning.ts`, `README.md`.

## ¬ß418 banked seeds (cycle 418, KIDS DEEP ‚Äî one concept, *the lab's first GAME-CONTROLLER instrument for a 4-year-old, off the glass*, via three interaction approaches on the Gamepad API + dual-rumble haptics; both losers build-complete [clean ESLint + tsc in isolation], `rm -rf`'d after losing to `587-kids-button-garden`; resurrect by re-spawning the brief, renumber on revival ‚Äî max committed folder now 587)

Keyboard (WASD/arrows + Z/X/C) + pointer-drag fallbacks + idle Lissajous auto-demo. Tags: gamepad INPUT ¬∑ Canvas2D OUTPUT ¬∑ continuous-portamento-chord-bend TECH ¬∑ warm-expressive VIBE. Refs: Toshio Iwai *Electroplankton*/*TENORI-ON*; May 2026 Steam Controller haptic-song demos. **LOST to 587 (legibility, not quality):** the most *musically expressive* of the three (continuous bend = real warmth), but **two-stick coordination + "push harder = higher" is the least graspable for a 4-year-old** ‚Äî it reads as a slightly-adult instrument. **Resurrect** for an adult/older-kid expressive-controller fire, or fold its continuous chord-bend into 587 as a held-trigger "expressive" layer. Built files (removed): `page.tsx` (701), `README.md`.

- **`588-kids-glow-drive`** (status: `queued` ¬∑ ~3/5 ¬∑ resurrect for a calm SPATIAL / exploratory kids fire, or as 587's one-stick younger sibling) ‚Äî **"What if you steered a glowing comet through a dark sky and woke sleeping stars that sing as you pass?"** Left stick steers a comet through a fixed autonomous field of light-seeds; the nearest seed wakes/ripples/sings when you pass, pitched by its *place* in the field (a just-intonation spatial scale, low-left‚Üíhigh-right) over a root+fifth drone; right stick blooms a halo, triggers leave a trail + swell, dual-rumble buzzes on each wake. Keyboard/WASD + pointer-drag-to-steer + idle wandering auto-demo. Tags: gamepad INPUT ¬∑ Canvas2D OUTPUT ¬∑ spatial-scale-field TECH ¬∑ calm-exploratory VIBE. Refs: same (Iwai; Steam Controller). **LOST to 587:** lovely and the cleanest answer to the frozen-creature ban (a *field*, not a pet), and one-stick steering is more accessible than 586's two ‚Äî but the reward is more **ambient/passive** than 587's instant mash‚Üíbloom cause‚Üíeffect, and "steer precisely enough to pass near a seed" needs more motor control than a 4-year-old's button-mash. **Resurrect** for a spatial/ambient kids fire, or as the younger-sibling one-stick mode of a controller suite. Built files (removed): `page.tsx` (646), `README.md`.

## ¬ß415 banked seeds (cycle 415, ADULT WIDE ‚Äî 3 orthogonal explorers all chasing the jury's WARMTH dare; both losers build-complete in isolation, `rm -rf`'d after losing to `580-tide-breath`; resurrect by re-spawning the brief, renumber on revival ‚Äî max committed folder now 580)

- **`581-still-bloom`** (status: `queued` ¬∑ ~2‚Äì3/5 ¬∑ resurrect to open the off-glass / audio-first / conceptual lane the jury keeps naming as 0√ó) ‚Äî **"What if music rewarded you for holding completely still?"** An INVERSE gate: a warm ~5‚Äì7-voice just-intonation drone (low root, detuned sine/triangle pairs) slowly BLOOMS out of silence ‚Äî voices entering one by one, master lowpass opening 400‚Üí3500 Hz ‚Äî only while you hold the device truly still; any motion above a gentle threshold scatters it (voices duck, bloom recedes). **Off the glass:** input is `DeviceMotion` agitation (rolling variance of acceleration; iOS 13+ `requestPermission()` inside the start gesture), falling back to pointer-stillness, then a guided "breathe-with-me" auto-demo. Audio-FIRST: the visual is a minimal warm Canvas2D radial bloom (NOT three.js/WebGL) that grows with integrated stillness and disperses into grain on motion. Tags: device-motion-stillness INPUT ¬∑ audio-first/Canvas2D OUTPUT ¬∑ stillness-integration TECH ¬∑ warm-meditative/conceptual VIBE. Refs: Pauline Oliveros *Deep Listening*, √âliane Radigue, John Cage *4'33"*, Christine Sun Kim. **LOST to 580:** lowest ambition of the trio, does NOT revive the jury-starved WebGPU, and its real payoff needs a held-still device ‚Äî at a silent 06:30 glance Karel sees only the breathe-demo, not the felt reward (580 plays autonomously). The strongest pure *concept* and the cleanest answer to the off-glass / audio-only provocation ‚Äî resurrect on a cycle where the data/WebGPU lanes are crowded and the lab needs to get off the screen. (No API route; pure client motion + Web Audio, nothing recorded/sent. Build-clean in isolation: `page.tsx`, `audio.ts`, `motion.ts`, `render.ts`, `README.md`.)

- **`582-piano-aurora`** (status: `queued` ¬∑ ~2‚Äì3/5 ¬∑ resurrect for a "use Karel's real music" fire / a warm WebGPU-painting fire) ‚Äî **"What if the *harmony* of a warm piano painted living light?"** A warm piano source (a built-in soft-attack generative I‚Äìvi‚ÄìIV‚ÄìV pad in D with 7ths/9ths so it always plays, PLUS a drop-your-own-recording file input ‚Äî the production path being the existing `/api/audio/[id]` route to Karel's Welcome Home tracks, documented not implemented) feeds an `AnalyserNode`; each frame folds the spectrum into a **12-bin chroma** (pitch-class energy, NOT onset/beat) + RMS. The dominant pitch-classes steer the HUE of a **WebGPU curl-noise flow-field aurora** (~40k particles via a WGSL compute pass; Bridson curl noise) confined to a warm amber‚Üírose‚Üíviolet arc (cyan/cold-green skipped), RMS drives brightness/flow; hot-downgrades to a ~4k-particle Canvas2D flow field if `navigator.gpu` fails. Tags: audio-file INPUT ¬∑ WebGPU OUTPUT (Canvas2D fallback) ¬∑ FFT-chroma flow-field TECH ¬∑ warm-intimate VIBE. Refs: Refik Anadol (data-painting), Memo Akten (*Learning to See* / flow fields), Robert Bridson (curl noise). **LOST to 580:** it is fundamentally an audio-reactive *visualizer*, and the lab's flow-field/aurora-visualizer lane is crowded (`130-tsl-particle-compute`‚ù§Ô∏è, `262-aurora-particle`‚ù§Ô∏è, `441-latent-listening-room`) ‚Äî close to the jury's standing "renderer flex in search of a concept" critique (its 541 call-out) ‚Äî and it carries no real-world data (jury #2). The "harmony-not-rhythm" chroma steering is a genuinely nice twist and it honors the under-served "use his real music" directive ‚Äî resurrect by binding it to his ACTUAL Welcome Home tracks via `/api/audio/[id]` (the differentiator that lifts it out of the visualizer pack). (No API route in the prototype itself; dropped audio decoded locally, never uploaded. Build-clean in isolation: `page.tsx`, `audio.ts`, `chroma.ts`, `field.ts`, `gpu.ts`, `README.md`.)

## ¬ß412 banked seeds (cycle 412, KIDS DEEP ‚Äî one concept, *today's REAL local weather, played as a kids music window*, via three renderer/interaction approaches on live Open-Meteo; both losers build-complete [clean tsc+eslint], `rm -rf`'d after losing to `575-kids-sky-song`; resurrect by re-spawning the brief, renumber on revival ‚Äî max committed folder now 575)

Cycle 412 fired a **kids DEEP** triple off ONE fresh concept ‚Äî *open a window onto the child's actual current weather (live Open-Meteo, no key) and play it as music; the live weather composes the key/mood/motion, the child performs within it* ‚Äî the directest answer to JURY 2026-06-13 #2 (real-world-data sonification, **0√ó the whole window**) + #4 (chase warmth, not a puzzle), and a grep-verified **kids-first real-data sonification** (the lab's prior weather toys `115`/`120`/`83` are all *synthetic*/"Zero API"; the only real-data pieces `355`/`337` are adult + out of frame). All three share: direct client-side fetch of `api.open-meteo.com` (public, no-key, CORS, **no api route, no guard, no secret**; CSP-verified ‚Äî enforced `connect-src ‚Ä¶ https:` allows it), optional geolocation with a silent default-coordinate fallback (no kids permission gate), a baked demo-sky for offline/failure so the 06:30 glance always shows + plays, WMO `weather_code`+`cloud_cover`+`is_day`‚Üíconsonant-scale/mood mapping, kids-safe `gain‚Üílowpass(‚â§8k)‚Üícompressor‚Üídestination` limiter, alive-from-frame-one visuals. Winner shipped = `575-kids-sky-song` (the *generative-composition* take ‚Äî the weather writes an evolving look-ahead song that plays itself; the biggest concept + best "reads as music at a silent glance" + most differentiated from the lab's existing synthetic-weather Canvas2D toys). Both losers below are complete, build-reviewed real implementations (own folder, pure client-side, no API route, brick-wall limiter, hands-free auto-demo + baked-sky fallback, graceful WebGL2‚ÜíCanvas2D / geo-denied fallbacks); folders removed per the no-half-built-folders rule, banked here as ready-to-resurrect TEXT.

- **`573-kids-sky-window`** (`queued`, the most *immersive renderer* take ‚Äî RESURRECT for a shader-forward weather/atmosphere fire, or as 575's richer sky layer) ‚Äî a fullscreen **raw-WebGL2 hand-written-GLSL** living-sky window: fbm volumetric-ish clouds (density from `cloud_cover`), wind-driven drift (`wind_speed/direction`), sun/moon glow+halo (`is_day`+clear), procedural rain streaks / snowflakes (from `precipitation`), night stars, storm lightning, touch ripples/bloom, window vignette ‚Äî alive from frame one, with a Canvas2D fallback sky that still shows weather + sings. Touch = play notes quantised to today's weather-set scale (7 moods: sunny major-pentatonic / starry-night octave-down / cloudy sus2 / fog low-drone / rain minor-pentatonic / snow high-bells / storm lydian); `temperature`‚Üítimbre+register, `wind`‚Üídrift+filter LFO, `precipitation`‚Üífalling-voice density. **LOST, not on quality:** the most beautiful sky of the three, but 575's generative engine is the bigger *concept* leap ("the weather *composes*" vs "the weather *tints* what you tap"), and an abstract shader sky reads less instantly than 575's self-blooming notes at a silent glance. **Its value:** the richest atmosphere renderer ‚Äî resurrect for an atmosphere/aurora-forward fire, or fold its volumetric sky in as 575's background. Ambition 3/5 (#1 qualified kids-first real-data sonification + #2 ‚â•3 subsystems + #3 Polli/Miebach/WeatherChimes; #5 not claimed). Complete: `page.tsx`, `audio.ts` (`SkyAudio`), `weather.ts`, `render.ts`, `README.md`. Clean tsc+eslint, not browser-verified.

- **`574-kids-weather-today`** (`queued`, the warmest / most 4yo-legible take) ‚Äî a cozy **Canvas2D cut-paper diorama**: paper hills, a smiling sun or moon, drifting clouds, falling rain/snow, and a friendly breathing paper critter ‚Äî the most instantly-readable "today's weather" for a 4-year-old. Tap the sun/clouds/critter/hill (each a different position in today's key) ‚Üí always-consonant music-box notes; scene + key/mode chosen by live `weather_code`+`cloud_cover`+`is_day` (sunny major meadow / overcast sus2 grey hills / rain minor-pentatonic chimes / snow glassy bells / fog hush / thunder weighty minor / starry night), `temperature`‚Üícolor temp + timbre, `wind`‚Üícloud drift, `precipitation`‚Üíparticle count; always-on ambient chord bed; tap targets ‚â•80px. **LOST:** despite the real-data headline it is the closest in *form* to the lab's existing synthetic weather toys (`115-kids-weather-music` zone-blend, `120-kids-rain-drum` cloud-tap) ‚Äî the "too similar in shape" risk ‚Äî and the least ambitious renderer of the three (Canvas2D vs WebGL2 + a generative engine). **Its value:** the warmest, coziest, most-legible-to-a-4yo + the most iPad-bulletproof (Canvas2D) ‚Äî resurrect when a warmth-forward / maximally-legible kids weather fire is due, or ship it as 575's more-literal sibling for the youngest users. Ambition 3/5 (#1 qualified kids-first real-data sonification + #2 ‚â•3 subsystems + #3 Polli/Miebach/WeatherChimes; #5 not claimed). Complete: `page.tsx`, `audio.ts`, `weather.ts`, `scene.ts`, `README.md`. Clean tsc+eslint, not browser-verified.

## ¬ß410 banked seeds (cycle 410, KIDS DEEP ‚Äî one concept, *a creature genuinely different each day you return*, via what evolves; both losers build-complete, `rm -rf`'d after losing to `569-kids-ember-keeper`; resurrect by re-spawning the brief, renumber on revival ‚Äî max committed folder now 569. NOTE: the ¬ß409 banked seeds named `569-conductor-batons`/`570-conductor-baton-motion` are stale number-labels ‚Äî those slugs are now taken by this cycle's Ember pieces; renumber the conductor seeds on revival.)

Cycle 410 fired a **kids DEEP** triple off ONE massively-bigger concept ‚Äî *a creature that lives in the device, remembers every visit, and is genuinely different each day you return; it never resets* ‚Äî attacked via three things that can evolve across days (BODY / SONG / WORLD), all sharing the lab-first **cross-session `localStorage` persistence** + day-aware growth + analysis-only mic-feeding + kids-safe limiter + a fresh-device demo seed + a üåô next-day time-travel button (so the cross-day payoff reads in <30s at the 06:30 review). Winner shipped = `569-kids-ember-keeper` (the *body-morphology* take ‚Äî the most literal answer to JURY #6 "a creature genuinely changed tomorrow", the most glance-legible [you SEE it grew], and the freshest vs the existing kids set). Both losers below are complete, build-reviewed real implementations (own folder, pure client-side, no API route, brick-wall limiter, hands-free seed/auto-demo, graceful mic-denied fallback, no-Canvas2D fallback); folders removed per the no-half-built-folders rule, banked here as ready-to-resurrect TEXT.

- **`570-kids-ember-song`** (`queued`, the biggest *concept* of the trio ‚Äî RESURRECT for a music-forward / non-glance-only fire) ‚Äî a child's creature **remembers every melody they ever hummed** and, each day they return, sings back a LONGER, RICHER song woven from everything taught ‚Äî never the same twice, never reset. Hummed phrases (autocorrelation pitch ‚Üí pentatonic snap ‚Üí silence-segmented into a phrase of scale-degrees + coarse rhythm, `pitch.ts`) are **appended forever** to a `localStorage` library (`song-memory.ts`, key `resonance.ember-song.v1`); a tiny seeded **generative weaver** (`weave.ts`, Eno *Music for Airports* lineage) recombines the library each visit, sizing the piece to distinct-days returned (growth 1 = one looped phrase; 3+ = several phrases + an octave-down counter-voice; 4+ = a fuller pad bed). Each remembered phrase = one **luminous orbiting strand** in a raw-**WebGL2** creature (`render.ts`, `MAX_STRANDS` 24) that flares when its notes sound. Look-ahead scheduler (25 ms pump, `start(t)` 120 ms ahead) keeps the weave tight; kids-safe limiter; fresh-device seed = 4 phrases across 4 days. **LOST, not on quality:** the cross-day payoff is *audible* (a longer song), so at a SILENT 06:30 glance the abstract strands read less clearly than 569's visibly-bigger body, and the üåô button's reward is a thing you must hear. **Its value:** no lab piece grows the *music itself* across sessions ‚Äî it's the strongest return-reward concept; ship it when a music-forward kids fire is due (or as 569's cycle-2 "it also sings your grown song"). Ambition 4/5 (#1 cross-session persistence [shared lab-first] + #2 ‚â•3 subsystems + #3 Eno/Tamagotchi/Oliveros + #4 Ember spine). Complete: `page.tsx`, `audio.ts`, `pitch.ts`, `render.ts`, `song-memory.ts`, `weave.ts`, `README.md`. Build-reviewed, not browser-verified.

- **`571-kids-ember-nest`** (`queued`, the warmest/most-legible take) ‚Äî the creature lives in a little **NEST/WORLD that fills up across days**: every visit leaves a permanent **cut-paper flower / star / friend** (matte inline-**SVG** via `feTurbulence`+`feDropShadow`, rAF-via-refs ‚Äî no per-frame React churn), and each object is also one **sustained C-pentatonic voice**, so a fuller world is literally a fuller, warmer (always-consonant) **additive chord** (per-voice gain `0.16/‚àön` keeps the sum kids-safe while the texture thickens ‚Äî the Eno density move). Day-aware: first wake of a new calendar day auto-sprouts one new permanent object; humming/tapping plants smaller flowers. `localStorage` key `resonance.ember-nest.v1`; fresh-device seed = 9 objects across 4 days with `lastVisitDay` = yesterday; üåô next-day button thickens the chord on demand. **LOST:** the accumulating-world framing is thematically **closest to the existing `116-kids-bloom-garden`** (self-seeding singing garden) and a glowing-pond/firefly lineage ‚Äî the "too similar" risk; and another SVG output nudges the rolling-10 SVG count toward the 4√ó ban (it sat at 3√ó). **Its value:** the warmest, most-immediately-legible-to-a-4yo of the three (a busy nest reads instantly), removes Tamagotchi's punishment/death loop (returning is *always* rewarded, never guilt ‚Äî a deliberate kindness), and the additive-chord-from-world-density is lovely; resurrect when SVG is clear and pair it with a non-overlapping placement pass + a voice cap for months-old worlds. Ambition 4/5 (#1 cross-session persistence [shared lab-first] + #2 ‚â•3 subsystems + #3 Tamagotchi/Reggio Emilia "revisiting"/Eno + #4 Ember spine). Complete: `page.tsx`, `audio.ts`, `pitch.ts`, `scene.ts`, `shapes.ts`, `README.md`. Build-reviewed, not browser-verified.

## ¬ß408 banked seeds (cycle 408, KIDS WIDE ‚Äî 3 orthogonal off-glass explorers; both losers build-complete, `rm -rf`'d after losing to `566-kids-shake-band`; resurrect by re-spawning the brief, renumber on revival ‚Äî max folder now 566)

Cycle 408 fired a WIDE kids triple off three orthogonal **off-glass** inputs (mic-sing / device-shake / camera-color) √ó non-banned renderers (WebGL2 / three.js / WebGL2), all dodging the rolling-10 **SVG-output ban (4√ó)** and every standing jury ban. Winner shipped = `566-kids-shake-band` (device-motion ‚Üí three.js batucada band ‚Äî the freshest off-glass input vs recent kids cycles + the directest "break the calm-sensory autopilot" answer, and ideal for Karel's mobile review since he can literally shake his phone). Both losers below are complete, build-reviewed real implementations (own folder, pure client-side, no API route, brick-wall limiter, hands-free auto-demo, graceful sensor-denied fallback); folders removed per the no-half-built-folders rule, banked here as ready-to-resurrect TEXT.

- **`565-kids-sing-choir`** (`queued`, the biggest *concept* of the trio ‚Äî RESURRECT when a non-mic kids cycle is due) ‚Äî a 4-year-old sings/hums one steady note and **four glowing creature-friends instantly join in live harmony** (major 3rd / perfect 5th / octave), the whole choir gliding with the child's pitch in real time ‚Äî the child *becomes* the lead of a four-voice chord. Implements **Jacob Collier's live vocal-harmonizer** honestly: detected pitch (YIN-style parabolic-interpolated autocorrelation, RMS-gated, `pitch.ts`) ‚Üí C-pentatonic snap ‚Üí 4 detuned-oscillator+vibrato voices tracking via `setTargetAtTime` 40ms glides (`audio.ts`), rendered as raw-WebGL2 additive particle creatures (`page.tsx`, ~746 lines, Canvas2D fallback). **LOST on the diversity gate, not quality:** its input is **mic**, exactly what the *immediately prior* kids cycle (406 clap-canon) used ‚Äî repeating the previous kids cycle's primary modality is the "too similar" trap. Also the riskiest unverified surface (pitch detection on a breathy 4yo soprano in a noisy room; it flags wanting a 3‚Äì5-frame median smoother + higher RMS gate). Distinct from the adult `114-live-harmonize` (piano-roll viz) and from `280-echo-canyon`/`179-voice-monster` (those echo *sequentially*, this harmonizes *simultaneously*). **Its value:** no kids piece does live simultaneous vocal harmony ‚Äî ship it the next time the diversity audit clears mic. Ambition 3/5 (#2 5 subsystems + #3 Collier; #1 not clean ‚Äî live-harmonize exists). Complete: `page.tsx`, `audio.ts`, `pitch.ts`, `README.md`. Build-reviewed, not browser-verified.

- **`567-kids-color-garden`** (`queued`, the off-the-couch explorer) ‚Äî point the tablet's **rear camera** at anything colorful in the real world and that color **grows a singing plant** in a glowing WebGL2 garden; warm reds = low notes, cool violets = high, every color welcome, no goal. Implements **Scriabin's *clavier √Ý lumi√®res*** hue‚Üípitch map (red‚ÜíC3 ‚Ä¶ violet‚ÜíD5) honestly. Subsystems: `getUserMedia(environment)` ‚Üí offscreen-2D central-patch `getImageData` ‚Üí RGB‚ÜíHSV with 20¬∞/24-frame **hysteresis** (`vision.ts`), Scriabin hue‚ÜíC-pentatonic map + brick-wall-limited synth + always-on C2+G2 pad (`audio.ts`), instanced additive WebGL2 plants growing root‚Üíbloom over ~1.5s with in-shader hsv2rgb (`render.ts`, ~392 lines), hue-cycling auto-demo for the camera-denied path. **LOST:** camera-color is thematically adjacent to the existing `368-kids-rainbow-quest` (foraging quest) and camera is the jury-flagged trodden input lane; it is also the most **lighting-fragile** for the 06:30 hands-free glance (low light / fluorescent flicker destabilize the patch average). **Its value:** the *open-creation* (vs quest) framing + the world-becomes-music exploration is genuinely lovely; resurrect when the camera lane is clear and pair with a more robust dominant-color extractor (k-means / saturation-weighted, not a flat average). Ambition 3/5 (#2 5+ subsystems + #3 Scriabin; #1 not clean ‚Äî color-foraging exists at 368). Complete: `page.tsx`, `audio.ts`, `vision.ts`, `render.ts`, `README.md`. Build-reviewed, not browser-verified.

## ¬ß407 banked seeds (cycle 407, ADULT DEEP ‚Äî splat spine cycle 2; both build-complete, `rm -rf`'d after losing to `564-piano-splat-cathedral`; resurrect by re-spawning the brief)

- **`563-piano-splat-ewa`** (`queued`, the reconstruction-grade renderer ‚Äî RESURRECT for cycle 3) ‚Äî the lab's most technically honest Gaussian-splat renderer: **full EWA covariance projection** (per-splat 3D covariance Œ£ = R¬∑diag(s)¬∑‚Ä¶¬∑R·µÄ from a quaternion; analytic perspective Jacobian J; Œ£' = TŒ£T·µÄ; 2√ó2 conic inversion; eigenvalue-bounded quad) with **premultiplied alpha-over** + per-frame back-to-front depth sort, driven by Karel's real piano (audio.ts + synth fallback). It LOST to 564 only because the *experience* is another orbitable glowing cloud (adjacent to 557's galaxy), where 564 is a categorically new experience (a building his music raises). **Its value:** the true-EWA math is the cleanest #1-technique in the splat spine ‚Äî the obvious **cycle-3 move is to swap 564's simplified two-axis anisotropy for 563's full covariance projection** ‚Üí a reconstruction-grade cathedral. Complete files existed: `page.tsx` (idle demo + Begin), `splat-renderer.ts` (~28kB, all math hand-rolled: quatToMat3 / computeCovariance3D / projectCovariance / invertConic / gaussianRadius), `audio.ts`, `README.md`. Build-reviewed, not browser-verified (numerical fragility on degenerate/off-axis conics is the main risk; it added a 0.3px low-pass regularizer).

- **`562-piano-splat-aurora`** (`queued`, the ribbon/sheet renderer) ‚Äî anisotropic splats stretched into flowing **aurora ribbons** (each splat carries a 3D principal axis ‚Üí screen-space rotation Œ∏ + (œÉMajor,œÉMinor); bright/treble notes = thin sharp ribbons, dark/bass = fat puffs), depth-sorted alpha-over with a small additive emissive core for luminosity (Anadol teal/magenta/violet palette). LOST: also a "cloud you orbit," conceptually adjacent to 557. **Its value:** the *anisotropy-from-velocity-tangent* trick (splats elongate along the audio-driven flow) is a clean, cheap way to make sorted splats read as silky sheets ‚Äî fold into any future splat piece that wants ribbons over puffs. Complete: `page.tsx`, `splat.ts` (~22kB), `audio.ts`, `README.md`. Build-reviewed, not browser-verified.

### Banked ¬ß406 (WIDE **kids** non-winners ‚Äî TEXT seeds, folders removed; renumber on revival, max folder now 559):
Cycle 406 fired a WIDE kids triple off three orthogonal **off-glass** inputs (mic / camera / shake) √ó three different renderers (SVG / three.js / Canvas2D), all dodging the rolling-10 finger-on-glass ban. Winner shipped = `559-kids-clap-canon` (mic‚ÜíSVG, Steve Reich phasing ‚Äî the jury #4 "time-tension piece, but for children" answer). Both losers below are complete, build-reviewed real implementations (own folder, pure client-side, no API route, brick-wall limiter, hands-free auto-demo, graceful sensor-denied fallback); folders removed per the no-half-built-folders rule, banked here as ready-to-resurrect TEXT.

- **`560-kids-shadow-band`** (camera silhouette, NO ML ‚Üí three.js particle creature ‚Üí zone-gated pentatonic band) ‚Äî `queued`. **What if a 4-year-old's SHADOW became a glowing creature, and dancing through the air played a band ‚Äî no controller, no reading, just their silhouette of light?** INPUT camera frame-differencing (no MediaPipe/TF ‚Äî `getUserMedia`‚Üí96√ó72 offscreen canvas‚Üí24√ó18 motion-energy grid vs captured background + prev frame, IIR-smoothed) ¬∑ OUTPUT **three.js** ~4,096 additive THREE.Points blending toward active grid cells (a luminous "moving aura," not a sharp outline ‚Äî correct for frame-diff) ¬∑ TECHNIQUE optical-motion-energy‚Üí3 vertical instrument zones (left bells / center warm-pad / right marimba, rate-limited, motion‚Üígain+brightness) ¬∑ VIBE embodied/awe/your-body-becomes-light. Master `gain‚Üílowpass8k‚ÜíDynamicsCompressor(‚àí10/20:1)`; synthetic Lissajous-blob auto-demo runs with zero permission, hands off to real camera, resumes after 4 s still; `text-rose-300` camera-denied + pointer fallback. Named refs **Myron Krueger *Videoplace* (1974)**, **Daniel Rozin** mirror works. **Ambition 3/5** (#2 ‚â•3 subsystems [no-ML silhouette + zone synth + three.js creature + auto-demo = 4] + #3 named refs; #1 contestable ‚Äî camera-body‚Üíinstrument is the most-used kids lane [537/545/554/258/423/419/268/295], the fresh element is the no-ML frame-diff silhouette-as-particle-creature, discounts cleanly; #5 not claimed). Built files (removed): `page.tsx` (565), `audio.ts` (191), `vision.ts` (149), `README.md`. **Why it didn't win (cycle 406):** the most embodied/"wow", but the **most redundant input lane** for the kids set (camera-body‚Üísound has the most siblings) AND the **most fragile for the 06:30 hands-free glance** ‚Äî frame-differencing is lighting-sensitive (fixed `MOTION_THRESHOLD=18` floods on variable light; needs adaptive calibration) and needs a lit room + camera grant, where the winner's SVG needs neither. **Resurrect** when the camera/body lane is scarce again, or pair the no-ML silhouette with adaptive-threshold calibration; a strong "body becomes light" register if Karel wants embodied.
- **`561-kids-rainmaker`** (device SHAKE / `devicemotion` accelerometer ‚Üí granular percussion ‚Üí Canvas2D storm) ‚Äî `queued`. **What if a 4-year-old could SHAKE the tablet like a rainstick to summon a storm ‚Äî gentle shake = drizzle, hard shake = downpour + thunder + lightning?** INPUT **`devicemotion`** linear-acceleration magnitude (NOT tilt/orientation ‚Äî raw shake energy, `‚àö(ax¬≤+ay¬≤+az¬≤)` capped 30 m/s¬≤, EMA Œ±=0.18 ‚Üí 0‚Äì1 intensity; iOS 13+ `DeviceMotionEvent.requestPermission()` in the Start gesture; desktop pointer-shake / mash-button fallback) ¬∑ OUTPUT **Canvas2D** pooled rain streaks (density ‚àù intensity¬≤, ~7¬∞ angle) + landing splashes + jagged lightning bolt with white-flash decay + breathing cloud + 8-dot intensity HUD ¬∑ TECHNIQUE **Xenakis stochastic grain cloud** ‚Äî per-frame Poisson spawn N grains ‚àù intensity¬≤ (triangle osc, C-pentatonic C4‚ÄìA5, 8 ms attack / 80‚Äì230 ms decay, 40-voice pool via counter + `osc.onended`), high-intensity Poisson check fires thunder (180‚Äì400 Hz noise, 2.8 s) ¬∑ VIBE weather/dynamic-sensory (RESPONDS to vigor ‚Äî not a static glow toy). Master `grains‚Üígain‚Üílowpass9k‚ÜíDynamicsCompressor(‚àí10/20:1)`; sine-envelope auto-demo storm (6 s period) from frame one, hands off to real shake >0.05; `text-rose-300` denied notice. The `intensity¬≤` quadratic knee makes the drizzle‚Üídownpour transition feel like *summoning* (~40% shake = storm). Named ref **Iannis Xenakis ‚Äî stochastic granular clouds** (*Achorripsis* 1957 / *Analogique B* 1959; shake magnitude = the Poisson Œª rate). **Ambition 3/5** (#2 ‚â•3 subsystems [devicemotion shake + granular engine + Canvas2D storm + auto-demo = 4] + #3 named ref Xenakis; #1 not claimed ‚Äî granular + accelerometer both have priors; #5 not claimed). Built files (removed): `page.tsx` (447), `audio.ts`, `drawRain.ts`, `README.md`. **Why it didn't win (cycle 406):** the most robust voice-pooling + a clean Xenakis bind, and shake is a genuinely fresh off-glass input (distinct from 553's tilt/orientation), but the **least conceptually surprising** of the three ‚Äî "shake ‚Üí weather texture" reads as a sensory toy where the winner's Reich-phasing-for-kids is a genuinely novel idea AND answers a named jury provocation. **Resurrect** when a shake/percussion input is wanted, or fold the granular grain-cloud engine into a richer weather-composition piece (steer rain *type* by tilt while shake sets density).
