### Banked ¬ß404 (WIDE **kids** non-winners ‚Äî TEXT seeds, folders removed; renumber on revival, max folder now 553):
Cycle 404 fired a WIDE kids triple off three orthogonal off-glass inputs √ó three non-banned renderers (winner shipped = `553-kids-tilt-fountain`, tilt‚Üíthree.js). Both losers are complete, build-reviewed real implementations (own folder, pure client-side, no API route, brick-wall limiter, hands-free auto-demo, graceful sensor-denied fallback); folders removed per the no-half-built-folders rule, banked here as ready-to-resurrect TEXT.

- **`554-kids-giant-shadow`** (camera full-body Pose ‚Üí raw WebGL2 GLSL metaball aura ‚Üí continuous choir) ‚Äî `queued`. **What if a 4-year-old stepped back and their whole body became a giant glowing creature whose SHAPE bends a warm choir ‚Äî arms-wide opens a big chord, crouch sinks to a low hum, a jump throws sparkles ‚Äî a body-sized theremin.** INPUT camera/MediaPipe PoseLandmarker (33 landmarks, EMA-smoothed) ¬∑ OUTPUT **raw WebGL2** full-screen quad, GLSL smooth-min SDF metaball field over all 33 joints (4 additive glow layers + procedural sparkle particles) ¬∑ TECHNIQUE pose-geometry‚Üíharmony (armSpan‚Üí#voices 1‚Üí7, verticalRise‚Üíregister, motionEnergy‚Üítremolo/shimmer/sparkle, openness‚Üíglow) ¬∑ VIBE awe/embodied warm-glowing-giant. 8-voice pool (sine+3rd+detune), `setTargetAtTime` crossfades, master `gain‚Üílowpass8k‚ÜíDynamicsCompressor(‚àí10/20:1)`; ghost-giant auto-demo (synthetic oscillating body) runs with zero permission, resumes after 4 s no-detection; `text-rose-300` camera-denied notice. MediaPipe via jsDelivr CDN module-inject (no npm dep), pattern adapted from `524`. Named refs **Myron Krueger *Videoplace* (1974)**, **Leon Theremin**, **wayang kulit**. **Ambition 3/5** (#2 ‚â•3 subsystems [Pose track + pose‚Üíharmony + WebGL2 metaballs + choir engine = 4] + #3 named refs; #1 contestable ‚Äî Pose/body used at 419/268/295, the fresh element is GLSL-metaball-body-as-choir, discounts cleanly; #5 not claimed). **Why it didn't win (cycle 404):** the biggest *visual/technical* swing of the three (and strongest "wow"), but the **riskiest for the hands-free 06:30 glance** ‚Äî PoseLandmarker-lite from CDN + a 33-joint metaball SDF loop in the fragment shader is the heaviest real-iPad perf/CDN/camera-permission risk, and full-body Pose is the most-used kids input lane (autopilot risk). **Resurrect** if Karel wants the big-glowing-giant register, or as the cycle that finally proves the GLSL-metaball-body ‚Äî verify shader perf on a real iPad first; consider a Canvas2D silhouette fallback. Built files (removed): `page.tsx` (781), `audio.ts` (189), `README.md`.
- **`555-kids-echo-canyon`** (voice/mic ‚Üí SVG canyon ‚Üí canon/round) ‚Äî `queued`. **What if a 4-year-old sang a phrase into a glowing canyon and friendly echo-creatures bounced it back as a staggered 4-voice ROUND ‚Äî each echo further away, transposed +3rd/+5th/+octave ‚Äî so one "la-la-la" self-harmonizes.** INPUT mic/getUserMedia ‚Üí AnalyserNode ‚Üí YIN-style autocorrelation (180‚Äì1200 Hz, clarity 0.38, babble fallback) ‚Üí C-pentatonic snap; phrase commit on ~900 ms silence (‚â§10 notes) ¬∑ OUTPUT **SVG** (5 depth-layered canyon ellipses + 4 receding blob-creatures with eyes/mouths, per-phrase pitch-contour path; rAF direct-attribute mutation, zero per-frame React; `feGaussianBlur` glow) ¬∑ TECHNIQUE pitch-capture‚Üícanon scheduler (4 voices, look-ahead 120 ms / 25 ms tick, transpose [0,+4,+7,+12], stereo-pan [0,‚àí.55,+.55,0], distance-attenuated gain, per-voice feedback-delay canyon reverb) ¬∑ VIBE playful social call-and-response warm canyon. Master `gain‚Üílowpass8k‚ÜíDynamicsCompressor(‚àí10/20:1)`; auto-demo round plays from frame one, ducks when the child sings, resumes after silence; `text-rose-300` mic-denied notice. **Explicitly distinct** from `549-kids-song-friend` (NO cross-day memory ‚Äî immediate, in-the-moment) and `142-kids-echo-canon` (bigger: a spatial receding canyon with a real staggered round + stereo/distance voices). Named refs the **round/canon form** (*Fr√®re Jacques* / Pachelbel), **alpine echo**, **Pauline Oliveros *Deep Listening***. **Ambition 3/5** (#2 ‚â•3 subsystems + #3 named refs; #1 not claimed ‚Äî echo/canon + voice‚Üípitch both have priors; #5 not claimed). **Why it didn't win (cycle 404):** the most *musically refined* and most reliable (SVG + voice), but the **least fresh relative to recent cycles** ‚Äî the immediately-prior kids piece (549) was also voice/mic, so a voice winner would repeat the input family back-to-back, and echo-canon has a prior (142). **Resurrect** when voice/mic is scarce again, or fold its canyon-canon scheduler into a different input (e.g. a hummed round that 549's friend learns). Built files (removed): `page.tsx` (641), `audio.ts`, `pitch.ts`, `README.md`.

## SPINE: Xenharmonic Drone (started Cycle 403 ‚Äî the jury 2026-06-12's named 4/5 chase: "538 capped at 3 only because it's a one-off ‚Äî make it a spine and it's the first 4")
**The thread:** *the just-intonation / Bohlen‚ÄìPierce harmonic lattice stops being something you TAP and becomes a living place ‚Äî a self-drifting La Monte Young "Dream House" drone that wanders the Tonnetz via the comma pump and never returns home.* Extends `538-xenharmonic-lattice` (cycle 1, a static SVG Tonnetz you click). **Cycle 2 (403, SHIPPED):** `552-dream-house` ‚Äî a fully **autonomous long-form (>5 min, ~14-min arc) generative drone pilgrimage**: a walker steps the lattice every 4‚Äì8 s biased to unvisited nodes, sustaining the nearest 2‚Äì6 nodes as a slow-crossfading pure-ratio chord; an 8-phase arc (gathering‚Üíascending‚Üíflowering‚Üítritave‚Üíradiance‚Üídissolving‚Üíreceding‚Üístillness) drives density (2‚Üî6 voices), a drifting master lowpass (700‚Üî4800 Hz), a ¬±0.4-octave register breath, and a 4-stop tuning migration (5-JI‚ÜíB‚ÄìP‚Üí19-EDO‚Üíback); the syntonic comma (81/80) guarantees the pitch never recloses; SVG Tonnetz with a glowing comet-trail + accumulating visited-ghost dots makes the journey visible; HUD shows elapsed/phase. Zero permission, runs itself (ideal 06:30 glance); tap = optional "exhale" to bloom a fresh region. Master `gain ‚Üí drifting lowpass ‚Üí DynamicsCompressor(20:1, ‚àí10 dBFS) ‚Üí destination`; per-voice gain `0.55/voiceCount`, 8-voice hard cap. Ambition **3/5** (#2 ‚â•3 subsystems [walker + crossfading drone synth + long-form arc engine + SVG render = 4] + #3 named refs [La Monte Young & Zazeela *Dream House* / √âliane Radigue / Euler Tonnetz / Bohlen‚ÄìPierce / Partch / syntonic comma] + #4 spine cycle 2 ‚Äî the jury's exact ask). **#1 the contestable case** ‚Äî *first deliberate long-form (>5min) stateful evolving piece* is a counts-as-#1 in the manual, but 514/526 are arguably already long-form autonomous, so it discounts to 3/5 honestly. **#5 NOT claimed** ‚Äî arxiv cs.SD June 5‚Äì12 2026 had zero JI/microtonal/pitch papers (still server-ML); said plainly. **No API route** (pure client Web Audio + SVG; no mic/camera/network/secrets). **Cycle 3 (planned):** fold in the banked steering layers below so the pilgrimage is *steerable but still autonomous by default* (grab-the-wheel), and/or bind a genuinely <14-day xenharmonic/JI finding for the long-missing #5.

### Banked ¬ß403 (DEEP non-winners ‚Äî TEXT seeds, folders removed; renumber on revival, max folder now 552):
- **`550-xenharmonic-pilgrimage`** (VOICE-steered) ‚Äî `queued`. Same autonomous drone-pilgrimage core, plus a "üé§ Sing to steer" layer: `getUserMedia` ‚Üí `AnalyserNode` ‚Üí autocorrelation pitch detector; the sung pitch pulls the walker toward the nearest JI node so you can *lead* the pilgrimage by gliding your voice, releasing back to free drift on silence. INPUT voice/mic (off-glass) ¬∑ OUTPUT SVG Tonnetz + comet-trail ¬∑ TECHNIQUE pitch-detection‚Üílattice-pull + sustained pure-ratio drone ¬∑ VIBE tune-yourself-into-the-drift. *Promising:* the most *musical/embodied* of the three ‚Äî "tune your own voice into pure ratios" is a lovely cross-modal hook; the right cycle-3 grab-the-wheel layer to port onto 552. *Caveat that lost it:* the builder honestly flagged the autocorrelation detector as basic (octave-confusion + noise-floor issues on a real voice), so the steering is the least reliable; and 552's pure-autonomous form is more glance-robust + the cleaner long-form #1-attempt. Built files (removed): `page.tsx` (837 lines), `audio.ts`, `tuning.ts`, `README.md`.
- **`551-tilt-pilgrimage`** (TILT-steered) ‚Äî `queued`. Same core, plus "üì± Tilt to wander": `DeviceOrientationEvent` (with the iOS 13+ `requestPermission()` flow) maps `gamma`‚Üífifths-axis bias and `beta`‚Üíthirds-axis bias so you *lean your body through* the lattice; a draggable on-screen tilt-pad is the desktop/no-sensor fallback; held level it free-drifts. INPUT device-tilt (off-glass) + pad fallback ¬∑ OUTPUT SVG Tonnetz + comet-trail + a level/horizon indicator ¬∑ TECHNIQUE orientation‚Üílattice-drift-bias + sustained pure-ratio drone ¬∑ VIBE lean-into-the-harmony. *Promising:* the most *physical* embodiment (you tilt through fifths and thirds); the desktop tilt-pad makes it laptop-usable too ‚Äî a strong second cycle-3 steering axis alongside 550's voice. *Caveat that lost it:* tilt needs the iOS permission dance + a real device to shine (Karel's 06:30 glance would mostly see the pad/autonomous), and it's a steering layer on the same core rather than a bigger swing than 552's long-form arc. Built files (removed): `page.tsx` (775 lines), `audio.ts`, `tuning.ts`, `README.md`.

## SPINE: The Living Earth (started Cycle 373 ‚Äî a SECOND live multi-cycle spine, jury 2026-06-09's "restart a multi-cycle commitment" + "extend 437's live-data lineage, nobody is")
**The thread:** *play the living Earth from a live planetary data stream, tuned to a non-Western / expressive system, on a luminous data-world.* Extends `437-wiki-pulse` (the lab's only live-external-data piece ‚Äî jury called it "the freshest concept" and flagged it unextended) from human-activity into the **geophysical/planetary** domain, and breaks the recent adult **latent-image monoculture** (AI-image OUT was count-banned at 4√ó). Born from RESEARCH ¬ß373 (real-time stream sonification is going *infinite/fatigue-resistant* [arXiv 2605.21874, May 21 2026] and *non-Western-tuned* [arXiv 2602.14560, gamelan, Feb 2026]). **Cycle 1 (373, SHIPPED):** `463-terra-gamelan` ‚Äî USGS live earthquakes ‚Üí **gamelan-slendro/pelog-tuned** bell-globe (three.js Earth; depth‚Üípitch, mag‚Üíloudness, lon‚Üípan; 24h stateful accumulation + an energy-driven drone bed). Ambition 3/5 (#2+#3+#4). **Cycle 2 (375, SHIPPED):** `471-helios-orbit` ‚Äî NOAA **live space-weather** (solar-wind plasma + IMF Bz + planetary Kp) conducts an **EDM build-and-drop** seen from orbit: a geomagnetic storm is the DROP (sub-bass + 4-on-the-floor kick + harmony **resolves minor‚Üímajor** + auroral ovals igniting around the IGRF poles + magnetosphere bloom), on a dark Earth that carries forward `463`'s Fibonacci-dot-globe. Ambition **4/5** (#2+#3+#4+#5 ‚Äî the EDM arc cites the ¬ß373 arXiv 2605.21874 anchor, within 14 days). Won a **DEEP** 3-renderer fire; the two non-winners (`469-helios-aurora` fbm sky-curtain, `470-helios-raymarch` volumetric raymarch) were **deliberately NOT banked** ‚Äî a grep-audit found they duplicate the register of the loved `262-aurora-particle`‚ù§Ô∏è (whose own banked siblings are `aurora-raymarch`/`aurora-drone-field`); `471`'s orbital globe was the one approach distinct from 262 AND the one that continues this spine. *(The ¬ß373 banked `464-helios-aurora`/`465-terra-pulse` EDM siblings are now **superseded** by `471` ‚Äî same concept, shipped; leave as historical, do not resurrect as-is.)* **Cycle 3 (planned):** add a second live planetary layer (space weather ‚äï seismic on ONE Earth) and polish; OR a genuinely-fresh space-weather angle 262 does NOT cover ‚Äî sonify the **far-upstream CME forecast** (arXiv 2508.13892, Solar Orbiter predicts a storm hours ahead) so the music *anticipates* a drop before it reaches Earth. **No API route needed** anywhere in this spine ‚Äî all sources are public CORS-enabled, read-only, no auth, no secrets; each piece has a synthetic fallback so it demos with no network.

## SPINE: The Resonant Room (started Cycle 377 ‚Äî the adult #4 spine; explored across temperatures)
**The thread:** *a sympathetic resonant room ‚Äî an instrument played INTO a tuned acoustic space that rings back ‚Äî explored from warm to cold.* **Cycle 1 (377, SHIPPED):** `475-resonant-room` ‚Äî Karel's *Welcome Home* piano into a **key-tuned N=8 Householder-matrix Feedback Delay Network** (Jot / Stautner‚ÄìPuckette) that rings IN KEY and **resolves to the tonic on release** (the warm partner). **Cycle 2 (379, SHIPPED):** `483-two-rooms-apart` ‚Äî the *opposite temperature*: a **live-MIDI**-played room split into **two coupled resonator banks tuned a tritone apart** that beat against each other (no tonic, suspended dissonance), with a **real-time Plomp‚ÄìLevelt/Sethares roughness** readout; **resolution is the player's act** ‚Äî drag detune‚Üíunison and the beating fuses. Ambition **4/5** (#1 first-Web-MIDI-INPUT + #2 + #3 + #4; #5 not claimed ‚Äî refs foundational). Won a DEEP 3-way fire; the jury (2026-06-10) explicitly asked to *deepen a spine* (not grab a 5th DSP primitive) ‚Äî this is an *extension*, one room ‚Üí two. **Cycle 3 (381, SHIPPED):** `486-third-room` ‚Äî adds a THIRD room a **just-fifth (702 ¬¢ = 3:2)** above Room A (its partials lock onto A's, so A‚ÜîC is consonant) as a **bridge**: blend C in, find the **pivot note** where all three rooms momentarily agree, then collapse the tritone ‚Äî *negotiate* the dissonance to rest rather than just collapsing it. Folds in BOTH banked cycle-3 ideas at once ‚Äî the **steepest-descent pivot-note suggestion engine** (from `481`, glows the notes that most lower three-room roughness) AND the **WebGPU compute vorticity field** (from `482`, the starved renderer; roughness ‚Üí per-room vorticity, Canvas2D fallback) ‚Äî plus the new third-room mechanic (implements `483`'s own documented cycle-3 idea). Ambition **4/5** (#2 5 subsystems + #3 refs + #4 spine cycle 3 ‚Äî the truest *extension*: one‚Üítwo‚Üíthree rooms; #1/#5 honestly not claimed). Hits jury (2026-06-10) #1 (tension resolved *through action*) + #2 (deepen, don't grab a primitive) + #3 (ban three.js, pick the starved WebGPU). Won a DEEP 3-way fire (resolution-mechanic axis). **Cycle 4 (planned, pre-scoped from this fire's banked siblings):** (a) port `485`'s **whisper panel** ‚Äî a fixed "Notes that ease the tension ‚Üí" list with predicted %-improvement + a derived gradient arrow on the detune slider (the most legible 06:30 read); (b) add `487`'s **roughness landscape + shared-control "descend toward rest" autopilot** as a second resolution axis (feel the slope of the tritone‚Üífifth negotiation; keep it opt-in/overridable so it never becomes a pre-baked release). **No API route** ‚Äî pure client-side Web Audio + Web MIDI + WebGPU/Canvas2D; no secrets.

### Banked ¬ß381 (DEEP non-winners ‚Äî TEXT seeds, folders removed; renumber on revival, max folder now 486):
- **`485-resolving-room`** (whisper-panel note-hint engine) ‚Äî `queued`. The two cycle-2 tritone rooms unchanged, but the centerpiece is the cleanest realization of the steepest-descent *partner*: for every candidate pitch C3‚ÄìC6 it analytically estimates the marginal Plomp‚ÄìLevelt roughness if that note were added, ranks by most-negative delta, and renders the top 3 as emerald keyboard glows + a live "Notes that ease the tension ‚Üí" panel with predicted %-improvement; a finite-difference detune gradient (derived, not hardcoded) drives a direction arrow on the slider. INPUT live-MIDI/on-screen/QWERTY ¬∑ OUTPUT WebGPU compute vorticity field (curl-noise, ~4000 particles, Canvas2D fallback) ¬∑ TECHNIQUE two-room roughness + steepest-descent note suggestion ¬∑ VIBE cold tension you resolve by following a whisper. *Promising:* the most legible hint UX of the fire ‚Äî the explicit named-notes panel reads instantly at a 06:30 phone glance; port it onto `486` as cycle 4. *Caveat that lost it:* the smallest conceptual step (a hint layer bolted onto the existing two rooms) vs. `486`'s structural third-room extension. Built files (removed): `page.tsx` (1625 lines), `README.md`.
- **`487-guided-descent`** (roughness landscape + shared-control autopilot) ‚Äî `queued`. The two cycle-2 rooms, but the roughness is sampled analytically at 120 points across the 0‚Äì600 ¬¢ detune axis and drawn as a live **landscape curve** (rose‚Üíemerald fill) with the current detune as a violet ball and an emerald dashed arrow to the nearest valley; a "Descend toward rest" **autopilot** walks ‚àí‚àároughness at ¬±15 ¬¢/step (80 ms) that the player can grab and override at any moment (shared control). INPUT live-MIDI/on-screen/QWERTY + the landscape ball ¬∑ OUTPUT WebGPU compute vorticity field (Canvas2D fallback) ¬∑ TECHNIQUE two-room roughness + visible gradient-descent landscape + note hints ¬∑ VIBE feel-the-slope-home. *Promising:* makes the steepest-descent idea *playable* and tangible ‚Äî add it to `486` as a second resolution axis (feel the tritone‚Üífifth negotiation's slope). *Caveat that lost it:* the auto-descent risks reading as a pre-baked release (the jury's #1 explicitly wants the *listener* to resolve through action), and the landscape-vs-detune curve leans clinical/Ikeda; keep any port opt-in + overridable. Built files (removed): `page.tsx` (1608 lines), `README.md`.

### Banked ¬ß379 (DEEP non-winners ‚Äî TEXT seeds, folders removed; renumber on revival, max folder now 483):

A single bank of 10 high-Q biquads tuned to the **whole-tone scale** (no tonic, no leading tone, no perfect fifth) so the sympathetic ring is permanently ambiguous; resolution via consonant play OR a "let it breathe" damper. INPUT live-MIDI/on-screen/QWERTY ¬∑ OUTPUT Canvas2D standing-wave lattice ¬∑ TECHNIQUE whole-tone resonator room + real-time Plomp‚ÄìLevelt/Sethares roughness ¬∑ VIBE cold suspended ambiguity. *Promising:* the cleanest single-room ambiguity model; its **steepest-descent dissonance-gradient hint engine** (highlight the 2‚Äì3 notes that most lower roughness) is the strongest cycle-3 *interaction* idea ‚Äî port onto `483`. *Caveat that lost it:* whole-tone tuning gives few consonant intervals to *play* toward, so its resolve-through-action leans on a discrete damper button rather than a continuous embodied gesture. INPUT live-MIDI/on-screen/QWERTY ¬∑ OUTPUT **WebGPU** compute field (starved renderer, jury flagged 1√ó) ¬∑ TECHNIQUE whole-tone resonator room + roughness-driven GPU vorticity ¬∑ VIBE cold turbulent dissonance settling to laminar. *Promising:* the lab's **starved WebGPU path** ‚Äî bank as `483`'s cycle-3 *renderer upgrade*. *Caveat that lost it:* WebGPU device fragility on Karel's phone-review workflow (he'd likely see the Canvas2D fallback anyway), and its resolve-mechanic was no stronger than `481`'s. Built files (removed): `page.tsx` (1322 lines), `README.md`.

### `464-helios-aurora` ‚Äî NOAA **space-weather** (live solar wind + Kp planetary index) ‚Üí an evolving aurora drone with an **EDM build-and-drop** arc keyed to geomagnetic storms (Kp‚â•5 = the drop, resolving warm). INPUT live-NOAA-SWPC-data ¬∑ OUTPUT three.js full-screen additive **fbm aurora-curtain shader** + starfield ¬∑ TECHNIQUE real-time space-weather sonification + EDM anticipation arc (solar-wind speed‚Üífilter/brightness, density‚Üíshimmer, IMF Bz-negative‚Üíharmonic tension, Kp‚Üímaster anticipation; build‚Üíriser/quickening pulse, storm‚Üísub-bass swell + minor‚Üímajor resolve + aurora bloom) ¬∑ VIBE warm-cosmic space-observatory. Sources: `services.swpc.noaa.gov` plasma-5-minute / mag-5-minute / planetary-k-index JSON, 60s poll + exponential glide so it breathes continuously; synthetic random-walk-plus-scripted-storm fallback (auto-storm ~22s in) + a "simulate a storm now" button. The strongest **cycle-2** candidate: it's the cleanest realization of the EDM-arc research AND the most distinct DATA SOURCE [space weather, not seismic], so cycle 2 = put this aurora/storm arc on the globe or as a second planetary layer. ### `465-terra-pulse` ‚Äî the SAME USGS seismic source, structured as a long-form **EDM build-and-drop journey** where the planet's *aggregate* energy is the conductor (not per-quake bells). ## SPINE: The Wave Field (kids) (started Cycle 378 ‚Äî a kids #4 spine paralleling the adult Resonant Room; physical-modeled acoustic fields you tap, see, AND hear)
**The thread:** *a real solved 2-D wave field (FDTD digital-waveguide mesh, Van Duyne‚ÄìSmith ICMC 1993) is the instrument ‚Äî the audio and the visual are the same field ‚Äî grown across cycles from one pond to many coupled ponds.* **Cycle 1 (378, SHIPPED):** `478-kids-wave-pond` ‚Äî tap ONE luminous pond, watch real waves spread/reflect/interfere, hear that solved field (three.js render; FDTD AudioWorklet). **Cycle 2 (382, SHIPPED):** `489-kids-pond-pair` ‚Äî the jury (2026-06-10 #2) named extension: **TWO coupled wave fields** joined by a sympathetic channel (`K=0.08` always-on whisper: a ripple in pond A excites pond B), plus a tangible floating **lily pad carrier** that bobs on the waves, drifts to the channel, and physically *carries energy across* to ring the other pond ‚Äî a visible sound-messenger for a 4yo. Dual pentatonic registers (A low C3-pentatonic, B high C4-pentatonic) ‚Üí always harmonize. Renderer moved OFF the jury-banned three.js ‚Üí **Canvas2D** (dodges three.js + the WebGL2 4√ó count-ban). Ambition 3/5 (#2 two FDTD solvers + cross-pond coupling + carrier physics + pickup synth ‚â•3 ¬∑ #3 Van Duyne‚ÄìSmith 1993 + sympathetic coupling ¬∑ #4 spine cycle 2 ‚Äî an *extension*, not a 5th primitive; #1/#5 honestly not claimed). Won a DEEP 3-builder fire (coupling-mechanic axis). **Cycle 3 (planned, pre-scoped from this fire's banks):** add `488`'s child-controllable coupling vine and/or its WebGPU-compute FDTD path; lean into `487`'s explicit cooperative two-kids framing.

### Banked ¬ß382 (DEEP non-winners ‚Äî TEXT seeds, folders removed; renumber on revival, max folder now 489):
- **`488-kids-echo-ponds`** (coupling-is-the-toy + WebGPU compute) ‚Äî `queued`. Same two coupled FDTD ponds, but the **coupling constant K (0‚Äì0.25) is a draggable glowing vine/bridge** the child pulls wide (a splash floods across) or pinches shut (each pond sings alone) ‚Äî the *coupling itself* is the mechanic. Renderer = **WebGPU compute** FDTD (WGSL storage-buffer ping-pong, `@workgroup_size(8,8)`) with a CPU **Canvas2D fallback** (identical sim). INPUT multi-touch + draggable bridge ¬∑ OUTPUT WebGPU compute (Canvas2D fallback) ‚Äî the lab's starved GPU-compute lane ¬∑ TECHNIQUE two coupled FDTD fields + child-set sympathetic coupling ¬∑ VIBE warm coupled water you tune. *Promising:* the sharpest answer to "the coupling is the mechanic you play" + the starved-renderer diversity prize. *Caveat that lost it:* a coupling-strength slider is abstract for a 4yo (vs. 489's concrete visible carrier), WebGPU likely falls back to Canvas2D on Karel's review phone anyway, and the GPU path has a documented half-step coupling lag; 486 also just refreshed WebGPU (no longer 0√ó). Build-clean (tsc + eslint zero-warning per builder). Built files (removed): `page.tsx` (~1250 lines), `README.md`.
- **`487-kids-two-ponds`** (clean cooperative core) ‚Äî `queued`. Two coupled FDTD ponds joined by a fixed glowing channel (`K=0.12`); a splash in A visibly/audibly wakes B; explicit **"two kids each claim a pond"** cooperative framing (the KIDS.md social-bonding gap). Dual-palette `ImageData` Canvas2D render (pond A navy‚Üícyan, pond B violet‚Üírose); channel strip brightens with cross-edge energy. INPUT multi-touch (two hands) ¬∑ OUTPUT Canvas2D ImageData height-map ¬∑ TECHNIQUE two coupled FDTD fields + fixed sympathetic channel ¬∑ VIBE warm cooperative water. *Promising:* the cleanest, lowest-risk cooperative two-player realization + a tidy dual-palette renderer. *Caveat that lost it:* the coupling is fixed (nothing to play with) ‚Üí lowest surprise of the three; 489's carrier object and 488's draggable vine both give the child more to *do*. Built files (removed): `page.tsx` (860 lines), `README.md`.

## Banked from Cycle 374 (WIDE **kids** fire ‚Äî three embodied registers deliberately fired OFF the GPU-physics-sim + JI-bell kids monoculture [451 soft-body / 456 ferrofluid / 460 collision were the last 3 kids cycles]; winner shipped = `468-kids-bottle-flute` [the lab's FIRST physically-modeled WIND instrument ‚Äî breath-excited digital-waveguide bore]. Both siblings are complete, **build-reviewed** real implementations [own folder, pure client-side, no API route, no `_shared` imports, brick-wall limiter, 3-s hands-free auto-demo, graceful sensor-denied fallback]; folders removed per the no-half-built-folders rule, banked here as ready-to-resurrect TEXT. **Numbered as built (466/467) ‚Äî renumber on revival; max folder now 468.**)

### `466-kids-sing-kite` ‚Äî a 4-year-old SINGS to fly a paper kite along a scrolling melody contour; matching the line makes the kite glow + harmonize, and each ~24s loop ends with the kite "singing your song back" (a gift-replay of the child's matched notes). INPUT mic **pitch** (YIN/CMND autocorrelation, 120‚Äì600 Hz, octave-collapse to D3‚ÄìD4) ¬∑ OUTPUT **inline-SVG** matte cut-paper sky (kite/clouds/contour-path/sparkles via rAF DOM-attr writes ‚Äî dodges both the jury-banned Canvas2D AND the WebGL-physics monoculture) ¬∑ TECHNIQUE continuous-pitch-as-flight-control + D-major diatonic snap + delayed melodic replay ¬∑ VIBE warm dusk sky that **resolves** (D-major, lands on tonic ‚Äî not a pentatonic lullaby). Always-on D3+A3+D4 drone; brick-wall limiter; no-mic ‚Üí synthetic-voice auto-demo sings the contour itself hands-free. Refs **Singing Carrots / SingSage** (sing-to-the-line browser pitch-training) ¬∑ **de Cheveign√© & Kawahara, YIN** (JASA 2002) ¬∑ **Chris Wilson, cwilso/PitchDetect**. Built files: `page.tsx` (971 lines), `README.md`. *(banked sibling ‚Äî ambition ~2/5 [#2 ‚â•3 subsystems: pitch-detect + scrolling-contour engine + SVG render + harmony/drone synth ¬∑ #3 named-refs]. **#1 NOT cleanly claimable** ‚Äî the lab already has voice-pitch‚Üíoutput [`158-kids-hum-paint`, `280-kids-echo-canyon`, `455-kids-sing-cymatics`, `413-kids-mouth-mirror`]; continuous-pitch-as-game-control is a fresh *application*, not a new primitive. Didn't win: the "sing onto the target line" mechanic is well-trodden, and child-voice pitch tracking is the least-certain subsystem [breathy/uncertain 4yo intonation vs CMND<0.20 + 46ms buffer latency]. **Strong bank ‚Äî the lab's purest embodied-voice kids toy** [the jury's "sing, don't poke"]; resurrect by de-risking child-voice [lower CMND threshold, shorter buffer] and adding cubic-bezier contour + a delay reverb.)*

### `467-kids-shadow-band` ‚Äî stand back and WAVE your whole body; a camera **motion-energy** field conducts a 3-zone band [bells / marimba / xylophone] and your movement explodes as a living glow of additive particles. INPUT **camera frame-differencing** (hidden 32√ó24 canvas, per-cell luminance-diff ‚Üí per-zone motion energy, analysis-only ‚Äî no ML, no identity, never recorded) ¬∑ OUTPUT **three.js** additive-blended particle aura (1800 sprites, spring-home + energy-burst ‚Äî a motion-driven particle field, explicitly NOT a physics sim) ¬∑ TECHNIQUE zonal motion-energy ‚Üí instrument activation + particle emission (more motion = louder/brighter/faster; all zones = a resolving I-chord arpeggio) ¬∑ VIBE joyful get-off-the-couch stage, warm I‚ÄìIV‚ÄìV‚ÄìI that **resolves**. Brick-wall limiter; no-camera ‚Üí roaming-Gaussian-blob auto-demo + WebGL-unavailable notice. Refs **Myron Krueger, *Videoplace*** (1974) ¬∑ **Golan Levin & Zach Lieberman, *Messa di Voce*** (2003) ¬∑ **Davis & Bobick, Motion History Image** (MIT 1997). Built files: `page.tsx` (767 lines), `README.md`. *(banked sibling ‚Äî ambition ~2/5 [#2 ‚â•3 subsystems: frame-diff analysis + three.js particle render + I‚ÄìIV‚ÄìV band ¬∑ #3 named-refs]. Didn't win: its OUTPUT is **three.js** ‚Äî the very WebGL-render axis at 5√ó in the trailing window [the monoculture this fire set out to break], so it dodges the GPU-**physics** rut but not the GPU-**render** one; and lighting-sensitive frame-diff is a real 06:30 risk. **Strong bank ‚Äî the anti-screen-bias, get-up-and-move lane** the jury keeps asking for; resurrect when camera INPUT is the diversity pick and pair with a non-WebGL OUTPUT, or extend with optical-flow direction [not just energy] so a *swipe* gesture conducts.)*

## SPINE: The Latent Piano Room (started Cycle 367 ‚Äî the jury's #1 ask, restarting the dead-since-414 multi-cycle axis)
**The thread:** run the closed audio‚Üíimage‚Üíaudio loop (born in loved `441-latent-listening-room`) on Karel's **actual recorded piano**, deepening over multiple cycles. **Cycle 1 (367, SHIPPED):** `448-piano-phrase-painter` ‚Äî musical-structure listening (onset/phrase/chroma/dynamics) drives *when & what* is dreamed; image color bends the real playback back. **Cycle 2 (369, SHIPPED):** `454-piano-caption-loom` ‚Äî the single template-prompt is replaced by an **iterative multi-agent caption refinement loom**: specialist proposers (scene/palette/motion/style) draft a caption, a **Critic agent** scores it against the music's valence-arousal target and the proposers **revise** over 3 visible rounds (Draft‚ÜíRevised‚ÜíFinal, confidence rising, changed words highlighted) ‚Äî the prompt visibly *improves* before the image regenerates. Realizes arXiv 2512.23320 (music-derived captions + VA emotion) + the iterative-refinement loop of T2I-Copilot/ImAgent (RESEARCH ¬ß369), and mirrors the lab's own planner‚Üíbuilders‚Üícritic orchestration. **Cycle 3 (371, SHIPPED):** `457-piano-image-duet` ‚Äî the *other* half of the loop finally closes: the dreamed image no longer only *filters* the piano, it **re-composes** it. A glowing scan-line sweeps the dreamed image left‚Üíright reading it as a **spectral score** (image-as-spectrogram): each column's vertical brightness profile drives a 24-partial additive bank, Y‚Üífrequency **quantized to the detected key** (always consonant/warm ‚Äî resolves on purpose), brightness‚Üíamplitude, producing a shimmer-choir voice that **duets** Karel's real piano. Realizes **Art2Mus** (arXiv 2602.17599, Feb 2026 ‚Äî direct visual‚Üímusic conditioning, no image‚Üítext; RESEARCH ¬ß371) in the **Xenakis UPIC / MetaSynth** image-as-score lineage. The scan-line makes "the image is singing the piano" *visible* ‚Äî the spine's legible/instructional lane. **Cycle 4 (planned, ~373):** resurrect a sibling below (the **improviser** counter-melody or the **granular cloud**), or fold them into 457 so the duet has melody + texture + spectral layers; and/or fold in `452`'s visible council HUD / `453`'s affect-atlas as a viz layer. **Open dependency:** a real **Welcome Home** track ID (the runtime `journey_paths` IDs aren't in the static repo ‚Äî `448`/`454` default to the Ghost recording `549fc519‚Ä¶`); the same ask blocks `424-welcome-erosion`. Karel: paste a WH recording ID and the spine runs on the album.

## Banked from Cycle 372 (WIDE **kids** fire ‚Äî three genuinely-unrelated grep-clean fresh registers, the named tool against the jury's "learned to go deep, forgot how to go weird"; winner shipped = `460-kids-ball-pit` [the lab's FIRST many-body collision physics]. Both siblings are complete, **build-reviewed** real implementations [own folder, pure client-side, no API route, no `_shared` imports, brick-wall limiter, 4-s hands-free auto-demo, WebGL fallback notice]; folders removed per the no-half-built-folders rule, banked here as ready-to-resurrect TEXT. **Numbered as built (461/462) ‚Äî renumber on revival; max folder now 460.**)

### `461-kids-storm-conductor` ‚Äî tap the storm sky and a **branching bolt of lightning SINGS as it cracks**. INPUT touch (tap / hold-to-grow / drag) ¬∑ OUTPUT WebGL2 additive-bloom line-tree (over a Canvas2D star/cloud backdrop) ¬∑ TECHNIQUE **fractal lightning via recursive random-midpoint-displacement** (`buildBolt()`: subdivide cloud‚Üítap segment, jitter midpoints ‚ä• by length√óroughness‚âà0.45, recurse depth 4‚Äì7; spawn 3‚Äì8 forks from interior points; two-layer white-hot-core + soft-blue-glow render) ¬∑ VIBE dramatic-but-warm storm. Audio: main channel a band-passed sawtooth gliss 180‚Üí2000 Hz ("crack"), each branch **tip** a staggered FM bell on a D-major JI hexachord (D E F‚ôØ A B C‚ôØ ‚Äî multiple taps = consonant stacked chords), warm low-passed-noise thunder ~120 ms later, continuous pink-noise + 38 Hz sub storm bed. Refs **Drilian "How to generate shockingly good 2D lightning effects"** (midpoint-displacement) + **Prusinkiewicz & Lindenmayer, *The Algorithmic Beauty of Plants* (1990)** (L-system branching). *(banked sibling ‚Äî ambition ~3/5 [#1 fractal-lightning is a grep-clean lab-first viz technique + #2 ‚â•3 subsystems + #3 named-ref]. Didn't win: lightning effects are well-trodden creative-coding ground [lower surprise than a singing collision-pit], and it flags a real 06:30 risk ‚Äî `gl.lineWidth` is clamped to 1 px on many GPU drivers, so the bolts can render thin; resurrect by switching to triangle-strip quads for the channel so width is driver-independent. Near-ship; the dramatic-weather lane.)*

### `462-kids-gear-garden` ‚Äî spin one cog and a **garden of meshing gears turns; the gear tooth-RATIOS ARE the harmony**. INPUT circular touch-drag (spin) + tap-to-add-gear ¬∑ OUTPUT WebGL procedural cogwheels (`geargl.ts`, GLSL ES 1.00, WebGL1/2-compatible) + Canvas2D glow/label overlay ¬∑ TECHNIQUE **meshing gear-train kinematic solver** (BFS from driver, œâ_child = ‚àíœâ_parent √ó teeth_parent/teeth_child, exponential angular friction) where **integer tooth ratios map directly to just-intonation frequency ratios** (24:16 = 3:2 fifth, 8:16 = 1:2 octave, 20:16 = 5:4 third); spin faster ‚Üí lowpass cutoff rises ‚Üí brighter chord. Per-gear triangle-osc + lowpass, Karplus-ish pluck on add, soft C2-E2-G2 drone so it's never silent, master compressor/limiter. Refs **Harry Partch *Genesis of a Music*** (small-integer ratios = consonance) + mechanical **music-box/barrel-organ** lineage + **Antikythera mechanism** (gears as ratio machines). *(banked sibling ‚Äî ambition ~2‚Äì3/5 [#2 ‚â•3 subsystems + #3 named-ref; #1 gear-ratio-as-harmony is a fresh framing but gears-as-instrument isn't a cleanly-novel primitive]. Didn't win: the most *contemplative* of the three [risk of reading as a quiet drone vs. the jury's loud/weird ask], and the "ratio = interval" payload is the subtlest to feel at age 4. The single most beautiful CONCEPT of the fire ‚Äî the machine's structure literally IS the chord; resurrect as an adult/all-ages piece where the ratio-harmony pedagogy can breathe, or pair with a visible ratio-readout. Near-ship.)*

### `458-piano-image-improviser` ‚Äî the dreamed image **improvises a counter-melody**: `extractImageFeatures()` downsamples the image to 16√ó12 ‚Üí k-means-ish hue buckets ‚Üí diatonic scale degrees (in the detected key), brightness-gradient ‚Üí melodic contour, local-variance/edge-density ‚Üí note density/rhythm; a plucked triangle voice (8‚Äì12 dB under the piano) improvises in-key, synced to phrase boundaries, with Markov anti-repetition and **always resolves to tonic on the final note**. ### `459-piano-image-grains` ‚Äî the dreamed image becomes a **granular synthesis cloud**: every sampled pixel is a grain seed ‚Äî x‚Üístereo pan, y‚Üípitch quantized to the detected key, hue‚Üíwaveform timbre (warm hues‚Üísine, cool‚Üítriangle), brightness‚Üígrain gain; a lookahead scheduler (100ms horizon, ‚â§24 concurrent grains, Hann-window envelopes, limiter) layers a shimmering textural cloud whose character *is the image's character* over Karel's piano, density tracking RMS. Glowing grain particles drawn at the exact sampled image positions. Refs **Art2Mus** + **Curtis Roads** *Microsound* / **Xenakis** stochastic grain clouds. *(banked sibling ‚Äî the most texturally beautiful / atmospheric; the textural cycle-4 candidate. ~927-line page + 353-line grains.ts, build-reviewed. Risk to verify: grain-cloud reads as ambience more than a legible compositional act, and ‚â§24-grain scheduling perf on a low-end phone.)*

### `452-piano-agent-council` ‚Äî the VISIBLE multi-agent studio: a council of specialist agents each propose a caption clause and a Director weights+merges them, live on screen *(banked sibling ‚Äî the most legible "agentic studio" take; the strongest cycle-3 VIZ layer + the most on-brand for Karel's managed-agent-design work)*
Same real-piano chain as `454` (`<audio crossOrigin>` default `/api/audio/549fc519‚Ä¶`, file-drop, warm C‚ÄìAm‚ÄìF‚ÄìG7‚ÄìCmaj9 resolving synth fallback ‚Üí `createMediaElementSource` ‚Üí AnalyserNode 2048 ‚Üí reverb/compressor/master). `council.ts` (336 ln): five deterministic agents ‚Äî **Scene** (locale from onset-density+energy+phrase), **Palette** (color from modality/consonance/dominant-pc), **Motion** (turbulence from RMS+onset-rate/arousal), **Style** (Anadol/Akten base + Russell-quadrant emotion modifier), and a **Director** that computes valence (modality+consonance) √ó arousal (RMS+onset-density), places the music in Russell's circumplex (serene/joyous/tense/melancholic), and **weights** the merge (Palette up when valence is decisive, Motion up when arousal is high). The visible centerpiece: a labeled **card per agent** that lights up and shows its proposed clause + a **Russell circumplex dial** with a moving valence-arousal dot + the Director's caption assembling clause-by-clause. Image at phrase boundaries / onset clusters (‚â•7s, ‚â§1 in flight); image‚Üíaudio loopback (brightness‚Üílowpass+shimmer, warmth‚Üíreverb); 501‚Üísynthesized plasma/blob/particle field, council stays live, amber "no image key" badge. Built files: `page.tsx` (1079), `council.ts` (336), `analysis.ts` (243), `field.ts` (278), `synth.ts` (180), guarded `api/route.ts` (28), `README.md` (97). Refs: arXiv 2512.23320 ¬∑ T2I-Copilot 2507.20536 ¬∑ ImAgent 2511.11483 ¬∑ Russell 1980 ¬∑ Anadol/Akten ¬∑ Bello 2005. Ambition: #2 (‚â•3 subsystems) + #3 (named) + #4 (spine cycle-2 alt). **Why it lost:** its council is a **one-shot** weighted merge ‚Äî closer to splitting `448`'s template across agents than adding a new *capability*; `454`'s self-critiquing refinement loop is the genuinely new mechanic (and the jury explicitly penalizes re-presenting an existing mechanic). **Why resurrect:** it is the **most legible at a 06:30 phone glance** ("oh, agents are writing the prompt together") and the **most directly on-brand for Karel's oCFO agentic-design competency** ‚Äî the literal planner‚Üíspecialists‚Üídirector studio made visible. **Cycle-3 fold-in:** use 452's agent-card + circumplex-dial HUD as the visible layer ON the 454 loom (show WHICH agent proposed each clause AND watch it get critiqued).

### `453-piano-affect-atlas` ‚Äî the EMOTION-CARTOGRAPHY take: the music's live valence-arousal coordinate is a glowing comet tracing the Russell circumplex, and the dreamed image is the "view from" that emotional point *(banked sibling ‚Äî the cleanest VIZ-diversity dodge; the emotional-arc view)*
Same real-piano chain + analysis as `454`/`452`. Separate `affect.ts` (127 ln) computes smoothed valence (modality+consonance) and arousal (RMS+onset-density) ‚àà[-1,1]; `council.ts` (199 ln) runs specialist proposers whose **weights are set by the affect coordinate's quadrant** (near serene ‚Üí palette warm-soft + motion sparse; near tense ‚Üí motion-dominant turbulence) ‚Üí one emotion-aligned caption. The centerpiece: a 2D **valence√óarousal ATLAS** (four soft-zoned/labeled quadrants) with the music's current emotion as a glowing **comet + fading trail**, so you literally watch the *performance's emotional arc* over time; the dreamed image is the "view from" the current coordinate, regenerating on phrase boundary OR quadrant-crossing. Image‚Üíaudio loopback + 501‚Üísynthesized field as the siblings. Built files: `page.tsx` (1112), `affect.ts` (127), `council.ts` (199), `analysis.ts` (250), `field.ts` (320), `synth.ts` (183), guarded `api/route.ts` (28). **NOTE: its `README.md` was left EMPTY by the builder (0 bytes)** ‚Äî a deliverable miss; the code is complete and build-reviewed, but a revival must write the README. Ambition: #2 + #3 + #4 (spine cycle-2 alt). **Why it lost:** the affect-atlas is more a **HUD than a new capability** (the agents still one-shot a caption), and the empty README broke the exit contract; `454`'s iterative critique is the stronger mechanic. **Why resurrect:** the affect-comet is the most **visually distinct / fresh-modality** of the three (emotion as a place you move through) and the natural cycle-3 alternate VIZ ‚Äî pair the comet-on-circumplex with the 454 loom so you see BOTH where the music *is* emotionally AND the caption sharpening toward it. **Fix-first on revival:** write the README.

`<audio crossOrigin="anonymous">` (default `/api/audio/549fc519‚Ä¶`, or file-drop, or 12-TET synth fallback that resolves) ‚Üí `createMediaElementSource` ‚Üí AnalyserNode ‚Üí lowpass‚Üíshimmer‚Üíreverb‚Üícompressor‚Üímaster. Every frame: RMS energy (8 log bands) + spectral centroid + 12-bin chromagram ‚Üí dominant pitch-class (exp-smoothed). Every ~6.5 s: centroid‚Üípalette / energy‚Üídensity / pitch-class‚Üíhue over the Anadol base style ‚Üí guarded FAL `flux/schnell` (‚â§1 in flight, ‚â•7 s apart) ‚Üí ~2.5 s cross-fade + Ken-Burns + vignette. Loop-back: 8√ó6 `getImageData` avg color ‚Üí brightness opens lowpass + shimmer, warmth lengthens reverb ‚Äî on the real playback. CORS-taint guard: analyser all-zero >2.5 s ‚Üí auto-swap to synth + amber status. Built files: `page.tsx` (728), `audio.ts` (458), `field.ts` (259), guarded `api/route.ts`. Refs: Anadol *Unsupervised*/*Machine Hallucinations* ¬∑ Akten *Learning to See*. Ambition: #4 (spine cycle-1 alt) + #2 (‚â•3 subsystems) + #3 (named refs). **Why it lost:** it is the *least differentiated from `441`* ‚Äî re-presents the existing mechanic on a new source, which the jury explicitly penalizes ‚Äî though it is the safest hands-free read. **Why resurrect:** the cleanest, most reliable spine foundation; ship it if a cycle wants the rock-solid real-audio loop with no analysis risk. **Next-cycle deepening:** it IS the spine's stable base ‚Äî layer the cycle-2 multi-agent captioning onto this if `448`'s onset-timed regeneration proves too eager/sparse on real piano.

### `449-piano-latent-grains` ‚Äî the AGGRESSIVE loop-back: the dreamed image RE-COMPOSES the piano as granular re-synthesis *(banked sibling ‚Äî the strongest cycle-3 deepening candidate)*
A `GranularEngine` schedules Hann-windowed grains (‚â§24 voices ‚Üí DynamicsCompressor) off the **decoded** piano buffer (default `/api/audio/549fc519‚Ä¶` ‚Üí fetch signed URL ‚Üí `decodeAudioData`; or file-drop; or an `OfflineAudioContext`-rendered C‚ÜíAm‚ÜíF‚ÜíG‚ÜíC resolving phrase). The image *re-composes the grains*: **brightness ‚Üí grain density + read-head scatter**, **warmth ‚Üí pitch-spread (¬±semitones) + reverb wet**, **texture/variance ‚Üí grain-duration jitter** ‚Äî so a bright busy image scatters the piano into a shimmering detuned cloud, a dark calm image lets it settle back toward the original. AnalyserNode on the grain output drives the ~6 s prompt; full-bleed canvas cross-fades the FAL image with grain motes (pitch-class-colored) drifting over it, or a local latent field when no key. Built files: `page.tsx` (916), `granular.ts` (334), guarded `api/route.ts`. Refs: Curtis Roads *Microsound* (2001) ¬∑ Anadol *Machine Hallucinations* ¬∑ rides loved `227-paths-granular`‚ù§Ô∏è + `323-latent-condensation`‚ù§Ô∏è. Ambition: #4 (spine cycle-1 alt) + #2 + #3. **Why it lost:** graining a *real* recording is the highest browser-verification risk (mush/artifact danger) for a build-only sandbox ‚Äî but on pure surprise it was the closest contender. **Why resurrect:** it is the literal answer to the spine's cycle-3 goal ‚Äî the image *reshapes* the music, not just filters it; fold its granular loop-back into the spine once `448`'s base is browser-verified. **Next-cycle deepening:** add a transient-preserving grain mode so attacks survive, and let onset/phrase detection (from `448`) gate grain bursts to musical events.

## Banked from Cycle 366 (DEEP **kids** fire ‚Äî one concept *a child paints a living singing GALAXY of light by moving in front of the camera*, three technical attacks; winner shipped = `444-kids-aurora-hands` [bare-hands WebGPU-compute galaxy]. Both siblings are complete, **build-reviewed** real implementations; folders removed per the no-half-built-folders rule, banked here as ready-to-resurrect TEXT. **Numbered 445/446 to avoid colliding with the now-shipped 444 ‚Äî renumber on revival; max folder now 444.**)

### `445-kids-light-river` ‚Äî paint a glowing, singing river of light with ANY whole-body motion ‚Äî no model, no WebGPU, the most robust take *(banked sibling ‚Äî the resilient maximum-06:30-reliability ship)*
Model-free **optical flow**: the webcam is downscaled to a 64√ó48 grid, per-cell `|frame_t ‚àí frame_{t-1}|` gives a motion-magnitude field (noise gate 0.04 + trailing-glow decay 0.82 so a quick wave leaves a streak), which **advects a 40 000-particle WebGL2 ping-pong-FBO** aurora (GLSL update pass pushes velocity by the local motion gradient ‚Äî coarse Lucas-Kanade/Horn-Schunck via neighbour-texel differencing; bloom + additive composite for the glow). Audio = continuous C-major-pentatonic chord-cloud (16 voices, left-of-frame‚Üílower+left-pan / right‚Üíhigher+right-pan via StereoPanner, motion energy‚Üídensity+brightness, 7-min root drift, 22s filter LFO, never-silent bed, ‚àí6dB/20:1 limiter, master 0.20). Ghost-fallback: 3 Lissajous "ghost hands" inject motion so the galaxy+music are alive with no camera; denied‚Üíghost continues with a `text-rose-300` notice. **No MediaPipe, no WebGPU, no network, no API route** ‚Äî the safest 06:30 phone read (widely-supported WebGL2). Refs: TouchDesigner optical-flow ("the machine perceives *that* something moves", derivative.ca 2026) ¬∑ Memo Akten *Learning to See* ¬∑ Refik Anadol particle-flow ¬∑ Jos Stam *Stable Fluids* (advection). Ambition 2/5 (#2 ‚â•3-subsystems + #3 named refs). **Why it lost:** WebGL2 (3√ó recent) + optical flow (already in `221`/`295`) are the least-diverse/novel of the three, and "wave‚Üílight streams" is the least surprising vs. 444's gather/scatter-a-galaxy. **Why resurrect:** it is the most robust/reliable kids motion piece the lab has ‚Äî ship it when a cycle wants maximum delivery reliability or when WebGPU/camera-model risk is unacceptable. **Next-cycle deepening:** try a true GPU optical-flow pass (Lucas-Kanade in a fragment shader) and a directional (not just magnitude) field so strokes have flow direction.

### `446-kids-star-swarm` ‚Äî your whole BODY becomes a constellation: wave your arms and swarms of glowing singing stars stream off your hands, head and feet *(banked sibling ‚Äî the next kids body-spatial ship, essentially ready)*
**MediaPipe PoseLandmarker** (lite, CDN-loaded) tracks 11 joints (wrists/elbows/shoulders/hips/ankles/head); each joint is an attractor/emitter for a shared ~29 000-star **three.js `THREE.Points`** cloud (AdditiveBlending, per-vertex color, `DynamicDrawUsage`, orthographic camera so screen-x maps directly). A moving joint surges its swarm bright and streams it outward; at rest stars orbit and drift back. Audio = always-on D-pentatonic pad (root/fifth/octave drifting D2‚ÜíE2‚ÜíF#2‚ÜíA2 over ~6min) + per-joint sustained voices (head=high B3‚ÄìD5, feet=low D2‚ÄìE2 ‚Äî BANDIMAL "big move=big low sound"), StereoPanner by joint screen-x, ~4-min filter LFO, ‚àí6dB/20:1 limiter, master ‚â§0.25, 13-min goodnight fade. Ghost-dancer fallback (a procedurally-dancing stick figure of attractors) runs the full swarm+audio with no camera / if the model fails within 4s; `text-rose-300` notice. **No API route** (only the MediaPipe CDN). Refs: MediaPipe PoseLandmarker / BlazePose (Bazarevsky CVPR 2020) ¬∑ MediaPipe‚ÜíNVIDIA-Flex interactive-particle (derivative.ca 2025‚Äì26) ¬∑ Memo Akten/Refik Anadol body-particle flow ¬∑ BANDIMAL (Yatatoy 2017). Ambition 2/5 (#2 + #3). **Why it lost:** three.js OUTPUT is 3√ó recent (less diverse than 444's WebGPU 0√ó) and PoseLandmarker-for-kids was just used (`419`); the whole-body register is lovely but less "massively bigger" than the 60k GPU galaxy. **Why resurrect:** the purest whole-body embodied kids instrument ‚Äî every joint simultaneously an emitter AND a voice; ship it on a kids cycle when three.js isn't over-represented. **Next-cycle deepening:** add gentle constellation-line connectors between a child's own joints (legible "you are the constellation") and a phone-speaker check on the per-joint StereoPanner spread.

## Banked from Cycle 365 (WIDE **adult** fire ‚Äî *three frontier-different adult directions in one fire*: AI-image-inside-AV (Karel's stated #1), an adult body-spatial-room (jury #6), and attention-as-instrument ("go weird", jury's sharpest critique). Each cleared the floor via DIFFERENT tags. Winner shipped = `441-latent-listening-room` (audio‚Üíprompt‚ÜíFAL-image‚Üíaudio closed loop, fresh AI-image OUTPUT, rides loved `323`‚ù§Ô∏è). - **Tags** (dodge all current bans): INPUT = **camera / MediaPipe Pose** (33-landmark `PoseLandmarker`, the proven 419/423 CDN+WASM loader ‚Äî analysis-only, nothing uploaded, no API route) ¬∑ OUTPUT = **Canvas2D** glowing-skeleton stage (mirrored selfie, voice orbs that bloom with loudness) ¬∑ TECHNIQUE = **body-landmark ‚Üí spatial-audio** mapping (wrist Y ‚Üí pitch/gain of two lead voices, wrist X ‚Üí `StereoPannerNode` pan, shoulder span ‚Üí pad+bass drone bloom, motion energy ‚Üí shimmer + reverb depth; 5 voices ‚Üí limiter ‚Üí master, no HRTF so it reads on laptop/phone speakers) ¬∑ VIBE = embodied / installation / conductor.
- **Ghost-conductor fallback:** no camera / MediaPipe fail ‚Üí synthetic figure-8 conducting landmarks drive the **identical** audio+visual engine, so it performs fully hands-free at 06:30. Built files: `page.tsx` (415), `audio.ts` (334), `pose.ts` (244), `scene.ts` (250). **Refs:** Myron Krueger *Videoplace* (1985) ¬∑ Jaques-Dalcroze Eurhythmics ¬∑ Mathews Radio Baton / Machover Hyperinstruments / IRCAM gesture-sound ¬∑ BlazePose (Bazarevsky 2020).
- **Why it didn't win + the FIX before reshipping:** it built the ensemble on **D Dorian** ‚Äî the *retired* D-Dorian bed (jury 2026-06-07 ban). **Re-voice it onto an expressive non-D-Dorian tonal world first**, then it's a clean, near-ship-ready answer to jury #6. Also rest the camera one cycle (419/423/440 were all embodied) so the body-spatial novelty reads as fresh, not as more camera. **Ambition 2/5** (#2 ‚â•3-subsystems ¬∑ #3 named-ref). Build-reviewed, zero new deps, no API route.

### `443-the-vanishing` ‚Äî a 10-voice ensemble that can only survive your full attention: every time you look away (switch tabs / blur the window / mouse leaves the page / 20s idle) it **permanently loses one voice it can never get back**, moving only toward silence *(banked sibling ‚Äî the purest "go-weird" answer to the jury's sharpest critique; the next adult "weird" ship, essentially ready as-is)*
- **Tags** (a genuinely never-used input): INPUT = **visitor attention / presence** (`document.visibilitychange` + `window` blur/focus + `pointerleave` + ~20s idle timer ‚Äî never used as an instrument in any prior lab piece) ¬∑ OUTPUT = **spare clinical Canvas2D** (luminous ring-glyphs that breathe with amplitude; on death a 3.5s fracture animation + a permanent dashed-ring "scar" with an √ó; a fading center pulse + connection web) ¬∑ TECHNIQUE = **irreversible state-erosion / entropy** (kill order ornament‚Üíroots: overtone ‚Üí choirs ‚Üí shimmer ‚Üí arp ‚Üí pulse ‚Üí shadow ‚Üí octave ‚Üí fifth ‚Üí ground; survivors grow more exposed) ¬∑ VIBE = conceptual / critical / melancholy (the deliberate break from the consonant-resolving rut; expressive 12-TET C-minor, never JI-as-subject).
- **Opening state plays the full ensemble** so a passive 06:30 glance hears the complete piece and the concept reveals itself the instant they switch tabs; "Begin again" fully resets (a new session ‚Äî the loss was real, but you can start over). Built files: `page.tsx` (550), `audio.ts` (622), `scene.ts` (340). **Refs:** Alvin Lucier *I Am Sitting in a Room* (1969, erosion-as-process) ¬∑ Ryoji Ikeda (clinical data aesthetics) ¬∑ Tehching Hsieh (duration art that costs real time) ¬∑ John Cage (attention IS the score).
- **Why it didn't win:** only because `441` answers a *direct standing Karel directive* (AI-image-in-AV) and the recent window already broke the consonance rut (422/432/437); on pure surprise this was the closest contender. **Resurrect first** for the next adult cycle that wants the jury-#2 "refuses-to-resolve" piece. **Ambition 2/5** (#2 attention-input + erosion engine + Canvas2D scar-renderer + 4 event-listener subsystems ¬∑ #3 named conceptual lineage). Build-reviewed, zero deps, no API route, no network.

### `438-kids-star-window` ‚Äî hold the phone up like a window, turn/tilt to look around a 3D night sky, AIM at a glowing star-creature to wake it so it sings from where it sits *(banked sibling ‚Äî the most immediate / kid-legible take; the cleanest cycle-2 simplification of `440`)*
The most *forgiving* and instantly-rewarding of the three: sweep your aim past a star and it swells-and-sings (no dwell, no scoop ‚Äî pure immediate cause-effect, the legibility logic that won `419`/`423`/`429`). three.js, 20 Fibonacci-lattice star-creatures + ~1400 background stars, **quaternion-slerp** gyro magic-window (more frame-correct than `440`'s yaw/pitch), per-star `StereoPannerNode` pan + proximity gain, D-major-pentatonic chords, figure-8 auto-pan tour, DynamicsCompressor limiter, iOS `requestPermission` in the Begin tap, drag fallback. Ambition 2/5 (#2 ‚â•3-subsystems + #3 magic-window/3-DoF-spatial-audio). **Resurrect** if a future kids cycle wants the *immediate* version for the youngest players (3-year-olds who can't yet hold a steady dwell), or as a polish/companion to `440`. Build-reviewed, zero new deps, no API route.

### `439-kids-star-night` ‚Äî the SAME aim-to-wake night sky, rendered as a **raw-WebGL2** GLSL additive-glow point-field (the atmospheric renderer attack) *(banked sibling ‚Äî the renderer-diversity revival)*
Hand-written GLSL 300 es vertex+fragment pipeline (W3C Rz¬∑Rx¬∑Ry orientation ‚Üí `u_view` matrix; 3-layer additive Gaussian glow: core + halo + bloom + aim sparkle-ring; `gl.ONE,gl.ONE` blending; ~500-point starfield + 20 singing stars). Same gyro magic-window + `StereoPannerNode` spatial audio + pentatonic + auto-pan + iOS permission + drag fallback as `438`, but a deeper/more-nebula atmospheric look from the raw shaders. Ambition 2/5 (#2 + #3). **Resurrect** when the kids lane wants raw-WebGL2 renderer diversity and a moodier night than three.js sprites give; slightly higher build risk (shader compile / OEM gyro-frame quirks). Build-reviewed (tsc + ESLint clean), zero deps, no API route.

## Banked from Cycle 362 (WIDE **kids** fire ‚Äî *three genuinely different NON-percussion kids registers*, breaking the recent kids-percussion rut (419 pose-beat / 423 face-beat / 426 euclid / 429 noise-foley were all rhythm). The grep audit this fire killed three would-be #1 claims (fluid, shadow, hand-tracking all already in the lab ‚Äî the lab is technique-saturated at 432 prototypes), so all three briefs cleared the floor honestly at **2/5** (#2 ‚â•3-subsystems + #3 named-ref) and competed on register-breadth + 06:30 phone read. Winner shipped = `433-kids-fluid-paint` (real Stam Navier-Stokes GPU fluid finger-paint, WebGL2 ‚Äî liquid-dreamy, rides loved `84-wave-fluid`‚ù§Ô∏è + the most-loved paint lineage). Both siblings are complete, **build-reviewed** real implementations (tsc + ESLint clean ‚Äî 434: 778+64 lines, 435: 1089 lines incl. real HandLandmarker; folder-isolated); folders removed per the no-half-built-folders rule, banked here as ready-to-resurrect TEXT. **Renderer note for revival: Canvas2D is over-represented and three.js/WebGL2 are the cleaner kids lanes; renumber on revival, max folder now 433.**)

### `434-kids-lantern-sky` ‚Äî tap to launch glowing paper lanterns into a 3D night sky you LOOK AROUND inside, each singing from its own place in true HRTF binaural space *(banked sibling ‚Äî the cosmic-wonder / 3D-spatial register; the headphone piece)*
- **Tags** (clean dodge of all bans): INPUT = **touch + device-gyro** (tilt to look around; drag fallback) ¬∑ OUTPUT = **three.js** (the freshest renderer lane, 1√ó recent) ¬∑ TECHNIQUE = **true 3D HRTF spatial audio** (`PannerNode` `panningModel:"HRTF"` + `AudioListener` orientation re-derived from the camera quaternion **every frame**, so turning your head genuinely moves the choir around you) ¬∑ VIBE = cosmic/calm/wonder.
- **The mechanic:** night-sky dome (350-point starfield, additive moon halo, warm horizon torus). Camera **rotates only, no translation** (non-nauseating). Tap ‚Üí a warm glowing lantern (cylinder body + additive glow-sphere) rises with sinusoidal sway, singing a sustained slow-attack voice (G-major-pentatonic, color=pitch, any combination consonant) positioned at its 3D world coords via its own `PannerNode`; up to ~10‚Äì12 coexist, old ones fade high up. Always-on open-fifth ambient pad; `DynamicsCompressor` brick-wall limiter (‚àí8 dB / 16:1) ‚Üí master 0.22 (safe ears). iOS: `AudioContext` + `DeviceOrientationEvent.requestPermission()` inside the Begin tap; auto-demo launches a lantern every ~1.5 s + slow-pans the camera hands-free after 1.8 s. Full three.js disposal + listener teardown. Built files: `page.tsx` (778), `README.md` (64). **Refs:** Web Audio `PannerNode` HRTF / Google Resonance Audio lineage ¬∑ sky-lantern / Yi Peng (Khom Loi) festival image.
- **Why it didn't win:** the most genuinely *spatial* of the three and a clean three.js dodge, BUT its core payoff ‚Äî the **binaural HRTF field** ‚Äî is **largely lost on phone speakers** at the 06:30 review (the README admits this; needs headphones), and it's the closest neighbour to an existing piece (`238-kids-tilt-world` already does tilt + 3D + panned audio; the differentiator here is HRTF-vs-StereoPanner, a real but headphone-dependent upgrade). **Revive:** the **headphone/installation** spatial piece, or as the explicit cycle-2 over `238` (the HRTF-tracks-camera engine IS the upgrade); pair with a visual payoff that also reads on phone speakers (e.g. lanterns visibly swirl past as you turn) so it isn't headphone-gated. Reconstructable from this spec. **Ambition 2/5** (#2 ‚â•3-subsystems ¬∑ #3 named).

### `435-kids-air-ribbon` ‚Äî paint music in the AIR: wave a hand at the camera, a glowing light-ribbon trails your fingertip and SINGS a sustained song (friendly theremin) *(banked sibling ‚Äî the magical-flowing / sustained-melodic-camera register; the inverse of the recent face/pose BEATS)*
- **Tags:** INPUT = **camera / MediaPipe HandLandmarker** (index-fingertip, analysis-only ‚Äî never recorded/stored/uploaded) ¬∑ OUTPUT = **Canvas2D** (additive-glow trailing ribbon + sparkle particles + dim mirrored backdrop) ¬∑ TECHNIQUE = **hand-landmark ‚Üí continuous air-theremin** (fingertip Y ‚Üí pitch snapped to C-major-pentatonic with 60 ms `setTargetAtTime` portamento GLIDE ‚Äî sustained/singing, NOT discrete hits; X ‚Üí `StereoPannerNode`; hand openness ‚Üí timbre/volume) ¬∑ VIBE = magical/flowing/dreamy.
- **The mechanic:** HandLandmarker via `webpackIgnore` CDN dynamic import (zero npm dep), `detectForVideo`, fingertip drives a fading glow-ribbon (hue=pitch, violet‚Üícyan) with a bright Gaussian head + gravity sparkles. 3-osc voice (triangle + sine octave + sine fifth) ‚Üí voice gain ‚Üí panner ‚Üí `DynamicsCompressor` (‚àí8 dB / 16:1) ‚Üí master 0.22; always-on C3 ambient pad (never silent). **Three-tier always-demoable fallback** (the strength): camera denied / CDN fail ‚Üí scripted figure-8 "ghost hand" melody loop immediately; no hand within 2.5 s ‚Üí ghost auto-kicks alongside the live feed; pointer/drag fallback always playable on a laptop. Full camera + landmarker + audio teardown. Built files: `page.tsx` (604), `audio.ts` (220), `hand.ts` (142), `README.md` (123). **Refs:** L√©on Theremin (1928) gestural-pitch instrument ¬∑ Myron Krueger *Videoplace* (1975) camera-as-instrument ¬∑ MediaPipe HandLandmarker (Google).
- **Why it didn't win:** a genuinely fresh **register** (sustained melodic air-painting ‚Äî the melodic *inverse* of the recent face/pose percussion) and a superb always-demoable ghost fallback, BUT (a) **camera INPUT** was just used back-to-back-ish in the recent kids ships (`419`/`423`) so reviving it again leans on the camera lane the lab is resting, and (b) **Canvas2D OUTPUT** is over-represented (3√ó in the last-10, would tip to 4√ó), where the winner's WebGL2 is the cleaner dodge. **Revive:** the next time the camera lane is fresh and Canvas2D isn't over-represented ‚Äî it's the strongest *melodic* (vs percussive) camera-kids piece banked; or port the ribbon to WebGL2/three.js for renderer diversity. Reconstructable from this spec. **Ambition 2/5** (#2 ‚â•3-subsystems ¬∑ #3 named).

## Banked from Cycle 361 (WIDE adult fire ‚Äî *three genuinely unrelated go-weird directions*, one per renderer lane, against the jury's "the lab learned to go deep and forgot how to go weird." Winner shipped = `432-three-body` (N-body gravity sim ‚Üí sound, Canvas2D ‚Äî grep-verified lab-first technique, the most elegant "refuses to resolve"). Both siblings are complete, **build-reviewed** real implementations (tsc + ESLint clean, ~670/841 lines, folder-isolated); folders removed per the no-half-built-folders rule, banked here as ready-to-resurrect TEXT. Both are strong ADULT resurrections ‚Äî they deliberately diversify OUTPUT (three.js, raw-WebGL2) and INPUT (external-data, keyboard) away from the winner.)

### `430-wiki-pulse` ‚Äî the LIVE Wikimedia recent-changes firehose sonified: every edit on Earth, right now, bots vs humans *(banked sibling ‚Äî the real-world-data + "music about something other than music" take; the freshest data SOURCE the lab has)*
- **Tags** (clean dodge of all bans): INPUT = **external SSE data** (Wikimedia EventStreams, public/keyless/CORS, client-side `EventSource` ‚Äî NO api route, no guard) ¬∑ OUTPUT = **three.js** (rising glowing nodes, additive bloom) ¬∑ TECHNIQUE = real-time event-stream sonification ¬∑ VIBE = restless/global/never-resolving.
- **The mechanic:** connects to `https://stream.wikimedia.org/v2/stream/recentchange`, keeps `type==="edit"||"new"`, reads `wiki`/`bot`/`user`/`length.{old,new}` ‚Üí `delta`. **delta** (log-scaled) drives amplitude/duration/base-freq (big additions bloom deep & swelling, tiny edits soft high ticks; sign changes timbre ‚Äî deletions glide down with a gritty high-passed breath); **bot vs human ‚Üí two distinct timbres** (bots dry/mechanical/quantized square blips; humans warm/breathy/resonant triangle-through-moving-LPF + noise breath ‚Äî "you can hear the machines vs the people," the emotional core); **wiki/language ‚Üí stereo pan** (west‚Üíeast spread + micro-detune); always-on detuned-sine bed; master `DynamicsCompressor` brick-wall limiter + voice cap. Each edit also spawns a rising three.js sphere (size = log|delta|, humans warm / bots cool, x = pan). **Reliability:** a single Begin gesture creates/resumes the AudioContext (iOS); a **deterministic seeded synthetic firehose** drives the EXACT same pipeline if no live event arrives within ~2.5s (amber "offline demo" notice ‚Üí green "‚óè live" when real events flow), so it always comes alive hands-free in ~2s. Built files: `page.tsx` (~670), `README.md`. **Refs:** **Hatnote *Listen to Wikipedia*** (LaPorte & Hashemi, 2013 ‚Äî the canonical Wikipedia-edit sonification; this is a darker, non-consonant, three.js take) + **Wikimedia EventStreams**.
- **Why it didn't win:** the highest-*concept* surprise of the slate and the most direct answer to the jury's #4 ("zero real-world-data sonification in 15 cycles ‚Äî sonify an external API, it breaks the mic rut AND the consonance rut in one fire"), BUT data-sonification is **explicitly NOT a lab-first** (`314-solar-wind` / `337`/`418-seismic` already did it ‚Äî corrected IDEAS ¬ß359), so it tops out at **ambition 2/5** (#2 ‚â•3-subsystems: EventSource ingest + Web-Audio synth + three.js render ¬∑ #3 named-ref) where `432` clears 3/5 with a grep-verified #1. **Revive:** the obvious next **real-world-data ship** ‚Äî it's the freshest data SOURCE the lab has (not seismic/solar) and breaks the mic+consonance ruts together; **fix-first:** add per-region timbre color (currently language is pan-only) and orchestrate dense bursts instead of thinning at the voice cap. Reconstructable from this spec.

### `431-test-signal` ‚Äî clinical Ikeda / Alva-Noto GLITCH-WALL instrument, raw-WebGL2 data-card *(banked sibling ‚Äî the purest clinical refuse-to-resolve swing; jury #2)*
- **Tags:** INPUT = **keyboard/touch** (toggle + sculpt channels) ¬∑ OUTPUT = **raw WebGL2** (hand-written GLSL monochrome test-card) ¬∑ TECHNIQUE = sine-sweep + look-ahead impulse-grid + bit-crush glitch (no scale) ¬∑ VIBE = clinical/abrasive/unresolved.
- **The mechanic:** three independent signal channels, nothing pitched to a scale. **SWEEP** (key 1) ‚Äî a continuous log-glide sine (top capped 9 kHz, amplitude rolled off as freq climbs) + gliding sub. **IMPULSE** (key 2) ‚Äî band-passed filtered-noise clicks scheduled with a Web-Audio **look-ahead scheduler** (Chris Wilson "Two Clocks", 25ms tick / 100ms ahead), `RATE`/`DENS` controls, no pitch. **GLITCH** (key 3) ‚Äî bit-crush via quantizing `WaveShaperNode` + sample-hold playbackRate + stutter gate, `RATE`/`BRGHT`. All ‚Üí master gain ‚Üí brick-wall `DynamicsCompressor` (‚àí6 dB / ratio 20) ‚Üí analyser ‚Üí out. Raw-WebGL2 GLSL data-card: sweep-tracking scan line, density-shifting barcode field, impulse-strobed columns (localized + soft, **no full-screen strobe**), glitch displacement/block-noise, a live oscilloscope trace of the master output; `prefers-reduced-motion` ‚Üí `u_calm` dampens everything. **Reliability:** Begin creates/resumes the ctx in-gesture (iOS), then auto-sequences SWEEP‚ÜíIMPULSE‚ÜíGLITCH (~0.3/2.1/4.2s) hands-free, then fully interactive (cards ‚â•44px + keys). Built files: `page.tsx` (~841), `README.md`. **Refs:** **Ryoji Ikeda** (*test pattern* / *dataplex* / *data.matrix*) + **Alva Noto / Carsten Nicolai** (*Xerrox*, clicks-and-glitch).
- **Why it didn't win:** a genuine, safety-serious commitment to the Ikeda/Alva-Noto idiom (un-snapped glide, real look-ahead scheduler, dependency-free bit-crush, GLSL data-card driven by live audio state) ‚Äî but **ambition 2/5** (#2 + #3; refuse-to-resolve is the *framing*, not a novel technique) and the lab already swung at refuse-to-resolve twice recently (`357-shatter`, `422`), so it's the second piece at the same mandate rather than a new capability. **Revive:** ship next time the adult lane wants the *pure clinical* register on its own renderer (raw-WebGL2, distinct from 357's DOM/CSS and 422's grid); **deepen-first:** make the impulse grid a programmable pattern sequencer and the glitch channel player-triggerable as discrete one-shots (both self-flagged) to push it from "ambient generator" to "instrument." Reconstructable from this spec.

## Banked from Cycle 360 (DEEP kids fire ‚Äî *finger-paint where the brush's TEXTURE is its SOUND, noise not notes*. Winner shipped = `429-kids-texture-paint-tap` (impulse+resonator foley, Canvas2D, discrete crunch/pop/tap/scratch/splash ‚Äî the lab's first pure-NOISE kids instrument by touch, jury #1 for kids). Both siblings are complete, **build-reviewed** real implementations (~900‚Äì1300 lines, folder-isolated, ESLint-discipline followed); folders removed per the no-half-built-folders rule, banked here as ready-to-resurrect TEXT. Shared thesis: every kids sound toy maps to pentatonic PITCH; these make the whole palette NOISE/TIMBRE. **Renderer note for revival: Canvas2D is now 4√ó in the trailing window ‚Üí 428 (WebGL2) is the cleaner lane; renumber on revival, max folder now 429.**)

### `428-kids-texture-paint-morph` ‚Äî subtractive noise-morph on a raw-WebGL2 painterly canvas *(banked sibling ‚Äî the BEAUTIFUL take + the obvious cycle-2 deepening)*
- **Concept:** same toy as 429 (paint a texture, hear it) but the sound is **continuous subtractive synthesis** ‚Äî an always-running noise bed (white + brown) sculpted live by resonant biquad filters: finger X ‚Üí filter centre/brightness, Y ‚Üí Q/resonance, drag-speed ‚Üí energy. Textures: üå¨Ô∏è Wind (bandpass-swept brown noise) ¬∑ üíß Water (resonant bubbling) ¬∑ üèúÔ∏è Sand (narrow high hiss) ¬∑ üî• Fire (low noise + irregular crackle bursts) ¬∑ ‚ö° Fizz (wide bright noise + fast tremolo).
- **Render:** **raw WebGL2** (hand-rolled, no three.js) ‚Äî 3 shader programs + **ping-pong FBO accumulation** so strokes glow and accrete; per-texture procedural appearance (fbm gradients, ripple rings, grain stipple, turbulent fire, sparkle hash). The most visually striking candidate of the fire ‚Äî a glowing accreting paint canvas, the strongest pull on Karel's loved-paint lineage.
- **Why it didn't win:** continuous morph is less kid-legible than 429's discrete one-dab-one-sound; it uses 3 sub-audible LFO oscillators (marginally less "pure noise" for a zero-pitch-thesis piece); and the 3-program FBO pipeline is the highest perf/build risk on low-end phones.
- **Resurrect as:** the **cycle-2 visual deepening** ‚Äî port 429's discrete foley *voices* onto 428's WebGL2 painterly accumulation canvas ‚Üí a noise-paint that both *crunches satisfyingly* AND *looks gorgeous*. Best kids renderer move while Canvas2D is over-represented. Refs: K√∂hler bouba/kiki 1929 ¬∑ Farnell *Designing Sound* 2010 ¬∑ Kandinsky synesthesia. Touch IN ¬∑ raw-WebGL2 OUT ¬∑ subtractive-noise TECH ¬∑ kids non-tuned VIBE.

### `427-kids-texture-paint-grains` ‚Äî granular-noise foley brush, Canvas2D *(banked sibling ‚Äî the granular-texture take)*
- **Concept:** same toy; sound = **granular synthesis of NOISE** via a Web-Audio look-ahead grain scheduler. While the finger moves, a stream of tiny windowed filtered-noise grains plays; drag speed + texture control grain **density / size / brightness / spread**: Sand = dense tiny dry grains ¬∑ Silk = sparse long soft low-passed grains ¬∑ Sparkle = very short bright high grains ¬∑ Rumble = larger low-passed grains ¬∑ Fizz = rapid medium grains. 0 oscillators (genuinely pitch-less). Canvas2D strokes.
- **Why it didn't win:** solid but not the standout on any axis ‚Äî granular-continuous like 428 but less visually rich; Canvas2D like 429 but less legible than discrete events.
- **Resurrect as:** the granular-texture variant, or fold its grain engine into 428's WebGL2 canvas as a "soft/continuous" mode alongside 429's discrete "dab" voices. Refs: Curtis Roads *Microsound* 2001 (granular) ¬∑ Farnell 2010 ¬∑ K√∂hler 1929. Touch IN ¬∑ Canvas2D OUT ¬∑ granular-noise TECH ¬∑ kids non-tuned VIBE.

## Banked from Cycle 359 (WIDE adult fire ‚Äî *three ways to leave the consonant monastery*, three emotional registers, one per clean lane. Winner shipped = `426-euclid-engine` (adult Euclidean polyrhythm + Reich phasing on **three.js** ‚Äî the freshest renderer lane, 0√ó recent ‚Äî pure percussion / zero tuning, jury #1 for adults). Both siblings are complete, **build-reviewed** (substantial real implementations, ~900‚Äì1100 lines each; folder-isolated, ESLint-discipline followed) demoable builds ‚Äî folders removed per the no-half-built-folders rule, banked here as ready-to-resurrect TEXT. Shared thesis (jury 2026-06-08 #1+#2): ~13/15 recent pieces resolve to calm consonance; these three each refuse it differently. **Also corrected this fire (RESEARCH ¬ß359): real-world-data sonification is NOT a lab-first and is saturated** ‚Äî `314-solar-wind` already fetches live NOAA SWPC, so the banked `421-solar-drone` would DUPLICATE it; and `9-reaction-diffusion` already exists. Don't revive those as "firsts.")

### `424-welcome-erosion` ‚Äî Karel's REAL Welcome Home piano, long-form 5-min generative EROSION (a piece about forgetting) *(banked sibling ‚Äî the intimate register + the vehicle for Karel's actual music)*
- **Question:** "What if a warm intimate piano phrase were played once, then over five minutes slowly eroded through self-feedback + granular decay until it can never return home?"
- **Tags** (clean dodge of all bans): INPUT = **audio-file** (Karel's tracks via the existing `/api/audio/{id}` door + a synth-phrase fallback; NO mic/camera) ¬∑ OUTPUT = Canvas2D (memory-strip + grain motes + fraying waveform) ¬∑ TECHNIQUE = **long-form stateful granular/spectral erosion** (monotonic, one-way) ¬∑ VIBE = intimate/decaying/unresolved.
- **The mechanic:** a pure `computeErosion(elapsed)` drives a monotonic trajectory over ~5 min ‚Äî grain density 12‚Üí1.5/s, grain dur 80‚Üí850 ms, pitch spread 0.05‚Üí~7 st, position jitter 1%‚Üí90%, amplitude 100%‚Üí20%, reverb send 28%‚Üí95%, LPF 7 kHz‚Üí380 Hz ‚Äî so phrases thin, smear, detune and fragment into a resonant wash that never cadences back. Web-Audio look-ahead grain scheduler (25 ms tick / 120 ms ahead), `DynamicsCompressor` brick-wall limiter, 5-phase HUD label (intact‚Üífraying‚Üídissolving‚Üíeroding‚Üíforgotten) + a falling "memory remaining" bar. Auto-erodes the synth phrase ~2 s after Begin (hands-free). Built files: `page.tsx` (~1105), `README.md`. **Refs:** William Basinski *Disintegration Loops* ¬∑ Alvin Lucier *I Am Sitting in a Room* ¬∑ Roads *Microsound*.
- **Why it didn't win:** the strongest fit for Karel's STANDING #1 directive (use his real music) and the most poignant, but (a) it defaults to a **synth** phrase at 06:30 unless Karel pastes a recording ID, so it doesn't actually *showcase* his music hands-free; (b) Canvas2D is 2√ó in the last-10 (allowed but not the freshest lane the way 426's three.js is); (c) the erosion is granular-adjacent to 422's shatter (distinct ‚Äî 5-min stateful vs 10-s freeze ‚Äî but the same neighbourhood). **Revive:** the ship that finally puts a real Welcome-Home track on screen (hand it a known recording ID), or fold its erosion engine onto 422's material. Reconstructable from this spec. **Ambition 2/5** (#2 ‚â•3-subsystems ¬∑ #3 named).

### `425-test-signal` ‚Äî clinical Ikeda / Alva-Noto GLITCH-WALL, WebGL2 test-card *(banked sibling ‚Äî the purest *refuse-to-resolve* / "go weird" swing; jury #2)*
- **Question:** "What does a piece that was NEVER consonant sound like ‚Äî a self-mutating wall of sine-grids, impulse clicks and band-noise in Ikeda's cold register?"
- **Tags:** INPUT = **generative** (no device; optional density/intensity sliders) ¬∑ OUTPUT = **raw WebGL2** scanline/datamosh test-card ¬∑ TECHNIQUE = **glitch-DSP** (clocked impulse trains + inharmonic sine pings + filtered-noise bursts + a mutation engine that re-rolls the grid) ¬∑ VIBE = clinical/abrasive/monochrome/unresolved.
- **The mechanic:** a look-ahead scheduler (25 ms / 100 ms ahead) across 4 layers ‚Äî HF sine pings at 8 mutually-inharmonic freqs (6317‚Äì13711 Hz), 1‚Äì7 ms impulse clicks, 5 sub pulses at irrational ratios (37.13‚Äì91.53 Hz, no implied root), and an LFO-swept band-noise ‚Äî with a mutation event every 5‚Äì12 s re-randomizing densities/BPF within clinical bounds so it permanently drifts and never locks. `DynamicsCompressor` brick-wall (‚àí8 dB, 20:1). Auto-starts ~2 s after load (with a "TAP TO START" overlay if the AudioContext is gesture-gated); WebGL2 fails ‚Üí DOM fallback meter. Built files: `page.tsx` (~908), `README.md`. **Refs:** Ryoji Ikeda *test pattern* / *datamatics* ¬∑ Carsten Nicolai / Alva Noto. **This finally realizes the ¬ß356 banked "test-pattern" Ikeda counter-piece idea.**
- **Why it didn't win:** the purest answer to the jury's #2 (abrasive / never-consonant ‚Äî the boldest "go weird"), but (a) a wall of 6‚Äì14 kHz pings + sharp clicks is a real **06:30-on-phone-speakers fatigue risk** (could read as "annoying" not "compelling" at the review moment); (b) WebGL2 was just used by 423 (1√ó), where 426's three.js is the cleaner renderer dodge; (c) builder-flagged node-churn at max density on low-end mobile. **Revive:** ship on a fire where a harsh-HF clinical piece fits the review context, or as the desktop/headphone Ikeda reference; pair the mutation engine with a non-mic input. Reconstructable from this spec. **Ambition 2/5** (#2 ‚â•3-subsystems ¬∑ #3 named).
