## Cycle 337 banked seeds (adult DEEP ‚Äî 2 non-winner *approaches* to "The Accompanist", build-verified clean, ready to revive)

> These are the two losing **algorithms** from the DEEP-3 score-follower fire (winner shipped: `375-tempo-canon`, online DTW). All three follow the same concept ‚Äî *an accompanist that plays the bass+chords in sync with YOUR rubato through Beethoven's "Ode to Joy"*, driven by a built-in baked rubato demo / computer-keyboard / Web MIDI (NO mic, dodging the JURY ban), self-verifying with zero hardware the way `358-beat-mirror` does. The winner's README lists both of these as its **"next-cycle deepening"** directions. Numbers 374/376 are as-built labels (no folders on disk now ‚Äî `rm -rf`'d, code reconstructable from these specs + the winner's shared structure).

### 374-the-accompanist ‚Äî HMM / forward-algorithm probabilistic note-event follower `[queued ‚Äî strong, the natural cycle-2 deepening]`
**Question**: what if the accompanist tracked you with a *belief distribution* over score positions ‚Äî so wrong, extra, and missed notes never derail it ‚Äî and showed you its confidence?
**What was built (build-clean, exit-0 in the 3-up diagnostic build)**: an **HMM-style online forward estimator** (`follower.ts`) over discrete score states (one per melody note). Per played note: **predict** via a transition kernel (advance 0.80 / stay 0.10 / skip 0.08 / skip2 0.02), **update** by pitch-observation likelihood (exact 1.0 / octave 0.45 / step 0.18 / third 0.06 / **non-zero floor 0.02** = robustness, belief never collapses), **normalize** (underflow-guarded). Committed position = argmax(belief); a smoothed expectation eases the cursor; **confidence = belief mass at the committed state** (a meter the others lack). Online tempo = EMA-smoothed IOI (40‚Äì220 BPM); the accompaniment clock is anchored to the committed note's score-time and extrapolated forward at the current BPM between notes. Visual: a raw-**WebGL2 "score river"** piano-roll (reactive shader bg + instanced note-blocks, pulsing cursor, teal matched / rose unmatched hits). Files: `page.tsx`, `score.ts`, `follower.ts`, `audio.ts`, `gl.ts`, `README.md`. **Ambition 3/5**: #2 (‚â•3 subsystems: input layer + HMM follower + Web-Audio accompaniment + WebGL2 river) + #3 (The ACCompanion arXiv:2304.12939 ¬∑ Antescofo) + #4 (multi-cycle: the probabilistic core the elastic-dynamics-following cycle wants).
**Why it didn't ship**: two reasons. (a) **Legibility/diversity** ‚Äî its score-river visual is the lab's most *familiar* form (close to existing `24-piano-roll` / `13-piano-canvas`), where `375`'s **warping path** is a genuinely fresh, more-legible alignment picture and a better answer to the JURY's "stop relocating the screen-viz" complaint. (b) **Surprise** ‚Äî "watch the diagonal bend as you rush" beats a piano-roll cursor at the 06:30 glance. **Revive**: this is the **strongest cycle-2 deepening of the `375` thread** ‚Äî the HMM belief is the right substrate for the ACCompanion's *expressive* following (dynamics + articulation), and the **confidence meter** is the missing legibility readout. Fold the HMM follower in behind a DTW/HMM toggle (Matchmaker's exact comparison), or ship standalone with the confidence field as the headline.

### 376-the-prompter ‚Äî cue / anchor finite-state-machine follower (robust to wrong notes) `[queued ‚Äî strong, the "live-performance reliability" take]`
**Question**: what if the accompanist forgave genuinely messy playing ‚Äî wrong notes, ornaments, hesitations ‚Äî by committing *only at musical cues*, like a stage prompter waiting in the wings for each line?
**What was built (build-clean; a build-time simulation against its own demo proved 8/8 cues fire correctly)**: a **cue-based reactive follower** (`cues.ts`, Antescofo-style) ‚Äî the score is segmented into **8 phrases**, each ending in an **anchor pitch** (the cue point). Incoming notes accumulate *progress* toward the current anchor (matching lead note = lots, out-of-order = some, unexpected = a tiny nudge it can't break); reaching the anchor (¬±1 semitone tolerance, ~180 ms **refractory** so a trill can't double-fire) transitions the FSM and **fires that phrase's accompaniment cue** (bass thump + chord stab + a tempo-paced arpeggio sweep, paced to fill the time to the next expected anchor; tempo from anchor-interval). Deliberately trades fine-grained per-note positional accuracy for **robustness** (stated honestly). Its built-in demo bakes in **deliberate wrong/extra notes** (a stray B4, a G4 intrusion, a C#5 ornament) and the log shows them classified `stray`/absorbed while the cues still land on the right anchors (I‚ÄìV‚Äìvi‚ÄìIV‚ÄìI‚ÄìV‚Äìvi‚ÄìI), BPM climbing in the accelerando (~224) and settling in the ritard (~175) ‚Äî the **strongest verification posture** of the three (a build-time proof, echoing `358`'s known-answer ethos). Visual: raw-**WebGL2 cue track** ‚Äî phrase tiles flow to a NOW line, the anchor glows/brightens with progress, a **bright violet commit burst** vs. a **dim rose stray flicker** per note. Files: `page.tsx`, `score.ts`, `cues.ts`, `audio.ts`, `gl.ts`, `README.md`. **Ambition 3/5**: #2 (‚â•3 subsystems) + #3 (Antescofo ¬∑ The ACCompanion) + #4 (multi-cycle: the robustness layer a real-stage tool needs).
**Why it didn't ship**: it's the most *forgiving* but the **least legible about position** ‚Äî it intentionally doesn't know *which note* you're on, only *how close to the cue*, so the "watch it follow you" payoff is coarser than `375`'s continuous warping path; and the arpeggio-fill is a generative flourish rather than the literal left-hand part. **Revive**: the right build when the thread turns toward **live-performance reliability** (the `375`/`374` precise followers desync on a fumble; this one survives it) ‚Äî ship as "The Prompter" robustness mode, or fuse: DTW/HMM for fine position + a cue-FSM safety net that re-anchors when confidence collapses (the production hybrid Matchmaker describes). The wrong-note demo is the keeper feature.

---

## ¬ß339 ‚Äî banked seeds from the cycle-339 DEEP fire (Accompanist thread, cycle 2) ‚Äî both build-verified, both designated cycle-3/4 deepenings of the winner `380-expressive-accompanist`

The cycle-339 adult fire was a **DEEP-3**: one concept ‚Äî *an accompanist that follows your **expression**, not just your tempo* ‚Äî attacked three ways. Winner shipped = **`380-expressive-accompanist`** (follows tempo + **dynamics** + **articulation**; the ACCompanion's central thesis). The two siblings below were build-reviewed (TS + ESLint clean per builder; not authoritatively built since only the winner is built), `rm -rf`'d after banking. They are the natural **cycle-3 deepenings**: fold robustness + anticipation *into* 380's expressive follower (the thread's runway is now expression ‚Üí +robustness ‚Üí +anticipation ‚Üí +Karel's *Welcome Home* score).

### Seed A ‚Äî `381-resilient-accompanist` (the ROBUSTNESS axis ‚Äî strongest next-cycle deepening)
**Brief:** "What if the accompanist could survive your MISTAKES ‚Äî you fumble, skip, or play wrong notes, and it stays with you?" A **dual-follower** architecture answering the ACCompanion's "Reactivity + Robustness" axis and the DTW-vs-HMM comparison in **Matchmaker** (arXiv:2510.10087, Oct 2025). Two alignment engines run in parallel: (1) the online **DTW** (Dixon MATCH 2005, bounded forward-only, confidence = cost-margin between best/second-best window cell), and (2) a lightweight **HMM / forward note-event follower** ‚Äî a belief distribution over all N score positions, transition probs (advance 0.72 / skip 0.10 / stay 0.12 / back 0.06), pitch-class emission (exact 1.0 / octave 0.55 / nearby 0.05‚Äì0.15 / unrelated 0.03), re-seed on belief collapse, confidence = normalized peak posterior. A **confidence supervisor** hands off: trust DTW until its confidence < 0.25 ‚Üí defer to HMM until DTW recovers > 0.45. Twinkle-in-D melody; the baked demo bakes **four deliberate fumbles** (wrong note, skipped note, 3√ó hesitation, rushed run) so the resilience is *provable on a phone with no MIDI* ‚Äî confidence meter dips and recovers, the trusted-follower indicator flips, accompaniment never derails. WebGL2 viz shows the **two followers racing** (DTW teal path + HMM violet belief bars + a confidence band that collapses/recovers at each fumble). **What was promising:** technically the most sophisticated of the three (two followers + supervisor), and it directly solves 375/380's known weakness (a long wrong-note run outruns the bounded DTW window). **Why it didn't win:** its expressive layer is thinner (velocity‚Üídynamics only, no articulation), so it's a robustness *refinement* rather than the headline expressive leap the thread's cycle-2 promised. **To resurrect:** fold the HMM backup + confidence-gated supervisor *behind* 380's expressive follower as a `Solo‚áÑResilient` toggle ‚Äî you get expression-following AND fumble-recovery in one piece. Files were: `score.ts` (Twinkle-D + 4-fumble `makePerformance()`), `dtw.ts` (+confidence), `hmm.ts`, `audio.ts` (velocity‚Üídynamics), `gl.ts` (dual-track + confidence band), `page.tsx` (supervisor + 3 input doors + HUD). Refs: ACCompanion (IJCAI 2023, arXiv:2304.12939) ¬∑ Matchmaker (arXiv:2510.10087, Oct 2025) ¬∑ Dixon MATCH (2005).

### Seed B ‚Äî `382-anticipating-accompanist` (the ANTICIPATION axis)
**Brief:** "What if the accompanist ANTICIPATED you ‚Äî predicting where your next beat will land so it plays *with* you, like a duet partner leaning in, instead of always a beat behind?" Implements the ACCompanion's **tempo-prediction / online tempo-adaptation** component. Pipeline: online **DTW** (which score position you're at) ‚Üí a **predictive tempo model** (`tempo.ts`, the star): a **Kalman-style EMA** on inter-onset intervals maintains a beat-period estimate + a phase anchor nudged toward each real onset, then **forecasts the next onset time** = last onset + predicted period √ó next note's nominal beat count, tracking a rolling prediction error ‚Üí **anticipatory scheduling** in `audio.ts` (`accompanyAt(harmony, predictedWallMs, dynamics, period)` converts the predicted wall-clock time to the AudioContext clock and passes it as the `when` arg, so the chord is enqueued *ahead* of time and lands *with* you). Ode-to-Joy-in-D; the baked demo bakes a deliberate **abrupt tempo-step at note 10** (‚âà160‚Üí105 BPM) so the prediction-error trace **visibly spikes then re-converges** over 4‚Äì5 notes ‚Äî self-verifiable on a phone with no MIDI. WebGL2 scrolling 4-lane timeline: soloist onsets (amber), accompaniment at predicted times (violet), a prediction-error oscilloscope (red‚Üígreen as it locks), the DTW slope, and a bright **ghost prediction marker ahead of "now"** showing where it expects your next beat. **What was promising:** the most *musically ambitious* (the felt difference between "chases you" and "breathes with you"), and the ghost-marker viz is a genuinely fresh legibility object. **Why it didn't win:** the anticipation payoff is the **hardest to feel/verify** at a 06:30 phone glance (it's a sub-beat latency effect) and the headline expressive leap was 380's. **To resurrect:** layer the predictive tempo model onto 380 so the *expressive* accompaniment also arrives anticipatorily ‚Äî combine with Seed A and you have the full ACCompanion (reactive + robust + expressive + anticipatory). Files were: `score.ts` (Ode-to-Joy-D + tempo-step `makePerformance()`), `dtw.ts`, `tempo.ts`, `audio.ts` (`accompanyAt`), `gl.ts` (4-lane timeline + ghost marker), `page.tsx`. Refs: ACCompanion (IJCAI 2023, arXiv:2304.12939) ¬∑ Dixon MATCH (2005).

---

## ¬ß340 ‚Äî banked kids WIDE losers (both build-reviewed, grep-verified lab-first techniques)

### Seed ‚Äî `383-kids-coral-garden` (Diffusion-Limited Aggregation ‚Äî the calmest, most Resonance-toned)
**Brief:** "What if a 4-year-old could GROW a singing coral reef by gently shaking the tablet?" **Shake** (devicemotion accel magnitude = stir intensity) ‚Üí drifting glowing plankton random-walk through the water and **stick** when they touch the growing coral, accreting into a branching dendritic reef; **each new branch that locks plays one soft note up a D-Dorian scale**, so the coral you grow *is* the song ‚Äî it never resets, just blooms denser/calmer. **Technique:** genuine **Diffusion-Limited Aggregation** (Witten & Sander, *Phys. Rev. Lett.* 1981) ‚Äî stuck-particle set + Brownian walkers + stick-radius freeze + spatial-hash neighbour buckets + recorded branch-depth (the screening effect makes real branches, not blobs). **Output:** Canvas2D (matte underwater gradient, caustic shimmer, hue drifts pink‚Üímint by branch depth ‚Äî a *fresh* renderer, 0√ó in the last-10). **Tags:** shake INPUT ¬∑ Canvas2D OUTPUT ¬∑ DLA TECHNIQUE ¬∑ kids-underwater-calm D-Dorian VIBE. Fallbacks: pointer-drag "swish" + 3s-idle synthetic breathing current (self-grows hands-free). Files were: `dla.ts` (CoralSim), `audio.ts` (ReefAudio, drone + D-Dorian bells + limiter), `page.tsx` (3 input doors + renderer), `README.md`. **What was promising:** the calmest, most parent-tolerable, most "looks like Resonance" of the three; real accreting *memory* (the jury's kids-lane ask after `350` regressed to a memory-less poke-toy); shake is a fresh input (last tilt/shake kids piece was `360`/`303`). **Why it didn't win:** the *wow* is gentler than 384's chaos‚Üíunison emergence; DLA growth is beautiful but less of a single legible "magic moment." **To resurrect:** ship as the next kids fire ‚Äî it's build-clean and the obvious calm-lane companion to 384's emergence. Ref: Witten & Sander 1981.

### Seed ‚Äî `385-kids-ant-garden` (ant-colony stigmergy ‚Äî the most subsystems, the riskiest demo)
**Brief:** "What if a 4-year-old could wave at the camera to drop magic flowers, and a swarm of glowing ants would build singing trails between them?" **Camera frame-difference motion** ‚Üí a glowing flower (food source) blooms wherever the child moves; a swarm of ants forages from a nest, and via **pheromone deposition + diffusion + evaporation** reinforced nest‚Üîflower **trails self-organize** and then fade when a flower exhausts; **each established trail sustains a soft D-Dorian drone** (strength = trail strength), so the trail network is a slowly-shifting living chord. **Technique:** genuine **ant-colony stigmergy / ACO** ‚Äî two pheromone layers (HOME/FOOD), sensor-biased (L/C/R) wandering, deposit-on-walk, pickup-at-flower, return-to-nest; **distinct from reaction-diffusion** *and* from `327-physarum-choir`'s slime-mold field (discrete foraging agents, not a continuous trail-field). **Output:** three.js (top-down ortho, pheromone `DataTexture` + shader plane, point ants colored by carrying-state, glow flowers, pulsing nest). **Tags:** camera-motion INPUT ¬∑ three.js OUTPUT ¬∑ ant-stigmergy TECHNIQUE ¬∑ kids-night-garden D-Dorian VIBE. **4 subsystems** (camera + colony sim + three.js + audio) ‚Äî the ambition-floor #2 piece of the fire. Privacy: camera analysis-only frame-diff, never recorded/stored/uploaded. Fallbacks: tap-to-plant + 3s-idle synthetic flower bloomer (self-organizes hands-free). Files were: `colony.ts` (stigmergy engine + RGBA texture packing), `audio.ts` (drone + per-flower sustained voices + limiter), `page.tsx` (camera + three.js scene), `README.md`. **What was promising:** the most ambitious (4 subsystems, real emergence), and camera input is fresh (1√ó in last-10). **Why it didn't win:** camera adds a permission step that hurts the 06:30 phone-review fit (mitigated by auto-demo but still); the trail-emergence payoff is *slower* and less instantly legible than 384's sync; and stigmergy sits adjacent to the already-shipped `327-physarum-choir` slime-mold lineage. **To resurrect:** the strongest "AI-pipeline-free camera kids game" ‚Äî ship once a calmer/faster trail-tuning pass is done, or as the kids camera-lane companion to `368-rainbow-quest`. Refs: Deneubourg et al. 1990 (double-bridge) ¬∑ Dorigo ACO 1992.

## ¬ß342 ‚Äî banked kids DEEP losers (one concept "a singing DLA coral reef grown by shaking", 3 approaches; winner = 390-kids-coral-tide)

### Seed ‚Äî `kids-coral-garden` (the CANONICAL directional-DLA take ‚Äî cleanest, most immersive)
**Brief:** "Grow a singing coral reef by shaking the tablet ‚Äî and watch it climb up from the seabed toward you." **Shake** (devicemotion jerk = stir) ‚Üí glowing plankton random-walk *down* with a settling bias and **stick** on contact, accreting a branching dendritic reef that grows **upward** from a seabed; each locked branch rings one D-Dorian note up the scale, depth ‚Üí scale degree. **Technique:** directional DLA (Witten & Sander 1981 + ACS Omega settling-bias enhancement); spatial-hash buckets, branch-depth tracking. **Output:** full-screen Canvas2D ‚Äî deep teal‚Üíindigo underwater gradient, caustic shimmer, plankton glow (`lighter`), pink‚Üímint coral hue by depth, per-lock "pop" brighten. **Tags:** shake INPUT ¬∑ Canvas2D OUTPUT ¬∑ directional-DLA TECHNIQUE ¬∑ kids-underwater-calm D-Dorian VIBE. Fallbacks: pointer "swish" + 3s-idle breathing current (self-grows hands-free). **What was promising:** the cleanest, most immersive full-screen render and the most parent-tolerable; the directional climb (up from a seabed) is more legible than an isotropic blob. **Why it didn't win:** no *audio* legibility hook ‚Äî it's the gentle-wow problem that has lost DLA-coral curation three times (323/361/383); 390 fixed exactly this with chord-stacking. **To resurrect:** ship as the calm full-screen companion to 390, OR fold its caustic/pop visuals into 390. Files were: dla.ts, audio.ts, page.tsx, README.md. Ref: Witten & Sander 1981.

### Seed ‚Äî `kids-reef-glow` (bioluminescent night swarm, size-dependent diffusivity ‚Äî most mesmerizing)
**Brief:** "Shake to stir a glowing night-ocean of plankton that drift like a living swarm and crystallize into a bioluminescent singing reef." **Shake** ‚Üí a current sweeps a swarm of glowing plankton with **size-dependent diffusivity** (big = slow lazy drift, small = fast darting) that settle downward and stick; each lock pulses a bioluminescent flash + rings a D-Dorian note (depth ‚Üí degree). **Technique:** DLA with size-dependent diffusivity (Witten & Sander 1981; ACS Omega realistic-DLA). **Output:** full-screen Canvas2D, near-black ocean, additive `lighter` glow, teal‚Üíviolet by depth, flash-on-lock blooms. **Tags:** shake INPUT ¬∑ Canvas2D OUTPUT ¬∑ size-dependent-DLA TECHNIQUE ¬∑ kids-bioluminescent-night D-Dorian VIBE. Fallbacks: pointer-drag current + 3s-idle roaming current. **What was promising:** the most *mesmerizing* ‚Äî the living swarm before the stick is genuinely alive; the size-dependent diffusivity is a real research-driven twist. **Why it didn't win:** the bioluminescent-glow aesthetic sits adjacent to the lab's well-represented cool-glow lineage (firefly-song/glow-bug/ocean-presence), and the swarm payoff is less *legible* than 390's chord-stacking. **To resurrect:** ship as the night-mode glow companion; or graft the size-dependent-diffusivity walkers + additive bloom onto 390. Files were: dla.ts, audio.ts, page.tsx, README.md. Refs: Witten & Sander 1981 ¬∑ ACS Omega realistic-DLA.

## ¬ß344 ‚Äî banked from cycle 344 WIDE (kids listening mirrors; winner shipped = 393-kids-vowel-color)

Cycle 344 ran WIDE: three kids "the machine shows what it heard" mirrors, each a *different* analysis + a different non-GPU renderer + a non-D-Dorian tonal world (JURY 2026-06-07 provocation #6: turn the adult analysis-reflex into a kids toy; bans on WebGL2/three.js OUTPUT, MIDI/touch INPUT, D-Dorian). All three were built clean and folder-isolated. 393 won (freshest research anchor + most-avoided renderer + lab-first formant technique). The two below are build-reviewed and ready to resurrect ‚Äî both retained in `/tmp/dream-losers/` this fire.

### 392-kids-voice-mirror (banked ‚Äî the robust pitch sibling)
- **Brief:** child sings ‚Üí real-time **autocorrelation pitch detection** ‚Üí a living **SVG ribbon-creature** traces the melody (pitch‚Üíy, RMS‚Üíwidth/glow, friendly face at the head) ‚Üí a warm **5-limit just-intonation choir on G3** sings the recorded contour back. Renderer = inline SVG (no canvas/WebGL). Named ref = **"Visible Speech" sound spectrograph (Potter/Kopp/Green, Bell Labs, 1947)**.
- **Why strong:** the most *reliable* of the three ‚Äî autocorrelation pitch is robust on any voice (unlike formants), and the "sing ‚Üí hear your own wobbly hum returned as a real choir" payoff is the most emotionally direct. JI-on-G3 sing-back is a genuine non-D-Dorian tonal world.
- **Why it lost:** pitch detection already exists in the lab (331/341/345), so the *concept* is the most familiar of the three ‚Äî closest to existing pitch toys (341-star-pair, 322-voice-garden). Lowest surprise; named ref is foundational (1947), not recent.
- **Fix-first to resurrect:** swap autocorrelation for YIN/pYIN to kill plosive/breathy glitches; add the "conduct multiple stored ribbons at once for layered choir" and "two creatures = sibling/parent harmony" ideas from its README. Files in `/tmp/dream-losers/392-kids-voice-mirror/` (page.tsx 607 / pitch.ts / synth.ts).

### 394-kids-sound-monster (banked ‚Äî the most robust + most legible runner-up; STRONGEST resurrect)
- **Brief:** child's voice **timbre/dynamics** (RMS loudness + spectral centroid brightness + ZCR roughness) ‚Üí a **morphing SVG blob-monster** (soft/dark ‚Üí round/cool/calm; loud/bright ‚Üí spiky/hot/excited; size=loudness, spikiness=brightness) ‚Üí **purr‚Üîroar sing-back** (JI C3 4:5:6, lowpass cutoff tracks brightness). Renderer = morphing inline SVG path (fbm-perturbed Catmull-Rom). Named ref = **Bouba/Kiki cross-modal sound-symbolism (K√∂hler 1929; Ramachandran & Hubbard 2001)**.
- **Why strong:** the most *robust* analysis (loudness+centroid are trivially reliable on a high child voice ‚Äî exactly where 393's formant detection is weakest) and the most *legible* magic moment (round‚Üîspiky creature locks in within ~50ms; the same "instant legible payoff" the JURY praised in 384-firefly). Cross-modal sound-symbolism is lab-first. A near-tie with 393.
- **Why it lost:** named ref is foundational (1929), not recent ‚Äî so it doesn't pay the JURY's freshness debt the way 393's AURORA (2026) does; and SVG output is a renderer the lab has touched, where 393's pure-CSS color field is the more radical rut-break. On reliability alone it arguably should have won ‚Äî noted honestly.
- **Fix-first / how it deepens 393:** **fold 394's centroid-robustness lesson into 393** ‚Äî when formant classification confidence is low (high kids voices, /i/ vs /u/), fall back to a brightness‚Üíhue mapping so the color *always* responds even when the vowel is uncertain (rescues 393's one real weakness with 394's one real strength). Standalone resurrect: add autocorrelation pitch so the purr/roar transposes to the child's own fundamental; SVG‚ÜíPNG "save my monster" snapshot. Files in `/tmp/dream-losers/394-kids-sound-monster/` (page.tsx / analysis.ts / blob.ts / synth.ts).

## ¬ß345 ‚Äî banked from cycle 345 DEEP (spatial-audio, one off-screen concept; winner shipped = 394-soundfield-room)

Cycle 345 ran DEEP on JURY prov #5 (the untouched off-screen/spatial-audio/haptic shelf): three orthogonal spatial-audio attacks on one concept ‚Äî "an eyes-closed room you navigate by turning your head, headphones on." All three built clean + folder-isolated, all dodge every jury ban (device-orientation INPUT not MIDI/touch; binaural-audio + dim non-WebGL radar OUTPUT; just-intonation VIBE not D-Dorian). 394 won (true coherent-field ambisonics = the genuine lab-first leap past existing per-source binaural pieces, + the tightest/freshest research chain). The two below are build-reviewed and ready to resurrect ‚Äî both retained in `/tmp/dream-losers/` this fire.

### 396-soundwalk (banked ‚Äî STRONGEST resurrect / the designated 394 cycle-2 deepening)
- **Brief:** an eyes-closed binaural room you move THROUGH, not just turn inside. 6 fixed-position HRTF `PannerNode` voices on an E2 just-intonation overtone palette scattered on a plane; the virtual listener **drifts forward continuously** while device-orientation yaw (or drag) sets the heading ‚Äî so voices approach, bloom (gain swell + arpeggio shimmer <3.5m), and recede behind you. **Lab-first haptic leg:** `navigator.vibrate()` fires a voice-specific rhythmic pattern on close approach (<5m) ‚Äî cross-modal sound+touch embodiment. Auto-pilot waypoint demo tours all voices hands-free; drag-to-steer + rose-notice fallbacks. Files: `page.tsx` 383 / `synth.ts` 262 (SoundwalkSynth, DynamicsCompressor limiter, triggerBloom) / `walk.ts` 287 (WalkEngine ‚Äî locomotion, orientation/drag/auto-demo heading, proximity events) / `map.ts` 114 (string-built top-down SVG map). Named ref = **Janet Cardiff audio walks** + W3C Web Audio spatialization + W3C Vibration API.
- **Why strong:** the lab's **first haptic output** (a genuinely new sensory modality, grep-clean) and the most *embodied* of the three ‚Äî directly the "haptic" half of jury prov #5 that 394 didn't take. "You walk through the music and *feel* each voice" is the highest-surprise of the slate.
- **Why it lost:** renderer/technique reuse per-source PannerNode HRTF (not a coherent rotatable field, so less lab-first than 394's true ambisonics); named refs foundational, so it pays less of the jury's #5 freshness debt than 394's Jun-2026 ambisonics papers.
- **Fix-first / how it deepens 394:** **this IS the 394 cycle-2 plan** ‚Äî fold locomotion (translate the listener *through* a true B-format ambisonic field, not just rotate it) + the haptic confirmation into 394. Standalone resurrect: tune the drift speed + proximity thresholds on real ears; add Karel's *Welcome Home* stems as the scattered voices. Files in `/tmp/dream-losers/396-soundwalk/`.

### 395-listener-orbit (banked ‚Äî the lightweight baseline / fallback path)
- **Brief:** "rotate the AudioListener, not the field" ‚Äî 6 voices as fixed-position HRTF `PannerNode`s around the origin (A-Lydian just intonation, 220/275/309/330/385/440 Hz, ratios 1/1¬∑5/4¬∑45/32¬∑3/2¬∑7/4¬∑2/1); device-orientation yaw/pitch drives `AudioListener.forwardX/Y/Z` AudioParams via smooth ramps (deprecated `setOrientation` fallback). The browser's built-in HRTF does the binaural ‚Äî minimal DSP. Inline-SVG radar with a yaw-driven compass arrow; auto-demo sweep + drag fallback. Files: `page.tsx` / `synth.ts` / `orientation.ts`. Named ref = W3C Web Audio PannerNode/AudioListener spec + Google Resonance Audio listener-pose model.
- **Why strong:** the simplest, most-robust path to head-tracked binaural ‚Äî if 394's hand-rolled 8-speaker decode proves CPU-heavy on mobile, this is the graceful fallback that gets 90% of the effect for 10% of the nodes.
- **Why it lost:** per-source PannerNode HRTF already exists in the lab (7-spatial, 29-scene-spatial), so it's the most *familiar* concept of the three ‚Äî lowest surprise, foundational ref only.
- **Fix-first to resurrect:** use it as the explicit A/B comparison against 394 ("does a true coherent field actually sound different from rotating the listener?" ‚Äî a real perceptual question worth a side-by-side). Files in `/tmp/dream-losers/395-listener-orbit/`.

## ¬ß346 ‚Äî banked from cycle 346 (kids WIDE ‚Äî 2 non-winners; winner shipped = 397-kids-crystal-bloom)

Cycle 346 ran WIDE: three kids pieces, each in a DIFFERENT foreign tonal world √ó a DIFFERENT non-GPU renderer √ó a DIFFERENT non-banned input (JURY 2026-06-07 bans: WebGL2/three.js OUTPUT, MIDI/touch INPUT, D-Dorian VIBE; provocations #1 force-a-renderer-you-avoid + #2 foreign-tonal-world). All three were build-reviewed clean and folder-isolated. 397 won (cleanest research‚Üíbuild chain + the expensive #5 recent-research criterion + the under-served breath/calm niche). The two below are build-reviewed and ready to resurrect.

### 398-kids-rainbow-roll ‚Äî tilt a ball of light around a gamelan courtyard, in PURE DOM/CSS `[banked ‚Äî cycle 346 kids WIDE explorer; the most literal answer to jury prov #1]`
- **Question:** "What if a 4yo could TILT the tablet to roll a glowing ball of light around a courtyard of gongs ‚Äî and every gong it bumps rings a shimmering Balinese **slendro** tone?"
- **Tags** (clean dodge of all bans): INPUT = **device tilt** (DeviceOrientation beta/gamma ‚Üí gravity vector; pointer-drag + auto-demo wandering-gravity fallbacks) ¬∑ OUTPUT = **pure DOM/CSS** (HTML divs + CSS transforms/box-shadow/keyframe ripples ‚Äî NO canvas, NO svg, NO WebGL) ¬∑ TECHNIQUE = 2D gravity physics (velocity + friction + speed-clamp + wall + circle-vs-circle gong collision) + gamelan synth ¬∑ VIBE = bright/playful, foreign-tonal.
- **The tuning:** Balinese **slendro** ‚Äî ~5 roughly-equal but non-equal-tempered steps, octave **stretched to ~1208¬¢**; each gong is two slightly-detuned voices producing the slow beating Balinese musicians call **ombak** ("waves"); soft mallet attack + medium decay; sustained drone so it's never silent; limiter. Sits *between the cracks* of 12-TET so it sounds genuinely non-Western. Ref: Balinese/Javanese gamelan tuning; Colin McPhee, *Music in Bali*.
- **Ambition:** #2 (4 subsystems: tilt physics + DOM/CSS render + gamelan synth + collision) + #3 (named cultural reference) + foreign tonal world + rare renderer (pure DOM/CSS, used ~once in the lab). Build files were: `page.tsx` (DOM courtyard + RAF physics + gravity-source selection + iOS motion permission), `gamelan.ts` (slendro table + inharmonic detuned-pair gong synth + drone + limiter), `tilt.ts` (DeviceOrientation permission + smoothed gravity + liveness check), `README.md`.
- **Why it didn't win:** the purest answer to jury #1 and very fun, but tilt-marble is a familiar lab interaction (cf. loved `169-marble-run`, `384` tilt) and it leans on a *foundational* cultural reference rather than a *recent* one, so it pays less of the #5 freshness debt the jury flagged. **Revive:** ship as the next kids tilt/DOM fire ‚Äî it's the cleanest pure-DOM/CSS piece the lab has. Reconstructable from this spec (folder `rm -rf`'d; numbers: max committed folder is now 397, so renumber on revival).

### 399-kids-aurora-step ‚Äî dance at the camera to paint a whole-tone aurora `[banked ‚Äî cycle 346 kids WIDE explorer]`
- **Question:** "What if a 4yo could DANCE or wave at the camera and their MOTION painted a flowing aurora across the sky while the places they move ring dreamy **whole-tone** chimes?"
- **Tags** (clean dodge of all bans): INPUT = **camera frame-difference motion** (getUserMedia ‚Üí 12√ó8 luminance-diff grid; ANALYSIS-ONLY, never recorded/stored/shown/uploaded; phantom-attractor auto-demo) ¬∑ OUTPUT = **Canvas2D** (additive `lighter`-glow aurora ribbons drifting upward) ¬∑ TECHNIQUE = frame-diff motion energy per cell ‚Üí particle spawn + debounced chime trigger (screen height ‚Üí scale degree) ¬∑ VIBE = dreamy/floating aurora, foreign-tonal.
- **The tuning:** **whole-tone** scale (6 equal steps, no perfect fifth, no leading tone ‚Äî the floating Debussy/Impressionist sound), mapped over ~2 octaves; soft chime voices (sine + partials, slow attack, long tail) + a two-note whole-tone pad so it's never silent; limiter. Ref: Claude Debussy / whole-tone Impressionism.
- **Ambition:** #2 (4 subsystems: camera + frame-diff analysis + Canvas2D aurora + whole-tone synth) + #3 (named reference) + foreign tonal world. Build files were: `page.tsx` (Canvas2D render + RAF + camera‚Üîauto-demo orchestration + privacy notice), `motion.ts` (`MotionTracker`: getUserMedia + frame-diff grid), `chimes.ts` (`ChimeEngine`: whole-tone tuning + chime synth + pad + limiter + per-degree debounce), `README.md`.
- **Why it didn't win:** camera-motion‚Üíglow is the lab's comfort zone (jury: 13/15 are screen-camera-ish) and Canvas2D additive glow is a well-represented aesthetic ‚Äî least *differentiated* despite the foreign whole-tone world; camera permission is also the jury-flagged 06:30-phone-review risk (mitigated by a strong phantom-attractor auto-demo). **Revive:** strong as the kids camera-lane companion (pairs with the loved `101-camera-song`/`104-mirror-draw` lineage); or graft its aurora field onto a future motion piece. Reconstructable from this spec (folder `rm -rf`'d; renumber on revival).

## ¬ß347 ‚Äî banked from cycle 347 (adult DEEP ‚Äî 1 non-winner; winner shipped = 400-soundwalk-room)

Cycle 347 ran DEEP on the spatial-audio thread's cycle 2 ‚Äî one concept (*walk through the ambisonic room + feel each voice*), two technical attacks. Both build-reviewed clean + folder-isolated. 400 won (audio-first ‚Üí robust on every device; the genuine 6DoF-translation leap past 394's rotation-only field; richer legible running view for the 06:30 glance). The runner-up below is the stronger *pure-embodiment* concept and is the designated next off-screen / cycle-3 candidate.

### 401-feel-the-room ‚Äî haptic-LED "metal detector for sound": feel your way to the voices eyes-closed `[banked ‚Äî cycle 347 adult DEEP explorer; the lab's most genuinely off-screen concept]`
- **Question:** "What if you could find your way through a spatial-audio room with your HANDS ‚Äî eyes closed ‚Äî a parking-sensor pulse train quickening toward the nearest voice, audio confirming as you arrive?"
- **Tags** (clean dodge of all jury bans): INPUT = auto-walk homing + drag-to-steer + device-orientation heading (NOT MIDI, NOT touch-as-instrument) ¬∑ OUTPUT = binaural HRTF + **Vibration-API haptics as the LEAD channel** + a deliberately dim/optional Canvas2D map (NO WebGL/three.js) ¬∑ TECHNIQUE = path-distance soundfield navigation + a continuous nearest-voice haptic pulse-train (audio‚Üívibrotactile, Sound2Hap-style) ¬∑ VIBE = ritual/echolocation, just-intonation D-root overtones, screen-optional.
- **The mechanic:** ~6 JI voices at fixed spots. A homing auto-pilot slews heading toward a target voice and steps forward; the nearest voice drives a **continuous `navigator.vibrate` pulse train whose cycle quickens as you near it** (metal-detector / white-cane); arrival fires a distinct triple-buzz + a harmonic bloom, then it retargets the next voice. The whole point is to work **eyes-closed** ‚Äî the haptic is the primary wayfinding sense, audio the confirmation. Built files were: `engine.ts` (`RoomEngine`: 6 JI voices ‚Üí amp/LFO ‚Üí bloom ‚Üí distance-LP ‚Üí inverse-distance gain ‚Üí per-voice HRTF PannerNode ‚Üí limiter; `update()` re-spatialiser returning the nearest-voice descriptor; `planHaptics` pulse-train mapping; `ARRIVAL_PATTERN`), `page.tsx` (homing auto-pilot + pulse-train scheduler that re-issues vibrate on a cycle-sized interval + dim map with proximity-pulsing target ring as the visual fallback + drag/tilt manual layers + full teardown incl. `vibrate(0)`), `README.md`.
- **Ambition:** same spine as 400 ‚Äî #1 (haptic output, lab-first) + #2 (‚â•3 subsystems) + #3 (Google soundfield-navigation ¬∑ Sound2Hap arXiv:2601.12245 CHI 2026 ¬∑ Cardiff / white-cane) + #4 (cycle of the spatial thread) + #5 (Sound2Hap, recent).
- **Why it didn't win:** the more surprising/novel *experience* and the most literally off-screen piece the lab has ‚Äî but its entire thesis (FEEL where to walk) is **invisible/unfelt on a non-vibrating device** (most desktops, iOS Safari), exactly the 06:30-review fragility 400 avoids by leading with audio; and on a phone the auto-walk + audio reads close to 400's. **Revive:** ship as the next dedicated off-screen / haptic-led fire (it directly answers jury prov #5's embodiment gap), OR fold its quickening homing-pulse into 400 as an optional "guide me to a voice" mode. Reconstructable from this spec (folder `rm -rf`'d; max committed folder is now 400 ‚Üí renumber on revival).

## ¬ß349 ‚Äî banked from cycle 349 (adult DEEP ‚Äî 2 non-winners; winner shipped = 404-comma-pump)

Cycle 349 ran DEEP on a single fresh foreign-tonal concept ‚Äî **adaptive / dynamic just intonation** (the lab had only *fixed-ratio* JI: 394/397/400 + the *static* click Tonnetz 37-ratio-lab) ‚Äî via three approaches: comma-drift (404, won), roughness-pedagogy (405), organic glide-drone (406). All three Canvas2D-only (jury #1), 5-limit JI (jury #2), generative-not-analysis (jury #6), autonomous (no MIDI/keyboard/tap). 404 won on: the biggest *concept* ("music that can never come home"), genuine long-form **stateful** drift (the under-served menu shelf ‚Äî the cents-from-home counter sinks indefinitely), the strongest "huh" surprise (pure tuning literally can't close the loop), AND an immediate JI‚áÑ12-TET audible payoff. Both seeds below build-reviewed clean + folder-isolated; `rm -rf`'d (max committed folder after this cycle = 404, renumber on revival).

### 405-pure-lock ‚Äî see AND hear sensory roughness fall to zero (Plomp‚ÄìLevelt meter) `[banked ‚Äî cycle 349 adult DEEP explorer; the lab's most legible/pedagogical JI piece]`
- **Question:** "Can you *watch* dissonance vanish? A held chord glides from 12-TET into pure 5-limit JI while a live sensory-roughness meter collapses toward zero as the partials lock."
- **Tags** (clean dodge of all jury bans): INPUT = autonomous chord-cycler + a manual 12-TET‚áÑJI morph slider + Hold-chord toggle (NOT MIDI, NOT tap-as-instrument) ¬∑ OUTPUT = **Canvas2D** roughness curve + beating strip (NO WebGL/three.js) ¬∑ TECHNIQUE = adaptive JI + **Plomp‚ÄìLevelt sensory-dissonance** computed over all sounding-partial pairs ¬∑ VIBE = clinical/instructional, foreign-tonal (roots Eb/Ab/F/B/Gb/E).
- **The mechanic:** each chord (Maj/min/dom7/Maj7/min7/sus2) starts every note at its 12-TET freq, then glides ~3s to its pure 5-limit ratio; ~4‚Äì6 real harmonic partials per note so the beating is genuine; roughness = Œ£ over partial pairs of `a1¬∑a2¬∑(e^{-3.5x} ‚àí e^{-5.75x})`, `x = (0.24/(0.0207¬∑fmin+18.96))¬∑(f2‚àíf1)` ‚Äî high under 12-TET mistuning, ~0 when JI locks. Built files: `page.tsx` (624 lines), `tuning.ts`, `roughness.ts`, `audio.ts` (additive partials + limiter), `README.md`.
- **Ambition:** #1 (lab-first roughness meter + first dynamic JI) + #2 (‚â•3 subsystems: adaptive-tuner + partial-pair roughness engine + additive synth + Canvas2D) + #3 (Plomp & Levelt 1965 ¬∑ Sethares *Tuning Timbre Spectrum Scale* ¬∑ Pivotuner arXiv:2306.03873 ¬∑ Ben Johnston).
- **Why it didn't win:** the clearest *explainer* in the set and the best single-glance see+hear demo, but it reads as a pedagogical instrument rather than a "massively bigger" *experience* ‚Äî 404's never-comes-home concept is the larger idea. **Revive:** ship as the legible/instructional flagship the jury keeps asking the lab to feed (cf. 358-beat-mirror lineage), or fold the roughness meter into 404 as an optional overlay so you can *watch* the comma-pump chords lock pure. Reconstructable from this spec.

### 406-drifting-choir ‚Äî sustained voices that keep gliding into pure harmony (Pivotuner drone) `[banked ‚Äî cycle 349 adult DEEP explorer; the lab's most immersive JI piece]`
- **Question:** "What does it *feel* like to hear a choir keep sliding into purity? 5‚Äì7 sustained additive voices retune in real-time to pure 5-limit ratios whenever a wandering pivot moves ‚Äî a soft shimmer of beats fading to silence as each voice locks."
- **Tags** (clean dodge of all jury bans): INPUT = autonomous ambient + density/drift-speed sliders + canvas drag-to-nudge-pivot (NOT MIDI, NOT tap-as-instrument) ¬∑ OUTPUT = **Canvas2D** drifting comet/orb field (NO WebGL/three.js) ¬∑ TECHNIQUE = **Pivotuner-style adaptive JI** with audible glissando re-tuning + multi-timescale generative pivot path ¬∑ VIBE = organic/ambient/Anadol, foreign-tonal, long-form.
- **The mechanic:** a `pivotHz` wanders on three incommensurable timescales (sub-pivot ¬±2 semitone sinusoid ~60‚Äì120s ¬∑ 10-chord-palette rotation 8‚Äì55s ¬∑ 6 base pivots Eb3/Ab3/B3/F3/Db4/Gb3); on each move every voice picks the nearest pure 5-limit ratio (across ‚àí1/0/+1 octave layers) and `linearRampToValueAtTime`-glides fundamental+partials over 1‚Äì2.5s ‚Üí you HEAR the beating slow to zero; a "lock" emits an audio+visual bloom. Genuinely different at minute 5 vs minute 1. Built files: `page.tsx` (705 lines), `voices.ts` (additive voices + vibrato + warmth LP + retune glide), `tuning.ts`, `README.md`.
- **Ambition:** #1 (lab-first dynamic JI glide-retune) + #2 (‚â•3 subsystems: voice bank + adaptive-tuner + generative pivot-path + Canvas2D) + #3 (Pivotuner arXiv:2306.03873 ¬∑ La Monte Young *The Well-Tuned Piano* ¬∑ Harry Partch ¬∑ √âliane Radigue) + long-form/stateful.
- **Why it didn't win:** the most beautiful and immersive of the three and the strongest long-form *drone*, but the glide-into-purity shimmer is subtle ‚Äî it can be hard to perceive at a 06:30 phone glance where 404's sinking counter + JI/ET toggle are immediately legible. **Revive:** the designated next **adult long-form JI** DEEP target (pairs with the loved spectral-drift lineage 267/243/323); or use Karel's *Welcome Home* piano as the voices' source so his real music drifts into purity. Reconstructable from this spec.

## ¬ß355 ‚Äî 2026-06-08 (UTC) ¬∑ banked from the cycle-355 WIDE fire (2 strong non-winners ‚Äî resurrect-ready)

**`419-webgpu-storm` ‚Äî the lab's FIRST real WebGPU compute shader** `queued` (HIGH priority ‚Äî the standing jury "first")
The single most-deferred "first" in the lab (JURY 2026-06-08 #3: "WebGPU compute is still never used ‚Äî claim it, on a non-mic input"). A **WGSL compute pipeline** advects a **500,000-particle storm** by **curl-noise** (incompressible 2D velocity = curl of a value-noise potential) each frame in a storage buffer, plus a pointer attractor; an additive **point-list render pass** draws it (blue/white, no warm tones); field turbulence + pointer force drive an **abrasive, non-tonal audio bed** (bandpass-swept noise + three detuned sub-saws at 41.3/42.9/57.7 Hz + FM grit, all through a compressor ‚Äî refuses to resolve). **Degrades gracefully:** no `navigator.gpu` ‚Üí an amber notice + a **standalone CPU Canvas2D fallback** (4k particles, same curl-noise, same audio). The full piece (`page.tsx`, `gpu.ts` with both WGSL shaders inline, `fallback.ts`, `audio.ts`, README) was **build-reviewed clean** this fire (the builder's `next build` compiled it; folder removed, banked as text). **Why it didn't win cycle 355:** its headline ‚Äî the GPU compute path ‚Äî is **unverifiable in the sandbox** AND degrades to the CPU Canvas2D fallback on **mobile Safari (no WebGPU)**, so Karel's 06:30 *phone* review would likely see the fallback, not the headline. **Revive on a desktop-review cycle** (or once you confirm Karel reviews on a WebGPU-capable browser). Ambition ‚â•2/5: never-used-technique(#1, WebGPU compute) + ‚â•3-subsystems(#2, compute sim + render pipeline + reactive audio + CPU fallback) + named-ref(#3, Bridson 2007 curl-noise ¬∑ Bileam Tschepe / TouchDesigner GPU particles). Diversity: pointer/autonomous INPUT (non-mic) ¬∑ **WebGPU OUTPUT** (never used) ¬∑ GPU curl-noise advection TECHNIQUE ¬∑ storm/abrasive VIBE ‚Äî clean of all bans. Loved `130-tsl-particle-compute`‚ù§Ô∏è is soft support. Renumber on revival (max folder now 418).

**`420-test-pattern` ‚Äî a Ryoji-Ikeda test-tone piece that flatly REFUSES to resolve** `queued`
The purest answer to JURY 2026-06-08 #2 ("build something that refuses to resolve ‚Ä¶ nothing abrasive, glitched, or left unresolved ‚Ä¶ make the one that *doesn't* harmonize"). 8 oscillator pairs log-spaced **40 Hz‚Äì7.8 kHz**, each pair detuned **1.3‚Äì7.2 Hz** (share no common divisor ‚Üí inescapable, never-locking **beating**); a square-LFO **gate** chops them into clicks/stutters (30‚Äì250 ms period, re-randomized each tick so no rhythm ever stabilizes); fast frequency **sweeps**; pointer X ‚Üí active band, Y ‚Üí gate density / beat rate. Visual = stark monochrome **64√ó32 data-grid** + barcode stripe + measurement-tick rulers + waveform trace + numeric readouts (Ikeda *test pattern* / *data.tron* aesthetic). Frequencies treated as **measurement data, not pitch** ‚Äî no scale, no chord, ever. Build-reviewed clean (`engine.ts`, `draw.ts`, `page.tsx`, README; tsc clean); folder removed, banked as text. **Why it didn't win cycle 355:** thinnest on the ambition floor (oscillators + canvas ‚âà 2 subsystems; no genuinely lab-first *technique* ‚Äî beating/gating are standard), where the seismic winner also satisfies the jury's *named-untouched* data-sonification menu. **Revive** as the lab's standing "abrasive register" counter-piece whenever the consonant-resolving rut needs breaking again. Diversity: touch INPUT ¬∑ Canvas2D OUTPUT ¬∑ test-tone/beating/gating TECHNIQUE ¬∑ clinical-abrasive-unresolved VIBE ‚Äî clean of all bans. Renumber on revival.

## ¬ß358 ‚Äî 2026-06-08 (UTC) ¬∑ banked from the cycle-358 DEEP kids fire (2 build-reviewed non-winners; winner shipped = 423-kids-face-beat)

Cycle 358 ran **DEEP**: one concept ‚Äî *make a BEAT/sound with your FACE, not a tune* (MediaPipe FaceLandmarker blendshapes ‚Üí percussion/timbre, the lab's first face‚Üírhythm mapping; answers JURY 2026-06-08 #1 rhythm-not-tuning + #6 face-tracking) ‚Äî attacked three ways, each in a different non-banned renderer. All three were built clean (tsc + ESLint zero in their folders) and folder-isolated. `423` won (most kid-legible discrete kit + the cleanest output-diversity dodge, raw WebGL2). The two below are build-reviewed and ready to resurrect (folders `mv`'d out and removed ‚Äî reconstructable from these specs + their READMEs were written).

### Seed ‚Äî `424-kids-face-jam` (face ‚Üí continuous NOISE/FOLEY texture, three.js point-cloud ‚Äî the "go weird" sibling) `[banked ‚Äî strong; the timbre/abrasive take the jury keeps asking for]`
**Brief:** "What if your face was a CONDUCTOR of a living texture ‚Äî open your mouth for a big WHOOSH, raise your eyebrows for sparkle/fizz, scrunch up for crunch ‚Äî a soundscape you sculpt continuously with your expression?" **Continuous** (not threshold/discrete): EMA-smoothed blendshapes drive five parallel **noise/filter foley layers** ‚Äî `jawOpen`‚ÜíLP-cutoff+gain of a wind/whoosh; `browUp`‚ÜíHP shimmer/fizz + tremolo; `mouthPucker/Funnel`‚Üíresonant bandpass "wooo" sweep; `cheekPuff`‚Üísub rumble; `noseSneer/browDown`‚ÜíWaveShaper **crunch** ‚Äî all through a `DynamicsCompressor` limiter, with an always-on faint bed and `setTargetAtTime` smoothing (no clicks). **NO pitch, NO scale, pure TIMBRE** ‚Äî it cannot be hummed. **Output:** **three.js** ‚Äî a glowing additive **point-cloud of the 478 face landmarks** (hue/size driven by foley intensity) + 200 drifting ambient particles that swirl harder as the texture brightens; resources disposed on unmount. Files were `page.tsx` (561 ln), `audio.ts` (302 ln), `face.ts` (110 ln), `README.md`. **Self-assessment (builder):** unambiguously continuous texture (no oscillator/quantized note anywhere); hands-free ghost-face auto-demo within ~2s; tsc+ESLint clean at `--max-warnings=0`. **What was promising:** the **most surprising / most "weird"** of the three ‚Äî the strongest answer to the jury's deeper rut ("learned to go deep, forgotten to go weird"; ~13/15 resolve to consonance) and the only one whose subject is *timbre/noise* rather than *rhythm*. three.js OUTPUT is also a clean diversity dodge (0√ó in the last-10). **Why it didn't win:** for a **4-year-old**, continuous foley is **less immediately legible** than one-face-one-drum (the same reason `419`'s discrete kit beat its continuous siblings) ‚Äî a toddler may not connect "scrunch = crunch" as fast as "open mouth = BOOM." **To resurrect:** ship as the cycle-2 **timbre/"go-weird"** lane piece (kids *or* adult ‚Äî it's genuinely an adult Ikeda/Akten-adjacent texture instrument too); or fold its landmark point-cloud render into a future face piece. Refs: Expotion (arXiv:2507.04955, 2025); Ekman-Friesen FACS 1978; MediaPipe FaceLandmarker. Renumber on revival (max folder now 423).

### Seed ‚Äî `425-kids-face-loop` (face ‚Üí looping step-sequencer with compositional MEMORY, Canvas2D) `[banked ‚Äî the jury's kids-lane "memory/consequence" ask, done as a face toy]`
**Brief:** "BUILD a beat one funny face at a time ‚Äî make a face to record a drum into a loop, add another face for a second layer, watch your beat grow and repeat forever." A steady **8-step loop at 96 BPM** with a sweeping playhead; threshold+rising-edge+cooldown face detection (`jawOpen`‚ÜíKICK, `browUp`‚ÜíHAT, `smile`‚ÜíSHAKE, `cheekPuff`‚ÜíTOM) **records the drum onto the step nearest the playhead** (quantized), so it joins the loop and repeats; a **4√ó8 grid** shows the accumulating pattern, cells light as they fire. The loop **grows** and keeps playing hands-free; a big ‚â•64px **Clear** button wipes it. Pure percussion through a `DynamicsCompressor` limiter; look-ahead scheduler. **Output:** **Canvas2D** (mirrored webcam + the loop grid + per-hit bursts + expression score bars). Files were `page.tsx` (821 ln), `audio.ts` (253 ln), `face.ts` (173 ln), `README.md`. **Self-assessment (builder):** loop visibly grows (ghost demo stamps kick‚Üíhats‚Üíshake‚Üítom over ~8s); every stamped cell is picked up by the scheduler next pass and repeats forever (genuine accumulating memory); auto-demo fires within ~1.2s, tap-grid fallback if CDN fails. **What was promising:** the only sibling with **compositional memory** ‚Äî the jury's standing kids-lane ask after `350-kids-bump-along` regressed to a memory-less poke-toy; "I made this and it keeps going" is a real consequence model. **Why it didn't win:** **Canvas2D is the over-represented screen renderer** this window (banned-by-count alongside SVG), and a step-sequencer is a more *familiar* form than the WebGL2 mirror ‚Äî least differentiated despite the nice memory mechanic. **To resurrect:** ship on a cycle where the renderer count has cooled, OR **fold its loop/memory mechanic into the shipped `423`** as a "record mode" toggle (the cleanest cycle-2 deepening of the face-beat thread ‚Äî gives `423` the memory it lacks). Refs: Expotion (arXiv:2507.04955, 2025); Ekman-Friesen FACS 1978; step-sequencer / loop-station lineage (loved `172-loop-station`‚ù§Ô∏è). Renumber on revival.

## ¬ß363 ‚Äî banked siblings from the WIDE adult fire (cycle 363, 2026-06-09)

Both were built to demoable in the cycle-363 fire and lost only to `437-wiki-pulse` on ambition (each 2/5 vs 437's 3/5) and freshness. Both are genuinely worth resurrecting ‚Äî they answer the JURY's "refuse to resolve / go weird" mandate via inputs the lab is thin on.

- **`436-flow-static` ‚Äî camera *motion energy* (not landmarks) ‚Üí abrasive Ikeda data-noise.** Point the camera at anything; **dense optical flow via 64√ó48 frame-differencing** (deliberately NOT a MediaPipe landmark model ‚Äî the genuinely fresh camera technique vs. the lab's `258`/`419`/`423` model-based pieces) drives an Ikeda glitch-synthesis chain (continuous pink-noise data-hum + motion-gated grain noise + 0.5‚Äì40/sec white-noise click-burst scheduler + sine-ping spike detector at incommensurable freqs, all through a ‚àí8 dB/12:1 limiter) and a **raw WebGL2** fullscreen clinical motion-texture shader (hard cell borders, scanlines, glitch bars, centroid cross-hair). Refs: Memo Akten (optical-flow "Forms") / Ryoji Ikeda *test pattern*. **Why it lost:** camera was used back-to-back recently (419/423) and ambition was 2/5; 437's live-data was fresher. **Why resurrect:** "movement energy, not a body model" is a real, distinct camera lane the lab has never shipped; it's complete and abrasive. **Next-cycle deepening:** pool the per-click `AudioBuffer` allocations (builder-flagged stutter risk under high motion on low-end phones); consider a true GPU optical-flow pass (Lucas‚ÄìKanade in a fragment shader) instead of CPU frame-diff.

- **`438-no-input` ‚Äî a no-input feedback-delay-network self-oscillator (the Nakamura lineage).** No mic, no sample, no oscillator-playing-a-note: a **feedback-delay-network** (Hadamard H4 mixing matrix + resonant bandpass bank in a feedback loop) self-oscillates, kept on the edge of Larsen instability; you sculpt feedback-gain / filter-center / delay-time and the emergent tone swerves wildly ‚Äî the instability *is* the instrument. Three safety layers (in-path hard clamp + code ceiling at gain ~0.93 + master `DynamicsCompressor`/`WaveShaper` soft-clip) make it impossible to blast. 10 s auto-demo blooms hum‚Üíhowl‚Üíhum; **Canvas2D** oscilloscope (zero-crossing-triggered) + Lissajous phase-portrait + live spectrum. Refs: Toshimaru Nakamura (no-input mixing board) / David Tudor (*Rainforest*). **Why it lost:** the riskiest of the three ‚Äî the FDN runs in a deprecated **`ScriptProcessorNode`** on the main thread and the self-oscillation "feel" is unverified without a live audio context; 437 was the safer, more surprising ship. **Why resurrect:** `INPUT=generative/no-input` does not exist anywhere in the lab's 320 prototypes ‚Äî a genuinely fresh category, and the purest possible "refuse to resolve." **Next-cycle deepening (REQUIRED before shipping):** port the feedback loop from `ScriptProcessorNode` to an **`AudioWorklet`** (off-main-thread, not deprecated, glitch-free), then verify in a real browser that it actually self-oscillates musically and that the edge-of-howl ceiling feels thrilling-but-safe.

## ¬ß368 ‚Äî banked siblings from the DEEP kids fire (cycle 368, 2026-06-09)

Both were built to demoable in the cycle-368 "Squish" DEEP fire and lost only to `451-kids-jelly-storm` on ambition (451 stacks 4/5: it binds today's research #5 and is the most literal answer to the jury's "loud/fast/chaotic, resolves on purpose" kids ask, with a legible energy bar). Both dodge every 2026-06-09 ban (WebGL output not Canvas2D; pitched melodic not drum-machine; loud/joyful/resolving not lullaby/clinical) and are genuinely worth resurrecting as cycle-2/3 of the Squish spine. Renumber on revival (max folder now 451).

- **`449-kids-squish-jam` ‚Äî Verlet mass-spring jelly creatures (three.js).** 4‚Äì7 closed rings of ~14‚Äì18 point masses with perimeter springs + a **pressure/area constraint** so each jelly squishes flat on impact and puffs back round (Jakobsen, *Advanced Character Physics*, GDC 2001; visual lineage Ash Weeks' *Medusae*). Drag-to-fling, tap-to-poke (dents inward then springs back), two fingers play two creatures; collisions trigger **C-major** mallet notes (size‚Üíregister, impact‚Üívelocity+brightness), energy swells the pad + lifts the register, hard-hit clusters fire a triumphant tutti chord; always-on warm pad; brick-wall limiter. Auto-demo at 3.5s. Files: `page.tsx` (~19 KB), `verlet.ts` (~8 KB), `audio.ts` (~6.5 KB), `README.md`. Builder: tsc + eslint clean. **Why it didn't win:** the cleanest, safest build, but the *least differentiated* of the three ‚Äî no fresh-research bind, and the squish-toy mechanic is closest to loved `169-marble-run` (whereas 451's escalate‚Üíresolve arc is a genuinely new kids mechanic). **To resurrect:** the calm-but-bouncy companion to 451; or fold its per-point grab (stretch/pinch a single creature like dough) into the Squish spine as cycle-2 ("knead the jelly"). 

- **`450-kids-goo-band` ‚Äî WebGL2 metaball / signed-distance-field merging goo.** A single full-screen WebGL2 fragment shader renders ~16 blobs as a 2D SDF combined with **Inigo Quilez's polynomial smooth-min** (`smin`, iquilezles.org), so the goo visibly **fuses with a fat liquid membrane and splits apart** ‚Äî the fusing is the whole point; merged regions blend their hues, with glow/rim/tonemap for a lava-lamp feel. CPU physics for centers (gravity, bouncy walls, spring repulsion, fling), a merge-event detector (deep overlap + closing velocity) ‚Üí **F-major** triad chord-stacks; sparkly glockenspiel/celesta bells (sine+triangle + inharmonic FM "ting"); always-on F-add9 pad; brick-wall limiter. Auto-demo ~3.2s. Files: `page.tsx` (~17 KB), `gl.ts` (~6.9 KB), `audio.ts` (~7.4 KB), `README.md`. Builder: tsc + eslint clean (note: builder flagged a mid-build folder-deletion race in the shared `next build` env ‚Äî verify with `next dev` on revival; the orchestrator confirmed the *winner* 451 builds clean, 450 was not in the authoritative build). **Why it didn't win:** the most visually *surprising* of the three (gooey SDF merge is a strong, kid-legible, lab-fresh visual) and the strongest runner-up ‚Äî but its physics is "simple CPU wobble + merge," not true soft-body deformation, so the squish is less physical than 451's PBD; and it doesn't bind recent research. **To resurrect:** ship as the SDF-render lane of the Squish spine, or fold the `smin` merge‚Üíchord mechanic into 451 (two jellies touching fuse into a louder chord) ‚Äî the cleanest cycle-2 deepening, marrying 451's real soft-body physics to 450's gorgeous gooey merge.

## Cycle 370 banked siblings (the two WIDE explorers that lost to `456-kids-ferro-magnet`)

Both were built to demoable in the cycle-370 WIDE kids fire and lost to the winner on the curation gate (the winner is the freshest/weirdest image AND a grep-clean lab-first at ambition 3/5). Both are genuinely strong, dodge every 2026-06-09 jury ban (WebGL2/three.js OUTPUT not Canvas2D; non-pentatonic/resolving not lullaby; voice/pull-release INPUT not over-leaned touch), and are worth shipping or extending. **Renumber on revival** (max folder now 456). Their full READMEs + code were build-reviewed; folders removed per the no-half-built-folders rule.

- **`455-kids-sing-cymatics` ‚Äî VOICE ‚Üí WebGL2 grain-migration cymatics (the namesake, embodied).** A child **sings or hums** and ~12,000 glowing sand-grains migrate across a vibrating square plate into the **Chladni standing-wave figure** of that exact pitch, then the plate "sings back" a warm inharmonic bell. The physics is real, not a pre-drawn pattern: each grain gets a jitter scaled by the local vibration amplitude `|f(x,y)|` (antinodes shake grains loose) and is nudged **down `‚àá|f|` toward the nearest nodal line** ‚Äî the actual Faraday/Chladni sorting mechanism. Chladni field `f(x,y)=cos(nœÄx)cos(mœÄy)‚àícos(mœÄx)cos(nœÄy)`; **pitch ‚Üí mode (n,m)** (higher pitch = more intricate figure) across **6 plates on a D-Dorian JI hexachord** (D E F G A C, **not** C-pentatonic); loudness ‚Üí vibration amplitude. Mic is **analysis-only** (autocorrelation/YIN pitch + octave-collapse + 4-frame vote ‚Üí plate; never recorded/played-back/sent, no network). No-mic / denied ‚Üí a full auto-demo cycles all six figures hands-free. Files: `page.tsx`, `cymatics.ts` (field + grain sim + GL), `audio.ts`, `README.md`. Builder + orchestrator: eslint/tsc clean. Refs: **Ernst Chladni** (1787) ¬∑ **Hans Jenny** *Cymatics* (1967) ¬∑ **Michael Faraday** (1831, Faraday waves). **Ambition 2/5** (#2 ‚â•3 subsystems: mic pitch/RMS analyser + Chladni eigenmode field + WebGL2 grain-advection sim + additive bell synth = 4; #3 named refs). **#1 NOT cleanly claimed** ‚Äî `165-cymatics` (adult, audio-FFT ‚Üí analytical pattern render) exists; the fresh move is **voice-driven + kids + true particle-migration sim** (grains actually flow to the nodes). **Why it didn't win:** the most "Resonance-shaped" and the strongest *embodiment* (the jury's "sing not poke") ‚Äî but it's the only 2/5 of the three (sits on the very floor the jury said to climb off), and its central bet (child-voice pitch-detection + 12k CPU-gradient grains at 60 fps on a phone) is the least certain. **To resurrect:** ship as-is (it's near-complete and the auto-demo de-risks the 06:30 read); de-risk by moving the grain sim to a GPU transform-feedback / WebGPU compute pass; or make it the embodied-voice lane of a "make sound visible" spine.

- **`457-kids-pendulum-wave` ‚Äî pull-and-release ‚Üí three.js length-graded pendulum wave (grep-clean lab-first).** Pull back a rack of **15 glowing pendulums** and let go: they start in phase (a straight line), drift into a rolling **traveling wave**, and every ~40 s **snap back into alignment**, ringing a **resolving descending D-major arpeggio** (D5‚ÜíD3) ‚Äî not a random shimmer. Classic length-graded tuning `T_k = T_cycle/(K_base+k)` (T_cycle=40 s, K_base=40) so the whole pattern cycles; pure SHM `Œ∏_k(t)=A¬∑cos(2œÄ t/T_k)` (Galileo small-angle isochronism); each bob rings a bell at its bottom-center zero-crossing; drag-amplitude ‚Üí swing size ‚Üí loudness; always-on D2+A2 drone; brick-wall limiter. three.js emissive spheres + trails + ring-flashes, violet‚Üíorange‚Üícyan color-by-pitch ramp. **Self-launches on Start** (fully autonomous hypnotic cycle ‚Äî the *safest hands-free 06:30 read* of the three); tap to relaunch. Files: `page.tsx`, `scene.ts` (three.js + pendulum sim), `audio.ts`, `README.md`. Builder + orchestrator: eslint/tsc clean. `pendulum.?wave` grep-clean (`196-kids-wind-chimes` is wind-driven pendulum *chimes*, not the Harvard length-graded demo). Refs: **Harvard Natural Sciences "Pendulum Waves"** ¬∑ **Galileo** isochronism (*Discorsi*, 1638) ¬∑ **Richard Berg** (Maryland apparatus). **Ambition 3/5** (#1 first length-graded pendulum-wave in the lab, grep-verified; #2 ‚â•3 subsystems: pendulum SHM integrator + zero-crossing note scheduler + three.js render + drone/limiter = 4; #3 named refs). **Why it didn't win:** equal ambition to the winner and the most reliable visual ‚Äî but pendulum waves are a *known classic* (less "huh, didn't know we could do that" than a singing ferrofluid), and it's the least embodied (mostly watch). **To resurrect:** the safe, gorgeous, near-ship kids ship; or extend it as a long-form/state piece (the cycle is already a built-in 40-s structure).

## Cycle 376 banked seeds (Scanned Synthesis ‚Äî Living Wavetable spine, DEEP non-winners)

Both are complete, build-reviewed real scanned-synthesis implementations (Mathews/Verplank/Shaw, ICMC 2000), folder-removed after curation. They are cycle-2/3 candidates for the new **"Living Wavetable"** kids spine started by `474-kids-scan-bloom`.

- **472-kids-scan-string (open-string variant)** ‚Äî a giant glowing horizontal STRING of light you PLUCK like a harp; pluck-x selects pitch (C-major C3‚ÄìC5), the wave rings then relaxes to a near-sine. Renderer: **inline SVG** glowing `<polyline>` (the clearest, most legible realization of "the wave you see is the sound you hear" ‚Äî a flat line is easier to read than a ring). Real AudioWorklet mass-spring + ScriptProcessor + no-audio triple fallback, DynamicsCompressor limiter, 3s auto-demo. **Resurrect** as the Living-Wavetable spine's "harp/zither" sibling, OR fold the pluck-position‚Üípitch glissando into 474. Honest wart: at low pitch the ~35fps SVG averages sub-period detail (cosmetic). Ambition 4/5 (#1 scanned-synth lab-first + #2 ‚â•3 subsystems + #3 named-ref + #4 spine).

- **473-kids-scan-ring (closed toroidal halo)** ‚Äî POKE a big glowing HALO; the dent travels around the ring (circular spring coupling) and the timbre warbles, then relaxes to a perfect calm circle. 6 colored note-zone pads set pitch. Renderer: **three.js** additive ring. The most reference-pure topology (toroidal = purely harmonic modes, Mathews' original). **Resurrect** as the spine's "gong/singing-bowl" sibling. Honest risk flagged by builder: `clientWidth/clientHeight` can be 0 at mount on some mobile browsers ‚Üí collapsed ring until first resize (fix: defer GL sizing to a ResizeObserver/rAF). Ambition 4/5 (same stack).

## ¬ß377 banked siblings (Resonant Room spine ‚Äî DEEP fire, 2026-06-10)

Two non-winning parallel approaches to cycle-1 of the new adult "Resonant Room" spine (winner shipped: `475-resonant-room`, a key-tuned Jot/Stautner‚ÄìPuckette FDN). Both are complete, build-reviewed implementations that were removed (per orchestration rule: never commit a non-winner's folder) ‚Äî resurrect as spine cycle-2/3:

- **`tarab-strings` (was 476)** ‚Äî Karel's real *Welcome Home* piano continuously cross-excites a bank of ~20 parallel high-Q BiquadFilter bandpass **sympathetic resonators** (sitar *tarab* / piano *aliquot* strings), tuned to a chosen key across 3 octaves; per-resonator AnalyserNode RMS drives a **three.js** field of glowing light-strings that brighten/thicken/vibrate, energy concentrating on the tonic strings as it resolves. Ref: sitar tarab + una-corda aliquot resonance + J.O. Smith *PASP* (CCRMA). Didn't win: the biquad-resonator-bank technique overlaps the lab's existing modal resonators (202/284/286), and three.js is the over-used renderer cluster (471/463/451). **Resurrect as:** the spine's "strings/harp" sibling ‚Äî its per-resonator energy portrait is the most *legible* sympathetic-resonance read; swap the renderer off three.js (WebGL2 or instanced) to dodge the cluster.
- **`resonant-caustics` (was 477)** ‚Äî same real-piano source feeding a bank of 16 tuned feedback **comb-filter / digital-waveguide** resonators (Karplus-family, continuous cross-excitation, AudioWorklet + ScriptProcessor fallback); per-comb RMS drives a full-screen **WebGL2 caustic interference field** (16 radial travelling-wave terms, light-on-a-pool-floor) that brightens in-key and calms to the tonic's slow ripple on release. Ref: J.O. Smith waveguide/comb + Schroeder. Didn't win: comb resonators are Karplus-Strong-family (140/184 exist) so the #1 lab-first claim is weaker than the FDN's. **Resurrect as:** the spine's "water/caustics" visual sibling ‚Äî the caustic field is the most beautiful render of the three; pair it with the FDN engine (475) rather than its own comb bank to keep the novel-technique claim.

## ¬ß378 ‚Äî banked non-winners (cycle 378 WIDE kids fire; winner shipped: `478-kids-wave-pond`, a genuine FDTD 2-D wave field)

Both are complete, build-reviewed real implementations, folder-removed after curation (orchestration rule: never commit a non-winner's folder). Both clear the ambition floor (‚â•2). Resurrect on a future kids cycle.

- **479-kids-duet-bloom (cooperative two-player)** ‚Äî the lab's **first genuinely cooperative kids instrument**: two glowing creatures, each playing a harmonically *incomplete* fragment (side A = root+fifth, side B = third+octave); the music only resolves into a **full warm chord** ‚Äî and a glowing **rainbow bridge blooms** across the centre ‚Äî when *both* players hold their creature at once. A `togetherness` scalar (both active, sustained) drives a build-then-resolve crossfade to the full triad+maj9 and the bloom richness, rewarding group synchrony over a quick tap. Renderer: **inline SVG** (radialGradient/feGaussianBlur creatures + animated bezier-arc bridge + sparkle particles). Dual Pointer-Events tracking by pointerId (left-half ‚Üí A, right-half ‚Üí B); single-pointer fallback keeps the auto-demo cycling so a solo child still sees the bloom. Refs: **Kirschner & Tomasello 2010** ("Joint music making promotes prosocial behavior in 4-year-olds") ¬∑ **Toca Band** (everyone-plays-in-key ensemble) ¬∑ Rabinowitch et al. 2012. **Ambition 2‚Äì3/5** (#2 ‚â•3 subsystems + #3 named refs; a *first cooperative* interaction model ‚Äî weaker #1 since it's an interaction pattern, not a DSP/render primitive). **Didn't win:** lost to 478's clean grep-verified #1 lab-first (FDTD) + higher ambition; and its rAF-triggered full-SVG React re-render is a perf smell. **Strong bank ‚Äî the highest-surprise concept of the fire and KIDS.md's explicitly-flagged social/cooperative gap.** Resurrect as: a true **2-device WebRTC** kids duet (genuinely two children, two phones), OR fold the synchrony-engine into an existing instrument as a "play-together bonus." Honest wart: switch the rAF loop to imperative DOM-attribute writes (drop the per-frame React re-render) before shipping.

- **480-kids-sing-bird (voice-pitch sing-along)** ‚Äî SING or hum and your **pitch** flies a glowing bird up/down through a warm night sky; gliding near a scrolling sparkly "perch" rings a warm bell note (C-pentatonic ‚Äî no wrong notes). Core: real-time **monophonic pitch detection** (YIN CMNDF, self-implemented, no libs) on the live AnalyserNode buffer each frame, with a clarity gate (0.55) + RMS floor so silence/noise doesn't jump the bird; log-smoothed f0 ‚Üí SVG bird Y; mic is **analysis-only** (never recorded/uploaded). Synthesized 21-note voice-contour auto-demo flies the bird with **no mic** so it always demos; mic-denied ‚Üí friendly `text-rose-300` notice + demo continues. Renderer: **inline SVG** (radialGradient/feGaussianBlur bird, scrolling perches, gradient sky). Ref: **de Cheveign√© & Kawahara, "YIN, a fundamental frequency estimator for speech and music," JASA 2002**. **Ambition 2/5** (#2 ‚â•3 subsystems + #3 YIN ref). **Didn't win:** sing-to-the-line is a well-trodden mechanic (twice-considered: it was banked ¬ß374 as `466-kids-sing-kite`), and pitch-tracking-as-controller, while fresh-ish, is a weaker lab-first than 478's FDTD. **Bank** as the lab's standing voice-pitch candidate; resurrect by pushing past "fly along a line" ‚Äî e.g. a **two-voice harmony-landing** game (two children must land a consonant interval ‚Üí genuine polyphonic pitch detection, a real next lab-first). Cleanup wart: the builder left 3 unused sibling helper files (`pitchDetect.ts`/`audioEngine.ts`/`perchScroll.ts` ‚Äî page.tsx is self-contained); delete them on resurrect.

## Cycle 380 banked seeds (DEEP polytonality explorations ‚Äî losers, NOT committed)

### 486-kids-mood-prism ‚Äî the truest Petrushka tritone (RESURRECT as cycle-2 deepening of the polytonality spine)
Twist a glowing prism with one finger; a single white drone (one key) splits into TWO fanning colored beams = two tonal centers pulling apart by the twist angle. Angle 0 = unison/white; wide = C major + **F‚ôØ major (the literal Petrushka tritone)** ringing against each other, panned by refracted angle. Canvas2D refraction fan + halo, AnalyserNode pulse, never auto-resolves (rests where the child leaves it). **Why it was strong:** the only sibling using the *real* tritone Petrushka pairing ‚Äî maximal genuine tension (the jury's #1 ask), and a beautifully legible "bend consonance into ambiguity and back" gesture. **Why it lost to 484:** single-finger rotational twist is slightly harder for a 4yo than dragging two glowing things; Canvas2D-only (fewer subsystems). **Resurrect:** fold the tritone-split + refraction-fan visual INTO the two-suns spine as an "intensity" mode, OR ship as cycle-2 to give the spine its harsher-tension variant. Tags: touch / Canvas2D / polytonality / spectral-luminous. Ref: Stravinsky *Petrushka* chord, Milhaud.

### 485-kids-two-rivers ‚Äî the braid metaphor (RESURRECT as the calm companion)
Two vertical rivers of falling note-droplets, LEFT = C major, RIGHT = A major (share E). A wide draggable "weaver" paddle: over one river ‚Üí that key clear (panned); centered ‚Üí both rivers braid through the mix zone, droplets interleaved, both keys ringing at full density. Pure Canvas2D additive glow + ripples, two-key drone bed, auto-flows on load. **Why it was strong:** the braiding is visible AND audible ‚Äî the clearest "you can SEE the two worlds weave"; deliberate C+A (shared E) so worlds touch not clash. **Why it lost:** single-touch paddle (no two-hand play); plainest visual; fewest subsystems. **Resurrect:** as the gentle/contemplative companion to the suns, or merge the falling-droplet "rain of two keys" as an alternate visual skin for the spine. Tags: touch / Canvas2D / polytonality / playful-aqueous. Ref: Stravinsky *Petrushka* chord, Milhaud.

## Banked ¬ß383 (WIDE non-winners ‚Äî TEXT seeds, folders removed; renumber on revival, max committed folder now 490):
*The cycle-383 fire was 3 orthogonal answers to the JURY (2026-06-10) #1 ("bring genuine tension back ‚Äî 15/15 consonant, 10/15 warm-resolve; build ONE piece the listener resolves through action, not a pre-baked cadence"). Winner shipped: `490-disintegration` (Basinski self-erasing loop). These two are complete, build-reviewed implementations ‚Äî strong seeds for the next adult cycle.*

### 491-room-resonance ‚Äî Alvin Lucier, *I Am Sitting in a Room* (1969), interactive (RESURRECT as the next adult conceptual piece)
Speak a phrase (mic, with a synthesized-seed fallback if denied/silent) ‚Üí the system iteratively **re-convolves** it with a synthetic room impulse response (a decaying noise burst colored by a bank of resonant biquad peaks), pass after pass, via `OfflineAudioContext` + `ConvolverNode`. Each pass the room's modal peaks compound multiplicatively while broadband speech/transient energy washes out ‚Äî after ~12‚Äì24 passes the words have dissolved entirely into shimmering room resonance. You **watch it happen**: a live scrolling Canvas2D **spectrogram** (log-frequency, violet‚Üícyan-white, with faint horizontal guide-lines at the room modes) shows energy abandoning the broadband texture and collecting onto the mode lines ‚Äî *language becoming architecture* ‚Äî plus a spectral-flatness **"intelligibility" meter** that drops as the room takes over. Auto-run walks the whole dissolution; step/replay/linger to dwell on any pass. `DynamicsCompressor` limiter; per-pass normalize; mic audio never leaves the browser (no API route). **Ambition:** #2 (4 subsystems: mic/seed capture + iterative offline-convolution engine + room-IR synthesis + live analyser) + #3 (Lucier 1969); lab-first iterative-convolution dissolution (345-speech-melody only *nods* at Lucier, never implements it). **Why it was strong:** the single most conceptually beautiful piece of the fire ‚Äî you literally see+hear meaning erode into space; anti-warm-resolve (transformation, not cadence). **Why it lost to 490:** mic-dependent (fallback exists but the *concept* wants your real voice) and the most runtime-complex (chained offline convolutions, pixel-scroll spectrogram) ‚Üí least robust for a no-touch 06:30 phone glance; 490 is pure-client and never needs permission. **Resurrect:** ship as-is next adult cycle; consider tuning the room IR to one of Karel's published journey keys so the dissolution rings in-theme. Tags: mic / Canvas2D-spectrogram / iterative-convolution-dissolution / cold-to-shimmering-transformation. Ref: Alvin Lucier, *I Am Sitting in a Room* (1969). Built files (removed): `page.tsx` (551 lines), `audio-engine.ts` (`makeRoomIR`/`makeFallbackSeed`/`runConvolutionPass`/`recordMic`/`spectralFlatness`), `README.md`.

### 492-automata ‚Äî Stephen Wolfram elementary CA as a real-time composer (RESURRECT ‚Äî grep-verified lab-first)
A row of N cells evolves one generation per musical pulse under an **elementary (1-D, 2-state, 3-neighbor) Wolfram rule** (presets 110 / 30 / 90 / 54 / 184). Live cells map onto a deliberately **tense scale field** (octatonic / whole-tone / phrygian ‚Äî real dissonance allowed, NOT a no-wrong-notes pentatonic); a complex rule (110 is Turing-complete) **never enters a short cycle**, so the harmony genuinely evolves ‚Äî different at minute 5 than minute 1, never exactly repeating. Voice-stealing 12-voice poly synth + always-on drone + density-tracking gain/lowpass so it breathes; `DynamicsCompressor` limiter caps the densest rows. The classic Wolfram **space-time diagram** scrolls in lock-step with the audio (both emitted from the same `stepGeneration` call, audio-clock-scheduled with a capped catch-up): you SEE the Sierpinski triangles / Rule-110 gliders forming exactly as you HEAR the chord they make; columns map low‚Üíhigh pitch, violet‚Üícyan; **tap to drop a single seed cell** and watch/hear the future cascade from it. 3 s hands-free auto-start. No API route. **Ambition:** #1 (discrete elementary-CA-as-compositional-engine ‚Äî grep-verified absent; `264-kids-lenia-pond` is *continuous* CA and a kids visual toy, not a harmonic engine) + #2 (CA engine + CA‚Üíharmony scheduler + poly synth + space-time renderer) + #3 (Wolfram, *A New Kind of Science*, 2002); also long-form evolving. **Why it was strong:** the cleanest "long-form evolving adult, different at minute 5" of the fire, structural emergent tension, robust pure-client. **Why it lost narrowly to 490:** 490 is *more radically* un-resolving (pure entropy, no scale safety net at all) and lands two starved categories at once (conceptual/critical + long-form); 492 is the strong runner-up on the same tension axis. **Resurrect:** ship next adult cycle; deepen with TWO rules in counterpoint (bitonal, √Ý la `484-two-suns`), or let the live density drive a slow modulation of the scale field. Tags: touch-seed / Canvas2D-space-time / elementary-cellular-automata-harmony / cold-emergent-structural. Ref: Stephen Wolfram, elementary CA / *A New Kind of Science* (2002). Built files (removed): `page.tsx` (466 lines), `ca.ts` (`stepRow`/`makeSeedRow`/`makeRandomRow`/`population` + 5 rule presets), `audio.ts` (voice-stealing poly synth + 3 scale fields), `README.md`.
