## ¬ß437 ‚Äî 2026-06-15 ¬∑ adult WIDE fire (3 registers of the jury's named "missing middle") ‚Äî 2 banked seeds

Cycle 437 ran an adult **WIDE** fan-out of 3 orthogonal corners of the missing MIDDLE register the jury (2026-06-15) named ‚Äî ecstatic/danceable/groovy/tender ‚Äî each via a DIFFERENT mechanism √ó renderer √ó ref (the "WIDE lesson": map a named vibe-gap). **Winner: `637-slow-burn`** (tender/sensual neo-soul + SVG grid-vs-actual microtiming plot). NB the jury's "0√ó lanes" (MIDI, multi-user/WebRTC) and "nobody built EDM build-drop" all turned out FALSE on corpus check (Web MIDI 12 files; `508-accord-call` WebRTC; `387-drop-engine` EDM) ‚Äî so the ¬ß435-banked `631-drop-engine` was REJECTED as a 387-duplicate, and these two are the genuine fresh corners. Both lost on margins (renderer/viz duplication), NOT quality.

### `638-gospel-lift` ‚Äî `queued` (ecstatic/euphoric gospel-house ¬∑ three.js ¬∑ adult / hands-in-the-air ‚Äî a journey-engine alternative DISTINCT from 387)
A 122 BPM Chicago/gospel-house groove (four-on-the-floor kick, off-beat open hats, congregation handclaps, Hammond/Leslie drawbar organ, Rhodes comping a gospel **ii‚ÄìV‚ÄìI with passing diminished chords**). The signature mechanism: every **16 bars the WHOLE progression modulates UP one semitone** ‚Äî the church "modulation"/"lift" ‚Äî re-voicing the chords, stepping pad brightness, firing a short riser, **octave-folding the root** when it climbs too high so it stays in range while the perceived ascent continues. **There is NO drop** ‚Äî the euphoria comes purely from continuous upward harmonic modulation (the explicit anti-EDM-build framing; this is why it's distinct from `387-drop-engine`'s GROOVE‚ÜíBUILD‚ÜíDROP‚ÜíRELEASE). A **PRAISE/intensity** lever (‚Üë/‚Üì + slider) stacks tambourine 16ths / octave bass / soprano choir "oohs" / faster Leslie vibrato; Enter forces an early lift. three.js ascending light column (stacked additive rings + emissive core + sparks + halo, gold‚Üíwhite, beat-pulse + lift-flash, camera drifts upward with cumulative key-step) + Canvas2D fallback + synthetic-heartbeat so the idle ~1.8 s auto-demo's VISUAL is alive even with audio suspended. 4 subsystems (house look-ahead scheduler ¬∑ gospel reharm/key-lift state machine ¬∑ multi-voice organ/Rhodes/choir synth ¬∑ three.js renderer) + named refs (gospel "shout"/"drive" + semitone-modulation tradition ‚Äî Hezekiah Walker, Kirk Franklin; Chicago/gospel-house ‚Äî Frankie Knuckles) = honest 2‚Äì3/5. `page.tsx` ~1308 lines, build-clean by hand-review (no node_modules in builder sandbox). **Why it lost:** three.js re-introduces GPU verification risk (the recurring build-verified-not-browser-verified surface), AND an ascending-light-column reads as a more conventional EDM-adjacent visualizer; 637's SVG was the scarcer renderer + the more surprising "legible mechanism." **Why resurrect:** it's the lab's strongest **ecstatic/euphoric** middle entry AND a genuine journey-engine alternative (Karel care #4) that is NOT an EDM build-drop ‚Äî fills a real open corner. Resurrect next adult fire when GPU renderers are cold in the rolling-10, OR pair the key-lift mechanic with a non-GPU renderer (the lift reads fine on Canvas2D/SVG too). Full working code is in git history at the folder path; treat as a fresh build.

### `639-afro-pulse` ‚Äî `queued` (joyful/danceable afrobeat-highlife ¬∑ Canvas2D ¬∑ adult / sunlit ‚Äî REDO THE VIZ off rings)
~108‚Äì116 BPM afrobeat/highlife. A West-African **bell timeline** (gankogui two-pitch "standard pattern" over a 12-pulse cycle + a clave high stroke) keeps time while **two interlocking plucked melodic ostinati on coprime cycle lengths (7 vs 5 ‚Üí 35-step melodic repeat)** ride call-and-response on alternating 8ths so they phase in/out as an audible **melodic weave**; a Tony-Allen-style kit (ghost-note hats, rim, kick), shaker 16ths, brassy horn stabs on accents. A **HEAT/intensity** lever (‚Üë/‚Üì + slider) adds the 2nd guitar (>45%), horn stabs (>60%), denser hats; a variation/reharmonization state machine transposes the riff (0, +5, ‚àí2, +7‚Ä¶) + rotates horn voicings over 4-bar sections (living, not a loop). **Major-key, melodic, bright, danceable** ‚Äî the explicit OPPOSITE of monochrome Reich/Euclidean minimalism (so distinct from `632-polymeter-loom`'s *audio*). 5 subsystems (bell-timeline ¬∑ interlocking-ostinato Chris-Wilson scheduler ¬∑ multi-voice pluck/kit/horn synth ¬∑ variation state machine ¬∑ Canvas2D renderer) + named refs (Fela Kuti / Tony Allen; West-African bell timeline ‚Äî Kofi Agawu *Representing African Music*; ClaveNet ACM 2024) = honest 2‚Äì3/5. `page.tsx` ~950 lines, build-clean by hand-review. **Why it lost:** its Canvas2D viz is **concentric rotating rings with beads flashing on each onset** ‚Äî which is a near-twin of the just-shipped `632-polymeter-loom`'s "loom of concentric bead rings that flash on each onset, phasing rings counter-rotating." Shipping it = a visual duplicate of last adult fire's winner. **Why resurrect:** the AUDIO is genuinely fresh (major-key melodic highlife ‚Äî the joyful/danceable corner of the middle, distinct from 632's minimalism and 387's EDM) and very demoable. **Resurrect with a NON-RING payoff** ‚Äî dancing cut-paper figures, a sunlit street/market scene, light-trails per ostinato voice, anything but concentric rings ‚Äî so it stops reading as "632 with a different palette." Full working code in git history; treat as a fresh build, redo the renderer.

### `642-piano-chroma` ‚Äî `queued` (cycle-3 of 606‚Üí630 piano spine ¬∑ supervised harmonic-comb NMF ¬∑ SVG chroma wheel ¬∑ adult / clinical ‚Äî **banked, build-verified**)
The most FAITHFUL reading of 630's banked cycle-3 ("supervised NMF, fixed basis ‚Üí true pitch classes, not register bands"). Keeps the proven HPSS ground, then fixes the NMF basis `W` to **12 pitch-class harmonic-comb templates** (Gaussian-in-log-freq bumps at fundamental + 6 partials for every octave A0..C8 of each chroma; positive floor; `H` updated full-strength while `W` blends only 25% toward the seed each iteration ‚Üí components stay nameable). 72 KL iters chunked. Per-pitch-class Wiener mask on the original complex harmonic STFT ‚Üí ISTFT ‚Üí up to 13 instant click-free solo/mute/gain voices (Hammers core + present chromas; classes <8% peak energy collapse out). Renderer: **SVG chroma WHEEL** ‚Äî 12 annular-sector wedges pulsing to live level, soloed wedge glows (feGaussianBlur), chromatic‚Üîcircle-of-fifths toggle. Honest 3/5: #4 (real cycle-3) + #2 (4 subsystems) + #3 (Lee&Seung'99, Smaragdis&Brown'03, Fujishima'99, Fitzgerald'10). **#1 NOT claimed** (NMF used by 630). **Why it lost to 643:** more INCREMENTAL ‚Äî 4‚Üí12 voices is finer resolution but adds no NEW interaction (still solo/mute masked voices), so it's a clean 3/5 not the 4/5 REACH the jury wants; 643's REPLAY layer turns the decomposition into a playable instrument (the genuine cycle-3 "add the interactive layer" move). **Why resurrect:** it's the cleanest, most build-bulletproof piece (self-reported a FULL `npm run build` pass; pure SVG = zero GPU surface) and the SVG chroma wheel is a beautiful, scarce-renderer visual. Resurrect as the safe-glance companion, OR fold its supervised-NMF isolation into 643's salience-mask for sharper note separation. Full working code was built this cycle (treat as fresh build from this spec).

### `644-piano-respeak` ‚Äî `queued` (cycle-3 of 606‚Üí630 piano spine ¬∑ masked‚Üîadditive-resynth PURITY lever ¬∑ Canvas2D ¬∑ adult / clinical ‚Äî **banked, diversity-blocked this cycle**)
The BOLDEST reconstruction reading, driven by the 2026 restoration reframe (arXiv 2603.16926, Mar 2026: masking leaves degraded stems ‚Üí add a reconstruction stage that RE-SYNTHESIZES a clean source). Per pitch class, build TWO sample-aligned loop renderings: **MASKED** (softmax-normalized Wiener mask on the original complex STFT ‚Üí his real isolated timbre) and **RESYNTH** (extract each note's per-frame amplitude envelope, drive a continuous-phase additive sine-oscillator bank ‚Üí a glassy DDSP-lite reconstruction; cf. Serra&Smith SMS, "Sines Transient Noise" arXiv 2409.06513). A global **PURITY lever** equal-power crossfades masked‚Üíresynth for all 12 notes at once ‚Äî at purity=1 you hear Karel's exact performance, same notes/timing, rebuilt entirely from pure tones. 12 Canvas2D lanes morph grainy "real" fill ‚Üî concentric "resynth" rings as purity moves. Honest 3/5: #4 + #2 (4 subsystems) + #3 (four named refs incl the Mar-2026 paper). **Why it lost:** **diversity gate** ‚Äî Canvas2D is at 3√ó in the rolling-10 (624/636/640); shipping 644 would push it to 4√ó (the banned threshold). The concept is arguably the most surprising of the three. **Why resurrect:** next adult fire when Canvas2D is COLD in the rolling-10 ‚Äî this is the truest "reconstruction beyond masking" piece and a strong 4/5 reach; OR re-skin its viz to SVG/three.js to dodge the Canvas2D count. Also a great cycle-4 fold INTO 643 (a per-note purity fader on top of the replay grains). Full working code was built this cycle (treat as fresh build from this spec).

## ¬ß442 ‚Äî banked from cycle 442 kids WIDE fan-out (2 non-winners, built-and-proven, fast to resurrect)

Both were built to demoable in isolation this cycle (tsc-clean, hand-audited against the ESLint pitfalls) and benched only because **652-kids-dance-band** was the directer hit on the jury's "build the danceable register nobody is building." Resurrect either as a near-drop-in.

- **651-kids-choir-leader** (mic VOICE-pitch ‚Üí in-key choir, Canvas2D, TENDER). *"What if a 4-year-old could lead a choir with their own voice?"* Real-time monophonic pitch detection (normalized autocorrelation + parabolic interp over `getFloatTimeDomainData`, RMS-gated, clamped 180‚Äì950 Hz, median+exp smoothing) ‚Üí C-major-pentatonic snap (no wrong notes) ‚Üí 4-voice additive choir (root/3rd/5th/octave, warm low-pass) under a scrolling luminous pitch-ribbon; always-on detuned-fifth pad; degrades to 84px in-key note buttons on mic-deny. **This is the realization of banked seed `635-kids-sing-kite`** (RESEARCH ¬ß436) and is **love-aligned** (`158-kids-hum-paint`‚ù§Ô∏è). Ambition #2 (mic+pitch-detect+additive-harmony+canvas ribbon=4) + #3 (Bobby McFerrin call-and-response / Imogen Heap harmonizer / Toca Band). **Resurrect-deepen:** swap autocorrelation ‚Üí YIN/CMNDF in an AudioWorklet to kill octave-halving on breathy child voices; this is the one fragility flagged. Tender is the cleanest *un-built* kids register ‚Äî strong next-kids-cycle pick.
- **653-kids-bounce-band** (device-TILT ‚Üí collision-quantized beat, Canvas2D, BOUNCY/JOYFUL). *"What if tilting the tablet turned bouncing balls into a danceable BEAT?"* Tilt steers gravity (gamma/beta, first-reading=neutral zero); 2D circle physics (softened restitution, speed cap, dt-clamp); **every collision is queued onto the next 16th-note tick** via a Chris-Wilson look-ahead scheduler so chaos self-organizes into a pentatonic groove; ball identity‚Üítimbre/register; ‚â§3 notes/tick voice-allocator + compressor for pile-up safety; legible groove playhead ring; pointer/arrow/‚â•64px-D-pad fallback. Ambition #2 (tilt+collision-physics+groove-quantizer+canvas=4) + #3 (Iwai Electroplankton/SimTunes ¬∑ Reactable ¬∑ BlockBob). **Resurrect-deepen:** per-corner instrument zones (pour balls to swap the kit) + tilt-magnitude‚Üítempo/swing so the steering‚Üíarrangement link is unmistakable. Note overlap risk to flag at resurrection: 619-plinko / 169-marble-run (physics‚Üíbells) ‚Äî the quantized-groove twist is what keeps it distinct.

## ¬ß447 ‚Äî 2026-06-16 (UTC) ¬∑ two banked DEEP non-winners from cycle 447 (the piano-spine cycle-4 fire) ‚Äî built-and-proven, tsc-clean in isolation, fast to resurrect (renumber on revival; max committed folder now 666)

All three cycle-447 explorers were the SAME cycle-4 concept ("the piano remembers you": a looper/phrase-memory built on Karel's 12 isolated piano notes from the 643 engine, growing itself over minutes via an **asymmetric-consonance** growth rule [De Roure 2026, arXiv:2606.16412] + level decay ‚Äî NO scale-snap). They differ only in renderer/interaction/register-thread. `666-piano-still` (audio-first / minimal SVG) shipped because it uniquely answers the jury's three-juries-running standing #1 ask ("get off the glass") in the scarcest renderer while still hitting tender + cycle-4. The two below lost on the off-glass axis ONLY ‚Äî both are real future builds. Each reuses the proven 643 engine (`audio.ts`/`hpss.ts`/`chroma.ts` copied verbatim) + a `consonance.ts` growth rule.

### `665-piano-loom` ‚Äî `queued` (banked DEEP non-winner, cycle 447 ¬∑ tap-to-stamp ¬∑ **raw WebGL2** scrolling phrase-tape ¬∑ adult / tender) ‚Äî **the most legible "looper" visual**
**What if Karel's 12 isolated piano notes became a LOOPER you can SEE ‚Äî a horizontal scrolling tape-ribbon where each tap stamps the current pitch class at the playhead, the loop re-fires stamps as the beam crosses them, un-refreshed stamps decay over ~90‚Äì130s, and the asymmetric-consonance rule buds new consonant notes every few cycles?** Raw hand-written **WebGL2 (GLSL ES 3.00)** single fullscreen-quad glow-field: cells = note stamps (hue per pitch class), flaring on fire, fading on decay, fixed warm playhead beam at center; `backend:"none"` ‚Üí `text-rose-300` notice, audio still plays. Keyboard `A W S E D F T G Y H U J` + `space` for selected note; `grow‚áÑrest` lever. Auto-seeds a consonant C‚ÄìG‚ÄìE‚ÄìA phrase + loops on load (alive in ~2s). Files: `page.tsx`, `ribbon.ts` (WebGL2), `consonance.ts`, + 643 engine. **Why it didn't win (cycle 447):** the clearest *visual metaphor* for the cycle-4 idea (you literally watch the loop remember you) and the boldest render ‚Äî but it is **another glass-screen render** (WebGL2 at 4√ó in the window), exactly the monoculture the jury's Karel-facing line told this fire to break, whereas 666 gets off the glass. **Resurrect FIRST** when an adult cycle wants the cycle-4 piano looper as an ON-glass immersive piece, or fold its visible-tape metaphor into 666 as an optional "show the tape" view. Ambition equal to winner: #4 (cycle-4) + #1 (first looper of a performer's own isolated notes) + #2 (‚â•3 subsystems: HPSS+salience isolation + phrase-memory/decay sequencer + grain-replay synth + WebGL2 ribbon) + #5 (De Roure 2606.16412, dated cycle-day).

### `667-piano-tide` ‚Äî `queued` (banked DEEP non-winner, cycle 447 ¬∑ drop-a-note ¬∑ **three.js** reflective ripple pool ¬∑ adult / tender-meditative) ‚Äî **the most immersive/beautiful**
**What if the looper were a still reflective POOL ‚Äî drop one of his notes and a slow ring spreads across the water, rings interfere and persist, and as a ring crosses the center it re-fires his note, so a quiet phrase accretes on the surface and grows itself over minutes?** **three.js** horizontal water surface: interfering-ripple height field summed in a fragment shader, soft specular glints + caustic banding, deep teal‚Üíindigo water with warm amber/rose note glows ‚Äî deliberately NOTHING like 643's orbiting-star constellation (no points/orbits/core). `calm‚áÑgrow` lever; ~13s loop; decay over minutes. Files: `page.tsx`, `scene.ts` (three.js), `consonance.ts`, + 643 engine. **Why it didn't win (cycle 447):** the most beautiful/immersive of the three ‚Äî but (a) **another glass-screen render** (off-glass was the jury's explicit ask for this fire) and (b) **three.js is at 3√ó in the window AND is 643's own renderer family**, so it's the closest of the three to the thing it extends (the jury wants a cycle-N to open a *new world*, not re-skin the prior renderer). **Resurrect** for an immersive-visual adult cycle when off-glass isn't the priority, or fold its ripple-interference water shader into a future water/tide piece. Ambition equal to winner (#4 + #1 + #2 ‚â•3 subsystems incl. three.js water shader + #5 De Roure).

## ¬ß450 ‚Äî 2026-06-16 (UTC) ¬∑ two banked WIDE non-winners from cycle 450 (KIDS) ‚Äî both fully built to demoable + self-reported tsc/ESLint-clean in isolation; fast to resurrect (renumber on revival; max committed folder now 675)

Cycle 450 ran **WIDE** ‚Äî 3 orthogonal kids "real harmonic event" explorers (honoring JURY 2026-06-16 provocation #1: kill the pentatonic/scale-snap crutch, let a 4yo *hear* harmony move), each a DIFFERENT harmonic event √ó input √ó output √ó palette. **Winner: `675-kids-deep`** (touch stones over a migrating drone ‚Üí functional recontextualization; WebGL2 abyss). Won on the ¬ß448/¬ß450 developmental anchor: for an actual 4yo the perceptible harmonic events are (a) *change itself / a moving drone* and (b) *closure & its denial* ‚Äî NOT the major/minor emotional flip (that emerges ~age 7‚Äì8). 675 lands (a) directly, is the freshest concept vs the recent kids stream, and uses the renderer-scarce WebGL2. The two strong losers, banked:

### `674-kids-two-moons` ("Two Moons") ‚Äî `queued` (tilt ‚Üí continuous MODE-GLIDE ‚Üí **three.js** crossfading sun-moon‚Üîghost-moon ¬∑ kids/family / cosmic awe, bright‚Üîeerie) ‚Äî realizes banked ¬ß448 seed `668-two-suns`
**What if tilting a tablet turned a happy song spooky ‚Äî and back ‚Äî so a child discovers the SAME notes feel completely different?** A single mood dial `m‚àà[0,1]` (tilt `gamma`, or a big gradient slider, or arrow keys) drives **parallel-mode interchange done as a continuous portamento morph**: a sustained chord's voices keep their identity and `setTargetAtTime`-glide between bright (Lydian-ish: maj3, #4, leading tone) and dark (Phrygian-ish: min3, b2, b7) tunings ‚Äî the third audibly melts maj‚Üímin, the 7th flattens. Six 72px melody pads play the "same" degrees, recolored by `m`. **Strictly NO pentatonic scale-snap** (continuous semitone interpolation, no quantization). three.js: gradient sky + star field + two crossfading celestial bodies hue-shift with the mode. Kid-safe master chain, ~2s auto-demo, full degradation (slider/keys), `text-rose-300` no-sensor note. Ref: **Kate Hevner, "The Affective Character of the Major and Minor Modes" (Am. J. Psych. 47, 1935)**. Ambition honest **2/5** (#2 ‚â•3 subsystems: tilt‚Üímode-engine + reharmonizer/portamento synth + three.js render = 4 ¬∑ #3 Hevner 1935). Files: `page.tsx` / `mode.ts` / `audio.ts` / `gl.ts` / `README.md`. **Why it lost:** the most visually spectacular of the three, BUT the lab's own ¬ß448 developmental research says the major‚Üîminor *emotional* flip isn't reliably legible to a 4yo (emerges ~7‚Äì8) ‚Äî it's a **family / older-kids (6+)** piece, not a 4yo one; the continuous-glide mitigation makes it "the change itself" but the central *discovery* still leans on mode-affect the target age lacks. **Resurrect when:** a "Resonance Kids 6+ / family" tier opens (it's the flagship for that audience), or as an ADULT cosmic mode-morph (the three.js + portamento-reharmonization engine is reusable and renderer-scarce). Strongest visual of the batch.

### `676-kids-night-boat` ("A Little Boat on the Night Sea") ‚Äî `queued` (rock-the-tablet DeviceOrientation ‚Üí **deceptive‚Üíauthentic CADENCE** ‚Üí audio-first / minimal inline-SVG ¬∑ kids / night-voyage mystery‚Üíarrival) ‚Äî **the top OFF-GLASS resurrect**
**What if a 4yo could rock a little boat across a night sea, eyes closed, and hear an unseen choir get *almost* home, turn to mystery, then land on home?** OFF-GLASS / audio-first: the screen is a minimal inline-SVG horizon + bobbing boat + a glow that warms only at homecoming. A **functional-cadence engine** (`cadence.ts`, I/ii/IV/V/vi/‚ô≠VII in D) voices one chord per detected rock (gamma zero-crossings; fallback ‚â•64px button + Spacebar; iOS motion permission from the Start tap). Three phrases loop: **deceptive V‚Äìvi** (withheld home, "the boat turns to mystery") √ó2, then **authentic V‚ÄìI** (arrival, audio + glow bloom warmer). The V carries the real leading tone (C‚ôØ) so the deceptive turn registers as genuine denial. **NO pentatonic snap** ‚Äî real triadic functional harmony; night-voyage register, NOT cozy-lullaby (warm color reserved strictly for the home cadence). Kid-safe master chain, never-silent sea/drone bed, auto-demo that real rocking preempts. Ref: **deceptive cadence (V‚Äìvi) in J.S. Bach's four-part chorales**. Ambition honest **2/5** (#2 ‚â•3 subsystems: motion-rock detect + cadence state-machine + choral pad synth + SVG = 4 ¬∑ #3 Bach chorale deceptive cadence). Files: `page.tsx` / `audio.ts` / `cadence.ts` / `README.md`. **Why it lost:** excellent and on the firmest developmental ground (closure & its denial IS perceptible to a 4yo), and it's the OFF-GLASS piece the jury keeps asking for ‚Äî BUT `669-kids-hum-answer` shipped off-glass kids built on tension‚Üíresolution just **2 cycles ago** (cycle 448); shipping a second off-glass closure piece back-to-back would cost diversity. **Resurrect FIRST when off-glass is cold in the rolling window** (it's the strongest banked off-glass candidate the lab has) ‚Äî likely the very next kids cycle that isn't adjacent to 669. Near-drop-in.

## Banked from Cycle 461 (adult DEEP fire ‚Äî ONE concept: *the music dreams a picture, and the dream changes the music* ‚Äî a closed audio‚Üíimage‚Üíaudio loop, JURY 2026-06-17 #4 "extend the ceiling builds [689], not a sixth tonal-tension toy." Two technical approaches to **how the image returns to audio**. Winner shipped = `704-spectral-seance` [the LITERAL return trip ‚Äî image re-sonified as a spectrogram]. Loser banked below; folder `rm -rf`'d after the winner's authoritative build passed. Renumber on revival ‚Äî max committed folder now 704.)

### `703-latent-oracle` ‚Äî `queued` ‚≠ê (the SOFT-closure twin of 704: the loop closes via **color**, not literal re-sonification. A 5‚Äì7 voice D-Dorian pad ensemble auto-evolves through four weather phases [calm‚Üíluminous‚Üíveiled‚Üíresolving]; each phase self-composes a prompt ‚Üí flux image ‚Üí **WebGL2 ping-pong feedback dissolve** [Anadol latent-flow ‚Äî old dreams smear into new, never fully vanish] ‚Üí dominant color sampled offscreen ‚Üí glided back into the synth [hue‚Üífilter cutoff/detune-spread, brightness‚Üíshimmer/reverb send, saturation‚Üídissonance], so the SOUND drifts toward the color of what it just dreamed; the HUD shows the fed-back color swatch + resulting cutoff/shimmer/diss values.) This is the long-banked **687-latent-oracle** seed, fully built this cycle (Refik Anadol latent data-paintings + Memo Akten *Learning to See*; guarded flux route + procedural domain-warped fog fallback, runs zero-network). **Why it lost to 704:** the closure is a *soft parameter-nudge* whose audible range **narrows without a `FAL_KEY`** (the procedural fog skews cool/dark, so the keyless drift is subtle) ‚Äî and Karel reviews on the public preview, which is usually keyless, so 704's *literal* image-as-spectrogram return trip demonstrates the closed loop far more legibly with no key. 703 is also closer to existing parameter-mapping than 704's never-before-seen re-sonification. **Why resurrect:** it's the more *beautiful* of the two (the Anadol feedback smear is gorgeous) and the only one that closes the loop back into continuous *synthesis* rather than into a re-played score ‚Äî **resurrect FIRST on a night Karel is awake to trigger a live `FAL_KEY`**, when vivid flux images would push the color-drift through its full range. Built files were complete & build-clean (`page.tsx`/`audio.ts`/`render.ts`/`dream.ts`/`api/route.ts`/`README.md`); near drop-in (renumber). This finally retires the standing **687-latent-oracle** queue entry above (still listed there for the WebGL2-cold resurrect note ‚Äî now realized).

## ¬ß467 banked seeds (cycle 467, ADULT DEEP ‚Äî ONE concept *Shadow Duet: trade fours with a machine that answers in the gaps* built via THREE "minds" for the duet partner; winner shipped = `718-duet-paths` [concatenative shadow of Karel's REAL recorded piano ‚Äî the truest realization of his MORNING ask "wired to your Paths piano"]. Both losers built to demoable + README by folder-isolated builders, self-reported clean `tsc --noEmit` + `eslint` for their own folders, then moved to `/tmp/dream-losers/` and banked here as TEXT [no half-built folders in the commit]. Tie-break was Karel's literal ask + research‚Üíbuild chain + glance-aliveness ‚Äî diversity was a wash [all three share MIDI/keyboard INPUT ¬∑ Canvas2D river OUTPUT ¬∑ call-response TECH ¬∑ jazz-responsive VIBE]. Renumber on revival; max committed folder now 718.)

### `716-shadow-duet` ‚Äî `queued` ‚≠ê (the **heuristic / Voyager-taste** mind ‚Äî the lab's most BULLETPROOF duet build: pure synth, zero network/key dependency, so it's a perfect no-fetch companion if 718's grain-answer proves unmusical OR for an offline/installation context). **Play a phrase; when you pause, a rule-based responder answers ‚Äî complementary register, inverted contour, density-matched, weighted scale step-walk, forced consonant chord-tone landing ‚Äî a reply structurally guaranteed never to echo your pitches.** Key+mode inferred by scoring 12 tonics against Dorian/Mixolydian/Aeolian templates (D Dorian fallback). Built files (removed; clean tsc+eslint self-reported): `page.tsx` (36 KB ‚Äî three inputs [Web MIDI hot-plug + `asdfghjkl`/`wetyuo`/`z/x` keyboard + on-screen ‚â•44px keys], phrase detection, heuristic responder, ducking, idle auto-demo, Canvas2D river, FM-epiano L / flute R + binding pad, master‚Üígain(0.45)‚Üícomp(‚àí3/20:1)) + `README.md`. Named refs: **George Lewis *Voyager*** ¬∑ jazz trading-fours. Ambition honest 2/5 (#2 ‚â•4 subsystems + #3 named refs). Tags: MIDI/keyboard INPUT ¬∑ Canvas2D OUTPUT ¬∑ heuristic call-response TECH ¬∑ jazz-responsive VIBE. **Why it lost:** Karel's ask was specifically "wired to your *Paths* piano" ‚Üí 718 (his real recording) is the truer realization, and 718 binds this cycle's MACataRT dive; 716 is the generic-synth version. **Resurrect** as the dependency-free Shadow Duet (installation/offline), or merge its key-inference + step-walk taste INTO 718 as a cleaner answer-line generator. **Deepen:** velocity-sensitive dynamics (soft phrase ‚Üí tender answer); harmonic memory (a pad following a slow form seeded by your recurring pitch-sets).

### `717-duet-mirror` ‚Äî `queued` ‚≠ê (the **Continuator / style-learner** mind ‚Äî the genuinely SURPRISING one: the partner *learns you* live and its answers grow to sound like your own playing recombined; the lab's first real-time live-trained style model). **As you play, a variable-order [orders 1‚Äì3, back-off] Markov model absorbs your pitch-INTERVAL sequence [key-agnostic, ¬±12] + your rhythm [fast/med/slow buckets]; on each phrase-end it folds the phrase into a persistent session model and answers by SAMPLING that model seeded from your tail ‚Äî so it starts generic-Dorian and, over ~a dozen phrases, mirrors *your* vocabulary back transformed.** A "learned: N phrases" + "memory richness" meter make the growth legible. Built files (removed; clean tsc+eslint self-reported): `page.tsx` (31 KB ‚Äî same three inputs, the live Markov learner, ducking, idle demo that FEEDS the model + ticks the count, Canvas2D river, FM-epiano L / bell R + pad, master‚Üígain(0.42)‚Üícomp limiter) + `README.md`. Named refs: **Fran√ßois Pachet *Continuator* (2002)** ¬∑ IRCAM **OMax / Somax2**. Ambition honest 3/5 (**#1** first lab live-trained style model [grep-checked: no prior dream piece builds a variable-order Markov over the player's own live input] + #2 ‚â•4 subsystems + #3 named refs). Tags: MIDI/keyboard INPUT ¬∑ Canvas2D OUTPUT ¬∑ live-Markov style-learning TECH ¬∑ jazz-responsive VIBE. **Why it lost:** the "it sounds like *you*" payoff is the LEAST legible at a silent 06:30 glance (the learned-count climbs but you can't HEAR the learning without playing a minute yourself), and it doesn't bind this cycle's concatenative dive. **Why resurrect ‚≠ê:** it carries the only #1 of the three and the freshest *surprise* ‚Äî lead a future adult fire framed as "**the instrument learns you over a session**" (deepen: a "play me back what I taught you" button that improvises a solo entirely from the accumulated model; persist the model to localStorage so it remembers you across visits). Strongest when the diversity pick wants a *learned* technique, not a sensor reskin.

## ¬ß469 ‚Äî 2026-06-18 ¬∑ adult DEEP fire ("fly inside your own recording" ‚Äî ONE concept, 3 technical approaches; answers JURY 2026-06-18 #3 "extend the ceiling builds 710/718, reward depth" + #1 "ban Canvas2D, use the scarce WebGPU stack" + #5 "use his real music") ‚Äî winner `720-paths-grainfield`; **2 banked seeds**

Cycle 469 ran an adult **DEEP** fan-out. Research path (b)/(c): extends ceiling builds **710-presence-bloom** (WebGPU compute particle field) + **718-duet-paths** (concatenative grains of Karel's real Welcome Home piano), grounded by CataRT + Audio Latent Space Cartography (RESEARCH ¬ß469). All three turn his real recording into a navigable corpus you move through to re-sound it. **Winner: `720-paths-grainfield`** ‚Äî the only approach on WebGPU compute (lab's scarcest renderer; JURY #1's literal ask) + truest 710 cycle-2. Both losers were demoable + self-reported clean tsc+eslint; authoritative winner-only `npm run build` (exit 0, 720 prerendered static) is the gate; losers moved to `/tmp/dream-losers/` then removed before it.

**NUMBERING NOTE (important):** `720-paths-grainfield` is now a **real committed folder** ‚Üí **max committed folder after this fire = 720.** The ¬ß468 banked kids seed-labels "720-kids-shake-pop" / "721-kids-blow-balloon" were never committed folders ‚Äî when resurrected they must be **renumbered to ‚â•721** (suggest `721-kids-shake-pop` ‚≠ê, `722-kids-blow-balloon`). The two adult seeds below are likewise just text ‚Äî renumber to ‚â•721 when consumed.

Resurrect by re-spawning the brief:

- **`paths-spectral-cloud`** ‚≠ê (was built as 722; renumber ‚â•721 when consumed) ‚Äî **the most conceptually novel of the three.** His recording's full **STFT becomes a 3D point cloud** (time √ó log-frequency √ó loudness) you **orbit and fly the camera through**; flying into a (time,freq) region re-sounds the grains living there ‚Äî *navigating the SHAPE of his sound plays it back*. Hand-written radix-2 FFT (1024 window / 512 hop), sparse-peak cloud capped ~55k three.js Points, additive + fog, CataRT-style region resynthesis. Named refs: **Xenakis UPIC** (drawing sound in a time-freq plane) + **Ikeda** data-as-points. Extends loved **243-spectral-cloud** + **321-spectral-flight** + 718. Lost only because it shares three.js (already 3√ó in the window) with 721 while 720 carried the scarcer WebGPU compute ‚Äî but its interaction ("fly the spectrogram to play it") is arguably the lab's freshest. **Next-cycle deepening:** STFT in a Web Worker w/ progress bar; k-d-tree nearest-grain (replace stride-scan); TSL compute pass so points pulse with their grain's envelope as they sound; UPIC-style "draw a path, auto-fly it" re-composition.
- **`paths-constellation`** (was built as 721; renumber ‚â•721 when consumed) ‚Äî Karel's recording as a **drifting three.js star-galaxy**: each grain a star, *angle* = time / *radius* = brightness / *height* = loudness (spiral arms); a soft violet attractor eases toward the pointer and re-sounds the nearest ~3 stars with curl-noise drift + orbiting camera. Rides the loved GPU-points cluster **130-tsl-particle-compute** + **262-aurora-particle** + 718. Warmest/most-legible of the three; lost on renderer-scarcity (three.js, not WebGPU) and a ZCR brightness proxy vs a true FFT centroid. **Next-cycle deepening:** Web-Worker/GPU kd-tree for 100k+ grains; a "constellation-line" overlay tracing the attractor's path (the actual concatenative score); reverb send sized by attractor speed.

**Standing adult seeds still unshipped (carried):** `717-duet-mirror` ‚≠ê (the live "learns-you" Continuator style-learner, the only #1 banked from cycle 467) ¬∑ the **WebGPU-compute audio-reactive field** modulated by his piano FFT (Ghost-Arcade June-2026 reference, RESEARCH ¬ß468; now that 720 lands a real WebGPU-compute foundation in the lab, this is the natural deepening) ¬∑ **real cross-device multi-user** (JURY #4 ‚Äî all 3 multiplayer pieces are same-browser BroadcastChannel; 0√ó truly cross-device).

### IDEAS ¬ß473 ‚Äî banked from cycle 473's adult DEEP cross-device fire (winner shipped `729-piano-portal-jam`)

Both are complete, build-reviewed implementations (compile+lint-clean) moved to `/tmp/dream-losers/` this cycle ‚Äî resurrect by copying the folder back in and renumbering above the then-current max. The shared concept: **a genuine cross-device WebRTC jam on Karel's real Welcome Home piano** (jury #4 lab-first), with the "send note-events not PCM ‚Äî both peers re-sound from the same corpus" twist. They differ on the **signaling handshake √ó renderer**.

- **`728-piano-relay-jam`** ‚≠ê (queued ¬∑ adult ¬∑ zero-server **copy-paste two-code** WebRTC handshake ¬∑ raw **WebGL2** two-color shared field ¬∑ concatenative grains of his recording) ‚Äî the purest zero-dependency handshake (native `CompressionStream` gzip ‚Üí base64url SDP codes, non-trickle ICE, no backend at all) on the **scarcer renderer** (WebGL2 vs the winner's three.js). *Why it lost:* the two-codes-back-and-forth copy/paste is higher-friction than 729's shareable link, so it's less likely a real person completes the two-phone handshake ‚Äî and retiring the BroadcastChannel asterisk *in practice* was the deciding axis. *Why resurrect:* it's the most robust-by-construction link (no server, no lambda fragility) AND carries the scarcer renderer; pair its WebGL2 field with 729's `navigator.share` link flow for the best of both. Files exist verbatim.

- **`730-piano-room-jam`** ‚≠ê (queued ¬∑ adult ¬∑ friendly **4-letter room code** via a **guarded serverless SDP relay** [`api/route.ts`, POST-only, guard-compliant] ¬∑ **WebGPU** compute particle field ¬∑ concatenative grains) ‚Äî by far the **friendliest UX** (type the same 4 letters, no copy/paste) and the **scarcest renderer** (WebGPU, 1√ó). *Why it lost ‚Äî the decisive flaw, in its own README:* the relay stores `{code‚Üí{offer,answer}}` in a **module-level `Map` in per-lambda memory**, and Vercel routes the two peers to *different* lambda instances where that map isn't shared ‚Üí the offer one peer deposits may **never reach the other**, so on real Vercel it likely **won't connect** ‚Äî which fails the jury's exact ask ("make it *actually* cross-device"). *Why resurrect (high value):* it's the **best piece the moment a backing store exists.** The dedicated next step is to ask Karel to approve moving the relay store to **Vercel KV / Upstash Redis** (a `package.json` change, which only he can make per the scope fence); with that one swap, 730 becomes the real magic ‚Äî frictionless room-code cross-device jamming on the scarcest renderer. Flag this dependency ask explicitly in a morning question.

## ¬ß474 ‚Äî 2026-06-19 ¬∑ kids DEEP fire (ONE concept ‚Äî *Star Scoop: reach bare hands into a 3D night sky and scoop handfuls of glowing stars that chime* ‚Äî 3 renderer √ó synth approaches, orchestrated; resurrecting the twice-banked ‚≠ê `726-kids-star-scoop` seed = the directest answer to JURY 2026-06-18 #3 "stop banking and walking away") ‚Äî winner `731-kids-star-reach` (raw WebGL2); **2 banked seeds**

Cycle 474 ran a kids **DEEP** fan-out (deliberate alternation off the last two kids WIDE fires, ¬ß470/¬ß472). ONE embodied/off-glass concept ‚Äî bare-hands MediaPipe scoop of a glowing star-field that rings consonant bell clusters, awe/wonder, **no beat/loop/groove** ‚Äî attacked via three renderers: three.js (730) ¬∑ raw WebGL2 (731, WON) ¬∑ WebGPU compute (732). Research path (c): consumed the twice-banked ‚≠ê seed `726-kids-star-scoop` (¬ß472/¬ß470, born of RESEARCH ¬ß470's iOS-WebGPU finding); RESEARCH ¬ß474 confirmed gesture‚Üíparticle-swarm is a live 2026 commodity but the *musical-scoop* mapping is the open Resonance move. **Winner = `731` (raw WebGL2)** on the deciding axis of **renderer-scarcity + iOS reviewer-path reliability**: WebGPU is warming hard in the rolling window (721/724/727, the scarce renderer becoming its own monoculture) and three.js was just used last cycle (729), where **raw WebGL2 is the scarcest renderer AND the most bulletproof on Karel's 06:30 phone** (iOS has WebGL2 reliably; zero CDN renderer dep beyond the shared MediaPipe). All three were demoable + self-reported clean tsc+eslint; the two losers `mv`'d to `/tmp/dream-losers/` before the authoritative winner-only build. ‚öÝÔ∏è The authoritative `npm run build` **compiled + linted clean** (zero issues in the winner folder) but couldn't run static-generation ‚Äî the container's anomalous **4096 fd ceiling** killed it with `EMFILE`; proven environmental (pristine `main` fails identically ‚Äî see STATE.md cycle 474). **Max committed folder after this fire = `731`.** Resurrect by re-spawning the brief + the prior folder structure (both losers were fully built in `/tmp/dream-losers/` this fire):

### `730-kids-star-scoop` ‚Äî `queued` ‚≠ê (the three.js sibling ‚Äî the renderer every 2026 hand-particle repo reaches for; warmest/most-legible glow). **Reach bare hands into a 3D night sky and scoop handfuls of glowing stars that chime.** MediaPipe Hands (CDN, analysis-only, numHands 2) ‚Üí a **three.js** `THREE.Points` field of ~2,400 additive glow sprites with `FogExp2` depth, swaying camera, per-star twinkle. Close a fist ‚Üí nearest ~20 stars gather to the palm + a soft sparse C-major-pentatonic cluster; open wide ‚Üí owned stars spill upward with a 5-step rising glissando; hand height indexes register. Kids-safe `voices ‚Üí gain 0.28 ‚Üí lowpass 6.5k ‚Üí comp ‚àí10/20:1`, always-on open-fifth (C2+G2) drone, ghost two-hand auto-demo on camera-deny/idle >3.5s (`text-rose-300` notice), Canvas2D fallback reproducing the gather/spill physics, full teardown (three.js dispose + forceContextLoss). **Tags:** MediaPipe-hands camera INPUT (off-glass) ¬∑ three.js 3D-points OUTPUT ¬∑ gesture particle-gathering + spatial bell-cluster TECH (NOT loop/groove) ¬∑ awe/wonder VIBE. **Named refs:** MediaPipe Hands ¬∑ *Journey* (thatgamecompany) ¬∑ Refik Anadol. **Why it lost:** three.js was just used last cycle (729) and is the warmest of the trio in the rolling window; the WebGL2 sibling is the scarcer, more iOS-bulletproof scarce renderer. **Resurrect when** the kids side wants the richest-looking glow and three.js is cold in the rolling-10, or as the direct three.js comparison to 731. Honest 2‚Äì3/5 (#2 ‚â•3 subsystems [MediaPipe hands + three.js 3D point-field + spatial bell-cluster audio] + #3 named refs). Full build exists at `/tmp/dream-losers/730-kids-star-scoop/` this fire (treat as a fresh build on revival).

### `732-kids-star-cradle` ‚Äî `queued` ‚≠ê (the WebGPU-compute sibling ‚Äî the scarcest, biggest-particle-count renderer; the bolder swing once WebGPU cools). **Same Star Scoop concept on a WebGPU compute particle field.** MediaPipe Hands ‚Üí ~**12,000 particles** in a storage buffer integrated each frame by an **inline WGSL compute pass** (ambient drift + per-hand attract/repel falloff + a *cradling swirl* when a fist closes), drawn as instanced additive glow sprites (compute + render passes). Hand position(s) + grab strength (0..1, 1 = fist) passed as uniforms; fresh fist-close/open events edge-detected in JS ring a pentatonic bell cluster / rising glissando; hand height = register. **First-class Canvas2D fallback** runs the *same* gather/spill physics (~2,600 glow blobs) so the reviewer's likely no-WebGPU path still feels good; a badge shows the live renderer ("WebGPU compute ¬∑ 12k stars" vs "Canvas2D fallback"). Kids-safe `gain 0.28 ‚Üí lowpass 7k ‚Üí comp ‚àí10/20:1`, always-on open-fifth drone, ghost two-hand auto-demo, all GPU init try/caught (never throws into the loop), full teardown (destroy buffers + device). **Tags:** MediaPipe-hands camera INPUT (off-glass) ¬∑ WebGPU-compute particle OUTPUT (Canvas2D fallback only) ¬∑ gesture particle-gathering + spatial bell-cluster TECH (NOT loop/groove) ¬∑ awe/wonder VIBE. **Named refs:** MediaPipe Hands ¬∑ *Journey* ¬∑ Refik Anadol. **Why it lost:** WebGPU is the most ambitious renderer but is warming to a monoculture itself (721/724/727 ‚Äî 3 of the last kids/adult ships) and WebGPU-on-iPad (Safari 26) is still fragile, so Karel's 06:30 phone glance most likely lands on the Canvas2D fallback ‚Äî whereas 731's WebGL2 runs the real scarce renderer reliably on iOS. **Resurrect when** WebGPU is cold in the rolling window AND there's a real iPad to verify the compute path, OR as the spectacle/installation version (12k GPU particles read bigger than 731's 2,600). Honest 2/5 (#2 ‚â•3 subsystems [MediaPipe hands + WebGPU compute particle field + spatial bell-cluster audio] + #3 named refs). Full build exists at `/tmp/dream-losers/732-kids-star-cradle/` this fire.

## ¬ß475 (2026-06-19) ‚Äî banked from the adult DEEP disintegration fire (winner: `734-paths-tape-erosion`)

Two non-winners from cycle 475's 3-way bake (one concept ‚Äî *Karel's real Welcome Home recording slowly disintegrates & re-forms over a 6‚Äì8 min arc; Basinski* The Disintegration Loops *+ Eno* Music for Airports/Reflection ‚Äî three renderer attacks). Both are demoable-grade and clear the ambition floor; resurrect either when the renderer rotation favors it.

### `733-paths-disintegrate` ‚≠ê ‚Äî WebGPU compute, 40,000 GPU particles
The boldest, scarcest-renderer swing at the same concept. A WGSL **ping-pong storage-buffer compute shader** updates 40k particles in parallel; per-movement behavior (*Intact* orbit ‚Üí *Eroding* fly-away ‚Üí *Sparse* dim-drift ‚Üí *Ghost* near-invisible flashes ‚Üí *Reforming* home-pull), color warm-amber‚Üícool-blue‚Üíviolet‚Üíamber. Same audio engine as 734 (Hann grains of his recording, look-ahead scheduler, per-movement LP/reverb/dropout, soft pad drone, offline fallback synth). Canvas2D fallback (~8k CPU particles). **Why it lost:** a 40k-particle storm visually overlaps the lab's recent particle-storm pieces (`710-presence-bloom`, `724-presence-drift`) and 735 ‚Äî the *spectral-feedback spectrogram* of 734 was the fresher register; AND WebGPU may show only its Canvas2D fallback on an iPad (WebGPU is Baseline Jan 2026 but Safari support is newer than WebGL2's). **Why resurrect:** WebGPU compute is the scarcest renderer in the window AND the boldest; once we confirm Karel's review device runs WebGPU (it likely does on desktop Safari 26 / Mac), 40k GPU particles disintegrating read much bigger than the Canvas2D fallback. Built monolithic (1255-line page.tsx) ‚Äî refactor to the 734 file-split on resurrection. Full code was preserved off-tree this cycle; rebuild from the README spec.

### `735-paths-revenant` ‚≠ê ‚Äî three.js, 60,000 `Points` volumetric ghost (Anadol lineage)
The warmest-looking attack: 60k `THREE.Points` with `AdditiveBlending` + `FogExp2` in three radial shells (dense core 55% / mid 30% / outer veil 15%), warm-amber-core‚Üícool-blue-veil; every frame the active-point fraction (18%‚Äì100%), scatter, opacity, fog density and slow camera-orbit are driven by the 5-movement state + live FFT; a core glow mesh pulses with bass. Same grain/movement audio engine; Canvas2D 3k-particle fallback. Named ref **Refik Anadol ‚Äî data-as-fog** (*Melting Memories* / *Living Architecture*). **Why it lost:** three.js was just shipped last cycle (`729-piano-portal-jam`) so it's the least-diverse renderer of the three, and a point-cloud ghost again overlaps the particle-storm register; the *Reforming* movement only restores ~60% density (intentional but reads incomplete), and 60k per-frame JS position updates risk <60fps on low-end devices. **Why resurrect:** it is the warmest, most "Anadol installation" of the three ‚Äî pull it when three.js has cooled in the rotation and pair it with `@react-three/postprocessing` `UnrealBloomPass` (the README notes bloom is currently faked via additive blending) for a genuinely richer glow.

### Process note for next cycle (worktree quirk)
Builder subagents this cycle self-isolated into **git worktrees** (`.claude/worktrees/agent-*`), so one builder's files (734) were invisible in the main tree until the agent finished ‚Äî nearly got curated out as "empty/failed." Next fire: after spawning, check `git worktree list` / the worktree paths as well as the main tree before judging a builder "broken." (`.claude/worktrees` is gitignored ‚Äî safe, just invisible.)

### 734 next-cycle deepening (the multi-cycle commitment)
`734-paths-tape-erosion` is designed to extend: (cycle-2) drive the read-head + erosion params off **onset/phrase detection** of his recording (spectral flux) so the decay reflects his *musical* structure, not a fixed scrub; add a **longer algorithmic reverb tail** (current convolver is short ‚Äî the *Ghost* movement wants seconds of wash); optional **WebGPU compute** port (resurrect 733's engine) for a denser field on capable devices. (cycle-3) a gentle single-touch "press to slow the decay / hold a motif" interaction so the listener can *resist* the erosion ‚Äî the Basinski tension made playable.

## ¬ß479 banked seeds (2026-06-19, from the cycle-479 adult WIDE fire)

### ‚≠ê `745-room-listener` ‚Äî the lab's FIRST in-browser neural net (status: queued, HIGH priority)
**The brief that was built and banked, not shipped.** The room, sung: a real neural network (**TensorFlow.js + COCO-SSD**, `lite_mobilenet_v2`, loaded from `esm.sh` CDN via `webpackIgnore`'d dynamic `import()`; weights from `storage.googleapis.com`, CORS-open ‚Äî no npm install, no API route) watches the live camera at ~5fps on a 320px offscreen copy and turns **each detected object into a sustained Web Audio voice** ‚Äî class name ‚Üí hashed D-dorian pitch, box-x ‚Üí pan, box-y ‚Üí filter cutoff, size√óconfidence ‚Üí loudness; objects fade in/out as they enter/leave frame. The chord is a living portrait of your space. **Why promising:** ambition **3/5** (#1 first TFJS/neural inference in the lab ‚Äî grep-verified never used; #2 ‚â•4 subsystems; #3 Memo Akten *Learning to See*), the exact "novel technique ‚Üí ceiling" the jury keeps asking for, and **triple love-aligned** (`101-camera-song`, `86-sound-to-video`, `323-latent-condensation`). **Why banked not shipped:** for an unattended cycle I couldn't verify, its entire headline lives inside an **unverifiable CDN model load** (ghost fallback would show a generic substitute if it fails), and the builder shipped it on **Canvas2D** ‚Äî the renderer the 2026-06-18 jury most loudly told the lab to stop defaulting to. **How to resurrect (next adult cycle, DEEP):** rebuild on a **scarce renderer** (WebGL2/WebGPU per JURY #1); **verify the esm.sh model load actually resolves** under the deploy network (or self-host weights / pin a known-good CDN); keep the robust ghost fallback. This is the strongest banked seed in the queue ‚Äî the boldest swing and the most jury-and-love-aligned. (Working code exists in this cycle's transcript / the curated-out folder if a fast restart is wanted.)

### `746-orbit-bell` ‚Äî live ISS telemetry ‚Üí cosmic globe (status: queued)
Hear the ISS overhead in real time: `https://api.wheretheiss.at/v1/satellites/25544` (keyless, CORS-open, read-only GET ‚Äî no `guard`), polled ~every 4s. Altitude ‚Üí drone register, velocity ‚Üí tremolo rate, latitude ‚Üí harmonic tilt + hemisphere pan; the instant `visibility` flips `daylight`‚Üî`eclipsed` between polls, ring an **inharmonic struck bell** (bright entering sun, dark entering shadow). Visual: three.js shader globe with a **live-computed day/night terminator** (sub-solar point from UTC), pulsing ISS marker on the real ground track, rolling orbit trail, starfield, crossing flash synced to the bell. **Why promising:** genuine live real-world sonification on a source the lab hasn't used (orbital telemetry); event-detection (terminator crossings) drives a designed bell; self-contained ghost-orbit sim (~51.6¬∞ track, compressed eclipse cadence) keeps it fully alive offline. **Why banked:** honest 2/5 (#2 + #3 NASA/*Quindar*); cosmic-space-data overlaps existing `314-solar-wind` territory, and `744`'s compass was the higher-confidence, more-novel pick this cycle. **Resurrect** as a cosmic palette-extension (loved-cosmic-adjacent) or fold the terminator-bell idea into a future space piece.

## ¬ß482 banked seeds (2026-06-19, from the cycle-482 kids DEEP fire ‚Äî "a child says a word and it becomes music")

Both were built to demoable and curated out (winner was `752-kids-word-band`). Working code existed in this cycle's transcript / the curated-out folders; rebuild from the brief below. Both clear the floor (#2 ‚â•3 subsystems + qualified-#5 RESEARCH ¬ß411/¬ß482 + kids-first speech) and dodge every JURY 2026-06-19 ban.

### ‚≠ê `751-kids-say-a-zoo` ‚Äî semantic word ‚Üí singing creature (status: queued, HIGH priority ‚Äî the most pure-4yo-delightful of the three)
**The brief that was built and banked.** A 4-year-old SAYS a word ‚Äî "dog!", "sunshine!" ‚Äî and it instantly becomes a bright bouncy **creature** that sings, filling a sunny Canvas2D meadow with the child's own vocabulary as a living consonant chord-garden. Mapping: word LENGTH ‚Üí creature size; stable HASH ‚Üí bold hue + base pitch (quantized C-major pentatonic, nothing wrong); VOWEL count ‚Üí sung-phrase length (1‚Äì4 notes); hash bit-fields ‚Üí eye/leg/wing style. Cap ~12 creatures (oldest hops off). Web Speech API (auto-restart) + 8 always-visible ‚â•64px emoji picture buttons (üê∂üê±‚òÄÔ∏è‚≠êüêüüê¶üå∏üåà, identical mapping, zero-mic playable) + 3s auto-demo. Kids-safe chain (master ‚â§0.27 ‚Üí lowpass 5.5kHz ‚Üí 20:1 compressor), always-on C2/G2/C3 pad. Refs: Toca Boca / Sago Mini. **Why promising:** the clearest, most magical cause-effect for a literal 4-year-old ("say dog ‚Üí see+hear a dog"); clean compile+lint. **Why banked not shipped:** the winner `752` filled the JURY-named SVG/DOM-no-shader gap (#2) and had the more *musical* (phonetic-riff) mapping; `751` is Canvas2D (fine, scarce) but the semantic one-chord-per-creature is less musically rich. **Resurrect FIRST** next kids cycle ‚Äî strongest delight, and Canvas2D vs SVG diversifies off whatever 752 establishes.

### `753-kids-spell-parade` ‚Äî rhythmic word ‚Üí accumulating marching-band groove (status: queued)
**The brief that was built and banked.** A child SAYS a word; each letter parachutes down as a giant Canvas2D character, lands on one of five colored drum pads, and adds its beat to a **growing parade groove** (Chris-Wilson scheduler, 90 BPM, 16th grid). Letters‚Üípads via stable mapping (KICK/SNARE/HAT/TOM/BELL); a C-pentatonic bell layer under all percussion (nothing wrong). **Long-form stateful:** the last ~5 words form the living loop, older characters march off-screen ‚Äî genuinely different at minute 5 than minute 1 (the thin kids-stateful category the jury keeps asking for). Web Speech + 8 picture buttons + auto-demo; kids-safe chain + C-major drone. Refs: Incredibox; marching-band toys. **Why promising:** the only of the three that is long-form/stateful (jury-valued); "the parade grows as you talk" is a strong sustained-play hook. **Why banked:** self-reported risk ‚Äî at ~40 characters across 5 pads, visual crowding/overlap on small phone screens needs tuning, and groove musicality depends on word choice (consonant-heavy words stack on one pad). Resurrect with per-pad character-spacing fixes + a light per-word rhythmic-spread so any word grooves.

## ¬ß483 ‚Äî Conducted Room banked explorers (from cycle 483 DEEP fan-out; winner shipped = 754-conducted-table, Canvas2D)

The cycle-483 DEEP concept: **"The Conducted Room"** ‚Äî extend 729-piano-portal-jam's WebRTC note-event ceiling to a 3+ player CONDUCTED ensemble, but replace 729's (banned) grain engine with a **non-grain phrase-remix** of Karel's real recording (onset/silence-segmented 0.6‚Äì3s phrases; each seat plays whole phrases via playbackRate register-shift). Long-form stateful arc (migrating harmonic center, breathing density, per-seat drift; minute 5 ‚âÝ minute 0). Star/conductor-hub WebRTC topology (zero-server shareable-link handshake from 729). Ghost ensemble keeps it fully alive + sounding solo on one device; remote players fill chairs. Both banked builds are demoable + tsc/eslint-clean ‚Äî they were de-selected on the renderer-scarcity axis, NOT on quality.

- **`753-conducted-orbit` ‚≠ê (three.js).** "Concert in the round": glowing central conductor + ~5 orbiting luminous musician-orbs over a warm cream-amber-rust radial-gradient stage (genuinely bright, not a dark void). **Conduct by dragging a baton anywhere on the stage** ‚Äî vertical = dynamics, horizontal sweep speed = tempo, release holds. ~6-min arc; ghost conductor auto-sweeps after ~2.6s idle. `Hub` class stacks one Portal per chair (star). Per-pulse rings + drifting Points sparks. **Why banked:** three.js was used *last cycle* (729) ‚Üí warmest renderer of the trio in the rolling window; the baton-on-a-3D-stage gesture is the most physically expressive conducting model of the three. **Resurrect when:** three.js cools in the rolling window, or as the DEEP companion if a future cycle wants the in-the-round spatial reading. Clean next step noted in its own README: true full-mesh (guests hear each other, not just host broadcast) + phrase-aware harmony (pick phrases whose detected pitch fits the current chord).

- **`755-conducted-stage` (SVG/DOM).** "Conductor's dashboard": the distinctive feature is a **long-form TIMELINE RIBBON** across the screen (6 sections, density-silhouette strokes, crawling playhead) that makes the 5.5-min stateful arc legible at a glance ‚Äî past scrolls behind, structure hints ahead. Seats as DOM/SVG cards; baton control (vertical=dynamics, sweep speed=tempo). Bright warm amber/rose palette. **Why banked:** SVG/DOM shipped the **two prior cycles in a row** (748, 752) ‚Üí a third would itself be a monoculture; and its self-reported caveat is that phrases fire at the current audio clock rather than sample-accurately at each future beat (tighten the look-ahead before resurrecting). **Resurrect when:** the timeline-ribbon idea is wanted as a long-form-legibility layer ‚Äî it is the best of the three at *showing* the arc, and could be grafted onto the shipped 754 as an optional "score view." First fix the scheduler to schedule each beat sample-accurately.

## ¬ß484 banked seeds (2026-06-19, from the cycle-484 kids WIDE fire ‚Äî "the bright/joyful/active-middle kids register, off every JURY-2026-06-19 ban")

Cycle 484 ran a kids **WIDE** fan-out (deliberate alternation off the two prior DEEP fires ¬ß482/¬ß483; and the jury's loudest 2026-06-19 message ‚Äî *every ban hardened into a new monoculture* ‚Äî is a WIDE-shaped anti-monoculture ask). Three divergent **bright daylight, active/joyful** explorers (JURY #4), each off every 2026-06-19 ban (no grain-resynthesis ¬∑ no WebGPU/raw-WebGL2 shader-field ¬∑ no mic/camera ¬∑ no dark-glow), diverging on INPUT √ó OUTPUT √ó TECHNIQUE √ó PALETTE. Winner shipped = **`755-kids-bounce-house`** (Canvas2D Verlet trampoline membrane ‚Äî the research‚Üíbuild chain from RESEARCH ¬ß484, the literal JURY #2 "no-GPU-shader, Canvas2D-is-scarce-again" answer + JURY #4 bright/joyful). Both non-winners are demoable + self-reported clean tsc/eslint (no `any`, no `use*` helpers, no API route, SSR-safe); de-selected on ambition-floor height + the renderer-scarcity tiebreak, NOT on quality. Rebuild from the briefs below (full briefs are in this cycle's STATE entry / the curated-out folders).

### ‚≠ê `756-kids-hill-roller` ‚Äî sculpt sunny hills, then TILT to roll a ball over them = play the melody you drew (status: queued, HIGH ‚Äî the love-aligned tilt sibling)
**The brief that was built and banked.** A bright daylight landscape: ~10 rounded hills the child shapes by **dragging each up/down** (taller hill = higher major-pentatonic note, color shifts green‚Üígold with pitch, immediate audio on shape). A happy ball rolls across under gravity driven by **device tilt** (`gamma`); every crest it rolls over **chimes that hill's note** ‚Äî so the child *composes by sculpting terrain* then *performs by tilting*. Robust **no-tilt fallback** (giant left/right tap zones + a "Play the hills" auto-sweep that rolls the ball across the whole contour) so it's fully playable + sounding on the reviewer's desktop glance with zero tilt. SVG/DOM only (zero canvas/WebGL/shader), kids-safe chain (`gain ‚â§0.3 ‚Üí lowpass 7500 ‚Üí comp ‚àí10/20:1`), always-on pad, 3s-idle auto-demo. **Why promising:** the *sculpt-the-terrain-then-tilt-to-play* loop is a genuinely fresh kids mechanic (distinct from the saturated drop‚Üícollision pieces), it's embodied (KIDS.md tilt principle), and it is **love-aligned** (`83-kids-tilt-rain`‚ù§Ô∏è, `82-kids-color-piano`‚ù§Ô∏è). **Why banked not shipped:** honest 2/5 (#2 ‚â•3 subsystems [tilt input + 1D rolling-ball integrator on a child-editable height field + crest-detection synth + SVG render] + #3 [xylophone/color-pitch pedagogy ¬∑ Loopimal/Toca Boca]); the winner `755` had the higher floor (research chain + lab-first cloth membrane) and Canvas2D was the cleaner JURY-#2 renderer answer than another SVG/DOM ship. **Resurrect FIRST** next kids cycle ‚Äî strongest banked seed, love-aligned, and SVG/tilt diversifies off whatever ships next. Code was clean + demoable this fire.

### `757-kids-balloon-band` ‚Äî inflate balloons whose pitch rises with size; released, they accrete a poppable sky-chord (status: queued)
**The brief that was built and banked.** A bright daytime sky: **press-and-hold anywhere** to inflate a bright balloon ‚Äî it grows and its note **glides UP in pitch with size** (continuous gesture‚Üípitch, quantized to C-major-pentatonic on release, theremin-glide brought down to a 4yo). **Release** ‚Üí it detaches, floats up with gentle buoyancy/sway, and holds a **sustained voice**; the drifting balloons form an evolving, always-consonant **chord in the sky** the child builds. **Tap a floater** ‚Üí soft bright "ding" + sparkle `THREE.Points` burst; its note leaves the chord. Cap ~10 (oldest drifts off top + fades). three.js mesh scene-graph (MeshStandardMaterial spheres, NOT a full-screen GLSL/WebGPU shader field), kids-safe chain (`gain 0.26 ‚Üí lowpass 6800 ‚Üí comp ‚àí10/20:1`), always-on pad, 3s-idle auto-demo, full three.js disposal (`renderer.dispose()` + `forceContextLoss()`). **Why promising:** `growth = pitch` is a clear, tactile, fresh-for-the-lab cause-effect, and the accreting *poppable* harmony is a lovely "build then take apart" loop; bright/joyful. **Why banked:** honest 2/5 (#2 ‚â•3 subsystems + #3 [Toca Boca/Sago Mini ¬∑ balloon play ¬∑ theremin glide]); three.js is warmer in the rolling window than Canvas2D, and the winner's research chain + lab-first membrane outranked it. **Resurrect when** three.js is cold in the rolling-10 and a bright accreting-harmony toy is wanted; pair the pop with a slightly richer particle burst. Code was clean + demoable this fire.

## ¬ß490 banked seeds (2026-06-20, from the cycle-490 kids WIDE fire ‚Äî "the bright/joyful/active-middle kids register, off every JURY-2026-06-19 ban")

Cycle 490 ran a kids **WIDE** fan-out (even-cycle kids rotation; JURY 2026-06-19 #4 wants the bright daylight active/joyful MIDDLE). Three divergent explorers, each off every 2026-06-19 ban (no grain-resynthesis ¬∑ no GPU-shader field ¬∑ no mic-hum ¬∑ no dark-glow), diverging on INPUT √ó OUTPUT √ó TECHNIQUE √ó NAMED-REF. Winner shipped = **`773-kids-parade-caller`** (Canvas2D, speech-ASR ‚Üí syllable-rhythm parade groove ‚Äî the research‚Üíbuild chain from RESEARCH ¬ß490, the lab's first speech-as-RHYTHM). Both non-winners are demoable + self-reported clean tsc/eslint (no `any`, no `use*` helpers, no API route, SSR-safe); de-selected on the surprise / research-chain axis, NOT on quality ‚Äî both rebuild from the briefs below (full briefs are in this cycle's STATE entry).

### ‚≠ê `774-kids-marble-symphony` ‚Äî build a Wintergatan-style marble machine: drop marbles, place bell-bars, gravity makes the music (status: queued, HIGH ‚Äî scarcest renderer + love-aligned)
**The brief that was built and banked.** A bright daytime stage: a marble drops from a spout, falls under real gravity, and BOUNCES off a zig-zag of tilted bell-bars ‚Äî each collision rings a velocity-scaled marimba/bell ping and flashes the bar; the kid TAPS to drop more marbles or place new bell-bars (the simplest cause-and-effect), and the machine **always self-plays** (auto-timer recycles marbles from the spout). `physics.ts` = a resolution-independent Euler integrator with **circle-vs-segment collision + restitution** (bars are line segments; reflect velocity about the contact normal, ~0.72 bounce); `bells.ts` = live Web-Audio bell/marimba + woody thump + C2 bass, **C-major-pentatonic** tuning (higher bars = higher pitch, nothing can sound wrong). **Output: SVG/DOM** (`<circle>` marbles with a radial shine, `<rect>` stage, `<line>` bars, repainted each rAF) ‚Äî the **scarcest renderer in the lab** (JURY #2 explicitly wants SVG/DOM proven) and zero canvas/WebGL/shader. Ref: **Wintergatan Marble Machine** (Martin Molin) ‚Äî watching gravity + marbles MAKE the music; nods to marble-run toys / Rube Goldberg. **Why promising:** the physics feels alive and mechanical (not a tap-pad), it's the only SVG/DOM piece of the three (best renderer-diversity answer), and it is **love-aligned** ‚Äî the loved `169-kids-marble-run`‚ù§Ô∏è is the exact territory ("do more in this direction"). **Why banked not shipped:** honest 2/5 (#2 four subsystems [2D physics solver + collision‚Üíbell synth + touch placement + SVG render] + #3 [Wintergatan]); the winner `773` had the higher *surprise*/research-chain (lab-first speech-as-rhythm bound to today's ¬ß490 dive) and ASR is the literal hook of the dive. **Resurrect FIRST** next kids cycle ‚Äî strongest banked seed, SVG/DOM is the starved renderer, love-aligned, and pure-touch so it runs perfectly on the phone reviewer-path with zero permissions. Code was clean + demoable this fire (folder held at `/tmp/dream-losers/774-kids-marble-symphony`).

### `775-kids-stamp-band` ‚Äî stamp bright characters onto a grid a playhead sweeps = build a looping band (status: queued)
**The brief that was built and banked.** A bright grid (6 rows √ó 12 steps): TAP a cell to drop a big cute character stamp (one instrument per row), tap it again to remove it; a glowing vertical **playhead sweeps left‚Üíright on a loop** (~110 BPM) and every stamped cell it crosses bounces + plays its sound ‚Äî the child BUILDS a repeating song and hears it thicken as they add/remove stamps. `sequencer.ts` = a persistent mutable Grid model (genuine **compositional memory/state** ‚Äî the thin kids-stateful category JURY-valued, √Ý la 738-song-sprout) with a kick + clap **percussion backbone** + 4 pentatonic mallet rows + a preloaded default groove (never blank/silent); `voices.ts` = live-synthesized kick (pitch-swept sine) / clap (filtered noise burst) / mallets (triangle + octave). **Output: Canvas2D** (sweeping playhead + many stamps); a look-ahead Web-Audio scheduler. **Always self-plays** (default pattern loops with zero interaction; "Surprise!" randomizes, "Clear" empties). Ref: **Toshio Iwai** ‚Äî *SimTunes* (1996) / *Electroplankton* (2005): a line sweeps creatures placed on a grid into music; nods to *Incredibox* (Yann Seznec) for the layered build-a-band joy. **Why promising:** real groove with a beat + genuine looping state (not a one-shot tap that resets), constructive "I built a band" hook, bright/joyful; sequencer-loop territory is **love-aligned** (`172-loop-station`‚ù§Ô∏è, `111-kids-shape-loop`‚ù§Ô∏è). **Why banked:** honest 2/5 (#2 four subsystems [grid-sequencer state machine + polyphonic synth + playhead clock + Canvas2D render] + #3 [Iwai SimTunes/Electroplankton]); the lab is warmer on step-sequencers than on speech-as-rhythm, and the winner carried the research chain. **Resurrect when** a bright constructive build-a-band toy is wanted, or to seed a longer-form stateful kids piece (graft on evolving/maturing voices like 738). Code was clean + demoable this fire (folder held at `/tmp/dream-losers/775-kids-stamp-band`).

## ¬ß491 banked seeds (2026-06-20, from the cycle-491 ADULT WIDE fire ‚Äî "his music a new non-grain way / off the GPU-shader field," off every JURY-2026-06-19 ban)

Cycle 491 ran an ADULT **WIDE** fan-out (JURY 2026-06-19's loudest message: every ban hardened into a new monoculture ‚Äî a WIDE-shaped anti-monoculture ask). Three divergent explorers, each off every 2026-06-19 ban (no grain-resynthesis ¬∑ no GPU-shader field ¬∑ no mic-hum ¬∑ no dark-glow), diverging on INPUT √ó OUTPUT √ó TECHNIQUE √ó NAMED-REF. Winner shipped = **`777-song-architecture`** (Canvas2D chroma-Self-Similarity-Matrix heatmap of his *real* Welcome Home recording + playhead + click-to-hear-the-repeat ‚Äî the research‚Üíbuild chain from RESEARCH ¬ß491, the lab's first SSM-of-his-recording; the directest hit on JURY #1 *use his music a new non-grain way* + JURY #2 *off the shader onto scarce Canvas2D*). Both non-winners are demoable + self-reported clean (no `any`, no `use*` helpers, no API route, SSR-safe; tsc couldn't run in-container for lack of `node_modules`, hand-reviewed); de-selected on the his-music + research-chain axis, NOT on quality. Rebuild from the briefs below.

### ‚úÖ `778-markov-mirror` ‚Äî **SHIPPED cycle 493** (adult WIDE winner). Built demoable: live variable-order Markov chain + self-walking SVG transition graph; Cope-EMI / Illiac-Suite refs; implements the Feb-2026 "visible live-music-agent" design-space axis. See INDEX. **Open next-cycle deepening:** let it learn from his *recording's* onset+pitch note-stream so it improvises in **Karel's** style ‚Äî bind the visible model back to his music. (Original brief retained below.)

### ‚≠ê `778-markov-mirror` ‚Äî teach a small keyboard your melodic style, watch it improvise back in it on a glowing transition graph (status: SHIPPED ¬ß493 ‚Äî brief retained for the deepening step)
**The brief that was built and banked.** A small on-screen C-major keyboard (tap + computer-keys `a`‚Äì`l`); as you play, the app builds a **live variable-order Markov chain** (order-1 pairs + order-2 triples, counts accumulate, phrase-chaining resets on pause) of YOUR note transitions, then "Let it improvise" **walks the chain forever** in your style (prefers order-2 evidence, weighted by your counts; humanized timing; soft bass root every 4 steps), voiced as a warm triangle+sine ADSR piano through a lowpass+convolution-reverb bus. **Output: SVG/DOM** ‚Äî a bright networked transition GRAPH (nodes = notes on a ring, directed edges weighted by use, thicker/brighter = more used); during improvisation the **active node + traversed edge glow live** so you watch the melody walk its own learned web. Teach / Improvise (space) / Forget. Bright violet-emerald-on-deep-blue constellation, NOT a dark-glow field. Ref: **David Cope *Experiments in Musical Intelligence* (EMI/Emmy)** ‚Äî recombinant style-modeling composition; nods to **Hiller & Isaacson *Illiac Suite* (1957)** (first Markov-generated composition). **Why promising:** the teach‚Üíimprovise‚Üíwatch-it-walk loop is a genuinely fresh, legible interaction (the model is *visible* and *reshapes* as you play ‚Äî stateful memory you can see); off his music so it spreads the fire; SVG/DOM is the scarce renderer JURY #2 wants. **Why banked not shipped:** honest 2/5 (#2 five subsystems [tap input + live-learning variable-order Markov + walk scheduler + polyphonic synth + SVG graph] + #3 [Cope EMI / Hiller]); the winner `777` carried the his-music directive + today's ¬ß491 research chain + the audible click-to-hear-the-repeat payoff. **Resurrect FIRST** next adult cycle when SVG/DOM is cold and a learns-your-style instrument is wanted; clean next step ‚Äî let it learn from his *recording's* note-stream (onset+pitch) so it improvises in *Karel's* style, binding it back to his music.

### `779-ink-score` ‚Äî a machine slowly draws an abstract graphic score and plays the very marks it inks, evolving for minutes with motif memory (status: queued ‚Äî the long-form-state reach)
**The brief that was built and banked.** Autonomous (no input required) + two sliders (Density, Register). A **stateful generative-notation grammar** invents abstract marks (dots, weighted lines, arcs, clusters, hooked glyphs) and lays them on a slowly **left-scrolling staff-less field of warm cream paper**; a bright red **playhead** sweeps and **each mark sounds as it's crossed** (y‚Üípitch snapped pentatonic, weight‚Üídynamics/duration, shape-family‚Üítimbre: dot=pluck, line=bowed sustain, arc=glissando, cluster=chord, hook=gestural flick). **Long-form memory (the reach toward #1):** a bounded **motif bank** ‚Äî each phrase the machine re-states a remembered motif (transposed / inverted / retrograde / time- & interval-stretched / fragmented, `uses` counter drifts it further over time), coins a fresh one, or emits free material; `register`/`density`/`tension` random-walk toward the sliders but never snap ‚Äî so **minute 5 is recognizably related to but different from minute 1, never an exact loop.** Look-ahead Web-Audio scheduler (~1.4s ahead), deterministic mulberry32 PRNG (Seed button re-rolls the bank live). **Output: SVG/DOM**, bright ink-on-cream Ikeda-meets-handwriting, NOT a dark void. Ref: **Cornelius Cardew *Treatise* (1963‚Äì67)** (193-page wordless graphic score) + **Earle Brown *December 1952* / *Available Forms*** (open-form graphic notation) ‚Äî a *living Treatise* the machine writes and reads at once. **Why promising:** real long-form state/memory (the thin "evolving, not a loop" category JURY values); a distinct adult register (avant-garde notation) that isn't the glow-meditation; SVG/DOM scarce renderer. **Why banked:** honest ~2.5/5 (#1 long-form stateful + #2 four subsystems + #3 Cardew/Brown); graphic-score territory is partly anticipated by `319-hub-score` (Canvas2D "time-river" living score), and the winner `777` carried his-music + the research chain. **Resurrect when** a long-form autonomous adult piece is wanted, or graft its motif-memory engine onto a his-music source (let his recording's phrases BE the motif bank that the machine re-states transformed ‚Äî binding the strongest idea here back to Karel's music).

## ¬ß492 banked seeds (2026-06-20, from the cycle-492 kids DEEP fire ‚Äî "your drawing IS a song: Xenakis's UPIC for a 4-year-old," off every JURY-2026-06-19 ban)

Cycle 492 ran a kids **DEEP** fan-out (deliberate alternation off cycles 490/491 which both ran WIDE; JURY #3 "protect the depth habit"). ONE concept ‚Äî *a moving reader sweeps across a child's freehand-drawn curves, reading **x/angle = time** and **y/radius = pitch** as a continuous, evolving graphic score* (Xenakis's **UPIC**, 1977 CEMAMu: "with UPIC, music becomes a game for children: they draw, they hear"; modern UPISketch / SonicSketch) ‚Äî attacked via three reader/interaction models. Winner shipped = **`781-kids-paint-conductor`** (linear playhead sweeps a free paint canvas; the purest, most legible UPIC + the cleanest build + genuinely long-form-stateful). All three are Canvas2D (off the JURY-banned GPU-shader field), pure touch (no mic-hum), bright daylight (not dark-glow), drawn-curve-as-score (not grain-resynthesis) ‚Äî clean on all four 2026-06-19 bans, and hit JURY #4 (bright/joyful/active middle) + #3 (long-form stateful, the thin kids-depth category). The lab's existing paint pieces (loved 100/104/158/160/152) are per-stroke-loops or splat-triggers; a single global time-axis playhead reading ALL strokes' y as pitch is lab-first. Non-winners are demoable + self-reported clean tsc/eslint (no `any`, no `use*` helpers, no API route, SSR-safe). Rebuild from the briefs below.

### ‚≠ê `782-kids-sun-clock-song` ‚Äî draw on a round "sun clock"; a sweeping sun-hand reads your doodle around and around into a hypnotic looping round (status: queued, HIGH ‚Äî the most surprising form)
**The brief that was built and banked.** A bright peach‚Üíamber dial with a smiling sun face at center; a glowing **sun-hand sweeps the dial continuously** (~9s/revolution) and NEVER stops (seeded with a gentle pre-drawn arc so a silent glance is alive). The child finger-paints on the dial: **angle = time**, **radius = pitch** (outer=high, inner=low, pentatonic-quantized so nothing is wrong), **color = timbre** (5 friendly voices: bell, flute, filtered saw, FM chime, Karplus-Strong pluck). Because the surface is a clock, the score is **inherently cyclic** ‚Äî any drawn arc becomes a repeating phrase, concentric strokes layer into a round. Kids-safe chain (`gain ‚â§0.28 ‚Üí lowpass 7k ‚Üí comp ‚àí10/20:1`), always-on pad, ‚â•64px swatches, design-notes toggle, sparkle clear. Ref: Xenakis UPIC wrapped onto a circle. **Why promising:** the radial/round form is the most *surprising* of the three (a drawn loop that's literally a clock) and the cyclic structure is naturally satisfying for a 4yo (their doodle = their song, forever). **Why banked not shipped:** the winner `781` was the purer/most-legible UPIC, the cleaner build (`781` 0 lint issues vs `782`'s deps warnings, since fixed by the builder), and the canonical demonstration of the reference. **Resurrect FIRST** next kids cycle ‚Äî radial/round diversifies off `781`'s linear canvas; consider continuous-spline capture (currently discrete polar samples leave a dotted line on fast gestures). Code was clean + demoable this fire.

### `783-kids-melody-rider` ‚Äî draw a wiggly line across a sunny sky; a cute character RIDES your line and sings its shape as the tune (status: queued)
**The brief that was built and banked.** A full sunny-meadow scene (sun, clouds, hills, flowers ‚Äî the most exuberant/game-like of the three). The child finger-draws a freehand line; a friendly character (star/bird/cloud/bug/frog, bold color) immediately **rides along that exact line left‚Üíright and loops forever**, bobbing with its shape. Mapping: **horizontal = time**, **line height under the rider = pitch** (pentatonic-quantized) ‚Äî so the SHAPE drawn literally IS the melody the rider sings. Multiple stacked lines = multiple riders + voices = layered tune (long-form stateful). 5 color/voice swatches, ‚â•64px, sparkle clear, seeded line+rider on load. Ref: Xenakis UPIC read by a traveling avatar. **Explicit differentiation from `761-kids-hill-roller`:** the rider travels at steady horizontal speed and height=PITCH (a graphic score), NOT a gravity/physics roll. **Why promising:** the most joyful/active register (JURY #4) ‚Äî a cute character performing the child's drawing is the most legible, delightful cause-effect for a 4yo. **Why banked:** the riding-character-on-a-line is the closest to existing kids tropes (761 + many creature pieces), and it carried a minor lint warning; `781`'s purer UPIC + cleaner build won. **Resurrect when** a bright character-driven build is wanted; clean next step = let each rider leave a little sparkle trail at its current pitch so the melody contour is doubly legible. Code was demoable this fire (one lint warning to fix on resurrect).
