## ¬ß493 ‚Äî banked from the adult WIDE fire (cycle 493) ‚Äî two non-winners, built to demoable then removed (folders not committed; rebuild from these briefs)

Cycle 493 ran an ADULT **WIDE** fan-out answering JURY 2026-06-20 ("Canvas2D is 10/15, spread don't flip; rest his recording (6√ó); body/camera collapsed 4√ó‚Üí0√ó; SVG/DOM is starved"). Three orthogonal explorers, none using a banned tag (no Canvas2D-primary, no his-recording). Winner shipped = **`778-markov-mirror`** (SVG/DOM ‚Äî the single starved renderer the jury named, purest research‚Üíbuild chain, lowest 06:30 glance-risk). The two non-winners below were complete, self-reported clean (TS strict + ESLint zero errors), and de-selected on the *"don't flee to one new wall"* axis ‚Äî both fled to a WebGL2 shader, exactly the trap JURY #1 cautioned against. Rebuild from these briefs under the fresh numbers shown.

### ‚≠ê `786-body-organ` ‚Äî your whole standing body becomes a chord: an off-glass, adult-meditative organ you play by moving (status: queued, HIGH ‚Äî the most-starved INPUT register; resurrect for a DEEP body cycle)
**The brief that was built and banked (was `784`).** Webcam ‚Üí **MediaPipe Tasks-Vision `PoseLandmarker`** (CDN, analysis-only, frames discarded ‚Äî nothing recorded/sent). Cross-modal map: **arm-span/openness ‚Üí chord width/voicing** (closed cluster ‚Üí wide open voicing, 2‚Äì6 tones of a D-Lydian field), **vertical reach ‚Üí register/brightness**, **lateral lean ‚Üí stereo pan + filter bend**, **overall motion energy ‚Üí density/shimmer/tremolo** (stillness rewarded with a held glowing chord, never silence). Voiced by 8 detuned triangle+sine organ voices ‚Üí pan ‚Üí gain ‚Üí lowpass ‚Üí compressor + reverb. **Output: full-screen WebGL2 GLSL field** (fbm-warped aurora ribbons + radial body-aura + energy sparkles; Canvas2D fallback). **Ghost-body fallback** = a slow Lissajous body auto-plays if no camera, so a 06:30 phone glance is alive + sounding. **Built clean** (page 409 / harmony 211 / pose 144 / field-shader 282 lines, TS+ESLint zero errors). **Named refs:** *Rhythm in the Air: Vision-based Real-Time Music Generation through Gestures* (arXiv 2511.00793, Nov 2025) ¬∑ MediaPipe Pose ¬∑ *A Design Space for Live Music Agents* (arXiv 2602.05064). **Ambition 3‚Äì4/5** (#2 ‚â•3 subsystems [pose tracking + cross-modal mapping + generative organ + WebGL2 field] ¬∑ #3 refs ¬∑ #5 RESEARCH ¬ß493). **Why banked not shipped:** the directest hit on JURY #4 (body 4√ó‚Üí0√ó) ‚Äî BUT MediaPipe was *just* used in 731 (kids hands) and 724 (pose), so shipping it 2 cycles later risks re-forming the very stack we just left; and it flees to a WebGL2 shader (one new wall) where 778's SVG/DOM is the genuinely scarce surface. **Resurrect FIRST for a DEEP body cycle** once the MediaPipe stack has cooled ~3+ cycles and a shader is wanted ‚Äî this is the strongest body-as-instrument adult concept in the bank. The full-body adult register (vs 731's kids hands) is the differentiation.

### `785-aurora-weather-organ` ‚Äî hear the Sun's weather right now: the live magnetosphere played as a slow organ, under the aurora that same weather would paint (status: queued ‚Äî the real-world-data-sonification lane, rests his recording)
**The brief that was built and banked (was `783`).** No mic/camera/recording ‚Äî the instrument is **live NOAA SWPC data** fetched client-side (planetary K-index + solar-wind plasma; 10s timeout; defensive header-column parsing; `simulateSpaceWeather()` sinusoidal-drift fallback live within 2s if CORS/fetch fails, amber notice). **Sonification:** 7 oscillators via `setPeriodicWave` (pipe-organ partials), base C3-Lydian; **rising Kp activates tension tones (Bb3/Db3), raises tremolo 0.15‚Üí2.8Hz and lowpass 700‚Üí2800Hz**; solar-wind speed ‚Üí swell tempo, density ‚Üí partial richness; all `linearRampToValueAtTime` over 3.5s, min-gain guaranteed (never silent/harsh). **Output: full-screen WebGL2 GLSL aurora** (6-octave fbm curtains, vertical rays, 1‚Äì4 Kp-driven layers, green‚Üíteal‚Üíviolet‚Üícrimson ramp, hash starfield, filmic tonemap; Canvas2D fallback). HUD shows live Kp/speed/density + a **manual calm‚Üîstorm override slider** so a reviewer hears the full range without waiting for a real storm. **Built clean** (5 files, TS+ESLint zero errors, full teardown via `WEBGL_lose_context`). **Named refs:** NOAA SWPC ¬∑ the lab's data-sonification family (588-tidal-organ, 744-magnetic-walk, 760) ¬∑ Inigo Quilez fbm. **Ambition ~3/5** (#2 ‚â•3 subsystems [live API + sonification mapping + WebGL2 aurora + organ synth] ¬∑ #3 refs ¬∑ #5 RESEARCH ¬ß493). **Why banked:** hits JURY #3 (rest his recording, data works) cleanly, but data-sonification is an established lab lane (less of a fresh *register* than 778's symbolic-model) and it flees to a WebGL2 shader. **Resurrect when** a cosmic data-sonification piece is wanted and the shader surface is cold; verify live NOAA CORS works in-browser (the manual slider + simulated fallback guarantee a glance regardless).

## ¬ß500 banked seeds (2026-06-21, from the cycle-500 kids WIDE fire ‚Äî "calm motion‚Üímusic for a 4-year-old," off every JURY-2026-06-20 ban)

Cycle 500 ran a kids **WIDE** fan-out (deliberate alternation off the 498/499 DEEP run; JURY 2026-06-20 was a *spread* signal ‚Äî "spread, don't flip onto one new wall"). Three orthogonal explorers, each calm (the jury's reintroduced "middle"), each dodging all four current bans (touch-primary / Canvas2D-primary / his-recording-as-conductor-analyzer / uniformly-bright-active-kids), spread across renderers (three.js / SVG-DOM / WebGL2). Winner shipped = **`805-kids-snow-piano`** (tilt ‚Üí Karel's real piano snow globe ‚Äî the milestone build using his recording, JURY #3, triple love-aligned, research-chain direct). Both non-winners were built to demoable and self-reported clean (`tsc --noEmit` zero errors in-folder; no `any`/`@ts-ignore`/`use*`-helpers). Folders were copied to `/tmp/dream-losers-500/` then removed before the authoritative build (not committed). Rebuild from the briefs below.

### ‚≠ê `806-kids-sky-lullaby` ‚Äî the REAL sky right now (sun, clouds, moon phase) sings a 4-year-old a slow dusk lullaby they stir with a tilt (status: queued, HIGH ‚Äî **RESURRECT FIRST**, the renderer-diverse pick)
**The brief that was built and banked.** The child's **live local sky** from **Open-Meteo** (free/no-key/CORS; `current=temperature_2m,cloud_cover,is_day,weather_code` + `daily=sunrise,sunset`; coords from `navigator.geolocation`, London silent default) is the **composer**: `is_day`+time ‚Üí key & register (day = warm major celesta; dusk/night = soft suspended pads, octave-down), `cloud_cover` ‚Üí note density, `temperature` ‚Üí timbral warmth (sine‚Üítriangle), **computed moon phase** ‚Üí a slow shimmering high voice (bright near full, near-silent at new). A **Chris-Wilson look-ahead scheduler** plays a sparse pentatonic ostinato that **mutates/re-seeds every ~45‚Äì90s** (Eno, never an exact loop); the child **tilts/drags** a glowing star-cursor across the sky and where it rests adds a key-snapped twinkle. **Output: inline SVG/DOM** (deep-blue‚Üíviolet‚Üígold gradients, blurred moon/cloud/star shapes; rAF repaint) ‚Äî the renderer the lab is genuinely scarce on (three.js shipped the last TWO cycles, 799+803, *and* the 805 winner is three.js #3 ‚Äî **this is the spread-correct renderer next cycle**). Kid-safe `gain ‚â§0.3 ‚Üí lowpass ‚â§7000 ‚Üí comp(‚àí10/20:1)`, soft attacks / 2‚Äì4s releases, always-on drone bed, ‚â•64px Start, iOS AudioContext + orientation perm both in the first tap, full teardown (scheduler/rAF/nodes/`audioCtx.close()`/abort fetch). Degrades: geo denied ‚Üí default city; fetch/CORS fail ‚Üí baked "example sky" + `text-rose-300` notice while audio plays on hands-free within ~2s. Refs: **Brian Eno** (*Music for Airports* / *Reflection*) ¬∑ **Andrea Polli** + **Natalie Miebach** (weather/data sonification) ¬∑ **Open-Meteo**. **Explicit differentiation from `575-kids-sky-song`:** 575 is a Canvas2D/WebGL *bright* weather toy you touch; 806 is its **quiet SVG/DOM twin** ‚Äî dusk-calm, moon-aware, tilt-stirred, generative-not-performed. Ambition ~2/5 (#2 ‚â•3 subsystems [live API + generative scheduler + SVG render + synth] + #3 refs + soft-#5 RESEARCH ¬ß500). **Why banked not shipped:** 805 was the milestone build on his real recording (JURY #3 + Karel's #1 directive + triple love-aligned). **Resurrect FIRST next kids cycle** ‚Äî it is the renderer-spread answer to three straight three.js ships; clean next step = verify Open-Meteo CORS in a real browser (the baked fallback guarantees a glance regardless).

### `807-kids-breath-bubbles` ‚Äî blow softly into the tablet and grow a slow drifting cloud of glowing bubbles that chime as they rise and pop (status: queued)
**The brief that was built and banked.** The mic reads **breath ENERGY only** (RMS off an `AnalyserNode`, analysis-only ‚Äî never pitch/speech/recognition, never wired to output, frames discarded, nothing recorded/sent). A soft blow spawns glowing bubbles; **harder blow = bigger, lower-toned** bubble; they drift up and sway, and on reaching the top (or a big gust) **pop with a soft C-major-pentatonic sine-bell chime** (big = warm/low, small = bright) ‚Äî accreting into a calm rising cloud over minutes, long-form, never a hard loop. **Output: raw WebGL2 (GLSL ES 3.00)** additive `gl.POINTS` glow-sprites (iq soft exponential falloff + faint rim, `SRC_ALPHA, ONE`) over a dim warm-dusk gradient ‚Äî the iPad-reliable scarce renderer (last WebGL2 ship was `788`, cycle 494). Synth only (rests his recording). Kid-safe `gain 0.28 ‚Üí lowpass 7000 ‚Üí comp(‚àí10/20:1)`, always-on C‚ÄìG‚ÄìC pad, soft attacks/long decays, ‚â•64px "Start blowing", iOS AudioContext+getUserMedia (EC/NS/AGC off) both in the first tap, full teardown (rAF + mic tracks + nodes + `audioCtx.close()` + delete GL program/buffer/VAO + lose-context). Degrades: mic denied ‚Üí a **ghost-breath auto-demo** spawns+pops hands-free within ~2s + `text-rose-300` notice; no WebGL2 ‚Üí Canvas2D bubble field that still drifts+chimes. Refs: **Toshio Iwai** (Electroplankton low-floor play) ¬∑ breath/wind-controller lineage (EWI) ¬∑ **Inigo Quilez** (additive glow). Ambition ~2/5 (#2 ‚â•3 subsystems [breath-RMS analysis + particle spawn/growth physics + WebGL2 render + pop-chime synth] + #3 refs). **Why banked:** honest self-framing ‚Äî blow-to-bubbles is a familiar toy form; and mic was just used in the prior kids cycle (799 voice), so a 3rd-in-the-window mic-input is the weaker diversity pick. **Resurrect when** a breath/vocalization piece is wanted on a cold mic window; clean next step = let bubble *clusters* harmonize (chord on a multi-pop) so the cloud sings, not just chimes.

## ¬ß503 banked seed (2026-06-21, from the cycle-503 adult DEEP fire ‚Äî "give the answering-room agent a long-form memory," JURY 2026-06-20 #2 return-and-extend of 770)

Cycle 503 ran an adult **DEEP** fire (two approaches to ONE concept: the live-music agent that REMEMBERS, so minute 5 ‚âÝ minute 1 ‚Äî the directest hit on JURY 2026-06-20 #2, "the depth ceiling collapsed 4‚Üí1, RETURN AND EXTEND 770 to a real 4/5"). Both built to demoable, self-reported clean. Winner shipped = **`814-remembering-room`** (SYMBOLIC motif memory ‚Äî an Adaptive Phrase Bank that quotes/develops its own and his material via transpose/augment/fragment/invert, with a legible "memory shelf"; the more novel ceiling build and the directest reading of "accrete a *motif*"). The non-winner below was de-selected because textural-residue accretion sits close to last cycle's `808-sympathetic-strings` sustain-pedal accretion and the lab's drone-bed family; 814's symbolic memory is the fresher register. Folder removed before the authoritative build (not committed). Rebuild from the brief below.

### `815-accreting-room` ‚Äî the answering agent with a TEXTURAL memory: every phrase it plays leaves a sustained ghost-layer residue, so the room thickens over minutes into an Eno tapestry (status: queued)
**The brief that was built and banked.** Soloist = Karel's real "Welcome Home" recording played whole (read-only GET `/api/audio/549fc519-‚Ä¶`, 4s abort, JSON/`{url}`/direct handling; `OfflineAudioContext` ~16s warm fallback + emerald badge, never silent, runs offline). Listener = FFT‚Üí12-bin chroma ‚Üí 24-template triad fit + tension, energy/flux, phrase-gap state machine (+`chordPitchClasses()` so the bed stays consonant). Agent = pad + gap-answer (as 770) PLUS the **accretion engine**: every agent note/phrase routes into a long-tail residue ‚Äî a feedback-delay bus (two self-feeding taps, feedback rising with a **memory** slider and the room's age, hard-capped 0.88) + a 7-voice ghost bank where each answer deposits its landing pitch as a slowly-decaying held tone. A global age/density rises over minutes (bed louder/thicker/brighter); a per-frame **consonance gate** fades out-of-chord ghosts fast and an **anti-mud duck** lowers the bed when crowded or when he's loud; master `DynamicsCompressorNode` limiter is the safety net. **Output: audio-forward DOM/CSS hearth + small `<canvas>` of accreting horizontal strata** (one faint layer per agent phrase, brightening with density) ‚Äî NOT fullscreen Canvas2D, NOT a shader. Sliders: **company** (shy‚Üîtalkative) + **memory** (room forgets quickly ‚Üî holds everything). **Named refs:** *A Design Space for Live Music Agents* (arXiv 2602.05064, the long-term-memory "Adaptation" gap) ¬∑ Christopher Raphael *Music Plus One* ¬∑ Brian Eno *Music for Airports* (accreting generative ambient). **Ambition ~2‚Äì3/5** (#1 long-form stateful accretion + #4 cycle-2 + #3 refs + #5 RESEARCH ¬ß503). **Honest limit (builder-reported):** at the extreme "holds everything" setting on a dense passage it can get hazy ‚Äî the honest edge of textural-only memory, recoverable by easing the memory slider. **Resurrect when** an ambient/installation register is wanted and a textural (non-symbolic) memory is the point; clean next step = make the residue *pitch-quantize* to the live chord (not just gate) so the bed is always a clean voicing, and add a slow spectral tilt so the oldest residue darkens as it ages (depth-of-time cue).

## ¬ß504 banked seeds (2026-06-21, from the cycle-504 kids WIDE fire ‚Äî "let a 4-year-old SHAPE harmony or rhythm," JURY 2026-06-21 kids verdict: ban pentatonic-crutch + UPIC)

Cycle 504 ran a kids **WIDE** fire ‚Äî three orthogonal explorers, each letting the child SHAPE harmony or rhythm (off the jury-banned pentatonic-never-wrong crutch and UPIC pitch-painter), on three spread renderers (SVG / WebGL2 / audio-forward; none Canvas2D, none three.js). All three built to demoable, self-reported clean. Winner shipped = **`816-kids-tone-tower`** (touch-stack ‚Üí just-intonation chord construction; harmony genuinely *shaped*, the directest answer to the jury and the rarer/harder register). The two below were de-selected for the reasons noted; folders saved to `/tmp/dream-losers-504/` and removed before the authoritative build (not committed). Rebuild from the briefs below.

### `817-kids-clap-back-band` ‚Äî the child claps a rhythm and a band plays the child's OWN groove back (status: queued)
**The brief that was built and banked.** Child claps into the mic; real-time **spectral-flux onset detection** (NOT speech recognition) captures the inter-onset timing of the claps as a ~1‚Äì2 bar pattern; an animal band echoes that exact rhythm back and, over repeats, layers a drum / shaker / bass thump onto the CHILD'S captured rhythm ‚Äî so the groove that grows is the one the child clapped, not a pre-approved beat. Light quantization (~50% strength) keeps the child's feel. **Renderer: raw WebGL2** (GLSL ES 3.00 additive particle bursts/character blooms; the cold/fresh surface) with SVG fallback. Kids-safe master chain, runtime-synthesized clap/drum/shaker/bass, soft drone bed. **Crucial fallback that's its strength:** the whole screen is an always-live **tap pad** feeding the identical `onInput ‚Üí capture ‚Üí quantize ‚Üí loop` path, so a 4-year-old with no mic gets the exact same toy (`text-rose-300` notice for the grown-up); a 3s hands-off **auto-demo** loops a built-in clapped pattern until real input cancels it. **Named refs:** **Orff Schulwerk** (echo-clapping / call-and-response, foundational children's rhythm pedagogy) ¬∑ **Konnakol** (clapped/spoken rhythm as material ‚Äî distinction: this captures real CLAPPED AUDIO ONSETS, not recognized speech syllables) ¬∑ **Steve Reich *Clapping Music***. **Ambition ~2‚Äì3/5** (#1 clapped-audio-onset rhythm capture [`773-parade-caller` used speech syllables, never real audio onset] + #2 ‚â•3 subsystems [mic onset detection + loop capture/quantize + multi-voice perc synth + WebGL2 render = 4] + #3 named refs). **Why de-selected (not a quality flaw):** rhythm-capture-from-the-child rhymes hard with the recent **`773-kids-parade-caller`** (cycle 490, speech-syllable rhythm) ‚Äî two kids rhythm-capture pieces ~14 cycles apart is the "too similar" trap the mandate guards against; the harmony-shaping winner (816) is the rarer register the jury explicitly asked for. **Resurrect when** the kids lane wants a bright/active mic (or tap) rhythm piece and 773 has cooled out of the window; clean next step = let the band's added layers play *complementary* counter-rhythms (off-beats, fills) derived from the child's pattern, not just doublings.

### `818-kids-pendulum-choir` ‚Äî pull back a row of pendulums and SHAPE a slowly-phasing polyrhythm (status: queued, FIX the pentatonic palette first)
**The brief that was built and banked.** A row of ~10 pendulums of graduated lengths; the child pulls one (or several) back with a finger and releases ‚Äî each swings with its own period (period ‚àù length) and CHIMES at the bottom of its swing. Because the periods differ, the chimes form a **pendulum-wave polyrhythm**: in sync at the start, fanning out into a shimmering scatter, re-converging ~30s later. The child SHAPES the rhythm by choosing WHICH pendulums to start and HOW FAR to pull each (amplitude ‚Üí loudness/brightness). A üåä "swing them all" button gives the classic wave reveal; üåô "calm them" resets; auto-starts on load. **Physics (builder-verified stable):** semi-implicit (symplectic) Euler on the small-angle ODE with a clamped fixed `1/240s` accumulator (frame-rate independent, no whip/explode after a backgrounded tab); pendulum k does `18+k` whole swings per 30s cycle ‚Üí guaranteed re-convergence every 30s; the visual swing AND the chime trigger come from the SAME simulated angle. **Renderer: audio-forward + minimal SVG** (spare glowing lines/bobs; the sound is the star). Calm/hypnotic bedtime register (the jury liked the kids calm register returning). **Named refs:** **Harvard Pendulum Wave** (15 graduated pendulums cycling through phase) ¬∑ **OSZILOT** (contemporary pendulum sound installation) ¬∑ **Gy√∂rgy Ligeti *Po√®me Symphonique*** (1962, 100 metronomes drifting in/out of phase). **Ambition ~2/5** (#1 pendulum-wave physics polyrhythm engine [grep-0√ó] + #2 ‚â•3 subsystems [physics sim + per-chime synth + SVG render] + #3 named refs). **Why de-selected (FIXABLE flaw):** the chimes use a **D-major-pentatonic** consonant set ‚Äî exactly the "pentatonic-never-wrong" crutch the JURY 2026-06-21 banned 6√ó. The polyrhythm (the actual point) is genuinely shaped by the child, so this is salvageable, but **before resurrecting, swap the pitch palette** so harmony isn't the pre-approved pentatonic ‚Äî e.g. tune each pendulum to a just-intonation overtone of one root (the longest = fundamental, shorter ones = 2nd/3rd/4th... partials) so the pitches come from the physics/length ratios themselves rather than a snapped scale. That makes the pitch *derive from the same lengths that set the rhythm* ‚Äî a tighter, non-pentatonic concept. **Resurrect when** a calm kids piece is wanted; it's the cleanest calm-register dodge in this fire once the palette is de-pentatonic'd.

## Cycle 505 ‚Äî adult DEEP fire (one concept √ó 3 feedback-synthesis approaches), shipped 1

Cycle 505 ran the lab's **first feedback-instrument** fire ‚Äî ONE massively-bigger concept ("an instrument born from a self-oscillating feedback loop you tame," grep-0√ó for any prior no-input/Larsen/feedback-as-sound piece) attacked via three Web Audio synthesis approaches, in parallel. All three reached complete implementations. Winner shipped = **`820-feedback-ecology`** (the coupled-dynamical-system "ecology" ‚Äî the conceptually biggest and the only one with genuine **long-form evolution** [minute 2 ‚âÝ minute 0], the depth-ceiling quality JURY 2026-06-21 prizes and the exact gap it flagged in pruning-candidate `805`). The two below are real, build-verified implementations ‚Äî resurrect from the briefs (and the code, where noted).

### `819-no-input-mixer` ‚≠ê RESURRECT-FIRST (adult) ‚Äî a faithful no-input mixer played by an XY pad (status: built complete, banked)
**Built to demoable (984-line page, self-reported tsc+ESLint clean; saved to `/tmp/dream-loser-819/` this fire).** Three parallel Web Audio feedback channels, each `feedbackGain ‚Üí bandpass(high-Q) ‚Üí delay ‚Üí waveShaper(tanh) ‚Üí back`, with a cross-coupling send bus so channels beat against each other (real no-input mixer send/return). **Hero control: an XY pad** ‚Äî X = bandpass center (80 Hz‚Äì6 kHz log), Y = loop gain / edge-of-chaos (low = hum, mid = singing Larsen, high = beating drones, top = controlled noise); CH1/2/3 select; ‚ö° Kick seeds oscillation; ‚Üì Calm tames a runaway; Q / delay(comb) / cross-coupling sliders. **Renderer: WebGL2 oscilloscope** (LINE_STRIP additive-blend glow) + Canvas2D spectrum/RMS-bloom overlay, Canvas2D fallback ‚Äî **the scarcest renderer in the window (WebGL2 1√ó)**, the cleanest diversity-renderer pick. Ear-safety: feedback hard-cap 0.97, brick-wall `DynamicsCompressor` (‚àí9 dB, 20:1), master default 0.28, no auto-start, Panic/Mute. Ambition ~3/5 (#1 lab-first no-input feedback instrument + #3 Nakamura / Body Synths Superbooth-2026 / David Tudor + #5 RESEARCH ¬ß505). **Why de-selected (not a quality flaw):** it is a *faithful emulation of an existing effect* ‚Äî superb and the more directly *playable* of the two, but `820` is the genuinely "massively bigger" concept (a dynamical SYSTEM that self-organizes and evolves) and answers the jury's depth-ceiling priority. **Resurrect when** an adult wants the most directly-playable feedback instrument on the cold WebGL2 surface; clean next step = the README's "spectral-freeze 4th channel" (convolver with long feedback IR = a no-input mixer with tape-loop memory).

### `821-larsen-field` ‚Äî live acoustic feedback (Larsen) sculpted by notch/peak carving (status: built complete in an isolated worktree, banked)
**Built to demoable (970-line page, builder self-reported tsc strict clean) ‚Äî but its builder wrote into an isolated git worktree, so it never entered this fire's tree; resurrect from the worktree/brief.** Mic ‚Üí feedback ring (`micSource ‚Üí inputGain ‚Üí loopGain ‚Üí series BiquadFilters ‚Üí delay(22ms) ‚Üí analyser ‚Üí loopGain`) plus a **virtual self-oscillating path** (two detuned saws ‚Üí bandpass ‚Üí loop) so it blooms and demonstrates fully and SAFELY **on headphones / muted speakers / no-mic** without acoustic coupling. **Hero interaction:** tap/drag the WebGL2 spectral heat-field to place **notch** (kill a ringing tone) and **peak** (encourage one) filters in series ‚Äî literally sculpting the feedback's pitch content live; an FFT **howl-guard** drops loop gain if sustained energy exceeds threshold; loop gain hard-capped 0.92, brick-wall limiter (‚àí10 dB, 20:1), master default 0.20, pre-start headphone/volume warning screen, graceful mic-denial ‚Üí virtual mode. Ambition ~3/5 (#1 first live acoustic-Larsen instrument + #3 Robert Henke / David Tudor / Alvin Lucier *I Am Sitting in a Room* / Nakamura / Body Synths Superbooth-2026 + #5 RESEARCH ¬ß505). **Why de-selected:** highest ear-risk of the three (real acoustic feedback) and the live-mic-on-speakers path can't be glance-verified on a single unattended phone; the virtual fallback makes it safe but `820`'s self-organizing evolution is the stronger concept. **Resurrect when** an adult live-performance mic piece is wanted (point a mic at the real piano/PA and conduct the feedback); clean next step = the README's second parallel ring (~37ms delay) cross-coupled to the first.

## ¬ß506 banked seeds (2026-06-21, from the cycle-506 kids WIDE fire ‚Äî "let a 4-year-old SHAPE rhythm or the *feeling* of harmony," JURY 2026-06-21 #4: kill pentatonic-never-wrong + #5: ship a banked sibling)

Cycle 506 ran a kids **WIDE** fire ‚Äî three orthogonal explorers, each letting the child SHAPE rhythm or harmony off the jury-banned pentatonic crutch, across three spread renderers (Canvas2D / SVG-DOM / WebGL2) and three inputs (touch / touch-drag / mic). Two of the three deliberately resurrected ¬ß504-banked siblings (JURY #5: clear the bank). All three built to demoable, self-reported clean. Winner shipped = **`822-kids-shape-drums`** (touch ‚Üí rotating-polygon polyrhythm; the child SHAPES rhythm, the grep-thin kids register, complementing 816's harmony and the most robust unattended ‚Äî no mic/network). The two below were de-selected for the reasons noted; folders saved to `/tmp/dream-losers-506/` and removed before the authoritative build (not committed). Rebuild from the briefs below.

### `823-kids-feelings-chords` ‚≠ê RESURRECT-FIRST (kids) ‚Äî drag one sun across a feelings-sky and SHAPE the chord's emotion (status: built complete, banked)
**Built to demoable (page + README, self-reported tsc/ESLint clean; saved to `/tmp/dream-losers-506/` this fire).** A big friendly **SVG sun** the child drags anywhere on a full-screen affect field; position **bilinearly blends four chord qualities** ‚Äî up-left bright **major** (happy), down-left **minor** (cozy/wistful), down-right **sus** (floaty/mysterious), up-right **add9/maj7** (dreamy). A fixed Web Audio pad keeps a single tonal center (root drone + steady fifth ALWAYS present, so it's always "in tune"); two **color voices** glide ‚Äî the third between maj3/min3/P4, plus a high added voice fading in toward the dreamy corner ‚Äî all via `setTargetAtTime`/ramps so the harmony morphs **click-free**. Sky gradient, sun face/expression, glow, and CSS motes all shift with the feeling; a bell sparkle trails the finger. iOS gesture-gate, no-audio notice, optional 12-min goodnight fade with tap-to-wake. **Renderer: SVG + DOM** (the renderer-diverse pick ‚Äî off Canvas2D and off the three.js 799/803/805 streak). **Named refs:** **Russell's circumplex model of affect** (valence√óarousal as a feelings-wheel) ¬∑ **Hevner's affective chord/mode associations** (major=happy, minor=cozy, sus=floating, add9=dreamy). **Ambition ~2/5** (#2 ‚â•3 subsystems [pad synth + chord-quality morph engine + 2D affect-field mapping + SVG render = 4] + #3 named refs). **Why de-selected (not a quality flaw):** it's harmony-shaping, and cycle 504 *just* shipped harmony-shaping (816, JI chord-stacking) ‚Äî shipping rhythm-shaping (822) this cycle completes the jury's "harmony OR rhythm" pair and hits the grep-thin kids register; 823's *continuous affect-morph* mechanism is genuinely different from 816's *discrete interval-stacking* though, so it's a strong distinct piece. **Resurrect FIRST when** the kids lane wants the renderer-diverse SVG/DOM surface and a harmony piece; clean next step = a second touch adds a harmonizing companion sun (parent+child, diatonic ‚Äî the dyadic-synchrony research, RESEARCH ¬ß506).

### `824-kids-clap-loop` ‚Äî clap a rhythm and a glowing band loops your groove back (status: built complete, banked; resurrects ¬ß504-banked 817)
**Built to demoable (800-line page + README, self-reported clean; saved to `/tmp/dream-losers-506/` this fire).** The ¬ß504-banked **`817-kids-clap-back-band`** brief, built. Mic ‚Üí `AnalyserNode`; per-frame **spectral-flux + energy onset detection** (140ms refractory) captures each clap's position in a ~3.2s bar; on release the claps **snap to a 16-step grid** (forgiving) and become a looping percussion layer played by a **Web Audio look-ahead scheduler** (25ms tick / 120ms ahead). Clap again to stack up to 4 layers, Incredibox-style; each layer is a tappable glowing creature (mute/remove). Synth drums (no samples): sine-drop kick, noise clap, woodblock, shaker; soft C-major pad so it's never silent. **Renderer: raw WebGL2** fragment shader (the scarcest renderer, 1√ó in the window). **Crucial fallback = its strength:** mic-denied ‚Üí **tap-to-clap** on the same capture‚Üíquantize‚Üíloop path (`text-rose-300` notice), so the toy works with no mic and never throws. **Named refs:** **Boss RC loop-station** (capture a bar, loop, overdub) ¬∑ **Incredibox** (stacked-layer groove of visible characters) ¬∑ **spectral-flux onset detection** ¬∑ (lineage: **Orff echo-clapping**, **Reich *Clapping Music***). **Ambition ~2/5** (#2 ‚â•3 subsystems [mic onset detection + capture/quantize + look-ahead loop scheduler + WebGL2 render = 4] + #3 named refs). **Why de-selected (not a quality flaw):** mic-onset reliability is the unattended-straight-to-prod risk the lab repeatedly flags (the EMFILE-free but ear/eye-unverifiable path), and rhythm-capture-from-the-child rhymes with `773-kids-parade-caller` (speech-syllable rhythm, cycle 490) ‚Äî the polygon-polyrhythm winner (822) authors rhythm via robust physics instead. **Resurrect when** the kids lane wants a bright mic/tap rhythm piece on the cold WebGL2 surface and 773 has cooled; clean next step = the band's added layers play *complementary counter-rhythms* (off-beats/fills) derived from the child's pattern, not just doublings.

## IDEAS ¬ß507 ‚Äî 2026-06-21 (cycle 507 ¬∑ ADULT ¬∑ WIDE) ‚Äî two banked explorers

Cycle 507 ran an adult **WIDE** fire ‚Äî three orthogonal explorers (inputs MIDI / pointer / mic; techniques waveguide-mesh / orbital-sequencer / spectral-paint), each deliberately **energetic/percussive** (the direct answer to the JURY 2026-06-21 adult-ambient ban) and dodging every this-cycle ban (his-recording, machine-listens, pentatonic, UPIC, adult-ambient). Winner shipped = **`827-waveguide-mesh`** (audio-rate 2-D FDTD membrane played by MIDI; live-performance fitness + starved MIDI input). The two below are built complete and de-selected only on the curate tiebreakers ‚Äî rebuild from these briefs.

### `825-gravity-sequencer` ‚Äî compose a polyrhythm by arranging GRAVITY, not a grid (status: built complete, banked)
**Built to demoable this fire (page.tsx + physics.ts + audio.ts + render.ts + README; `npx eslint` ‚Üí one harmless `react-hooks/exhaustive-deps` warning on a `useCallback`, otherwise clean; folder removed before the authoritative build, NOT committed).** A playable **cosmic step-sequencer where orbital mechanics is the clock.** Tap empty space to drop a heavy tuned **bell** (each new bell = the next pitch up a **D-rooted just-intonation set** 1/1¬∑9/8¬∑6/5¬∑4/3¬∑3/2¬∑5/3¬∑9/5¬∑2/1 ‚Äî deliberately NOT C-pentatonic, the 6/5 & 9/5 give a modal/minor color); press-drag-release to **fling a satellite** (dashed slingshot aim line shows predicted velocity). Each satellite is integrated under inverse-square gravity from every bell with a **velocity-Verlet (leapfrog) symplectic integrator** (energy-conserving so stable orbits *stay* stable and the rhythm doesn't decay), softening + 4√ó sub-stepping for close skims. **Perihelion = the trigger:** when a satellite's radial velocity `(r¬∑v)/|r|` flips negative‚Üípositive inside a bell's influence radius (the local distance minimum), that bell STRIKES ‚Äî a short percussive FM/mallet hit, velocity scaled by pass speed/closeness. Orbit period = the rhythmic interval, eccentricity = natural swing, multiple satellites on different orbits = drifting **polyrhythm**; three-body chaos and slingshots make grooves breathe; a satellite that falls all the way in is absorbed with a louder accent. Canvas2D motion-blur trails, glowing bells that flash on strike. Master `gain ‚Üí lowpass 12 kHz ‚Üí compressor`; audio gesture-gated; alive on load (3 bells + 3 satellites in stable-ish orbits before sound). **Named refs:** **Batuhan Bozkurt ‚Äî *Otomata*** (moving agents trigger notes on events) ¬∑ **Kepler / celestial mechanics** (perihelion/eccentricity/period as the musical clock). **Ambition ~2‚Äì3/5** (#2 ‚â•3 subsystems [symplectic integrator + perihelion trigger-detection + percussion synth bank + trail renderer = 4] ¬∑ #3 named refs ¬∑ arguable #1 *orbital-mechanics-as-discrete-rhythm-sequencer* vs the existing `432-three-body` which sonifies orbits as a continuous DRONE, not a sequencer). **Why de-selected (not a quality flaw):** 827 edged it on Karel priority #3 (live-performance/MIDI fitness ‚Äî a more directly stage-playable instrument) and the cleaner build; 825 is arguably *more novel* (less direct ancestor than 827's FDTD-pond family) and energetic. **Resurrect FIRST when** the adult lane wants a cosmic/geometric rhythm piece on the pointer+Canvas2D surface; fix the one `exhaustive-deps` warning (memoize `seedScene` or add it to deps) first; clean next step = orbit-period quantization to a global tempo grid (snap-to-groove toggle) and a Poincar√©-section HUD so the chaos‚Üîlock transition is legible.

### `826-spectral-loom` ‚Äî freeze your own voice as a spectrogram and paint the frozen sound (status: built complete, banked)
**Built to demoable this fire (page.tsx + audio.ts + render.ts + README; builder reports `eslint` + `tsc --noEmit` 0/0 on the folder; folder removed before the authoritative build, NOT committed).** A clinical / Ryoji-Ikeda **spectral instrument where the visitor's OWN LIVE MIC is the material** (NOT his piano, NOT any server audio ‚Äî dodges the his-recording ban). Make a sound (sing/speak/whistle/clap); a live STFT (`AnalyserNode`, fftSize 1024, dB‚Üí0..1, perceptual freq curve) scrolls a rolling spectrogram. **‚ùÑ Freeze** copies the rolling window into an editable magnitude matrix `buffer[TIME][FREQ]` ‚Äî a held "image." **Edit the picture to edit the sound:** pointer **smear** drags spectral energy sideways in time (freeze-stretch), **raise/lower** brushes magnitude with a falloff kernel, a **freeze-stretch slider** runs a per-frame IIR time-blur, and a **scrub head** sweeps the frozen frame with a **loop** toggle for rhythmic re-triggering (active, not a drone). Resynthesis = a capped **96-oscillator additive bank** reading the buffer column under the scrub head every frame with `setTargetAtTime` smoothing (no zipper). Master `gain ‚Üí lowpass ‚â§13.5 kHz ‚Üí compressor`. **Crucial mic-free path:** mic denied/absent ‚Üí visible `text-rose-300` notice + a **"Use demo sound"** button that routes an internally-generated evolving chord/whistle/noise through the identical capture‚Üífreeze‚Üíscrub pipeline, so the whole mechanic is fully demoable without a mic; canvas animates a demo spectrogram on load. **Named ref:** **Jean-Fran√ßois Charles ‚Äî "A Tutorial on Spectral Sound Processing Using Max/MSP and Jitter"** (Computer Music Journal 32:3, 2008) ‚Äî spectrogram-as-editable-image-buffer (GRM/IRCAM spectral-freeze lineage). **Ambition ~2‚Äì3/5** (#2 ‚â•4 subsystems [live STFT capture + frozen editable spectral buffer + additive resynth + spectrogram renderer] ¬∑ #3 named ref). **Why de-selected (not a quality flaw):** spectral processing has several lab ancestors (`34-spectral-morph`, `321-spectral-flight`) and mic was a warm input in the recent window; 827's MIDI input was the starved/diverse pick. But the *paint-the-frozen-spectrum-from-live-mic* framing is genuinely fresh and the surprise ("the picture IS the sound") is high. **Resurrect when** the adult lane wants a clinical-Ikeda mic piece; clean next step = a true IFFT/overlap-add resynth path (vs the additive bank) for transient fidelity, and a second frozen frame you cross-fade/morph between (the spectral-morph the title promises).

## ¬ß508 banked seeds (2026-06-21, from the cycle-508 kids WIDE fire ‚Äî "let a 4-year-old SHAPE the *feeling/color* of harmony," JURY 2026-06-21 #4: kill pentatonic-never-wrong + #5: ship a banked sibling)

Cycle 508 ran a kids **WIDE** fire ‚Äî three orthogonal explorers, each letting the child SHAPE the feeling/color/density of harmony off the jury-banned pentatonic crutch, across three spread renderers (SVG-DOM / WebGL2 / Canvas2D) and three shaping-mechanisms (feeling-position / color-mix / gather-orchestration). The winner deliberately resurrected the ¬ß506 ‚≠ê banked `823` (JURY #5: clear the bank). All three built to demoable, self-reported clean. Winner shipped = **`828-kids-feelings-sun`** (drag-a-sun affect-field chord-quality morph, SVG/DOM ‚Äî the directest "shape the FEELING of harmony," the most robust unattended, and the banked-sibling ship). The two below are built complete and de-selected only on curate tiebreakers; folders saved to `/tmp/dream-losers-508/` and removed before the authoritative build (not committed). Rebuild from the briefs below.

### `829-kids-color-blend` ‚≠ê RESURRECT-FIRST (kids) ‚Äî mix two glowing colored lights and the color you make IS the chord (status: built complete, banked)
**Built to demoable this fire (page.tsx + audio.ts + gl.ts + README; builder reports `eslint` 0/0 + `tsc --noEmit` clean on the folder; saved to `/tmp/dream-losers-508/`, removed before the authoritative build, NOT committed).** A **raw WebGL2** light-field (inline-GLSL additive glowing metaballs with domain-warp smear + bloom on near-black ‚Äî **the lab's COLDEST renderer, 0√ó in the last 10 shipped**, the renderer-diverse pick). Three bold draggable color-drops (warm gold-red ‚Üí major/add9 ¬∑ cool blue-violet ‚Üí minor ¬∑ teal-green ‚Üí sus/open) drift, collide and **additively mix**; the code samples the brightest blended region of the light field ‚Üí hue/warmth/greenness/saturation/brightness ‚Üí drives the live chord over ONE fixed in-tune center (C root + G fifth always sounding): warm‚Üíbright major/add9, cool‚Üíminor, green‚Üísus/open, mixing‚Üíthe in-between harmony, richness rising with overlap, all click-free via `setTargetAtTime`. So the child paints with light and hears the harmony they mixed ‚Äî color is literally the language (**love-aligned `82-kids-color-piano`‚ù§Ô∏è / `317-kids-color-bells`‚ù§Ô∏è**). ‚â•64px drag targets; iOS gesture-gated; **WebGL2-null ‚Üí CSS `mix-blend-mode:screen` glowing-blob fallback + `text-rose-300` notice** (never blank, never silent); full dispose (program/buffer/VAO + `WEBGL_lose_context` + `audioCtx.close()`). **Named refs:** **Isaac Newton's 7-color‚Üî7-note mapping** ¬∑ **Scriabin's *clavier √Ý lumi√®res*** ¬∑ additive/subtractive color theory for kids ¬∑ **Synesthesia "Color to Sound" 2026 AURA mode** (a captured color ‚Üí a multi-voice chord). **Ambition ~2/5** (#2 ‚â•3 subsystems [touch drag-physics + additive-light-field color sampling + color‚Üíchord-quality morph synth + WebGL2 render = 4] + #3 named refs). **Why de-selected (not a quality flaw):** color‚Üíquality is an approximation (audio reads the brightest *sampled* blend region from a handful of points, not a GPU pixel readback), so the mapping is musical/continuous but not pixel-exact under the finger; and 828's drag-a-feeling is a cleaner single-gesture 4yo cause‚Üíeffect + the JURY #5 banked-sibling ship. **Resurrect FIRST when** the kids lane wants the COLD WebGL2 surface and a color-as-language harmony piece; clean next step = a real GPU pixel-readback (`readPixels` a 1√ó1 under the finger) so the heard chord exactly equals the seen color, and a third primary so the child can reach every quality corner. **Builder note:** its folder *vanished once mid-build* (likely a transient working-tree reset by a parallel process) then was rebuilt and stable ‚Äî re-confirm folder presence before any future authoritative build.

### `830-kids-feelings-choir` ‚Äî gather singing creatures into a circle and the SET they make becomes the chord (status: built complete, banked)
**Built to demoable this fire (page.tsx + audio.ts + creatures.ts + README; builder reports `eslint` clean + `tsc --noEmit` no errors on the folder; saved to `/tmp/dream-losers-508/`, removed before the authoritative build, NOT committed).** A **Canvas2D** twilight meadow of 8 glowing radial-gradient creatures with friendly faces, gentle bobbing/drifting, a pulsing center gathering-circle, sparkle bursts on join. **Tap a creature** to call it into the circle (its voice fades IN, soft attack), **tap again** to send it home (fades OUT) ‚Äî all click-free via `setTargetAtTime`. Each creature carries ONE chord-tone + a bold **Plutchik-mapped color** on a fixed C center (root/fifth anchors + bright-major-third / cozy-minor-third / dreamy-9th-maj7 / open-sus families), so the SET currently gathered is voiced into a continuously-sounding chord: 2 creatures = an open dyad, 5 = a rich pad; pick the bright one = happy, the cozy one = wistful. The child shapes harmonic **density AND color**, never a "wrong note" (more friends never feels punished ‚Äî registers hand-picked non-colliding). Seeded with 2 creatures already singing so an untouched screen is alive + sounding. ‚â•64px tap targets; iOS gesture-gated; voice cap 8; always-on drone pad; no Web Audio ‚Üí `text-rose-300` notice + visuals alive; rAF + listener + `audioCtx.close()` teardown. **Named refs:** **Toca Band** (tap a character, it joins in key ‚Äî the combination is the music) ¬∑ orchestration/voicing (which notes present shapes openness + color) ¬∑ **Plutchik's emotion wheel** (color‚Üîemotion for the families). **Ambition ~2/5** (#2 ‚â•3 subsystems [touch gather/orchestration + voice fade-in/out engine + density/quality voicing + Canvas2D render = 4] + #3 named refs). **Why de-selected (not a quality flaw):** of the three it's the closest to existing lab patterns (Toca Band is the explicit ref; chord-by-assembly rhymes with `816`'s stacking), and Canvas2D is at ~3√ó while 828's banked-sibling ship (JURY #5) + continuous-emotion axis won. **Resurrect when** the kids lane wants a Toca-Band-style gather-a-chord toy; clean next step = a gentle bass-note that follows the lowest gathered creature so re-voicings have a moving root, and a "scatter/again" button that re-seeds the meadow colors.

## ¬ß509 ‚Äî 2026-06-22 (cycle 509 ¬∑ ADULT ¬∑ WIDE) ‚Äî two explorers built, but caught REDUNDANT at curate (NOT fresh seeds)

Cycle 509 ran an adult **WIDE** fire (three orthogonal energetic explorers; winner = **`833-spectral-loom`**, the resurrect, which clears the bank per JURY #5). The two non-winners below are **complete, demoable code** (saved to `/tmp/dream-losers-509/`, removed before the authoritative build, NOT committed) ‚Äî but a corpus grep at curate found **both re-tread registers the lab already owns.** They are logged here for honesty + a *salvageable* angle, NOT re-seeded as fresh ideas (re-seeding owned territory is exactly the "too similar" rut). **Process lesson (propose adding to AGENT.md Decide step): grep INDEX + READMEs for every brief's data-source / technique / arc BEFORE fanning out ‚Äî "fresh in the world" ‚âÝ "fresh in this 800-prototype lab."**

### `831-solar-wind-organ` ‚Äî REDUNDANT (‚âà `314-solar-wind` + `471-helios-orbit`); do NOT rebuild as-is (status: built, redundant, archived /tmp)
Live NOAA SWPC space-weather telemetry (plasma speed/density, mag Bz, planetary Kp, GOES X-ray flares) ‚Üí a **dynamic, event-punctuated** composition (NOT ambient): speed‚Üítempo, density‚Üívoice count, Bz‚Üímodal tension (Lydian‚ÜíLocrian), Kp‚Üístorm crescendo, flares‚Üíbell strikes; Canvas2D Sun‚ÜíEarth magnetosphere viz with flare-front-triggers-strike coupling; full synthetic-fallback so it's demoable with no network. Clean code, good "non-ambient" framing ‚Äî **but `314-solar-wind` already sonifies the same SWPC feeds (warm drone) and `471-helios-orbit` already does NOAA-space-weather ‚Üí an EDM build-and-drop arc ("storm = the drop").** So the data source AND the dynamic-arc angle are both taken. Anchored by genuinely-2026 refs (Helioradar AV ¬∑ Nexus Sonance AES Europe 2026). **The ONLY non-redundant salvage:** a *different* live data source the lab does NOT own ‚Äî e.g. live **aircraft/ADS-B** (OpenSky), **Wikimedia recentchange EventStream** (SSE, "Listen to Wikipedia" lineage), **transit/seismic-already-owned-skip**, **NOAA ocean buoys** ‚Äî sonified with this same defensive-fetch + fallback skeleton. Don't rebuild the *sun*; reuse the *engine* on an unmined feed.

### `832-drop-engine` ‚Äî REDUNDANT (‚âà `387-drop-engine` + `631-drop-engine`); do NOT rebuild as-is (status: built, redundant, archived /tmp)
A generative **EDM build-and-drop arrangement engine**: 25ms look-ahead scheduler, 16th-note sequencer @124 BPM, a section state machine (intro‚Üíbuild‚Üídrop‚Üígroove‚Üíbreakdown‚Üíbuild‚Üídrop‚Üíoutro), all-synth voices (kick/sub/supersaw lead/detuned-saw pads/hats/claps/noise riser), real **sidechain pump** (kick ducks a gain bus), minor 4-chord progression (Am‚ÄìF‚ÄìC‚ÄìG7), a "Drop it" trigger + rideable filter macro + autopilot, raw WebGL2 reactive tunnel locked to the pump. Clean code, directly serves Karel priority #4 (journey-arc alternatives) ‚Äî **but `387-drop-engine` already exists AND `631-drop-engine` was the lab's FIRST sidechain pump (an EDM build-and-drop + sidechain piece).** Even the slug collides. So the arc + the sidechain primitive are both taken. **The ONLY non-redundant salvage:** NOT another build-drop arc ‚Äî instead the *unbuilt* journey-engine alternatives from Karel priority #4 that the lab is genuinely thin on: **ritual** (slow accretive ceremonial arc), **jazz-responsive** (comps under a live player ‚Äî note `338-live-accompanist` is the banked candidate here), or **cinematic narrative** (scored-to-a-story arc). Use a journey arc the lab hasn't shipped, not a fourth drop.

## ¬ß513 banked seeds (2026-06-22, cycle 513 ¬∑ ADULT ¬∑ WIDE ‚Äî two built-complete explorers, de-selected on curate)

Cycle 513 ran an adult **WIDE** fire ‚Äî three orthogonal energetic explorers (INPUT external-API / pointer / mic), all dodging the JURY 2026-06-21 bans (his-recording, machine-listens, UPIC, adult-ambient-default). Winner shipped = **`842-air-veil`** (live air-quality data ‚Üí harmony that audibly *fouls* with pollution ‚Äî the genuinely-cold real-world-data category, grep-0√ó lab-first, JURY #3). The two below are **built complete + demoable** (each self-reported ESLint/tsc clean on its folder), de-selected only on curate tiebreakers (842 was the freshest, opening new ground vs. the lab's deep physics/spectral lineages). Folders removed before the authoritative build, NOT committed. Rebuild from the briefs below.

### `843-gravity-sequencer` ‚≠ê RESURRECT-FIRST (adult) ‚Äî compose a polyrhythm by arranging GRAVITY, not a grid (status: built complete, banked; = the ¬ß507 ‚≠ê banked `825` rebuilt clean)
**Built to demoable this fire (page.tsx + physics.ts + audio.ts + render.ts + README; builder reports ESLint + tsc clean on the folder, exhaustive-deps gotcha fixed ‚Äî all mutable physics/audio/canvas state in refs).** A playable **cosmic step-sequencer where orbital mechanics is the clock.** Tap empty space ‚Üí drop a heavy tuned **bell** (next pitch up a D-rooted just-intonation set `1/1¬∑9/8¬∑6/5¬∑4/3¬∑3/2¬∑5/3¬∑9/5¬∑2/1` ‚Äî the 6/5 & 9/5 give modal color, NOT C-pentatonic); press-drag-release ‚Üí **fling a satellite** (dashed slingshot aim preview). Each satellite is integrated under inverse-square gravity from every bell with a **velocity-Verlet (leapfrog) symplectic integrator** (energy-conserving so stable orbits stay stable and rhythms don't decay), softening + 4√ó sub-stepping. **Perihelion = the trigger:** when a satellite's radial velocity `(r¬∑v)/|r|` flips negative‚Üípositive inside a bell's influence radius, that bell STRIKES a short percussive FM/mallet hit (velocity scaled by pass speed/closeness). Orbit period = rhythmic interval, eccentricity = swing, multiple satellites = drifting **polyrhythm**; three-body chaos breathes the groove; a satellite that falls all the way in is absorbed with a louder accent. Canvas2D motion-blur trails, glowing bells flashing on strike, influence rings + ratio labels. Master `gain ‚Üí lowpass 12 kHz ‚Üí compressor`, 14-voice cap, gesture-gated, alive on load (3 bells + 3 satellites). **Named refs:** **Batuhan Bozkurt ‚Äî *Otomata*** (moving agents trigger notes on events) ¬∑ **Kepler / celestial mechanics** (perihelion/eccentricity/period as the musical clock); discrete strikes (a sequencer), NOT the continuous orbit-DRONE of `432-three-body`. **Ambition ~2‚Äì3/5** (#2 ‚â•3 subsystems [symplectic integrator + perihelion trigger-detection + percussion synth bank + trail renderer = 4] ¬∑ #3 named refs ¬∑ arguable #1 orbital-mechanics-as-discrete-rhythm-sequencer). **Why de-selected (not a quality flaw):** 842 opened genuinely-new ground (a never-touched data feed + the cold real-world-data category JURY #3 explicitly flagged) and the *fouling-harmony* inversion was higher-surprise; 843 re-treads the lab's deep cosmic/physics lineage. **Resurrect FIRST when** the adult lane wants a cosmic/geometric rhythm piece on the pointer+Canvas2D surface (clean, lint-fixed, ready to ship); clean next step = orbit-period quantization to a global tempo grid (snap-to-groove toggle) + a Poincar√©-section HUD so the chaos‚Üîlock transition is legible.

### `844-overtone-mirror` ‚Äî sing one note and climb its hidden tower of overtones (status: built complete, banked)
**Built to demoable this fire (page.tsx + pitch.ts + audio.ts + render.ts + README; builder reports ESLint + tsc clean on the folder).** A live-mic **harmonic-series instrument**: sing a sustained note; a hand-written **NSDF / McLeod Pitch Method** detector (energy-normalized, first-qualifying-peak to dodge octave errors, parabolic interpolation) locks your fundamental as the tower's base after ~0.3s of stable pitch (`LOCK_CENTS 28¬¢` / `LOCK_FRAMES 18`). A 16-node **just-intonation overtone tower** (1f..16f, labeled with ratios) rises; as you **slide your voice up** toward each integer multiple (within `CENTS_TOLERANCE 35¬¢` ‚Äî deliberately generous so the climb feels rewarding), that overtone node LIGHTS, a soft additive voice tuned to the exact overtone RINGS, and a bell blooms ‚Äî so you accumulate a harmonic-series chord by climbing it with your own pitch. Canvas2D crystal-lattice tower with depth shading, parallax sway, additive glow, and a climbing live-pitch comet. Soft base drone keeps it tonal; master `gain 0.25 ‚Üí lowpass ‚Üí compressor`, click-free `setTargetAtTime`. **Crucial mic-free path:** mic denied ‚Üí `text-rose-300` notice + **"Use a demo voice"** button running a slow sine glide UP through the overtones through the identical detect‚Üílight‚Üíring pipeline, so the whole mechanic is demoable with no mic; canvas animates on load. **Named refs:** **Stockhausen ‚Äî *Stimmung*** (overtone singing as a whole composition) ¬∑ **La Monte Young** / just-intonation overtone drones ¬∑ **McLeod & Wyvill ‚Äî "A Smarter Way to Find Pitch" (NSDF/MPM)**. **Ambition ~2/5** (#2 ‚â•3 subsystems [real-time NSDF pitch detection + overtone-tower additive synth + lock/climb game-state + lattice renderer = 4] + #3 named refs). **Why de-selected (not a quality flaw):** mic was a warm input this window and harmonic-series/JI is a well-trodden lab lineage, so it opened less new ground than 842's cold data feed; the meditative register also brushes the JURY adult-ambient-default caution. **Resurrect when** the adult lane wants a live-mic harmonic-series/overtone piece (the climb-the-tower game is genuinely fresh as an *interaction*); clean next step = tighten `CENTS_TOLERANCE` into a "strict intonation" mode toggle + let the locked base re-seat if the singer drops out, and a second tower you cross-fade between (modulate the overtone root).

### `845-kids-sing-meadow` ‚≠ê RESURRECT-FIRST (kids) ‚Äî one sung note ‚Üí exactly one flower on the literal golden-angle spiral (status: built complete, banked; the discrete/legible DEEP sibling of shipped 846)
**Built to demoable this fire (page.tsx + audio.ts + meadow.ts + README; builder reports tsc 0 / eslint 0 on the folder).** The *literal, most 4yo-legible* reading of the ¬ß514 sing-a-meadow concept: the child hums/sings, a hand-rolled autocorrelation (Chris Wilson ACF) pitch detector snaps each note to C-pentatonic (no wrong notes) and plants **ONE flower per note** at the next position on the **closed-form H. Vogel (1979) phyllotaxis spiral** (`Œ∏ = n¬∑137.508¬∞`, `r ‚àù ‚àön` ‚Äî flowers pack like a real sunflower head, never colliding). Pitch ‚Üí hue (warm rose/gold ‚Üí cool sky/violet) + petal count (5‚Äì8 by scale degree) + stem height; loudness ‚Üí flower size + glow + bloom speed; flowers accumulate over minutes (fuller meadow at minute 5). After ~1s silence the meadow **sings the melody back**: each flower's emissive center brightens in planted order while a soft bell voice replays the note ‚Äî a call-and-response that rewards even a single hum. three.js warm-daylight scene (sky-blue fog, grassy ground, soft sun disc) with `InstancedMesh` petals/centers/stems (smooth past 200+, capped 400). Kid-safe `gain 0.28 ‚Üí lowpass 7000 ‚Üí compressor(‚àí10/20:1)`; always-on C2+G2 pad; iOS gesture-gated; **mic-denied ‚Üí `text-rose-300` notice + ghost-hum auto-singer + 5 emoji tap-pads** (üåπüå∑üåªüåºüíê, fully demoable mic-free); full teardown. No API route ‚Üí no guard; zero new npm deps. **Named refs:** H. Vogel (1979) sunflower head ¬∑ Mort Garson *Mother Earth's Plantasia* (1976) ¬∑ Chris Wilson autocorrelation ¬∑ Pauline Oliveros *Deep Listening*. **Ambition honest 2/5** (#2 ‚â•3 subsystems [ACF pitch + phyllotaxis InstancedMesh bloom + melody call-response sequencer] + #3 named refs ‚Äî Vogel placement is the *static formula*, which the lab already owns, so NOT a #1). **Why de-selected (not a quality flaw):** the *closest call* ‚Äî it's the clearest one-note‚Üíone-flower cause-effect and the most immediately legible for a 4yo (a child sees their song accumulate as a real sunflower head fills). 846 won because it realizes today's *actual* research finding (emergent phyllotaxis, arXiv 2509.06498 ‚Äî #5) and is genuinely long-form (self-organizing + self-singing when quiet), where 845 plots the static Vogel formula. **Resurrect FIRST on the kids lane** when the want is maximal legibility / the cleanest pure-delight cause-effect ‚Äî it's the more "I made THAT flower" of the two and ships clean as-is. Clean next step: let a held note grow a visibly *bigger* blossom (stem keeps rising while sustained) and add a gentle butterfly that lands on the most-recently-planted flower as a "your turn" cue.

Rebuild from the briefs below.

### `860-marine-gamelan` ‚≠ê RESURRECT-FIRST (adult) ‚Äî the live ocean plays a bronze gamelan, and rough seas DETUNE the metal (status: built complete, banked)
**Built to demoable this fire (page.tsx + marine.ts + audio.ts + gl.ts + README; structure copied from the working `463-terra-gamelan` sibling ‚Äî lowest runtime risk of the three).** A keyless, CORS-enabled **Open-Meteo Marine API** fetch (no API route, no guard) pulls live `wave_height / wave_period / wave_direction / swell` for four contrasting presets the visitor switches between ‚Äî **Oahu North Shore ¬∑ Bay of Biscay ¬∑ Drake Passage (big seas) ¬∑ Maldives (calm)** ‚Äî re-polling every 5 min. A single normalized `seaToDrive()` signal feeds BOTH a **modal physical-modeling bronze engine** (each key = a noise exciter ‚Üí bank of high-Q bandpass resonators at inharmonic partial ratios, slendro/pelog tunings) AND a hand-written **WebGL2 caustic water-light shader** (domain-warped value-noise interference, ridged veins, foam, violet strike-bloom) ‚Äî so sound and image never disagree. **The consequence is the point and it's audible:** wave height drives a `roughness` value that simultaneously densifies the strike rate, drops the register, lowers resonator Q, and detunes each bronze partial up to **¬±35¬¢** ‚Äî heavy seas roughen into a beating clang, calm seas stay sparse and sweet; period‚Üítempo, direction‚Üípan, all through the same signal that drives the shader's churn/speed/drift. Full synthetic-fallback sea-state + `text-rose-300` notice if the fetch fails; full teardown (rAF, abort fetch, `audioCtx.close()`, delete GL program/VAO, `WEBGL_lose_context`). **Named refs:** gamelan slendro/pelog tuning ¬∑ **Ryoji Ikeda *datamatics*** (data-as-music clinical stance). **Ambition honest 2/5** (#2 ‚â•3 subsystems [live-data fetch + state machine ¬∑ modal physical-modeling synth ¬∑ WebGL2 caustic shader] + #3 named refs). **Why de-selected (not a quality flaw):** the *close* runner-up ‚Äî lowest runtime risk AND it hits the jury-PRAISED real-world-data-with-consequence register (842-air-veil was a standout). 859 won purely on answering the jury's loudest #1 ask (WebGPU 0√ó / GPU-only) with the scarcest surface + the cleaner today's-research chain. **Resurrect FIRST on the adult lane** ‚Äî it's built, clean, and on a jury-loved register; clean next step: a global tempo grid so the wave-period quantizes to a groove (snap toggle), and a second listening location cross-faded so you hear two seas at once.

### `861-flow-veil` (adult) ‚Äî conduct a luminous veil with the raw RIVER of your motion, not tracked joints (status: built complete, banked; **needs 1-line fix before resurrect**)
**Built to demoable this fire (page.tsx + gl.ts + audio.ts + README).** Deliberately **off-glass, embodied, NON-landmark** input: webcam **optical flow** (whole-frame motion energy via a brightness-constraint frame-diff `flow = ‚àíIt¬∑grad/(|grad|¬≤+Œµ)`, spatially smoothed + EMA'd ‚Äî NOT MediaPipe joints, NOT touch, NOT mic). Everything runs in hand-written WebGL2/GLSL ES 3.00: grayscale+mirror ‚Üí flow estimation ‚Üí flow smooth ‚Üí **advected dye ping-pong FBO** (semi-Lagrangian back-trace + dissipation + diffusion + motion-driven pigment injection) ‚Üí tone-map render, plus a downsample/`readPixels` path that aggregates motion into audio control signals. A Web Audio **granular engine** maps: energy‚Üígrain density/brightness, vertical centroid‚Üípitch register, horizontal balance‚Üístereo pan, bursts‚Üíaccent clusters, over a detuned-saw drone + convolution reverb. **Mic/camera-free demoability:** camera denied ‚Üí synthetic Lissajous-drifting Gaussian blobs feed the SAME flow field so the veil swirls and sound plays within ~1s + a `text-rose-300` "(no camera ‚Äî demo motion)" note. Full teardown (stop MediaStream tracks, rAF cancel, `audioCtx.close()`, delete GL programs/textures/FBOs, `WEBGL_lose_context`). **Named refs:** **`@epok.tech/glsl-optical-flow` (Eoghan Keeffe)** + **TouchDesigner Optical Flow TOP** ‚Äî reimplemented from scratch, no npm shader dep. **Ambition honest 2/5** (#2 ‚â•4 subsystems [GPU capture ¬∑ GPU optical-flow ¬∑ ping-pong advected dye ¬∑ motion‚Üígranular audio] + #3 named refs). **REQUIRED FIX before resurrect (the reason it didn't ship):** `gl.ts:388` passes an `OffscreenCanvas` straight to `texImage2D`, which the TS DOM lib rejects (`OffscreenCanvas` not assignable to the `texImage2D` source overloads) ‚Üí `npm run build` fails type-check. Fix: either `createImageBitmap(offscreen)` and upload the `ImageBitmap`, or draw the offscreen onto a regular `<canvas>` first, or upload the `<video>` element directly. Also note: the audio read uses an RGBA8 encode of the float flow (a precision compromise) and flow gain/dissipation constants are eye-tuned. **Why de-selected:** optical-flow is already in the lab (`221-optical-flow-music`, `295`), so it opened less new ground than the WebGPU surface, AND it carried the build-blocking type error. **Resurrect when** the adult lane wants an embodied off-glass camera piece (starved input) ‚Äî apply the texImage2D fix first, then it ships clean.

## ¬ß525 ‚Äî 2026-06-23 (adult ¬∑ DEEP, 1 concept √ó 2 GPU approaches, shipped 1)

Cycle 525 ran an adult **DEEP** fire (deliberate alternation off three WIDE nights 522/523/524; JURY #5 "develop what you have, don't open a new tab" ‚Üí develop the `820-feedback-ecology` thread to cycle-3). ONE concept ‚Äî **"Living Topology": coupled high-Q feedback resonators whose small-world coupling matrix is continuously rewired by a Lorenz attractor, so the network reorganizes and the drone never repeats** ‚Äî built via 2 parallel approaches on the two renderer-safe GPU surfaces (Canvas2D HARD-banned, raw-WebGL2 at the 4√ó audit-ban). **Winner shipped = `872-living-topology`** (three.js ‚Äî kept WebGPU off a 4th-night monoculture + most robust for a no-GPU review + most legible Lorenz ghost-curve). The seed below is **built complete + demoable** (own folder), `cp`'d to `/tmp/dream-losers-525/` and removed before the authoritative build (NOT committed). Rebuild from the brief.

### `873-living-topology-gpu` ‚≠ê RESURRECT-FIRST (adult) ‚Äî the SAME Living Topology, computed and rendered on WebGPU (status: built complete + verified clean [eslint 0 / tsc 0 after a 5-error fix pass], banked)
**Built to demoable this fire (page.tsx + lorenz.ts + topology.ts + audio.ts + gpu.ts + fallback.ts + README).** Identical dynamical concept to the shipped 872 ‚Äî 12 high-Q `BiquadFilter` bandpass feedback resonators on the A1 overtone series, per-edge coupling `GainNode`s retuned each frame, ring backbone always on + chaos-gated shortcuts, brick-wall limiter ‚Üí master 0.22, clamps (fb 0.88 / coupling 0.3) ‚Äî but the visual is **WebGPU**: a hand-written WGSL `@compute` pass swirls a **40k-particle** field around the graph and integrates the Lorenz driver **GPU-resident** (state in `GPUBuffer`s); instanced additive passes render ghost-cloud / particles / edges / nodes; `destroy()` frees buffers + device. Graceful **Canvas2D fallback** (rose-300 notice) draws the same node-graph-with-Lorenz-drift on the same audio when `navigator.gpu` is absent. Gesture-gated AudioContext, tap-to-perturb, always-visible master volume + panic mute, hands-free auto-demo (seed + self-resonance ramp on Start), full teardown. **Named refs:** Lorenz 1963 ¬∑ the 2026 *Chaos, Solitons & Fractals* Watts-Strogatz/Lorenz-sync paper (pii S0577907326001188) ¬∑ Tudor *Rainforest* / Nakamura no-input mixing. **Ambition honest 3/5** (#4 multi-cycle [820 thread] + #1 chaotic-attractor self-rewiring topology [lab-first] + #3 named refs). **Why de-selected (NOT a quality flaw):** (a) **renderer diversity** ‚Äî WebGPU led the last several adult nights (859/866/869), so shipping it would be a 4th-night monoculture; three.js (872) was 2√ó/10, the freshest GPU surface. (b) **robustness** ‚Äî its no-GPU path falls back to the jury **HARD-banned Canvas2D** aesthetic, where 872's WebGL renders anywhere for an unattended 06:30 glance. (c) **legibility** ‚Äî 872 draws the literal Lorenz ghost-curve + bead; 873's particle-cloud attractor reads less clearly as the driver. Genuinely strong (the GPU-resident integration + 40k-particle field are the richest visual of the pair) ‚Üí **resurrect FIRST on the adult lane the next time WebGPU is scarce.** Brief + code in `/tmp/dream-losers-525/873-living-topology-gpu/`. **Thread note:** the 820-feedback-ecology lineage is now 820 (Canvas2D static) ‚Üí 847 (WebGL2 FBO-trails, banked) ‚Üí 872 (three.js Lorenz-rewiring, SHIPPED) + 848 (3D-orbit, banked) + 873 (WebGPU, banked) ‚Äî consider consolidating into one "Feedback Ecology" piece with a render/mode toggle rather than shipping each variant separately.

### `920-kids-sing-a-sprout` ‚≠ê RESURRECT-FIRST (kids) ‚Äî hum and a glowing garden GROWS over minutes, then SINGS your melody back (status: built complete, banked)
**Built to demoable this fire (page.tsx + pitch.ts + audio.ts + garden.ts + README; builder reports `eslint` 0 + `tsc --noEmit` 0 on the folder).** The directest hit on the thin, **jury-valued long-form/STATEFUL depth** category (888's register) in the KIDS lane ‚Äî unlike instant per-tap loops, the garden **accumulates and evolves**: a child hums, a hand-written **Chris-Wilson autocorrelation (ACF)** pitch detector (RMS-gated, ~70‚Äì1000Hz, no library) snaps each stable note to C-major-pentatonic (no wrong notes), and plants **ONE luminous plant per note** at the next position on the **H. Vogel (1979) phyllotaxis spiral** (`Œ∏=n¬∑137.508¬∞`, `r‚àù‚àön` ‚Äî packs like a real sunflower head, never collides), rendered with two `THREE.InstancedMesh` buffers (stems + per-instance-colored blossoms, cap 400, smooth past 200+). Pitch ‚Üí blossom hue + height (color is the language); the garden is materially fuller + different at minute 5. **Long-form memory + call-and-response:** every plant remembers its note + planted order; after ~1s of silence the garden **sings back** ‚Äî each plant's center brightens in planted order while a soft bell replays its note, so even a single hum is rewarded. Always-on detuned C/G/C drone w/ filter LFO. three.js warm-daylight scene (sky-blue, grassy ground, soft sun, slow orbit). Kid-safe `gain 0.26 ‚Üí lowpass 7kHz ‚Üí compressor(-10/20:1)`; mic ‚Üí AnalyserNode only (no feedback); AudioContext + mic gesture-gated in the Start tap; seeded with 28 grown plants so an untouched screen is alive + sounding within ~1s. **Mic-free path:** mic denied ‚Üí `text-rose-600` notice + a **ghost-hum auto-singer** (slow sine glide through the safe scale) drives the IDENTICAL plant‚Üígrow‚Üísing-back pipeline, PLUS ‚â•72px emoji tap-pads (üåπüå∑üåªüåºüíê) that each plant a note ‚Äî fully demoable with no mic. Full teardown (rAF, stop mic tracks, `audioCtx.close()`, dispose InstancedMesh + `renderer.dispose()`). No API route ‚Üí no guard; zero new npm deps. **Named refs:** **H. Vogel (1979)** sunflower phyllotaxis ¬∑ **Mort Garson *Mother Earth's Plantasia* (1976)** ¬∑ **Chris Wilson ACF pitch detection** ¬∑ **Pauline Oliveros *Deep Listening***. **Ambition honest 2‚Äì3/5** (#2 ‚â•4 subsystems [ACF pitch + phyllotaxis InstancedMesh growth + long-form memory/call-response sequencer + three.js render + ghost-hum/emoji fallback] + #3 named refs; #5 in-README ¬ß542 citation). **Why de-selected (NOT a quality flaw ‚Äî the closest call + the most bulletproof of the three):** 918 answers the bigger 0√ó directive (multi-user, named every jury) where long-form-stateful has lab priors (734/754/888 adult; 845-kids-sing-meadow banked is a near-cousin). **Resurrect FIRST on the kids lane** when the want is depth/long-form or maximal unattended robustness ‚Äî it ships clean as-is. Clean next step: a richer plant taxonomy per pitch-class (deeper morphology, not just density/color) so "different at minute 5" is structural, and a held note grows a visibly bigger blossom.

### `919-kids-rain-bowl` (kids) ‚Äî gently tilt the iPad to pour glowing rain that chimes as it splashes (status: built complete, banked)
**Built to demoable this fire (page.tsx + wgsl.ts + webgpu-pour.ts + three-pour.ts + audio.ts + README; builder reports `tsc --noEmit` + `next lint` clean on the folder, and the production build compiled its files with zero refs).** The **JURY 2026-06-24 #1 "swing back to GPU"** answer on the scarcest surface ‚Äî a from-scratch **WebGPU compute** particle system: a single `GPUBuffer` holds ~30k droplets (GPU-resident), a WGSL `@compute` (workgroup 64) spawns from a tilt-following spout, integrates under gravity + sideways tilt-gravity + air drag, and on each droplet crossing the pond line does an **`atomicAdd` into an impacts buffer** packing `(x, speed)`; that buffer is `MAP_READ`-mapped async (one-frame latency) so the CPU turns each impact into one velocity-scaled **C-pentatonic bell + one ripple** ‚Äî **physics is the composer** (rhythm/texture from where/when drops land, pitch held deliberately dumb). Instanced additive render tints blue‚Üíteal‚Üíviolet. **Input:** `deviceorientation` tilt (primary) + a ‚â•64px `üåß pour` tap-pad fallback. **Crucial fallback (what most review phones show today):** `navigator.gpu` absent ‚Üí a **deliberately-lush three.js/WebGL2 `THREE.Points` pour** (~9k drops, custom additive sprite + a shimmering pond-glow ripple band) running the SAME gravity‚Üíimpact‚Üíchime/ripple logic + same audio, w/ a `text-rose-300` "(showing the simple rain)" notice. **Unattended glance:** an **auto-pour** starts within ~1s (the bowl auto-tilts on a slow sine); real tilt overrides, ~4s idle resumes it. Kid-safe `gain ‚â§0.26 ‚Üí lowpass ~6.5kHz ‚Üí compressor(-10/20:1)`; ambient water pad + rain hiss never silent; AudioContext + iOS `DeviceOrientationEvent.requestPermission()` gated in the Start tap. Full teardown (rAF; remove resize/deviceorientation; WebGPU `device.destroy()` + buffer `.destroy()` OR three.js dispose + `renderer.dispose()`; `audioCtx.close()`). No API route ‚Üí no guard; zero new npm deps. **Named refs:** the **rainstick** (tilt a tube ‚Üí cascading chimes) ¬∑ **cymatics / water-surface physics** ¬∑ **Houdini / TouchDesigner GPU particle "pour" sims** (reimplemented from scratch in WGSL). **Ambition honest 2‚Äì3/5** (#2 ‚â•4 subsystems [tilt sensor + WGSL @compute gravity sim + atomic impact-readback chime synth + GPU render + three.js fallback] + #3 named refs). **Why de-selected (NOT a quality flaw):** (a) the **WGSL is unvalidated headless** (no GPU/WebGPU device in-container ‚Äî buffer layouts, the `atomic<u32>` channel, and `createShaderModule` compilation are untested at runtime ‚Äî runtime risk if WebGPU IS present and a shader bug slips the init guard), and (b) **a phone shows the three.js fallback anyway**, so the WGSL "win" wouldn't land at the 06:30 glance. 918 answered the bigger 0√ó directive. **Resurrect when** the lab wants the cold WebGPU surface on a **desktop/Tauri** window (where the WGSL actually renders + can be validated), OR simply **ship the three.js pour** on a GPU kids cycle (it's lush and bulletproof). Clean next step: validate the WGSL on a real device; add a true accumulating pool (a small fluid-height field) so the pond visibly fills.

### `923-aeolian-globe` ‚≠ê RESURRECT-FIRST (adult) ‚Äî spin a small planet and hear its WINDS sing (status: built complete, banked)
**Built to demoable this fire (page.tsx + field.ts + peaks.ts + globe.ts + audio.ts + README; builder reports `next lint` + full-project `tsc` clean).** The directest browser homage to **arXiv:2601.15982** ("Real-Time Inviscid Fluid Dynamics and Aero-acoustics on a Sphere", Jan 2026): a glowing deep-blue planet, ~36k luminous cyan‚Üíwhite **GPU wind particles** (THREE.Points, Euler-advected on-sphere + re-projected each step ‚Äî the *spirit* of the Closest Point Method) streaming over the surface around a steerable zonal jet + drifting Gaussian vortices; 7 fixed glowing **peaks** (lat/long, varied effective diameter `d`) each shed an **Aeolian whistle `f=St¬∑U/d`** as wind streams past, **stereo-panned by on-screen longitude** so spinning the globe pans the choir. INPUT drag-to-orbit + device-tilt to tip the prevailing wind (the *wind* is the instrument, so it dodges the touch-as-primary ban). Visuals + audio read the SAME `field.ts` (`sampleWind`/`windSpeed`/`windShear`) ‚Äî no GPU readback, one velocity field. Broadband wind-noise bed tracks total wind energy; always-blowing from load so peaks sing within ~1s, slow auto-rotate when idle. Master gain ‚Üí 7kHz lowpass ‚Üí compressor ‚Üí destination; full three.js dispose + audio teardown. No API route ‚Üí no guard; zero new npm deps. **Named refs:** arXiv:2601.15982 (CPM surface flow + FW-H aeroacoustics) ¬∑ Aeolian tone/Strouhal ¬∑ FW-H analogy ¬∑ Jos Stam advection. **Ambition honest 3/5** (#2 ‚â•4 subsystems [on-sphere wind field + 36k GPU particle render + per-peak aeroacoustic synth + tilt/drag input] + #3 named refs + #5 ¬ß543 citation). **Why de-selected (NOT a quality flaw ‚Äî most visually striking of the three):** its flow is an **analytic prescribed** field (sum of vortices + zonal jet), NOT an integrated fluid PDE ‚Äî honestly documented ‚Äî so it's the weakest on the concept's core "live *simulation* aeroacoustics" claim that 922 nails with a real GPU stable-fluids solver. **Resurrect FIRST on the adult lane** when the want is the cosmic/planet visual or the on-a-sphere paper homage; **fix-first:** replace the prescribed field with a real integrated surface-flow solve (CPM or an equirectangular stable-fluids step advected onto the sphere) so the winds are *solved*, then the "live simulation aeroacoustics" claim is fully earned. Clean next step beyond that: terrain that grows/erodes with wind, and a true FW-H far-field (retarded-time) term.

### `921-aeolian-loom` (adult) ‚Äî stir a fluid with your hand and the vortices past tuned rods sing (status: built complete, banked)
**Built to demoable this fire (page.tsx + fluid.ts + audio.ts + README; builder reports `eslint` + `tsc` clean on the folder).** The same lab-first technique as the SHIPPED 922 ‚Äî **live GPU stable-fluids aeroacoustics** ‚Äî but driven by **pointer-drag "stir the air"** instead of breath: a full Jos Stam pipeline (curl, vorticity confinement, divergence, 24/14-iter Jacobi projection, gradient subtract, semi-Lagrangian advection, Gaussian wind/dye splats along pointer velocity) on raw WebGL2 float RTs (256¬≤ RGBA32F, half-float/128¬≤ fallback), soft circular rod obstacles via in-shader `rodMask`, an 8√ó1 GPU probe `readPixels` feeding per-rod `f=St¬∑U/d` whistles (bandpass noise + sine partial, hysteresis-smoothed, vorticity FM/AM warble) + a kinetic-energy wind bed. Idle >2s ‚Üí wandering auto-breeze so it sings hands-free; first stir injects two splats so it sounds instantly. A "top tone" HUD shows the highest current shedding frequency tracking the physics. No API route ‚Üí no guard; zero new npm deps. **Named refs:** Jos Stam GDC 2003 ¬∑ Strouhal aeolian tone ¬∑ FW-H / arXiv:2601.15982. **Ambition honest 3/5** (#1 live-fluid aeroacoustics + #2 ‚â•3 subsystems + #3 named refs + #5 ¬ß543). **Why de-selected (NOT a quality flaw):** pointer-drag is **touch on mobile** ‚Äî the input JURY 2026-06-24 banned (~6√ó); 922's mic-breath dodges it cleanly and adds an embodied 4th subsystem with higher surprise. **Resurrect when** the lab wants a **desktop/trackpad** "stir the air" instrument (where mouse-drag isn't a touch violation), OR fold its **tuned-rod-loom obstacle layout** into 922 as a touchable variant on big screens. Clean next step: let the rods be *placeable* (drag rods into the flow to compose your own loom) and add vortex-shedding *lock-in* (the resonance feedback where the tone pulls the shedding rate).

## ¬ß545 banked seeds (2026-06-25, cycle 545 adult WIDE ‚Äî 2 non-winners of the depth-room fire)

Both were built to demoable in this fire (clean tsc+eslint on their folders) and removed un-committed per the orchestration rule. Each is a tight resurrect spec.

### 928-tilt-orrery ‚Äî `queued` ‚≠ê RESURRECT-FIRST
**Tilt a little cosmos and the gravitational rhythm of orbiting bodies becomes the music.** INPUT device-tilt (`DeviceOrientationEvent`, iOS `requestPermission`-gated, pointer/slider + auto-tilt-demo fallback). OUTPUT a **real WebGPU compute shader** (the scarce GPU surface the jury keeps asking for; loved `130-tsl-particle-compute`): inline WGSL integrating ~1,400 bodies in storage buffers (central-gravity + tilt, semi-implicit Euler, perihelion flag), double-buffered async readback so render never stalls; CPU N-body fallback (‚â§256 bodies) ‚Üí Canvas2D additive-glow when no `navigator.gpu`. TECHNIQUE physics‚Üírhythm: a body reaching **perihelion** fires a granular percussion hit, timbre from radius/speed (close+fast = bright bell; far+slow = soft thud), **stereo pan from angular position** so you hear the cosmos rotate; density from clustering, pulse-rate from tilt steepness; pitch a fixed drone + pentatonic, so music lives in **rhythm/density/space, not pitch**. Ref: the **orrery** (clockwork solar-system) + Houdini-POPs / Barnes‚ÄìHut GPU N-body lineage. Ambition 3/5 (#1 WebGPU-compute + tilt both lab-fresh ¬∑ #2 ‚â•3 subsystems [gyro + GPU sim + granular synth + viz] ¬∑ #3 named lineage). **Why resurrect first:** it's the cleanest unbuilt swing at the WebGPU-compute surface, fully built and isolation-clean ‚Äî a near-drop-in next adult fire. Full code existed at `src/app/dream/928-tilt-orrery/` this cycle (page.tsx / gpu.ts / sim.ts / audio.ts / README) ‚Äî rebuild from the ¬ß545 brief.

### 929-cathedral-rhythm ‚Äî `queued`
**Stack interlocking rhythmic pulses, pitch held to a drone, and a raymarched stone cathedral breathes them into reverberant space.** INPUT three-way: Web MIDI + computer-keyboard (`a s d f`‚Ä¶) + on-screen ‚â•44px lane pads; no-MIDI ‚Üí rose notice, keys+pads still play; auto-demo pattern on Start. ENGINE a lookahead Web Audio scheduler over several lanes, each a **Bjorklund Euclidean rhythm `E(k,n)`** the user grows/shrinks and phase-shifts so lanes drift like **Reich phasing**; one timbre at one fixed pitch per lane over a root+fifth drone ‚Äî all interest is the polyrhythm + the space; spatialized through a **procedurally-synthesized ~4.2s cathedral impulse ‚Üí `ConvolverNode`**, per-lane stereo pan. OUTPUT **WebGL2 raymarched SDF nave** (box + domain-repeated columns + arched ceiling) lit *only* by per-lane onset-flash lights, camera drifting down the nave; Canvas2D arch-frame fallback. Music from **rhythm + reverberant space, not pitch theory** (JURY #3). Refs: **Steve Reich** *Piano Phase* (1967) + **Godfried Toussaint** *The Euclidean Algorithm Generates Traditional Musical Rhythms* (2005). Ambition 3/5 (#2 ‚â•3 subsystems [MIDI/key input + Euclidean/phasing sequencer + convolution-space + raymarch] ¬∑ #3 two dated named refs). Full code existed at `src/app/dream/929-cathedral-rhythm/` this cycle (page.tsx / engine.ts / euclid.ts / renderer.ts / README) ‚Äî rebuild from the ¬ß545 brief.
