## ¬ß551 ‚Äî banked seed (adult DEEP non-winner) ‚Äî 2026-06-25

### `943-depth-tonnetz-room` ‚≠ê RESURRECT-FIRST (adult) ‚Äî the raw-WebGL2 twin of 942
Status: `queued` (built complete + build-green this fire, removed before the authoritative commit per the curate rule ‚Äî code lives only as this seed). The **same** concept as the shipped `942-depth-harmonic-room`: webcam **monocular depth** (Depth Anything V2 small via Transformers.js on WebGPU) ‚Üí position on a neo-Riemannian **Tonnetz** ‚Üí real **P/L/R voice-led chord changes** with SATB nearest-tone voicing + portamento glides, over a warm drone bed (`gain‚â§0.26 ‚Üí lowpass‚â§7kHz ‚Üí compressor`). The difference is the renderer: **943 uses a hand-written raw WebGL2 fragment shader** (`scene.ts`) painting a warm depth-room + a glowing Tonnetz constellation with a pulsing moved-node, where 942 leads with raw WebGPU/WGSL. **Why banked, not shipped:** WebGPU is the scarcer GPU surface (1√ó/10) and the directest answer to jury #1, so 942 won on diversity + jury-responsiveness; 943's raw-WebGL2-as-primary sits at the audit 4√ó/10 ban-edge. **Why ‚≠ê resurrect-first:** it's the **bulletproof no-WebGPU render path** ‚Äî if 942's WebGPU init disappoints on a real machine, 943 is the proven WebGL2 twin; resurrect it when WebGL2 is cold again in the diversity window, or to A/B the two render paths on real hardware. **Honest limitation the builder surfaced (carry it forward):** a *rectangular* (col,row) grid can't make **both** orthogonal axes globally P/L/R-parsimonious ‚Äî the Tonnetz is triangular ‚Äî so 943 keeps the lateral axis perfectly parsimonious (R/L chain) and lets the proximity axis use **P / Slide**. A future deepening could lay the cells on a true triangular/hex lattice so *every* adjacency is a clean single transform (this is also 942's latent limitation ‚Äî 942 sidesteps it by applying P per-row to the current cell). **Next-cycle deepenings (shared with 942's README):** seventh chords + Douthett‚ÄìSteinbach *Cube Dance*; two-body harmony (two near-regions ‚Üí two lattice walkers voice-leading against each other); hexatonic colour-field tinting; bass-line inversions driven by horizontal velocity.

## ¬ß553 ‚Äî 2026-06-25 (adult WIDE fire) ‚Äî 2 banked sibling explorers (non-winners; seeds, not committed code)

Cycle 553 ran WIDE (3 orthogonal pitch-is-the-idea explorers), shipped `947-overtone-loom` (WebGPU-compute dissonance-curve instrument). The two non-winners ‚Äî both returned **complete, build-green, self-reported clean**, `cp`'d to `/tmp/dream-losers-553/` then `rm -rf`'d (never committed):

- **`948-cantus-engine` ‚≠ê RESURRECT-FIRST (adult).** A **long-form (5+ min), stateful, self-developing counterpoint** piece ‚Äî the lab's thin **long-form/memory** menu category. Seed a 3‚Äì6-note motif (on-screen keyboard or Web MIDI); a deterministic engine develops it via real **Fux/Bach** operations ‚Äî diatonic transposition, **inversion**, **retrograde**, **augmentation/diminution**, **stretto**, **modulation** ‚Äî over a ~330s escalating arc (Exposition ‚Üí Episode ‚Üí Modulation ‚Üí Stretto ‚Üí Coda, looping with key drift) so minute 5 ‚âÝ minute 1. 2‚Äì4 voices with a Fux voice-leading scorer (rewards stepwise/consonance, penalizes parallel 5ths/8ves); clean additive/FM-triangle voices with ADSR + a Web-Audio look-ahead scheduler; **raw-WebGL2** scrolling piano-roll score that color-codes the subject's transformations as they enter (Canvas2D fallback). Files banked at `/tmp/dream-losers-553/948-cantus-engine/` (page.tsx 924 + engine.ts 191 + README 116). **Why it lost (close):** WebGL2 is healthier at 4√ó/10 vs WebGPU compute's 1√ó (947 answered jury #1 harder), AND the counterpoint/voice-leading lane was *just* mined three cycles running (550 choir-bloom, 551 Tonnetz, 552 bird-round canon) ‚Äî so 947's psychoacoustic angle was the fresher pitch-is-the-idea bet this fire. **Resurrect FIRST on the next adult fire** where WebGL2 isn't over-represented; it is the lab's strongest answer to the long-form-with-memory gap and squarely on Karel's "workspace for composers" line. Ambition 3/5 (#1 first long-form>5min stateful counterpoint engine ¬∑ #2 4 subsystems ¬∑ #3 Fux *Gradus ad Parnassum* 1725 / Bach *Art of Fugue*).

- **`949-maqam-road`.** A **non-Western microtonal** melodic instrument ‚Äî a vertical **maqam ladder** (Rast, Bayati, Hijaz, Saba) tuned by **exact cents, not 12-TET**, so the **quarter-flat / neutral-third** degrees land true; tap a step (with a grace-note ornament) or **drag to slide** (portamento) continuously through the neutral intervals ‚Äî the slide through a microtone is the whole point. Warm saw‚Üíbandpass voice with vibrato, a soft tonic+fifth drone, a glowing amber **Canvas2D "road"** tracing the melodic contour; idiomatic auto-demo loop. Files banked at `/tmp/dream-losers-553/949-maqam-road/` (page.tsx 695 + maqam.ts 128 + README 97). **Why it lost:** lowest ambition (2/5 ‚Äî #2 subsystems + #3 maqam/ajnas) and Canvas2D is the least-scarce GPU-wise surface; the spread/jury-#1 pull favored the WebGPU-compute pick. **Resurrect** when the lab wants a "surprise / non-Western structure" beat or a warm-ritual vibe off the cosmic cluster ‚Äî it brings genuinely new pitch *material* (microtonal cents a 12-TET keyboard can't make). Honest gap: the cent values are reasonable common-practice approximations, not a single canonical source ‚Äî tighten against maqamworld.com on resurrect.

## ¬ß554 ‚Äî 2026-06-25 ‚Äî banked from the kids DEEP fire (deepen 918 ‚Üí two-player role ensemble); winner shipped = 950-kids-sky-conductor

Three approaches attacked ONE concept ‚Äî "two 4-year-olds, two devices, two DISTINCT interlocking musical ROLES, one song" (the jury-#4 deepening of the 918-starlight-friend WebRTC opener: 918's two kids fling *identical* twinkles; these give them complementary parts that combine into real music). Shared spine in all three: serverless WebRTC (gzip-SDP `#join=` link, no signaling server, reimplemented from 918), tiny role-tagged events, both peers synth+render locally, a **ghost friend** that auto-plays the other role within ~1s so one untouched device reads as a duet, kids-safe chain, no api route/guard, zero npm deps. All three make **pitch/structure the idea** (jury #2) WITHOUT a voice-leading engine (the harmony is social/structural ‚Äî the role split). Winner 950 (conductor+player, raw-WebGPU) shipped; the other two banked:

- **948-kids-duet-grove ‚≠ê RESURRECT-FIRST (kids)** ‚Äî roles = **GROOVE-KEEPER** (tap/shake a looping bass+drum pulse) + **MELODY-MAKER** (drag up/down to sing a C-pentatonic melody), both quantized onto ONE shared ~96 BPM 8-step clock so the two parts lock into a single song. Renderer = **hand-written raw WebGL2** (dawn meadow; warm "mine" groove bar + cool "friend" melody motes). Returned complete + self-reported ESLint-0 / tsc-clean in-folder, full teardown, ghost-friend works. **Why banked not shipped:** 948 is arguably the most *viscerally* legible "we made a song" for a 4yo (groove + tune is the clearest two-part music), and raw-WebGL2 is the bulletproof no-WebGPU path ‚Äî but its WebGL2 renderer sits at the diversity audit's 4√ó/10 ban-edge (946/939/931/927), whereas the winner 950 lands the scarce raw-WebGPU surface the jury's #1 keeps begging for. **Resurrect-first** if 950's WebGPU disappoints on a real iPad, or as the safe-renderer twin. (The groove+melody role split is also the most different from 950's chord-context split ‚Äî resurrecting it keeps the two-player lane diverse.)
- **949-kids-echo-birds** ‚Äî roles = **CALL-AND-RESPONSE turn-taking**: the CALLER taps a 3‚Äì4-note bird-phrase (tap height = pentatonic pitch), the birds fly to the friend's tree and sing, then that child becomes the ANSWERER and replies ‚Äî roles trade every exchange, the accumulating question‚Üíanswer IS the song. Renderer = **Canvas2D** (bright daytime sky, two trees warm/cool, perches glow to show whose turn). Returned complete + self-reported tsc/eslint clean, ghost auto-answers within ~1s + runs a hands-free call‚Üíanswer demo on cold load. **Why banked:** the most *charming/dialogic* of the three and Canvas2D is the freshest renderer (0√ó in the last-10 committed) ‚Äî but it does not advance the jury's #1 GPU ask and is harmonically the thinnest (melodic dialogue, not chord-over-melody). Resurrect when a Canvas2D / turn-taking-conversation surface is wanted, or pair with 948 as the no-GPU kids-multiplayer twin.


## ¬ß556 ‚Äî banked from cycle 556 (kids DEEP, Iron Garden, 1 concept √ó 2 approaches)

- **954-kids-iron-garden-gl ‚≠ê (resurrect-first, kids)** ‚Äî the **raw-WebGL2 GPU sibling** of the shipped `953-kids-iron-garden`. Same concept (drag magnet-flowers, iron filings trace Faraday's lines of force, two flowers' fields bridging blooms a pentatonic chord), but the filing field is GPU-advected: ~65‚Äì80k filings live in an `RGBA32F` ping-pong float texture; a fragment pass steps each filing along `B(P)=Œ£ q¬∑(P‚àípole)/|P‚àípole|¬≥` with hash-based respawn; a `gl_POINTS` additive pass colors each by nearest-magnet hue. Hand-written GLSL ES 3.00, no three.js. Returned complete + self-reported lint-0 / tsc-clean / context-loss + full teardown handled. **Why banked, not shipped:** for a *kids* fire, robustness + reach won ‚Äî 953's Canvas2D runs on any iPad with no `EXT_color_buffer_float` dependency and no per-frame React state churn (954 calls `setHandles`/`setGlow` every frame, 60 re-renders/sec fighting the GPU), and Canvas2D is the freshest renderer per the diversity audit (WebGPU is over the cap; the jury flagged a 12-of-15 GPU monoculture). **How to resurrect:** (1) refactor the per-frame `setHandles`/`setGlow` into imperative ref-driven DOM transforms (no React state in the rAF loop) so it holds 60fps with 80k filings; (2) ship on an adult/older-kid fire where raw-WebGL2 is the scarce surface and float-render-target support can be assumed; (3) consider it the "big-screen / installation" lens of Iron Garden (projector-dense field) vs. 953 as the "any-iPad" lens. Code preserved this fire at `/tmp/dream-losers-556/954-kids-iron-garden-gl/`. **Multi-cycle deepening for either 953 or 954:** (a) a *third* magnet whose field line threads through two others ‚Üí a triad; (b) "bar-magnet" vs "horseshoe" flower shapes (different dipole geometries ‚Üí visibly different fields); (c) a parent-corner toggle for scale/key; (d) the long-asked **A/B "what magnetism vs. cymatics each reveal"** companion. Named refs available to extend: Faraday's lines of force; Daniel Rozin (if a camera/pin-relief portrait mode is ever added).

## ¬ß558 banked seeds (2026-06-26, cycle 558 ¬∑ KIDS ¬∑ WIDE ‚Äî two built-complete explorers, de-selected on curate)

Cycle 558 ran a kids **WIDE** fire ‚Äî three orthogonal "**real melody/harmony IS the toy**" explorers (the directest answer to JURY 2026-06-25 #2 "make music from PITCH again" on the kids axis, where every prior kids build held pitch deliberately dumb ‚Äî the 935 pruning flag). All three dodged every JURY ban (no three.js / no mic / no granular / no pitch-dumb stance) across DIFFERENT renderers (WebGL2 / Canvas2D / WebGPU) √ó inputs (drag / tap / tilt) √ó techniques (cadence / contour / interval-harmony). Winner shipped = **`957-kids-come-home`** (drag a firefly up a hill, feel it want to fall *home* ‚Äî a real V‚ÜíI leading-tone cadence, grounded in Pachet's 1-day-old attractor paper arXiv:2606.24911). The two below returned **built complete + demoable** (each self-reported tsc/ESLint-clean on its folder), `cp`'d to `/tmp/dream-losers-558/` then `rm -rf`'d before the authoritative winner-only build (NOT committed). Rebuild from the briefs below.

### `959-kids-chord-kites` ‚≠ê RESURRECT-FIRST (kids) ‚Äî tilt to fly two kites; the DISTANCE between them is the harmony (status: built complete, banked)
**Built to demoable this fire (page.tsx + wgsl.ts + gpu.ts + harmony.ts + audio.ts + README; builder reports tsc-clean after filtering the container's missing-dep artifacts, same as the known-good `130`).** The highest-ambition of the three and the first KIDS piece about **harmony as the interval between two voices** (medieval parallel organum for a 4yo). Tilt the iPad (`DeviceOrientationEvent`, iOS `requestPermission()`-gated; drag/slider + auto-fly fallback) to fly a glowing kite up/down = a pentatonic melody note; a friend kite follows and the **interval you hear** is the harmony ‚Äî together = warm unison/octave, a third = sweet, a fifth = open/strong, a second = a gentle playful *spice* (kid-safe detune beat, never harsh). A **raw WebGPU `@compute` particle wind-field** (~18k GPU-resident particles) blooms the supporting **triad's color** around the kites ‚Üí WebGL2 point-sprite fallback (identical physics) ‚Üí Canvas2D last resort (no three.js anywhere). Kids-safe chain (`gain 0.26 ‚Üí lowpass 7000 ‚Üí comp ‚àí10/20:1`), always-on wind bed, ~1s auto-breeze (interval opens/closes octave‚Üíthird‚Üífifth‚Üísecond‚Üíhome) with 4s idle-resume, full teardown. Named refs: parallel organum / parallel thirds-fifths ¬∑ infant consonance>dissonance preference ¬∑ **Pachet arXiv:2606.24911** (home interval as attractor) ¬∑ loved `130-tsl-particle-compute`. **Ambition 3/5** (#1 WebGPU compute ¬∑ #2 ‚â•3 subsystems [tilt + GPU particle sim + interval/triad synth + render] ¬∑ #3 named refs ¬∑ #5 ¬ß558 research). **Why de-selected (closest call, NOT a quality flaw):** for a *kids* fire robustness + reach won (the 953/954 lesson) ‚Äî (a) the **WGSL is unvalidated headless** (no GPU in-container; uniform byte-layouts 32B/48B + `arrayLength` are the watch-points ‚Äî JURY #3 verification debt), (b) **most review phones show the WebGL2 fallback** anyway at the 06:30 glance, and (c) WebGPU-compute is at the diversity audit's edge (~4√ó across recent committed 954/950/947/942), where 957's raw-WebGL2 is the fresher + bulletproof surface. **Resurrect FIRST** on a kids GPU fire where float-render-targets/WebGPU can be assumed, or on a **desktop/Tauri** window where the WGSL actually renders + can be validated; OR simply ship the lush WebGL2 kite-sky on any GPU kids cycle. Clean next step: validate the WGSL on a real device; let the child *grab and place* the friend kite to build their own two-part harmony line. Code preserved at `/tmp/dream-losers-558/959-kids-chord-kites/`.

### `958-kids-stepping-stones` (kids) ‚Äî place lily-pad stones at heights; a frog hops your melody's CONTOUR, step vs leap (status: built complete, banked)
**Built to demoable this fire (page.tsx + melody.ts + audio.ts + README; builder reports its non-React helper modules type-check fully clean, real errors swept).** The first KIDS piece teaching **melodic contour** (the *shape* of a tune): tap a bright Canvas2D pond to drop glowing lily-pad stones at different heights (x = time, y = pitch, snapped to C-major pentatonic ‚Äî no wrong notes); a cheerful **frog HOPS** stone to stone left‚Üíright singing each note. Adjacent stones close in height = a gentle **step** (small hop, legato, white-dot marker); far apart = a big **LEAP** (high arc, brighter accented note, gold-star marker), with a dashed contour line so the tune's shape is legible. Soft diatonic auto-harmony (I/IV/V) follows the contour; loops continuously; seeded with a ~7-stone rise-then-fall arc so an untouched screen sings within ~1s. Web Audio **look-ahead scheduler** (25ms clock, ~0.12s ahead) for tight hop timing; soft-FM music-box voice + 3-note pad; kids-safe chain; iOS gesture-gated AudioContext; full teardown; dark text on the bright pond per the light-bg contrast rules. Named refs: **Carnegie Hall Music Educators Toolbox Grade-1 *Melodic Contour*** ¬∑ **Kod√°ly** solf√®ge stepping ¬∑ contrast with Xenakis **UPIC** (781 is continuous-paint; this is the discrete, contour-legible version with a walking character). **Ambition 2/5** (#2 ‚â•3 subsystems [stone-placement model + walking-character look-ahead sequencer + contour-following auto-harmony + Canvas render] ¬∑ #3 named refs ¬∑ #5 ¬ß558 research). **Why de-selected (NOT a quality flaw):** lowest ambition of the three and the place-notes/contour idiom rhymes with the existing UPIC/paint lineage (781-kids-paint-conductor, 100/152/160), so lowest surprise; Canvas2D is the freshest renderer but the concept overlaps priors more than 957's never-done felt-cadence. **Resurrect** when a bright daytime Canvas2D / melody-building beat is wanted, or as the bulletproof no-GPU kids piece. Clean next step: drag-to-reposition stones (currently tap-to-add / tap-to-remove); a held stone = a longer note. Code preserved at `/tmp/dream-losers-558/958-kids-stepping-stones/`.


## ¬ß559 banked seeds (2026-06-26, cycle 559 ¬∑ ADULT ¬∑ WIDE ‚Äî two built-complete NON-harmony explorers, de-selected on curate)

Cycle 559 ran an adult **WIDE** fire of **3 orthogonal NON-harmony explorers** (the sharpest answer to the JURY 2026-06-25 "too similar / hardened house style" critique ‚Äî the live monoculture being *harmony itself*, the idea in 7 of the last 10 committed builds). The two non-winners returned **built complete + self-reported eslint/tsc-clean** with READMEs, `cp`'d to `/tmp/dream-losers-559/` then `rm -rf`'d before the authoritative winner-only build (NOT committed). Rebuild from the briefs below.

### `961-listen-to-the-world` ‚≠ê RESURRECT-FIRST (adult) ‚Äî the live planet, sonified (status: built complete, banked)
A generative **event-sonification of the live Wikimedia recent-changes SSE feed** (`https://stream.wikimedia.org/v2/stream/recentchange` via browser `EventSource`, public CORS, no key) ‚Äî every edit/new-page/new-editor across all Wikipedias becomes a discrete consonant **bell/marimba bloom** + an expanding **Canvas2D ripple**. A direct homage to **Hatnote's *Listen to Wikipedia*** (Stephen LaPorte & Mahmoud Hashemi, 2013). Mapping: `log10(|byte-delta|)` ‚Üí C-pentatonic pitch (bigger edit = lower/larger), type ‚Üí timbre (edit = soft inharmonic bell, new page = warm triangle swell, new user = bright FM chime); ‚â§6 voices; `master 0.22 ‚Üí lowpass ‚Üí compressor`; ring radius ‚àù magnitude, hue by wiki-language, position hashed from title; ‚â§70 concurrent rings with slow fade. **Discrete event-bells, NOT a drone** ‚Äî which is exactly the answer to **JURY 2026-06-25 #6** ("a third live-data sonification needs a *different OUTPUT* than the 898/939 feed‚Üídrone template"). Robust **synthetic Poisson fallback** (fires if EventSource throws / no message in ~4.5s / onerror), amber "‚óã demo stream" vs emerald "‚óè live feed" status, bots toggle. Self-reported eslint-clean (EventSource closed in timeout/onerror/unmount). **Why banked (close):** 960 cleared 4/5 with a never-used technique + a direct research chain + Node-tested DSP; 961 is 3/5 (#2 ‚â•3 subsystems + #3 named refs) and its live path is unverifiable in-container. **Why ‚≠ê resurrect-first:** it is the lab's freshest *output register* (calm data-poetry / Hatnote-Ikeda, off the cosmic + kids clusters), Canvas2D is a scarce renderer, and it's the standing jury-#6 answer. **Resurrect** on an adult data/ambient beat; clean next step = add a second feed (earthquakes/flights) as a counter-voice, or route the feed to a *different* modality again (data‚ÜíAI-image) per the jury. Code preserved at `/tmp/dream-losers-559/961-listen-to-the-world/`.

### `962-effort-ink` (adult) ‚Äî the FEEL of a gesture makes the music, not its shape (status: built complete, banked)
A cross-modal calligraphic instrument where the **kinematics of your pointer gesture** (velocity / acceleration / curvature / jerk / stylus pressure) drive an expressive continuous synth + a living **Canvas2D ink** trail ‚Äî *how* you move is everything, *where* is almost irrelevant. Grounded in **Rudolf Laban's Effort theory** (Weight / Time / Space / Flow), surfaced live in a HUD; lineage = **Golan Levin's *Yellowtail* / *Scribble***. Slow+smooth ‚Üí warm legato + clean thick ink; fast+jerky ‚Üí bright/noisy/percussive + thin fraying/splattering ink; a flick ‚Üí a filtered-noise transient accent. Pitch drifts gently through a just-intoned minor-pentatonic so it's always consonant but deliberately **secondary** (expression/timbre is the subject ‚Äî honors jury #2 without being a harmony engine). Continuous-excitation synth (saw+triangle ‚Üí lowpass ‚Üí gain; looping noise ‚Üí bandpass for breath/transients; vibrato LFO; `master ‚â§0.22 ‚Üí lowpass ‚Üí compressor`), **NOT granular**. Files: `page.tsx` + `kinematics.ts` (rolling-history Effort tracker, EMA-smoothed) + `audio.ts` (`setExpression` mapping) + README. Self-reported eslint/tsc-clean, full cleanup. **Why banked:** 3/5 (#2 + #3); strong concept but 960's friction model was the higher-ambition, more-novel, research-chained pick this fire. **Resurrect** when a cross-modal / embodied-gesture / Canvas2D-calligraphy beat is wanted (it's a distinct INPUT ‚Äî gesture *dynamics*, not position-to-pitch like the paint lineage). Clean next step: bow-stroke "ink memory" that re-sings a saved gesture; map curvature to a second timbral axis. Code preserved at `/tmp/dream-losers-559/962-effort-ink/`.

## ¬ß567 banked seeds (2026-06-27, cycle 567 ¬∑ ADULT ¬∑ DEEP ‚Äî one concept "Echo Room", 3 render/sensor approaches; shipped 977-echo-room-gpu)

ONE concept: **build polyphony from your own past selves in space** ‚Äî move through an invisible C-major harmonic field; your motion is recorded over a ~7s bar and becomes a permanent **HRTF-spatialized ghost** that re-traces its path and re-sings its chord while you (the `AudioListener`) walk among up to 6 of your past selves. Research-chained to RESEARCH ¬ß567 (DanXeReflect, CHI 2026 ‚Äî perform against your spatio-temporal past self) + Cardiff *Forty-Part Motet* + Reich phase-looping. All three returned **built complete + self-reported tsc/eslint-clean** (975/976 + winner 977 each ~31‚Äì40KB page.tsx + README). Winner 977 shipped (Pose + raw WebGPU particle field ‚Äî the scarcest renderer, 1√ó in last-10 vs Canvas2D 5√ó / WebGL2 4√ó). The two non-winners `cp`'d to `/tmp/dream-losers-567/` then `rm -rf`'d before the authoritative build (NOT committed). Rebuild from the briefs below.

- **`976-echo-room-flow` ‚≠ê RESURRECT-FIRST / VERIFICATION-DEBT CANDIDATE (adult).** Same Echo Room concept but **webcam optical-flow / frame-difference motion-centroid (NO ML model download)** + pure **Canvas2D** warm plan-view. x-axis walks I‚ÄìIV‚ÄìV‚Äìvi‚Äìii‚ÄìI in C; per-ghost HRTF panner; auto-demo + mouse fallback; verified HTTP 200 under `next dev`. **Why it's the resurrect-first:** it is the lab's **most laptop-verifiable** Echo Room ‚Äî no CDN MediaPipe model, no WebGPU dependency, pure frame-diff + Canvas2D ‚Äî so it is the right one to **hand-verify on a real device** to start paying down the jury's #1 verification-debt liability. De-selected only because Canvas2D is at the diversity audit cap (5√ó/10) and it's the least GPU-ambitious of the three. Code at `/tmp/dream-losers-567/976-echo-room-flow/`. Clean next step: hand-verify the optical-flow centroid thresholds + bar length on a real webcam; let the performer drag-place a ghost to reposition it.
- **`975-echo-room` (adult).** Same concept, **MediaPipe Pose** (torso/hip centroid) + raw **WebGL2** GLSL-ES-3.00 floor-plan render (bright comet performer + glowing ghost trails). x walks vi‚ÄìIV‚ÄìI‚ÄìV‚ÄìI (common-tone voice-led); per-ghost HRTF; auto-demo + pointer fallback. De-selected because WebGL2 is at 4√ó/10 (vs WebGPU's 1√ó) and 977's particle-cloud ghosts are the more evocative realization of "stand among your past selves." Code at `/tmp/dream-losers-567/975-echo-room/`. Resurrect as the WebGL2 twin on a fire where WebGPU is over-represented, or fold its cleaner floor-plan legibility into 977.
- **Multi-cycle deepening for 977 (or any Echo Room):** (a) a **conductor mode** ‚Äî raise/lower a hand (Pose wrist-y) to swell/mute individual ghosts you're facing; (b) **harmonic memory across sessions** (persist a ghost ensemble to localStorage and re-enter your room tomorrow); (c) **two-performer** Echo Room over WebRTC (still-COLD adult multi-user lane) ‚Äî two people layering ghosts into one shared field; (d) a **decay/forgetting** axis so the oldest selves slowly fade in volume (long-form evolution).

## ¬ß568 banked seeds (2026-06-27, cycle 568 ¬∑ KIDS ¬∑ WIDE ‚Äî two built-complete non-winner explorers, de-selected on curate; shipped 978-kids-cloud-weather)

All three explorers were a *non-pointer input into real (non-pentatonic) harmony* ‚Äî answering JURY 2026-06-26 #1 (ban pentatonic-no-wrong-notes; give kids a real mode/progression/tension) + #3 (force a non-pointer input) three different ways. Winner 978 took the camera/body + Laban-Effort + Lydian path (freshest input, most ambitious). The two below returned complete + builder-reported tsc/eslint-clean (READMEs written); code at `/tmp/dream-losers-568/` (ephemeral ‚Äî rebuild from these briefs).

### `979-kids-happy-sad-tree` ‚≠ê RESURRECT-FIRST (kids) ‚Äî feel major‚Üîminor by flipping one switch (status: built complete, banked)
**The single most literal "feel real harmony" kids toy, and the most ROBUST one in the lab ‚Äî zero permissions, plays instantly on any phone/laptop/desktop.** A glowing musical tree on a WebGL2 sky (Canvas2D fallback). The child **taps** big glowing fruit (~68px targets, discrete taps only ‚Äî NOT drag) to pluck diatonic melody notes; an always-on warm pad plays a I‚Äìvi‚ÄìIV‚ÄìV (minor: i‚ÄìVI‚Äìiv‚Äìv). A giant ‚òÄÔ∏è/üåô toggle flips the whole world between **parallel C major ‚Üî C natural minor** ‚Äî the SAME melody and SAME taps instantly feel happy vs. tender, with the held chord re-voicing (tones glide to the new mode) + a ~600ms warm-gold‚Üîcool-indigo crossfade. Real diatonic tonal harmony, NOT a pentatonic safety scale ‚Äî the lowered 3rd/6th/7th are exactly where the feeling lives. Idle auto-player taps a phrase + breathes between modes so a silent glance demos the whole idea. Named ref: **Kate Hevner's mode/affect experiments (1935‚Äì37)** (major=bright/minor=tender, independent of melody). **Why resurrect-first:** (a) it is the **easiest kids piece to hand-verify** (no camera/mic/accelerometer/GPU-required ‚Üí directly dents the jury's #1 verification-debt liability on a real device), and (b) it's the most universally-playable 06:30 demo. De-selected only because tap-on-glass + WebGL2 (4√ó recent) is a more familiar idiom than 978's camera/Laban novelty, and the diversity audit favored the camera/body input (0√ó recent) for this fire. Code at `/tmp/dream-losers-568/979-kids-happy-sad-tree/`. Clean next step: ship as-is on a fire where camera/body input is over-represented; or deepen with a 3rd "dreamy" Lydian/Dorian mode on the same toggle.

### `980-kids-shake-bells` (kids) ‚Äî shake the tablet to build a real cadence (status: built complete, banked)
**SHAKE the device like a tambourine of magic handbells (accelerometer ‚Äî fully off-glass) to ring bells tuned to a real functional I‚ÄìIV‚ÄìV‚ÄìI in C major that advances on a slow clock and resolves home to the tonic when you stop.** Shake intensity ‚Üí how many bells ring + burst brightness (gentle = 1‚Äì2 soft tones, big = full sparkling arpeggio). `devicemotion` shake-onset detection (adaptive threshold + 150ms refractory), iOS 13+ `requestPermission()` in the Start gesture, a 176px round SHAKE! tap-button for desktop, and a ghost auto-shaker for the hardware-free glance. Visuals = WebGPU/WGSL quad particle field ‚Üí WebGL2 point sprites ‚Üí Canvas2D radial-glow bells (full fallback chain); warm gold bells over deep-blue-violet night. Always-on root drone keeps it grounded. Named refs: **Orff Schulwerk** body-percussion/handbell pedagogy + a textbook functional authentic cadence. De-selected because the I‚ÄìIV‚ÄìV‚ÄìI cadence rhymes with the two most-recent kids ships (957 V‚ÜíI, 974 V7‚ÜíI), WebGPU shows its fallback on most phones + is unverifiable headless, and the camera/Laban winner was both fresher-input and more ambitious. Code at `/tmp/dream-losers-568/980-kids-shake-bells/`. Clean next step: resurrect on a kids fire where the recent ships are NOT cadence-based; swap the I‚ÄìIV‚ÄìV‚ÄìI for a modal color (e.g. a Mixolydian bell journey) to dodge the cadence rhyme; or make shake-direction (not just magnitude) choose the chord.

## ¬ß573 banked seeds (2026-06-27, cycle 573 ¬∑ ADULT ¬∑ WIDE ‚Äî 3 orthogonal explorers, 2 built-complete non-winners; shipped 986-empty-words)

Three explorers, each a *non-pointer, non-cosmic, sound-as-object* adult build clearing the ambition floor via a DIFFERENT input (text / live-data / tilt) ‚Äî directly attacking the jury's pointer-7√ó input monoculture. Winner 986 took the text lane (freshest input ever + research-chained flagship + strongest verification dent). Both below returned complete + builder-reported tsc/eslint-clean (READMEs written); code at `/tmp/dream-losers-573/` (ephemeral ‚Äî rebuild from these briefs).

### `985-quake-choir` ‚≠ê RESURRECT-FIRST (adult) ‚Äî hear the planet's last 24h of earthquakes (status: built complete, banked)
**The live USGS earthquake feed ‚Üí a slow, evolving choir of inharmonic modal bells over a sustained drone, plotted on a raw-WebGL2 equirectangular seismographic map.** Client-side `fetch` of `https://earthquake.usgs.gov/earthquakes/feed/v1.0/summary/all_day.geojson` (24h) / `all_week.geojson` (no key, permissive CORS, NO api route ‚Üí no guard). Each quake = one struck modal bell scheduled on a compressed ~4-min timeline by its real timestamp (so the texture genuinely evolves ‚Äî quiet stretches then swarms, minute 5 ‚âÝ minute 1, then loops). Mapping: **magnitude ‚Üí register+amplitude** on a 5-octave **D Dorian** ladder (real mode, NOT pentatonic ‚Äî M6+ rings low near the D1/D2 drone, M1 pings high/quiet); **depth ‚Üí timbre** (shallow = bright/near-harmonic, deep = dark/inharmonic partial-stretch + lowpass); **longitude ‚Üí stereo pan**; **latitude ‚Üí octave nudge + filter**. Sustained tonal bed (detuned sines on D1/D2/A2/F3) + small convolution reverb. **The sound is the object, the map is its readout ‚Äî NOT a GPU-sim-pulse-bell.** Named refs: **Andrea Polli** environmental data-sonification + **Florian Dombois** earthquake *audification*. **Robust offline path:** fetch fail/CORS/empty ‚Üí amber "‚óã simulated quakes" status + the IDENTICAL pipeline on a seeded, deterministic, **Gutenberg‚ÄìRichter-distributed** synthetic catalog (with injected notable large events); emerald "‚óè live USGS feed ¬∑ N quakes" when live; `text-rose-300` on hard error. **Why banked (very close):** the builder **fetched the real feed end-to-end ‚Äî 202/202 features parsed, HTTP 200, permissive CORS ‚Äî and dry-ran both sources (0 invalid events, GR-shaped M1‚ÜíM7.1, bell freqs ~110Hz‚Äì2093Hz)**, making it genuinely near-shippable and one of the lab's more verifiable live-data pieces. De-selected only because 986's text input is the freshest-ever (0√ó vs live-data's prior 842/961) AND was the research-chained flagship. **Why ‚≠ê resurrect-first:** it's near-shippable, verifiable (real feed + deterministic fallback), fills the live-data lane with a *different* output than 842/961 (seismographic globe + modal-bell-choir, not feed‚Üídrone), and Karel's love cluster leans toward data/tool pieces. Code at `/tmp/dream-losers-573/985-quake-choir/`. Clean next steps: hand-verify the bell timbre + timeline-compression by ear; add a 2nd live feed (e.g. flights, ISS) as a counter-voice; let a click on the map solo a region; real coastlines instead of the procedural land mask.

### `987-tilt-spectra` (adult) ‚Äî sculpt a sound's spectrum with your body (status: built complete, banked)
**Tilt the device through a 2-D field and you're not picking notes ‚Äî you're shaping the *spectrum itself* of one sustained drone; the WebGL2 CRT oscilloscope on screen draws the literal output waveform, so sound and image are the same signal.** Input: `DeviceOrientationEvent` (iOS 13+ `requestPermission()` in the Start gesture) with resting-orientation calibration + frame-rate-aware smoothing; desktop fallback = mouse-as-tilt + an idle auto-orbit "ghost" so a hands-free glance still evolves. **X (gamma) ‚Üí brightness / spectral centroid** (a `1/n^rolloff` partial roll-off, exponent ~4.2‚Üí0.3); **Y (beta) ‚Üí inharmonicity** (stretched-partial law `f_n = n¬∑f0¬∑‚àö(1+B¬∑n¬≤)` ‚Äî bell/metallic forward, slack/compressed back). Pitch stays anchored to a low ~D2 drifting slowly through a real mode, so it's musical while the *spectrum* is the subject. 16-oscillator additive bank rebuilt every frame with `setTargetAtTime` (no zipper noise); AnalyserNode feeds the scope. Raw GLSL ES 3.00 CRT renderer: beam pass + phosphor-persistence ping-pong + present pass (scanlines, barrel curve, vignette, green‚Üíamber tint warming with brightness). **Sound-as-object ‚Äî directest hit on JURY #2** (like 960-friction / 965-oscilloscope). Synth math validated across axis extremes (0 non-finite partials; max ~1.6kHz hard-clamped to 0.92¬∑Nyquist). Named refs: **Daphne Oram's Oramics** (drawing timbre) + the **oscilloscope-music** tradition (Jerobeam Fenderson). **Why banked:** 2/5 ambition (#2 + #3) and tilt is 3√ó-recent (allowed but not the freshest); its core embodied feel needs a real tilt device to judge (the riskier unheard ship vs 986's type-and-hear). De-selected for 986's fresher input + headless-proven determinism. Code at `/tmp/dream-losers-573/987-tilt-spectra/`. Clean next steps: hand-verify the tilt feel + permission flow on a phone; add a 3rd axis (compass/heading ‚Üí formant shift); record/replay a gesture as a re-sung timbre envelope; let two-finger pinch set the fundamental.

**Brief (resurrect ~as-is):** the same "walk a building of harmonic chambers, doorway = pivot-chord key modulation" concept as the shipped `997-resonant-halls`, but rendered top-down as a **floor-plan** and ‚Äî crucially ‚Äî with the resonating body being a **real WebGPU compute shader**: a 12,000-particle storage buffer advanced every frame on the GPU (particles swirl around loud chambers, drift toward the active doorway, shimmer), drawn as additive sprites tinted by circle-of-fifths hue. Six chambers C‚ÜíG‚ÜíD‚ÜíA‚ÜíE‚ÜíB in a hexagonal ring; `chamberWeights(lx,ly)` Gaussian wells; pivot-tone holding (each chamber's 3/2 == its neighbour's 1/1, gains cross-fade only). Three-tier render fallback WebGPU-compute ‚Üí WebGL2-instanced ‚Üí Canvas2D with a live path badge. Pointer-drag primary, WASD fallback.

**Why it's the resurrect-first:** it is the directest answer to **JURY 2026-06-27 #3** ("WebGPU compute collapsed 6√ó‚Üí1√ó; force a real GPU-compute path") ‚Äî a genuine *compute*-shader instrument body, the scarcest capability in the lab right now. The ¬ß577 fire shipped 997 over it only because 997's **per-room procedurally-synthesized convolution acoustics** (the reverb itself changes as you cross a threshold) is the more direct realization of the ¬ß577 research finding (acoustics-as-navigable-field) and the bigger first-person-embodiment concept; 996's acoustics are fixed (only the drone mix changes). Resurrect 996 next adult GPU-compute cycle ‚Äî the build returned **lint 0/0 + tsc clean** from its builder, so it should drop in fast. **Pre-allocate a fresh number** when resurrecting (996 folder was removed un-committed per the non-winner rule).

## ¬ß584 ‚Äî banked from the cycle-584 KIDS WIDE fire (2 non-winner explorers, built complete, removed un-committed per the non-winner rule)

### ‚≠ê `kids-breath-flute` (RESURRECT-FIRST, kids) ‚Äî blow/hum into the tablet to play a real jet-drive waveguide flute
**Built complete this fire as `1014-kids-breath-flute` (removed un-committed). Pre-allocate a fresh number when resurrecting.**

**Brief (resurrect ~as-is):** a 4-year-old blows or hums into the mic and a glowing aurora air-column actually *sings* ‚Äî the mic's **breath/RMS envelope (NOT pitch detection)** is the excitation pressure for a genuine **jet-drive digital-waveguide flute** (Perry Cook / Julius Smith STK Flute): a single fractional-delay bore, a two-pole lowpass reflection that anchors the fundamental and kills octave-jumping, a non-inverting loop, a cubic non-linear jet for breath-brightening/overblow, a DC blocker. Pitch is picked by tapping big glowing recorder-holes (G-Mixolydian); harder breath = louder + brighter. Builder returned a **passing headless `selfTest()`** (all 7 scale notes oscillate, bounded, within ¬±57¬¢ of target, no octave errors) and tsc/next-lint clean. AudioWorklet (Blob-URL) primary + ScriptProcessor fallback + tap-puff + 2s-idle auto-demo so it always sounds. Files: `flute.ts` 309 / `worklet.ts` 116 / `render.ts` 134 / `page.tsx` 542 / `README.md` 125.

**Why it's the kids resurrect-first:** it is the **lab's first wind/jet physical-modeling instrument** (grep ‚âà0√ó) ‚Äî a genuine never-used-technique ambition hit, and the rare kids build that ships with passing verification. It lost to `1015-ink-garden` this fire ONLY on 06:30-phone-review friction: a flute needs the child to actually blow correctly into a mic (mic permission is the lab's recurring de-select reason) and the ~40‚Äì60¬¢ intonation wobble is verified by offline Goertzel sweeps, not by ear. Resurrect next kids cycle on a fire where mic friction is acceptable; the waveguide is the genuinely novel asset.

### `kids-scarf-choir` ‚Äî wave a scarf/hands in front of the camera; optical-flow MOTION conducts a glowing choir
**Built complete this fire as `1016-kids-scarf-choir` (removed un-committed). Pre-allocate a fresh number when resurrecting.**

**Brief (resurrect ~as-is):** the front camera computes a dense **optical-flow motion field** (Lucas-Kanade-style energy/centroid/swirl on a 48√ó36 downscaled hidden canvas ‚Äî explicitly NOT silhouette segmentation [15√ó in the lab] and NOT MediaPipe landmarks [just shipped in 1008]; pure frame-to-frame motion). Total motion energy swells a warm choir; horizontal motion sweeps a real **I‚Äìvi‚ÄìIV‚ÄìV in C**; vertical maps cutoff/octave; fast swirls trigger chime sparkles. Heavy lerp smoothing (Œ± 0.12‚Äì0.2) makes a 4-year-old's jittery waving gentle. WebGL aurora flow-ribbons (teal‚Üíviolet‚Üígold) + Canvas2D additive fallback. Camera-denied ‚Üí finger-wave pointer mode (same mapping) + 2s-idle auto-demo. Privacy: frames processed locally, never uploaded. Files: `page.tsx` 428 / `flow.ts` 203 / `mapping.ts` 160 / `audio.ts` 219 / `render.ts` 284 / `README.md` 75.

**Why banked (not shipped):** lovely and embodied (the loved camera-kids cluster: `101-camera-song`‚ù§Ô∏è/`217-dance-avatar`‚ù§Ô∏è), and motion-energy‚Üíharmony is fresh-for-kids ‚Äî but (a) camera permission is 06:30-phone friction, (b) it's the *third* recent camera build (1008 hand-cloud just shipped), and (c) the motion-energy normalization divisor (`CELLS¬∑0.16`) is feel-tuned, not validated on a real camera, so lighting/exposure could read a room consistently hot/cold. Resurrect when the camera lane has cooled and after a normalization auto-calibration pass.

## ¬ß585 (cycle 585, adult DEEP ‚Äî *Echo Halls* runners-up, banked un-committed)

The fire took ONE big concept ‚Äî **Echo Halls: turn `977-echo-room-gpu`'s single room into a suite of harmonic rooms you physically walk between** (JURY 2026-06-27 #2 "extend 977 into a multi-cycle spatial instrument" + #3 "make the sim the resonating body") ‚Äî and attacked it 3 ways. Shipped `1019-echo-halls-tonnetz` (neo-Riemannian Tonnetz + WebGPU-compute Gray-Scott body). The other two are strong and worth resurrecting; **pre-allocate fresh numbers when reviving.**

### ‚≠ê RESURRECT-FIRST ‚Äî `echo-halls-walk` ‚Äî first-person, full-sphere, FIND-THE-CHORD-BY-EAR
**Built complete this fire as `1018-echo-halls-walk` (removed un-committed).**

**Brief (resurrect, add a compute body):** a first-person 3D hall where **six harmonic rooms float on a sphere around you at varied azimuth AND elevation** (above / below / behind). Each room owns an HRTF `PannerNode` at its 3D position; **you are the AudioListener and your orientation updates every frame** (`forwardX/Y/Z`+`upX/Y/Z`, `setOrientation` fallback) so the field re-pans as you look ‚Äî you can close your eyes and walk toward a chord by ear. Real C-major neighbourhood (I¬∑vi¬∑IV¬∑ii¬∑V¬∑iii), ghost loops kept from 977, pointer-look+WASD primary / MediaPipe Pose head-turn upgrade / ~1.5s auto-tour between a low and a high room. Hand-rolled WebGL2 first-person render (camera basis copied from `997-resonant-halls`) + Canvas2D top-down minimap fallback (elevation = brightness + ‚Üë/‚Üì). Files: `page.tsx` ~1360 / `README.md`.

**Directly implements this fire's research** (RESEARCH ¬ß585 / **arXiv:2606.24367, 24 Jun 2026** ‚Äî full-sphere human static sound-localisation), and the "Spatial Orchestra" walk-into-bubbles ancestor. **Why banked (not shipped):** the most embodied + spatially-novel of the three, BUT it renders in **WebGL2 with no GPU-compute** ‚Äî and raw-WebGL2-as-primary-output was the over-represented tag in the last-10 audit, so shipping it would have missed JURY #3. **Resurrect by giving it a WebGPU-compute body** (a particle/fluid field that fills each room) ‚Äî that turns its one weakness into the jury's exact ask and makes it the embodied-spatial flagship.

### `echo-halls-flock` ‚Äî top-down plan; a WebGPU-COMPUTE flock that pools in your room DRIVES the sound
**Built complete this fire as `1017-echo-halls-flock` (removed un-committed).**

**Brief (resurrect ~as-is):** a top-down floorplan of 6 rooms (3√ó2 with walls/doorways), each a functional C-major region (V‚ÜíI across the top, ii‚ÜíV‚ÜíI on a diagonal, vi‚ÜíIV deceptive); vertical position re-voices. A **WebGPU compute shader advects ~120k particles** by curl-of-value-noise flow + inverse-square attraction to your body, so they **pool/bloom in your current room**; an in-pass **atomic reduction + non-blocking `mapAsync` readback** feeds the field's local density‚Üílive-voice filter cutoff and energy‚Üía gated shimmer layer ‚Äî **the compute sim literally drives the audio**, the cleanest "sim is the body" loop of the three. Pose‚Üípointer‚Üíauto-demo; WebGPU-compute ‚Üí CPU-advected WebGL2 ‚Üí Canvas2D fallback chain; ghost loops + HRTF from 977. Files: `page.tsx` ~1551 / `README.md`.

**Why banked (not shipped):** excellent compute‚Üíaudio coupling and curl-noise is fresher than the shipped winner's reaction-diffusion ‚Äî but the **top-down plan view is closest in form to 977's existing top-down layout**, the least conceptual leap of the three. Resurrect as a cycle-2 deepening of the Echo Halls thread (graft its compute-readback‚Üíaudio loop onto the Tonnetz or the full-sphere walk), or ship standalone on a fire where the lab wants a clean compute-drives-sound demo rather than a new spatial metaphor.

### ‚≠ê `kids-marble-bells` RESURRECT-FIRST (kids) ‚Äî tilt the tablet like a tray; a marble rolls and rings chime-pegs on a moving chord clock
**Built complete this fire as `1021-kids-marble-bells` (removed un-committed). Pre-allocate a fresh number when resurrecting.**
**Brief (resurrect ~as-is):** hold the tablet flat, *tip* it, and a glowing marble rolls under a **gravity vector taken from `DeviceOrientationEvent` tilt** across a deep-night board studded with jewel-colored chime-pegs. A small **semi-implicit (symplectic) Euler** gravity sim with **real elastic circle‚Äìcircle collisions** (peg + marble‚Äìmarble), wall bounce, rolling friction, speed clamp; resting-orientation calibration on Start; frame-rate-aware tilt smoothing; tilt *magnitude* scales gravity. Harmony is a slow **functional I‚ÄìIV‚ÄìV‚Äìvi clock in C** (NOT pentatonic) with pegs colored by scale degree; a strike resolves to the nearest chord-tone of the *current* chord via **inharmonic struck-bell** synthesis (a few stretched partials + exponential decay) over an always-on root-triad pad. Pointer-drag fallback (set gravity / flick) + 2s-idle auto-tilt demo. Files this fire: `page.tsx` 424 / `physics.ts` 187 / `harmony.ts` 149 / `audio.ts` 135 / `harmony.test.ts` 99 (passing) / `README.md` 86. Named refs: pachinko/pinball + music-box-on-a-tray; Orff Schulwerk handbells; authentic I‚ÄìIV‚ÄìV‚Äìvi pop harmony.
**Why banked (very close):** lovely, embodied, tilt is a loved kids input (`83-kids-tilt-rain`‚ù§Ô∏è), and it copies the jury's praised 1015 recipe (real harmony + sim-as-body + a passing unit test). De-selected ONLY because **"a physics sim IS the resonating body" is the exact conceptual groove the 2026-06-28 jury flagged as calcifying (7√ó)** ‚Äî shipping it would have fed the groove the jury wants varied, whereas the breath-flute adds a *never-used synthesis class*. Resurrect-first next kids fire (the groove will have cooled); clean next steps: two-marble two-player mode; let tilt *direction* (not just gravity) pick the chord; a "drain hole" that re-seeds.

**Built complete this fire as `1022-kids-scarf-choir` (removed un-committed). Pre-allocate a fresh number when resurrecting. (Also banked at ¬ß584 as `1016` ‚Äî this is the second build-complete pass; the optical-flow + auto-calibration are now solid.)**

Reduce to energy / centroid / h-v bias / swirl. **Total energy** swells a warm vowel-pad **choir**; **horizontal centroid** sweeps a real **I‚Äìvi‚ÄìIV‚ÄìV in C**; **vertical** maps cutoff/octave; **fast swirls** trigger diatonic chime sparkles. Heavy lerp smoothing (Œ±‚âà0.15) + an **auto-calibrating energy normalizer** (running max) so bright/dim rooms read consistently. WebGL2 aurora flow-ribbons (teal‚Üíviolet‚Üígold) + Canvas2D fallback; camera-denied ‚Üí finger-wave pointer mode; 2s-idle auto-demo. Privacy: frames processed locally, never shown/uploaded. Files this fire: `page.tsx` 386 / `flow.ts` 181 / `mapping.ts` 110 / `audio.ts` 180 / `render.ts` 300 / `mapping.test.ts` 158 (selfTest 28/28). ## ¬ß587 banked seeds (2026-06-28, cycle 587 ¬∑ ADULT ¬∑ WIDE ‚Äî 3 orthogonal live-data sonifications, shipped `1024-wiki-bells`; 2 built-complete non-winners below, removed un-committed per the non-winner rule)

The fire ran three orthogonal **live-real-world-data** adult briefs ‚Äî each non-pointer (the jury banned pointer-as-primary), each Canvas2D (the jury flagged WebGL2-flat at ~9√ó), each long-form/evolving (minute-5‚âÝminute-1), each "music about something other than music" (JURY 2026-06-28 #5). Shipped the Wikipedia carillon (named Hatnote reference + most-reliably-live + most surprising). The two runners-up are strong and clean ‚Äî **pre-allocate fresh numbers when reviving.**

### `airspace-organ` (adult) ‚Äî the live airspace overhead as a slowly-recomposing just-intonation chord on a phosphor radar
**Built complete this fire as `1023-airspace-organ` (removed un-committed). Pre-allocate a fresh number when resurrecting.**
**Brief (resurrect ~as-is):** the live OpenSky Network anonymous states feed (bounding-box per region preset) ‚Üí each aircraft is one sustained additive voice (sine + triangle sub through per-voice gain + `StereoPanner` + vibrato LFO), capped at the 16 contacts nearest the scope centre into master ‚Üí compressor. **Mapping:** baro-altitude ‚Üí pitch snapped to a 5-limit just-intonation lattice `[1, 9/8, 5/4, 4/3, 3/2, 5/3, 15/8]` (higher = higher); bearing/scope-X ‚Üí stereo pan; ground-speed ‚Üí vibrato depth+rate (Doppler-like shimmer); proximity ‚Üí amplitude; appear/cross/leave ‚Üí voice spawn/retire so the chord recomposes (minute 5 ‚âÝ minute 1). **Viz:** a clinical ATC phosphor Canvas2D radar ‚Äî range rings, bearing ticks, a sweeping conic-gradient radar line, blips green‚Üíamber by altitude, voiced contacts haloed with thin monospace callsign/FL labels + velocity leaders. **Infra:** guarded POST proxy route (`api/route.ts`, OpenSky is CORS-blocked in-browser; `maxDuration=30`, treats 429/non-200 as failure), client polls ‚â§1/~75s + interpolates between polls; synthetic-airspace fallback (planes enter/cross/leave with realistic altitude/speed/bearing) so it's fully demoable with zero network; auto-start after ~2s idle; `text-amber-300/95` "simulated airspace" notice. Files this fire: `page.tsx` 771 / `api/route.ts` / `README.md`. **Ambition 3/5** (#2 ‚â•3 subsystems = 4; #1 varies-relationship via non-pointer world-as-instrument).
**Why banked (not shipped):** the most *visually* striking of the three (the radar is gorgeous) and the cleanest "the sky tunes itself" metaphor. De-selected on **data-source fragility** ‚Äî OpenSky's anonymous endpoint is CORS-blocked from the browser AND has been heavily rate-limited / auth-gated since 2025, so for a 06:30 solo reviewer it would fall to **synthetic far more often** than the shipped `1024`'s keyless, CORS-open EventStreams, weakening the "the live world plays it" promise that is the whole point of a data piece. Resurrect when paired with a reliable aircraft source (or an authenticated OpenSky token via env, if Karel adds one); clean next steps: Doppler pitch-shift on true radial velocity; a "follow one flight" listen mode; altitude-layer filtering.

### `isobar-winds` (adult) ‚Äî a continent's live weather as one breathing just-intonation wind-organ on an isobar map
**Built complete this fire as `1025-isobar-winds` (removed un-committed). Pre-allocate a fresh number when resurrecting.**
**Brief (resurrect ~as-is):** live METAR airport observations (`aviationweather.gov/api/data/metar`, keyless) for a ring of ~8 stations per region preset (US West / Europe / Pacific) ‚Üí each station is one organ pipe (fundamental sine + detuned chorus partial + band-pass-filtered noise "breath" ‚Üí per-pipe gain ‚Üí pan ‚Üí master), capped to the ring. **Mapping:** barometric pressure ‚Üí drone pitch snapped to a just-intonation lattice (low = lower/ominous, high = brighter); wind speed ‚Üí amplitude + noise breathiness; wind direction ‚Üí pan; temperature ‚Üí filter cutoff/brightness; dewpoint spread ‚Üí chorus detune. A gentle LFO breath keeps it alive between ‚â§1/~90s polls; the continental chord recomposes as the real weather drifts. **Viz:** Canvas2D inverse-distance pressure heat field + cyan iso-pressure contour lines + drifting dashed wind streamlines with barb heads + glowing station nodes (amber warm / cyan cold). **Infra:** guarded POST proxy route (`api/route.ts`, `maxDuration=30`, station list in body, 502 on upstream failure); synthetic-weather fallback (summed slow sines + bounded random walks per station) so zero-network demoable; auto-start after ~2s idle; `text-amber-300/95` "simulated weather" notice. Files this fire: `page.tsx` 746 / `api/route.ts` / `README.md`. **Ambition 3/5** (#2 ‚â•3 subsystems = 4; #1 varies-relationship via non-pointer atmosphere-as-instrument).
**Why banked (not shipped):** strong, clean, and the warmest/most meditative of the three. De-selected because it's the **third just-intonation slow-drone of the set** ‚Äî the least *surprising* axis, and the shipped `1024`'s event-driven inharmonic bells + the named Hatnote reference carry more novelty and a higher ambition score. Resurrect on a fire where the lab wants a warm continental ambient piece; clean next steps: add precipitation/`wxString` ‚Üí granular rain texture; let a front passing (pressure-gradient spike between adjacent stations) trigger a swell; a "your location" auto-geolocated ring.

### `kids-marble-bells` (kids) ‚Äî ‚≠ê RESURRECT-FIRST ‚Äî tilt a tray of glowing marbles so gravity rolls them into a ring of pitched bells
**Built complete this fire as `1026-kids-marble-bells` (removed un-committed). Pre-allocate a fresh number when resurrecting.**
**Brief (resurrect ~as-is):** device tilt (DeviceOrientation beta/gamma ‚Üí a 2D gravity vector; pointer-drag fallback) rolls ~10‚Äì16 marbles in a circular tray under cheap circle-circle + circular-wall collisions; a ring of ~6‚Äì8 "bell" pegs sits around the rim. A marble striking a peg above a velocity threshold rings an **inharmonic struck-bell** (partials ~1/2.0/2.76/5.4, fast decay, velocity‚Üíamp+brightness). **Harmony:** the pegs are the chord-tones of a slow **I‚ÄìIV‚ÄìV‚Äìvi** progression in C (C/F/G/Am), advancing every ~4s and gliding to the new chord ‚Äî real moving functional harmony, never pentatonic. Always-on C2+G2 drone. Canvas2D additive-glow tray (amber/gold marbles + trails, violet/teal rim, pegs bloom on strike). ‚â•88px Start gesture-gates AudioContext + iOS tilt permission; 2s-idle auto-demo swirls gravity; full teardown. Files this fire: `core.ts` (pure sim+harmony+selfTest) / `audio.ts` / `render.ts` / `page.tsx` (371) / `marble-bells.test.ts` (5 cases) / `README.md`. **selfTest passes** (every peg freq is a C-major-scale member AND a chord-tone across all 4 chords; integrator bounded over thousands of swirling steps; I‚ÜíIV‚ÜíV‚Üívi‚ÜíI cycle). Named refs: Risset/Chowning inharmonic bell timbre; loved tilt input `83-kids-tilt-rain`‚ù§Ô∏è. **Ambition 2/5** (#2 ‚â•3 subsystems = 4; #3 named ref). 
**Why banked (very close):** lovely, tilt is a loved kids input, copies the jury's praised 1015 recipe (real harmony + sim-as-body + a passing unit test), zero permission friction. De-selected **only** because it is the *safe, jury-recipe* entry while `1027`'s chaotic double pendulum is the genuinely **never-used technique** the ambition mandate keeps demanding ‚Äî 1027 is the bigger swing. **Resurrect-first next kids fire.** Clean next steps: let tilt *direction* (not just gravity magnitude) pick the chord; a two-marble two-player mode; the builder modeled the bell glide per-strike rather than via a sustained per-bell `setTargetAtTime` ramp (no sustained bell oscillators) ‚Äî give each peg a sustained quiet oscillator and ramp it for smoother retuning.

### `kids-star-weaver` (kids) ‚Äî tap glowing stars into the sky, connect them into a constellation, and a comet-spark walks the shape and SINGS it
**Built complete this fire as `1028-kids-star-weaver` (removed un-committed). Pre-allocate a fresh number when resurrecting.**
**Brief (resurrect ~as-is):** touch ‚Äî tap empty space ‚Üí place a glowing star (node) that chimes when born; tap a star then another ‚Üí draw a glowing string (edge) that plays the interval. A **graph-traversal sequencer**: a glowing "spark" continuously walks the edges (least-visited-edge greedy walk, never teleports) at tempo, ringing each star it arrives at ‚Äî so the *shape the child drew* becomes a self-playing melody/loop. Stars gently drift under a **bounded force-directed layout** (springs on edges + mild repulsion + center pull, clamped) so the constellation feels alive. **Harmony:** each star's vertical position picks a C-major scale degree (higher=higher); a slow **I‚Äìvi‚ÄìIV‚ÄìV** bed advances every ~4s; on arrival the star's note is nudged to the nearest live-chord-tone ‚Äî real moving harmony, never pentatonic. Always-on drone. Canvas2D additive-glow night sky (twinkling dust, glowing lines, comet-spark with fading tail, sing-blooms; indigo‚Üíviolet). ‚â•72px Start gesture-gates AudioContext; 2s-idle auto-demo builds a small constellation + walks it; full teardown. Files this fire: `page.tsx` (411) / `audio.ts` / `graph.ts` (pure model + walk + layout) / `harmony.ts` (pure) / `render.ts` / `weaver.test.ts` (8 cases) / `README.md`. **selfTest 8/8** (every nudged note in-scale + a chord-tone across all 4 chords incl. F and B [impossible in C-pentatonic]; layout bounded/NaN-free over 1500 steps; walker visits only valid neighbors over 500 steps). Named refs: constellation/star-myth; force-directed graph layout (Eades; Fruchterman‚ÄìReingold); graph-traversal sequencing (network music). **Ambition 2‚Äì3/5** (#2 ‚â•3 subsystems = 4; #3 named refs). 
**Why banked (not shipped):** genuinely charming, the most *compositional* of the three (the child authors a reusable little loop by drawing topology), and a clean, well-verified build. De-selected because the **graph/constellation-sequencer concept** is adjacent to the lab's well-worn orbit/star cluster (`308-orbit-choir`, `932-tilt-orrery`, plenty more) and its core technique isn't new, whereas `1027` brings a genuinely never-used engine. **Resurrect** for a "compose-and-it-plays-back" fire; clean next steps: a two-child shared-screen mode (two sparks, two voices); let edge *length* set the interval/duration so the drawn geometry is the rhythm; persist/replay the constellation.

## ¬ß589 banked seeds (2026-06-28, cycle 589 ¬∑ ADULT ¬∑ DEEP ‚Äî *Echo Halls II* runners-up, banked un-committed)

The fire took ONE big concept ‚Äî **Echo Halls II: cycle 2 of the lab's only-5/5 spatial-harmony thread (`1019-echo-halls-tonnetz`) ‚Äî a walk-BY-EAR spatial-harmony instrument where you orient yourself among HRTF harmonic rooms in 3D and a WebGPU-compute particle field is each room's resonating body** (JURY 2026-06-28 #2 "run the Echo Halls thread, don't abandon it" + #3 "keep WebGPU compute as the body" + #1 "vary the human‚Üîsound *relationship*") ‚Äî and attacked it via **3 competing technical approaches**. Shipped `1029-echo-halls-sphere` (full-sphere first-person find-by-ear; the explicitly-banked ¬ß585 ‚≠ê resurrect-first). The other two are strong and built-complete; **pre-allocate fresh numbers when reviving.**

### ‚≠ê RESURRECT-FIRST (adult) ‚Äî `echo-halls-flock` ‚Äî a 100k WebGPU-compute swarm POOLS in your room and its MEASURED density/energy DRIVES the sound
**Built complete this fire as `1030-echo-halls-flock` (removed un-committed). Pre-allocate a fresh number when resurrecting.**
**Brief (resurrect ~as-is):** a top-down floor of 6 harmonic rooms (C-major regions: V‚ÜíI across the top, ii‚ÜíV‚ÜíI diagonal, vi/IV deceptive; vertical position re-voices). A **WGSL compute shader advects ~80‚Äì120k particles** by curl-of-value-noise flow + inverse-square attraction toward YOUR presence point, so they pool/bloom in your current room; an **in-pass atomic reduction** measures local **density** (count within a radius of you) + total **kinetic energy** (fixed-point `atomicAdd` of v¬∑v), copied to a **double-buffered staging buffer** and read back via **non-blocking `mapAsync`**; density ‚Üí the active live voice's low-pass cutoff (more pooled = brighter), energy ‚Üí a gated shimmer gain ‚Äî **the swarm you see literally controls the sound, no proxy** (the honest "sim IS the body" loop, the cleanest of the three). Per-room HRTF `PannerNode`, listener follows your presence, Gaussian wet-gain cross-fades. WebGPU-compute ‚Üí CPU-flock(~3‚Äì8k)-in-WebGL2 (identical aggregate math) ‚Üí Canvas2D fallback chain. Pointer/WASD presence (continuous, not tap-to-trigger) + 2s auto-walk. **Verified:** `harmony.test.ts` **8156 assertions, 0 fail** (room weights sum‚Üí1 & peak at occupied room; chords diatonic; density‚Üícutoff & energy‚Üígain monotonic+bounded); builder ran full `next build` ‚Üí "Compiled successfully". Files this fire: `page.tsx` 894 / `harmony.ts` 228 / `flock.ts` 188 (CPU reference = fallback) / `harmony.test.ts` 177 / `README.md` 124. Named refs: Reynolds *Boids* (1987); Bridson curl-noise (SIGGRAPH 2007); Cardiff *Forty Part Motet*; WebGPU baseline 2026. **Ambition ~4/5** (#1 first honest GPU-readback-drives-audio flock in the lab ¬∑ #2 ‚â•4 subsystems [compute flock + atomic readback + per-room HRTF + harmony state + render] ¬∑ #3 named refs ¬∑ #5 RESEARCH ¬ß589).
**Why banked (very close, the strongest runner-up):** it is the most *honest* sim‚Üíaudio loop of the three and the directest #3 answer ‚Äî but JURY 2026-06-28 #1 explicitly warns that **"a physics sim IS the instrument's resonating body" is the calcifying 7√ó groove** to *vary*, and its own ¬ß585 ancestor note called the top-down-plan form "the least conceptual leap." The shipped sphere makes spatial *orientation* (find-by-ear) the primary mechanic while keeping a compute body as resonator ‚Äî so it varies the relationship (#1) where the flock leans into the groove. **Resurrect-first next adult fire** as cycle 3 of the thread: graft this honest density‚Üícutoff readback loop onto the sphere's per-room compute bodies (the sphere ships one shared body driving shimmer-gain only; the flock's per-room density‚Üícutoff is the deepening). Clean next steps: per-room buffer sets so all 6 fields evolve at once; two-presence WebRTC shared field (the lab is still 0√ó multi-user).

### `echo-halls-gyro` (adult) ‚Äî hold the phone, CLOSE YOUR EYES, and TURN YOUR BODY to find a chord; the gyroscope IS the listener orientation
**Built complete this fire as `1031-echo-halls-gyro` (removed un-committed). Pre-allocate a fresh number when resurrecting.**
**Brief (resurrect ~as-is):** six HRTF harmonic rooms on a full sphere (one overhead, one underfoot, vi/iii behind). **`DeviceOrientationEvent` (alpha/beta/gamma ‚Üí forward vector) drives the Web Audio `AudioListener` orientation every frame** (iOS `requestPermission` gated behind the Start tap, narrow-typed; `setOrientation` fallback) so physically turning re-pans the binaural field ‚Äî you find a chord with eyes closed. **Dwelling your gaze on a room ~1.2s LOCKS it** (swell + confirming bell) so turning becomes *playing a progression* I‚Üívi‚ÜíIV‚ÜíV by looking ‚Äî a pure dwell-lock state machine, no touch. Calm full-bleed Canvas2D aurora brightens toward the focused room with a compass ring + elevation chevron (screen deliberately secondary ‚Äî eyes-closed is the point; Pauline Oliveros *Deep Listening* framing). Desktop/no-gyro ‚Üí pointer-drag auto-fallback (mode shown in emerald); 2s auto-rotate hands-free demo visiting a low + high room. **Verified:** `geometry.test.ts` **50 assertions pass** (euler‚Üíforward unit + cardinal cases; focusWeights sum‚Üí1 & peak; dwell locks after threshold & releases on turn-away; chords diatonic; full-sphere spread). Files this fire: `geometry.ts` 301 / `geometry.test.ts` 149 / `page.tsx` 631 / `README.md` 109. Named refs: Cardiff *Forty Part Motet*; Spatial Orchestra (2510.23848); full-sphere localization (2606.24367); **Pauline Oliveros *Deep Listening***; Sonic4D (2506.15759). **Ambition ~3/5** (#2 ‚â•3 subsystems ¬∑ #3 named refs ¬∑ #1 varies relationship via embodied gyro).
**Why banked (not shipped):** the most embodied + zero-friction-on-mobile of the three (gyro-as-listener is a genuinely fresh input, and dwell-lock-to-play-a-progression is a lovely interaction) ‚Äî de-selected ONLY because its resonating body is **Canvas2D-only** (no GPU-compute ‚Üí misses the jury's #3 scarce-capability ask, which the sphere keeps), and the gyro magic needs an on-device iOS permission flow that a 06:30 *desktop* reviewer won't trigger (it falls to pointer-drag, where the sphere already lives). Resurrect on a fire where the lab wants a deliberately mobile/eyes-closed piece, or graft the **dwell-lock-to-play-a-progression** mechanic onto the sphere (the sphere currently only blooms the faced room; dwell-to-lock would let you *compose* a progression by gaze). Clean next steps: quaternion sensor fusion (the current alpha‚Üíyaw is linear & drifts); WebXR head-tracking; the measured eyes-closed localisation mini-game (target chord plays ‚Üí turn to it ‚Üí score angular error).

---

### Banked S590 (cycle 590, KIDS ¬∑ WIDE ‚Äî built but not shipped; strong, resurrect-ready)

**`1034-kids-rain-chimes` ‚Äî tilt a waterfall of light through singing chimes (WebGPU compute sim-as-body, kids).** *Built to demoable this fire, not shipped (winner was 1033).* Tilt (`deviceorientation` gamma/beta, iOS `requestPermission` from the Start tap; arrow-key/auto-sway fallback) sets a gravity vector for ~3000 glowing droplets. A **WebGPU compute shader** (semi-implicit Euler, line-for-line mirror of a pure `sim.ts`; degrades to the SAME integrator on CPU/Canvas2D) rains them through 4 horizontal chime bands; per frame the sim is **read back** and band-crossing counts ring inharmonic bell tones ‚Äî the 4 bands are the 4 voiced tones of a live **I‚Äìvi‚ÄìIV‚ÄìV**, so heavier rain = louder chord tone (genuine functional harmony, not pentatonic snap). Ships with `sim.test.ts` (pure TS + `console.assert`, no framework, no auto-run) verifying free-fall position vs analytic and exactly-one band crossing. **Why strong:** directest kids-lane copy of 1015's celebrated recipe (sim-IS-body + real harmony + unit test) AND uses the scarce/encouraged WebGPU-compute capability; aurora rain palette. **Why not shipped:** the jury flagged "a sim IS the instrument body" as the 7√ó monoculture ‚Äî vary the human‚Üîsound *relationship* ‚Äî and the kids lane already leans physics-sim (1027/1015/1005/1008); shipping another would lean into the groove. **Resurrect:** next KIDS fire that wants the WebGPU/sim slot ‚Äî it's ~95% done (the full build sits in git history of this cycle's losers; rebuild from the README spec). Known limit noted by builder: GPU readback is async (one in flight) so crossing counts lag render by 1‚Äì2 frames (inaudible).

**`1032-kids-shadow-choir` ‚Äî your body's shadow sings (camera silhouette ‚Üí functional harmony, kids).** *Built to demoable this fire, not shipped.* `getUserMedia({video})` ‚Üí 64√ó48 **luminance background-difference segmentation** (auto-captures ~1s reference, slow background adaptation for lighting drift) ‚Üí silhouette **centroid-Y** picks which of 4 diatonic chords (**I‚Äìvi‚ÄìIV‚ÄìV**) sounds, **coverage area** stacks 1‚Äì4 "ah" choir voices. Canvas2D additive glow auras + drifting light particles; warm sunset palette; graceful no-camera fallback (drifting blob runs the same engine, `text-rose-300` notice). Non-pointer (body position/size is the only control). **Why strong:** fresh camera-body input on kids lane, four live subsystems (capture + segmentation + harmony + glow), real harmony. **Why not shipped:** no unit test (vs 1033/1034 both committed one), Canvas2D glow over WebGL, and segmentation is lighting-dependent; 1033's voice-pitch input was the more novel grep-0√ó direction + closed the research chain. **Resurrect:** add a `seg.test.ts` for the centroid/coverage math, consider MediaPipe selfie-segmentation for robustness, pair with the shadow as a *duet* (two silhouettes = two chord voices).

## ¬ß592 ‚Äî banked from the first psychedelic WIDE fire (cycle 592)

Cycle 592 was the lab's first altered-states cycle (Karel's 2026-06-28 steer). WIDE fire of 3 explorers across the spectrum; shipped `1038-form-constant` (the log-polar/form-constant engine, intense pole). Input **non-pointer** (breath). ## ¬ß599 banked seeds (2026-06-29, cycle 599 ‚Äî adult psychedelic WIDE, de-selected non-winners)

The other two built to demoable and were removed un-committed per the non-winner rule ‚Äî both strong, both discharge standing jury asks, resurrect-ready:

- **`1052-piano-bloom`** ‚≠ê RESURRECT-FIRST ¬∑ status: `queued` ¬∑ `state: psilocybin warm-waves ¬∑ pole: cosmic-ambient` ‚Äî **discharges jury #2 (use Karel's real Path piano as carrier) AND #4 (bring WebGPU compute back as the resonating body) in one piece.** Karel's recorded piano (Welcome Home, same-origin `/api/audio/[id]`) is the carrier wave; its amplitude/onset envelope feeds a **WebGPU-compute Gray-Scott reaction-diffusion field** (WGSL compute + render passes, ping-pong storage buffers, 256¬≤ grid) that IS the resonating body ‚Äî and you **actively drag the canvas to inject growth seeds** (touch + music both drive it; the field accumulates so minute-5‚âÝminute-1). Robust Canvas-2D RD twin (`CpuField` in `field.ts`) on any WebGPU failure; both paths detected + labelled. Audio carrier is best-effort: a track-URL/id input follows same-origin `/api/audio/[id]` `{url}` ‚Üí MediaElementSource‚ÜíAnalyser, with a guaranteed always-warm **Karplus-Strong felt-piano** (`startFeltPiano`, slow diatonic progression) fallback. Files were `page.tsx` + `field.ts` + `README.md`; builder verified `tsc --noEmit` clean + eslint clean on the folder. **Why not shipped:** 1051 was the directest answer to jury #1 (a *played* instrument vs. a conduct-the-field piece) and is lab-first hand-tracking; 1052's real-piano leg is only best-effort here (unknown track ids / signed-URL CORS in this env) so it usually lands on the felt-piano fallback. **Resurrect when:** the next psych fire wants the WebGPU-compute slot or to finally land the real-piano carrier ‚Äî pair it with a known-good Welcome Home track id so the carrier is real, not fallback. - **`1053-skin-membrane`** ¬∑ status: `queued` ¬∑ `state: salvia 2D-membrane / hypnagogic surface-melt ¬∑ pole: intense (uncanny)` ‚Äî a tactile instrument for the salvia "reality is a thin sheet you can press, pull and tear" phenomenology, and the lab's **first mass-spring physics sim in the psych lane**. A 60√ó60 mass-spring membrane (`membrane.ts`: structural/shear/bend springs, pinned boundary, semi-implicit Euler, 2 substeps) you press into with **multi-touch Pointer Events** ‚Äî it resists, ripples, rings on release, and **tears/phase-shifts** under high tension to reveal a shifted color-world. Canvas-2D deformed lattice (shaded quads, `lighter` glow, afterimage bloom). Audio is **physically-grounded inharmonic modal synthesis** tuned to real circular-drum-head Bessel-zero ratios (1, 1.59, 2.14, 2.30, 2.65, 2.92, 3.16, 3.50) + a resonant noise "skin grain": pressing excites, releasing rings/decays, harder pushes = brighter/more dissonant (the uncanny edge). Auto-ripple + mouse fallback so it's never dead. Builder confirmed full `npm run build` ‚úì (651 pages). **Why not shipped:** 1051's hand-tracking input was the more novel lab-first and the directest screensaver antidote; both are genuine instruments. **Resurrect when:** the lane wants a tactile/physics piece ‚Äî it's ~complete, rebuild from RESEARCH ¬ß599 brief + the self-assessment above. Named refs: salvia 2D-membrane phenomenology (PSYCHEDELIC.md); mass-spring cloth/membrane sim + physical modal synthesis of a vibrating drum head.

## ¬ß600 banked seed ‚Äî 2026-06-29 (cycle 600 ¬∑ ADULT ¬∑ PSYCHEDELIC ¬∑ DEEP ‚Äî the de-selected non-winner sibling, built complete by a parallel builder + builder-audited TS/ESLint-clean, then `rm -rf`'d per the non-winner rule; shipped `1052-piano-bloom` = the psych lane's first WebGPU-compute Gray-Scott RD instrument + first real-piano carrier)

An INSTRUMENT you conduct, not a screensaver. **Technique:** WebGPU-compute **boids flock** (separation/alignment/cohesion + a conductor attractor/repeller + swirl term; positions/velocities in ping-pong storage buffers; additive point-list render; aims ~120k particles, clamped to adapter limits; strided-neighbour approximation ‚Äî honestly noted), with a real **Canvas2D ~3k-particle CPU fallback**. **The played loop (bidirectional):** pointer = conductor (gather / swirl / scatter-on-shift-or-right-button); the flock's **centroid x ‚Üí grain scrub, dispersion ‚Üí grain spread/density, mean speed ‚Üí grain pitch, proximity-to-conductor ‚Üí gain**; a spectral-flux `OnsetMeter` (AnalyserNode) makes audio transients **spawn particle bursts** ‚Äî notes become flocks. **Audio tiers:** file-drop ‚Üí `decodeFile` ‚Üí `GranularVoice` over the real recording; zero-file ‚Üí `FeltVoice` felt-piano pad steered by the same stats (so it sounds on a phone glance). **Palette:** deep space-indigo void, particles glowing teal‚Üígold‚Üíwhite at high velocity, additive nebula. **Why banked not shipped:** equally clears the ambition floor + all three JURY bans (played, not passive-mic; particle nebula, not fragment-shader; WebGPU compute body), and is **love-aligned** (the loved particle cluster 130-tsl-particle-compute / 236-particle-life-song / 262-aurora-particle / 321-spectral-flight) ‚Äî but in a DEEP two-approach fire 1052 edged it on (a) tighter research‚Üíbuild chain (1052 directly implements the ¬ß600 Reactive-Audio-"Growth" reader-on-RD-field model) and (b) diversity (a warm psilocybin RD body is *further* from the lab's already-heavy, already-loved cosmic-particle cluster ‚Äî the hard anti-monoculture gate beat the soft love-bias). **Resurrect next:** it's the love-aligned counterweight; ship it when the lane wants the cosmic-ambient particle pole. Builder reported `tsc --noEmit` 0 errors + eslint clean on the folder; uses `_shared/prototype-nav` + `_shared/webgpu.d.ts` (READ-only). Folders: `page.tsx` + `flock.ts` (dual WebGPU/CPU backend) + `granular.ts` (GranularVoice/FeltVoice/OnsetMeter/decodeFile) + `README.md`.

Resurrect this one.)_

The lab's **first Shepard‚ÄìRisset endless-glissando engine**, made into a PLAYABLE instrument paired with the shared form-constant tunnel. **Technique (`shepard.ts`):** 9 sine partials spaced one octave apart, each amplitude-weighted by a fixed Gaussian window over log-frequency, a wrapping global `transpose` so pitch seems to rise forever; a warm detuned drone (sines + fifth + lowpassed saw) + convolution reverb tail (Deep Listening space) + compressor. ## ¬ß609 banked seeds ‚Äî 2026-06-30 (cycle 609 ¬∑ ADULT ¬∑ PSYCHEDELIC ¬∑ WIDE ‚Äî the two de-selected non-winner siblings, each built complete by a parallel builder + builder-audited tsc/ESLint-clean, then `cp`'d to /tmp/dream-losers-609/ and `rm -rf`'d per the non-winner rule; shipped `1066-cosmic-web` = the lab's first Physarum WebGPU-compute slime-mold instrument)

- **`1067-boundless-breath`** ‚úÖ SHIPPED cycle 610 (was ‚≠êRESURRECT-FIRST) ¬∑ `demoable` ¬∑ live at `/dream/1067-boundless-breath` ‚Äî **CONSUMED** as the cycle-610 DEEP winner (a fresh rebuild from this spec; the ¬ß609 build was `rm -rf`'d). **Correction:** the ¬ß609 ambition claim "lab's FIRST Shepard‚ÄìRisset engine" is **false** (`40/132/187-shepard-tone` predate it); the honest fresh axis kept in the shipped README is the **breath-coupled, vection-paired, played** Shepard, grounded in today's auditory-vection research (RESEARCH ¬ß610). See STATE/INDEX ¬ß610. Original ¬ß609 spec kept below for provenance ‚Äî "What if you could breathe a galaxy in and out ‚Äî inhale draws a vast point-cloud of stars into a single luminous core, exhale releases them to boundless drift ‚Äî while an endless Shepard‚ÄìRisset tone rises forever and never arrives?" The scarce **three.js** slot + the lab's **first Shepard‚ÄìRisset endless-glissando engine** + a genuinely fresh **active-breath-as-control** input (the jury explicitly wants breath-as-*control*, not breath-as-ambient). **Technique:** a `THREE.Points` buffer of 120k stars, each with a *drift* rest position (flattened nebular sphere) and a *core* target; a single `uGather` uniform lerps every point between them on the GPU (additive blending, FogExp2, center bloom that brightens as stars gather, slow rotation + parallax; indigo void, stars white‚Üígold‚Üícyan). **Breath envelope (`breath.ts`):** mic mode samples low-band FFT energy through two followers (fast ~0.5 s vs slow ~4 s baseline) ‚Äî rising = inhale (gather‚Üí1), falling = exhale (release‚Üí0) ‚Äî integrated into the gather scalar; **auto-paced 5.5 breaths/min raised-cosine LFO** when no mic (so it breathes itself, fully alive headless); pointer-hold = manual inhale. **Shepard‚ÄìRisset (`shepard.ts`):** 9 octave-spaced sine partials under a fixed Gaussian log-frequency window with a wrapping global transpose (fresh partial always enters at the bottom as the top one leaves ‚Üí rises forever); ascent rate `0.012 + 0.05¬∑breath` oct/s, breath also opens a 500‚Üí3200 Hz master lowpass + brightens a high-shelf. Under it: a just-intonation drone (fund + 3:2 + 2:1, detuned twin) + code-generated ~4 s convolution reverb (deepens on exhale) + compressor. Canvas2D star fallback if no WebGL; visuals breathe even with no audio device. **Why banked not shipped:** all three cleared the ambition floor + dodged every ban; 1066 edged it on the stronger research‚Üíbuild chain (it IS this cycle's slime-mold dive) + higher surprise. **Resurrect FIRST when** the lane wants a meditative breath piece, the scarce **three.js** output, or to finally land **Shepard‚ÄìRisset in-lane** (banked in 1062 too, still 0√ó shipped). Ambition (honest 3/5): #1 never-used (Shepard‚ÄìRisset engine; first three.js point-cloud breath instrument) ¬∑ #2 ‚â•3 subsystems (point cloud + breath analyser + Shepard bank + drone + reverb + render) ¬∑ #3 named refs (Roger Shepard / Jean-Claude Risset; Lutz & Davidson PNAS 2004 jhƒÅna gamma; resonant 5.5/min breathing; Pauline Oliveros *Deep Listening*). Files were `page.tsx` + `scene.ts` + `shepard.ts` + `audio.ts` + `breath.ts` + `README.md`; builder verified scoped tsc + eslint clean. **The one allowed external import is `three` (already a project dep ‚Äî do NOT add three.js addon packages; use pure-core or hand-write OrbitControls).** Brief: RESEARCH ¬ß609.

- **`1068-attractor-temple`** ¬∑ status `queued` ¬∑ `state: hypnagogic geometric drift / DMT-adjacent "machine-elf architecture", kept calm ¬∑ pole: cosmic-ambient` ‚Äî "What if you could reach into the hidden machinery behind the visions ‚Äî a glowing strange-attractor whose two secret constants you bend with your hands, watching hundreds of thousands of points reorganize into impossible self-symmetric architecture, while the trajectory's own motion is the music?" The lab's **first strange-attractor flow piece**, and another **WebGPU-compute resonating body** (JURY #4). **Technique:** each point a `vec4f` (xyz + per-point speed) in a storage buffer (~350k, clamped to adapter limits); per frame an **integrate** compute pass advances every point a few Euler substeps of the chosen ODE ‚Äî **Thomas** cyclically-symmetric `dx=(sin y ‚àí b¬∑x)¬∑s` (cyclic; `b` damping ~0.10‚Äì0.34 bifurcates a lattice volume ‚Üî tidy limit cycle near ~0.32) or **Aizawa** (toroidal shell + spike; expose `a` stretch + `b` shell) ‚Äî with the live hand-bent params + autonomous drift as uniforms; diverged/NaN points re-hashed into the spawn volume so the cloud never dies. A **reduce** compute pass (every ~4 frames) writes partial sums (speed/centroid/radius); read back via `mapAsync` (one in flight) ‚Üí `FlowState { meanSpeed, spread, cx, cy }`: mean-speed ‚Üí drone lowpass/brightness, spread ‚Üí reverb send + voicing width, centroid-x ‚Üí pan, centroid-y ‚Üí which degree of a just pentatonic a sparse bell plays; **bell triggers fire on speed/spread threshold crossings so a bifurcation is an audible shower of bells.** Additive point-sprite render, self-built orbiting camera, teal‚Üíviolet‚Üígold palette. CPU 28k-point fallback (same ODE, same `FlowState`, additive Canvas2D with trail fade). **Play:** drag bends the two constants, shift-drag orbits, wheel pushes the 2nd constant, thomas/aizawa toggle; autonomous LFO drift keeps it reorganizing (minute-5 ‚âÝ minute-1). **Why banked not shipped:** strong + genuinely playable, but its state tag ("hypnagogic/DMT-adjacent, kept calm") is a slightly looser fit to the cosmic-ambient brief than 1066's awe-scaled cosmic web, and 1066 carried the research‚Üíbuild chain. **Resurrect when** the lane wants a WebGPU strange-attractor played body or a deterministic-chaos instrument. Ambition (honest 3/5): #1 never-used (strange-attractor flow; first GPU attractor integration in the lab) ¬∑ #2 ‚â•3 subsystems (attractor integrator + GPU reduce readback + JI drone/bell engine + orbit camera + render = 5) ¬∑ #3 named refs (Ren√© Thomas; Aizawa; Clifford A. Pickover; Edward Lorenz). Files were `page.tsx` + `gpu.ts` + `attractor.ts` + `render.ts` + `audio.ts` + `README.md`; builder verified scoped tsc + eslint clean. Brief: RESEARCH ¬ß609. **`1068-attractor-temple` STILL QUEUED** (this cycle's `1068` slot went to a different `vection-well` build ‚Äî renumber the attractor to a fresh ‚â•**1069** on revival).
